content: after using duolingo for over a decade, and with a current 2200+ day streak, i think i am finally done with this app. it was fun to learn at first, but with the addition of AI, things have just gotten worse. a lot of the words or sentence structures are wrong. now i can't even just "practice for hearts" - instead you have to watch ads. no thanks, i will just learn elsewhere. not wasting time watching ads or paying to learn wrong things. on to the next app.

userName: Do as I say Not as I do!
content: Edit: so, they changed the ability to just practice which the worst update they could've done. One main reason I loved it was the ability to just practice, HOWEVER learning a new language is hard enough but forcing you to move forward when your not ready or lose your streak. They need to change it back. Other updates are fine but they should have never changed practice😞 Original (part): Love it. I keep forgetting you can tap on the words to get the translation. They don't make that an issue.

I used to love duolingo. one of the best features of being able to practice for hearts, that way when you were doing poorly, it gave you additional practice. they changed it so you could only get one heart through practice, and now they've completely taken away the option to practice for hearts. it's nothing but a big money grab overwhelming you with advertisements. I guess it's time for me to find a new language app.

Been using this consistently for almost a year and I hate it more than ever. You can't look up vocab words you've learned, I have to go to a different app for that. The rewards are super buggy and frequently don't reward. The AI voices are incomprehensible a lot of the time. And many native language speakers find their choices to teach people absurd. These are all easy fixes and other apps do them well. Duolingo is living off of its marketing team, because the underlying program is abysmal.

too much repetition of some easy things, then" you learned 25 new words today." not enough explanation for how to construct sentences and tenses, just a lot of guessing. I've been doing this for 250 days and it goes back to review simple words, but I feel like I'm missing so much understanding. I like the game format, but even bought a book to try and understand more. when my year's up I'll try something else I think.

The ads for Super are too much. It's cool that Super helps bring free learning to people if you buy it, but as one of those people who can only afford the free version of Duolingo right now, the number of times that I have to sit through the Super advertisements is not worth it and it makes me want to stop using the app. I feel like I'm being punished for not subscribing to it. Every single lesson I get a normal ad and then an ad for Super. I would give the app zero stars right now if I could.

I am completely baffled at the decision to remove the toggle for dark mode. It was already in the app. It worked. Why remove it? It's supposed to go by the setting of the phone, but it just doesn't work. I get flash banged every time I open the app. It's been like this for weeks. Even if it was working as intended, if I want the rest of my phone in light mode but Duo in dark, why take that toggle away? It's nonsensical. Just have a "Light, Dark, and System" preference like every other app.

Duolingo is a great app, and I only have a few issues with the language learning aspect of it, and for that, I would give the app four stars... HOWEVER The fact that Android still doesn't have math and music and it's been over a year that Apple has had these is just unacceptable to me. Therefore, my rating is two stars for now, although I am genuinely tempted to give just one until this is finally resolved or at least addressed.

DO NOT PAY FOR THIS APP!!! Unintuitive teaching style that is often incomplete and discouraging. The in-lesson guidance, or "hints," often have no regard for word order or explain all the correct words to use. You'll often get marked wrong for correct answers that are just worded differently, even though there is more than one correct way to write something. Completely non-existent customer service for paying subscribers. Feedback to them seems pointless because they NEVER respond.

I like this app because it is FREE. I am NOT interested in upgrading to Duolingo Plus, especially since you use AI SLOP to explain wrong answers instead of consulting with real human beings who speak the languages you offer. Stop forcing me to watch ads for Duolingo Plus every single ad break. These ads don't make me want to upgrade. They actually make me want to quit using your app all together, especially if I'm going to lose progress in my lessons when I close and reopen the app to avoid ads

This app can be pretty fun in the beginning, but after a while, the lessons get very hard. Nothing gets explained to you which means you have to figure everything out for yourself. The stories and adventures are insufferable. Don't waste your money like I did on the premium subscription. I haven't used anything else, but I will be trying out Rosetta Stone soon. It's also extremely competitive if you want to move up in leagues.

It is a good learning application, that is tailored to mobile devices. I have found it much easier to stick with Duo than Roseta. Comparing the two Duo is better for simple conversation upfront, while Roseta dives into understanding more grammer. The one feature that repeatedly bothers me about Duo (4 vs 5 stars) is the timer extensions, which it is continuously prompting you to use and the purchase button is where you are trying to quickly enter answers leading to accidental purchases.

Incessant advertisements. Free version has gotten rid of practicing to earn hearts. They take longer to regenerate now as well, and even when I complete goals, the XP boosts only work occasionally. They glitch all the time and I lose a lot of XP that way. I used to love duolingo. Now it just feels less about making learning accessible, more about being a money grab. Also, there is pretty obvious AI garbage now in a lot of lessons.

New issue: The “practice to earn hearts” button is completely gone. A big loss to my learning routine. On major curriculum changes: Quality German learning content, as far as I can tell, but the newer “pathway” design actually makes me feel a little lost. I made fewer mistakes with the old castle style, even though the lessons I'd unlocked were at a higher difficulty than after the curriculum reboot. Even after using the new layout much longer, it's still not my favorite of the two.

It's fine. I don't find it particularly enjoyable, but I've learned a bit. I do find the streaks and leagues to be very annoying though, as they distract me from actually making any progress. It's all about getting experience, not losing the streak, and rising in leagues, and this has resulted in me almost never actually doing lessons to learn (and I barely do any lessons outside of the practice ones, so I'm not making progress). It's actively detrimental to my experience.

Fun, gamified language learning, but I would say it's a great "starter" app for learning a new language, not if you want to develop deep understanding or expertise in a new language. It's good for learning vocabulary. After talking with several of my Spanish speaking friends, it's not always accurate, and the example phrases are sometimes bizarre, but I get why they do the silly phrases. Overall, it's been a fantastic family learning tool for my wife and kids.

Purchased an annual subscription for this and regretting. New higher tier subscription came out since and I'm constantly nagged to updated. The whole trophy system is just silly and greatly flawed. Lessons don't scale in difficulty linearly. It goes from 0-60 quite quickly. Duolingo definitely helped at first, but I didn't need the premium version to get there, and now it's lessons are just too difficult and there is no bridge. I'll definitely be switching to another app to continue learning.

There are parts where you speak into the mic. In theory, this is great. But they don't work 90% of the time and it kills motivation and progress, especially when you're doing timed challenges because the clock still ticks. It keeps listening after you're done talking and then just says it'll skip those challenges without telling you if you were correct.

I LOVE everything about this app. Over the years it's just gotten better. The free version has everything you need to enjoy the app, but I did the free trial of the paid version and I kept it because the experience just gets better. In spare time, I've learned two languages enough to understand and hold basic conversations. The rankings and challenges are great for competitive people like me. Very entertaining AND useful. Not something you get every day!

I would've given the app a 5 star review if not for the latest update. I liked using this app a few years ago because if I took a break and came back, I could practice what I knew before jumping in, now that's gone. It feels like it's way harder to track my progress now. Before, I could see my end goal, what I'll be learning about, and structure my learning in groups to better understand the vocab. But now I can't see the end or what comes next, it feels like some neverending video game :(

I have been using Duolingo to learn Spanish for two years. I have the free version, and there are a LOT of ads. Also, the XP boosts used to multiply points as long as you started a lesson during the countdown. Now, if you don't complete the lesson before the XP boost runs out, you get regular points. I just wasted 100 coins thinking I'd get 120 points and only received 43. Duolingo should change this back.

I love duo! I used to use it only for Spanish practice, but have started learning french too. It's helped loads in both. The way it intoduces new letters and combined letter sounds is great and I love the new games and listening exercises they've added. I love how it teaches grammar very intuitively, and allows for essentially unlimited practice. Helped a lot with understanding spoken language, reading/writing, and using grammar. Great for practice and learning.

App is no longer a free language app. You can only refill 1 heart for free. Gotta pay and it is very ticky tacky with mistakes. You could be off by one letter that doesn't change the word and it's an error but you could have a typo that is all sorts of crazy and it's accepted. They should spend less time on their ad campaigns and more on the ux.

Duolingo is actually doing pretty well at helping me learn Spanish! I am improving quite well, and at a good pace! It lost a star because of the ads, which are annoying. Also the fact that when a new word is introduced, you have to guess it most times, and then you lose your LIMITED hearts. So that's annoying. Other wise for a launguage app, it's pretty good!

this would be the best app for helping you learn new language!🎀😊 so, if you are preparing to flight this is great! and free with no ads also!!! and I love how duo looks!!! and the animation is 😘!! I am learning French and it's awesome, but I don't recommend this app if you are learning a language and know some or a friend is the language and you want to speak their language, cause it take time of fun, so take time and learn slow with much fun💖💖💖

Pretty good for learning languages. You aren't going to develop fluency or anything, but you can definitely get to a conversational level in it. However I have a couple complaints: -I get bombarded with ads every time I complete a lesson, which can get a little annoying. -Hearts regenerate so slow, 5 hearts for me barely lasts 10 minutes. Yes I know super duolingo solves this all but still paying $8 a month for this is pretty hefty. Just wish there were a bit more features in free duolingo.

After using Duolingo for the past 15 days, I found that learning a second language is surprisingly easy and enjoyable. The app is designed in a way that makes learning fun and engaging, without any pressure. The interactive exercises, gamified approach, and bite-sized lessons keep you motivated and make the process feel more like a game than a chore. That said, while Duolingo is a great tool, achieving fluency in a new language still requires time and effort.

I do like duolingo but there are some issues. I paid for the year of base version. You really only get extra lives and no ads. My biggest issue is they hardly explain anything. Many things don't make sense without at least a basic explanation. You'll realize when you make a mistake it has a link to explain the mistake but you have to pay for the max version. No discount (that I'm seeing) even though I've already bought the base. You would think basic explanations would be included at least.

It's ALMOST a perfect app. Great way to learn different languages. Nice supportive messages. Hell, you even get super for FREE for 3 days if you just use the app. The only issue is that sometimes that mic doesn't work for the speaking lessons. Though it's a small issue that barely happens, it cost me most of my progress on a lesson, EVEN THOUGH I said everything right. And I doubt it's my phone, this thing is only a few months old. Fix this issue, and this is a 5 star app.

I'm actually a little surprised with how popular this app is. I'm gonna guess it's because of Duolingo's presence on social media, because honestly, this isn't that good. It uses AI for it's lessons, which sometimes results in sentences that aren't even grammatically correct. Overall, I think this would be great for learning to READ a language, not speak it, since it doesn't do a good job at teaching accents (which are in almost every language).

Update: I recently switched to Duolingo Max and I find it very buggy. It's wasting my time constantly. It seems to get stuck whenever I am using the call Lily function and often when one of the characters is hosting a radio show. With call Lily, I find that she cannot comprehend complex sentences. I am so busy figuring out exactly what I can say and how to say it that I am not able to practice my fluidity. I often have to do call L or radio shows lessons two or three times. Please fix this.

I do not like that you can no longer do a practice session when you run out of hearts to try to keep your streak going. Super Duolingo is incredibly expensive and entirely out of my budget so I relied on that to be able to avoid using up my streak freezes. Now I have to watch a video about Super to gain a single heart. Maybe if you didn't ask for my card for the FREE trial of super I would give that week long trial a chance, but not having you automatically start charging me what I can't afford!

Fine for hobbyists & building habits in a fun way, but not for efficient language learning. XP is a meaningless indicator of progress and fluency. There's no way to set desired proficiency by some date, you can't practice speaking with prompts on your own, and they don't have real convos you can listen to & translate. (Busuu does great with this.) Premium is also not worth it. I'd look elsewhere for real language learning, or expect to supplement Duolingo with other learning.

I've been using this app almost 3 years now and have been pretty happy with my progress and the way it operated. I have the free version. But you've changed some things and I'm not really happy about it. When I ran out of hearts I had the option to refill with points or I could practice more lessons to win them. And I could practice enough lessons to gain 5 hearts. Now I only have the option to gain one and if I watch a commercial I can gain 2. I really liked having to do practice lessons.

The interface is decent, though slightly difficult to navigate. Most of the exercises are helpful for pronunciation and vocabulary, but there isn't really any conversational practice. The speaking exercises are also finicky and seem to miss certain words consistently (like "Euro" in German) regardless of my audio setup or enunciation. Sometimes the point values also bug out, usually reducing rather than increasing my score. Fun app to use, but it needs some serious work.

Simply not enough structure to the learning and lessons - they want to get you on a streak and keep you subscribed (which I still am, for now), but their CEO recently touted that most of the content is now generated by AI. That shows, because there are so many mistakes and answers that should not be accepted, not to mention the pace of learning varies wildly. Duolingo CANNOT be used to learn a language without being paired with formal learning and lots of time spent speaking.

Paid for a year of Super that recently expired, but I wasn't feeling like the app gives enough to warrant paying for another year. There were a few bugs throughout the past year, but resolved within a few days. Taking French in app & working with French colleagues to find out lessons are teaching me wrong is embarrassing. Without paying for Super, the app actually sucks. Annoying ads + hearts for mistakes that prevent further lessons? + pay to play certain games? Unfair to those on a budget.

Over all, it's a fun app to use when you want to casually learn another language. I say casually, because there are times the AI voices are hard for me to understand, and I end up getting listening questions wrong because of it. I had to turn the listening feature off because of that. Great app for those curious enough to learn a language for free though!

I really love the app. it's a nice intro to languages. I switched to a free trial so that I had endless hearts and it shows in my use of the app. considering paying for a family subscription but sadly that's the only benefit which doesn't justify cost. what would make this app 5*? providing more friend interactions so that it is more engaged between friends. let us chat or send things in the languages were learning to make it more interactive. but still a great base lang app.

I love this app. I've been using it over a year. Over the past year it's become very glitchy. I do not receive my double points /1.5x XP points when it says I do (almost half the time)! They also changed how you receive points. Despite purchasing full version, I am still prompted to buy "max." It would be nice if this was included considering it costs over $100 per year for lessons that repeat themselves. Although I do enjoy this app and learning language, I'm not sure if it's worth it.

This is an awesome app. I've been learning Italian right now for 100 days. It's an awesome course, simple enough for me not to throw my phone but hard enough to actually learn. I have no comments overall. There isn't a bombardment of ads, barely any if I'm being honest, the lessons are great, it's just great. The only "thing", I wish there was an Icelandic course, but I'm not about to give a lower rating just because there isn't one, there are already a ton of languages, I can't be disappointed.

I've been using this app for a while (my current streak is 400+). I think it's useful but I often learn things that native speakers will never use/speak from Duolingo. The math course is ok but weird as I have to estimate my answer on a line. I'm not a big fan of its music course. It's basically a game and doesn't really teach music effectively. I know how to play two music instruments and I was trying to use the music course with someone who knows nothing about music and it didn't go well.

When I first started, I loved this. I would have given it 5 stars and I was strongly considering buying the super version. Then about 2 months ago I could only earn 1 heart. Tonight I can earn more hearts, but no practice at all, only through ads. This is ridiculous and makes me want to quit the entire app. I am learning Korean and it's hard, I go through hearts like nothing, this used to be a great option for people who are tight on funds, but the changes are ridiculous.

This app is ridiculous. I regret actually paying for it. You pay for one version, and it doesn't even unlock all the perks that are actually helpful. They're always trying to get you to upgrade to a more expensive version. It's fun at first, but after a certain level, it feels more like a chore. The people in the higher leagues score so many so quickly that I'm not convinced they're real people. I went from finishing 1st place every week to not even being able to keep up with the leaders.

This app started out okay. The ads were annoying so I subscribed to super and made use of the extra tools to learn better. Recently though, things have gone downhill. I've had bugs where words I've never seen are in the review lessons, the speech recognition has stopped working reliably, and sometimes the translations and pronunciation used by the app's characters are wrong, so when I learn it that way I look like an idiot. Stop pouring resources into AI! It's ruining everything else.

I've been using this app for a while now and with the change to practicing to get free hearts, I cannot give this app more than one star. I need to go at my own pace to learn languages and not being allowed to practice unless all of my hearts are depleted is a terrible change and makes the buy-in for Pro/Max feel really pushy and I don't like that. If that feature was a restored so that I could practice whenever I want with the automatically generated lessons. I would easily give 5 stars again.

This went from a decent little app to them constantly taking away features to push Duolingo Plus. Not long ago they took away the ability to practice as much as you want to in order to earn hearts...to force you into paying for the hearts. Defeats the whole purpose of a learning app. Give us back the ability to practice for hearts as much as we want!

Overall, this is an excellent app. I've been using the paid version to supplement my Spanish learning for months and will continue to use it. There are two changes I'd like to see: 1. I'd like to be able to download as many lessons as I want for offline learning. 2. I'd like to be able to opt out of leagues but still be able to track my friends' progress. As of now, it's both or neither. Once I see those changes, I will update my review to five stars because overall it's a great app!

The free version is almost unusable with ads that last forever and all of the community stuff is gone. I did a trial on the MAX version and it's not worth the money. The little listening exercises are just gimmicks and you don't learn anything worth the time. It's such a shame because the old version was so much better. Also AI sucks. This is a lame GAME you earn points at with very basic language matching. It is no longer a language learning app that is just gamified enough to motivate people.

Each lesson is VERY long and drawn out to the point that it's hard to retain anything. The little indicator doesn't move accurately to give a clear idea of how many sentences are left, but if you count it's about 15 each lesson. The characters roll their eyes or get angry if you translate things properly, which is very discouraging. XP/Gems gained for each lesson or challenge is very low. All the good features are behind a paywall other than the fun interactive game

Was a Super subscriber after an hour of using the app. Was on an 1100 day streak. Then recently they launched Max. Max is a premium service that costs TRIPLE what the Super subscription costs. It just comes with some AI lesson stuff, but to sell it they decided to remove the "Explain my Mistake" from both the Free AND Super tiers. basically making the ability to learn effectively locked behind a 180/yr subscription. AMAZON isn't even that much. it's greedy, predatory, and lost them my business

This app is really handy and makes learning the basics of learning new languages easy... BUT it does an absolutely abysmal job of explaining any form of conjugation (read: it doesn't even try), only penalizes you for not guessing the proper conjugation when you've been exposed to both forms without any explanation at all as to when one or the other apply. If this were fixed, it would be a great app and get 5 stars. As it isn't and is a major issue when it comes to the learning of languages, two.

The app has become extremely buggy. I constantly have to close and reopen it to update my progress after finishing a lesson. Sometimes, it will erase two or three full levels of progress. Last night, I needed to pass a test to be permitted to return to where I belonged. It will also say I have 3x XP for 30 minutes, which somehow turns out to be 1.5 XP for 10 minutes. I've submitted multiple bug reports without any reply or, worse, any signs of a fix.

This app has undergone many changes in just the past couple of years. Once, it contained lessons, lectures, explanations of grammatical and syntactical structures, and comment threads for learners to "study together," to engage with each other to offer a hand where needed. It used to offer techniques to guide users away from making mistakes. The developers have removed those features, as well as the opportunity to practice. If you can't practice, you can't learn; I advise choosing another app.

I used to love this app. I've been using it for over a year, but they've disabled "practice to earn more hearts" in the free version. I thought it was a random bug, but it's happened to a couple of my friends with long streaks too. I think it's their way of getting people to do the paid version. We've all sent in bug reports but havent received any responses and theres nothing on the website. It's been a couple months. Without that feature, it's almost impossible to do multiple lessons in a day

Edit: This app gets worse and worse with every update. Now you can no longer practice?! And the practice available on paid version is different from free and awful. Original: I got it to refresh me after losing much of the Spanish I used to know. It's nice for that, too easy in some ways, for me, but that would be good for actual beginners! If I was really learning the language, I'd want some explanation of the types of phrase constructions that don't match English. Five stars!

No accented Polish letters to choose from on the phone app! I'm constantly told I have errors, when it's the app's fault for not giving me the correct Polish alphabet options! They provide those letters on a computer, yet you get more XP on the phone. Also, lessons are limited! I already reached the end after only 3 sections, and now am just given daily review lessons. No new content! They are not consistent with vocabulary - always switching "go" and "walk." Very confusing.

Not so slowly starting to make the free version unusable. 5 hearts, no way to get them back, with bugs in the ai powered lessons that fail you before you even get a chance to try. || The game-ification sounds good at first but it's predatory in nature and designed to get you to spend gems. Not to mention it's easy to lose yourself in the 'game' and forget to actually learn as opposed to just racking up xp to top leaderboards or slam out as many lessons as possible to get more xp during boost.

I love the app. I've used it for years. I use the free version so there are ads. At first, it was reasonable but lately it feels like they've significantly increased the frequency of the ads and it's getting very annoying. Makes me not even want to watch the optional ads for extra rewards because it's already forcing me to watch 2 ads after EACH lesson.

I still use the app, but getting rid of the "practice to earn hearts" feature has really decreased my motivation. That was a great way to stay active and engaged, as well as practice your skills. You already earn less XP for doing the practice. Please bring this feature back. The ads are expected for a free version, but changing this practice feature really kills it for me and for my children who are just starting their language learning.

Less useful than it used to be, but still the best free language learning app I know of. They've deliberately removed a couple of features (like practice when you'd run out of hearts for lessons and explanations if you get a question wrong) that greatly diminish the true learning potential of this app for free players. It's hard to truly learn if you don't get regular feedback on your mistakes and practice. If you're struggling, stars can disappear in one bad lesson. Very disappointing.

I used to love this app but they've made it nearly unusable. It's just constantly trying to get you to buy and use things for points, freezes, etc. You can't just use the app without being forced to participate in tedious annoying stuff that derails your learning. I paid for a subscription for years and finally canceled it because it's gotten so unpleasant to use. Edit: Took off another star, they somehow managed to make it even worse while doing absolutely nothing to make the courses better.

I enjoy the gamification of Duolingo and bought Plus for no ads. This is a complete lie - advertising for their other subscriptions is everywhere. I will not be renewing. Other than that, it works as intended. It can be a fun way to learn the language. However, an improvement I would recommend is making you write a sentence on your own - it's too easy to piece them together with the word tiles provided. If you know a few words you can guess the others which doesn't feel like real learning

Fun interactive experience marred by ads. There are so many ads, the Duolingo ones are the worst as they are loud, obnoxious and after every lesson. Constant messaging and pressure that is in your face nonstop unless you disable all notifications. Duolingo is the overly friendly ex that doesn't understand you need space and is constantly interjecting themselves every opportunity to "help". The high pressure sales tactics might appeal to some, for me it just made me anxious and stressed.

The app itself is really good, and the lessons are great for begining to learn a new language or keeping your skills up-to-date. My one issue is that after EVERY lesson, there is now a short advertisement followed by a longer advertisment for Max or Super Duolingo. I'm now resigned to doing one lesson a day as the repetitive advertisements is so annoying. Also, the phrase "Advertising helps fund Duolingo's mission" is a bit misleading -- advertising helps line your shareholders' pockets ;-)

It's nice for encouragement and practice. But not learning properly. It doesn't state what is formal or not. I want to like it because of the easy interface. It's ok for in-between lessons. Update: Now practicing for hearts isn't working. I can only do it when prompted and not just practice for hearts until I have 5 again. The commercials say that this is to help to support people, but I'm under poverty level and can't afford this. So, who's being supported to "learn free"?

The pace of progression in the music course feels too fast, and hard to grasp. Maybe I'm just bad at this, but it feels hard to learn and progress at the app's pace. I have fun and feel like I can learn and review at my pace with Japanese, but find myself infinitely frustrated trying to keep up with the music lessons. I also don't understand why the dark theme is no longer an option. I know I can change my phone settings, but it seems silly not to have the option in the app.

I mostly enjoyed the free version of the app and found it helpful for learning even though the ads continue getting longer and more frequent. With the update today that removes the ability to earn hearts with practice, I'm considering leaving the app altogether unless it's reversed. It discourages learning and makes it difficult to return to lessons. They've also made it more difficult to earn gems and XP, so there are only so many times you can buy more hearts with gems before you run out.

The option to opt out or into the weekly leader boards as desired would be appreciated, as that part adds a bit of stress to the fun of learning, which makes it less fun sometimes. They also keep changing how many people get demoted and promoted in each league, which randomly adds an extra element of stress as well. Slowly becoming less and less of a fan each time these get changed.

Apparently, now Duolingo is forcing everyone to pay, so let me talk about the 'paid mode'. I've tried with a trial, but 'unlimited heart' never helps my learning. Do you know our brain learns more when there is some stress to learn them? Heart system was that little stress for my brain to learn things. But paid mode eliminates it. I get more progress, but I don't know if I'm learning more. So stop forcing us to pay and bring us back 'practice to earn heart'!!!

It's okay. I think the fact you can't move words around when you're supposed to fill in what you hear you have to delete the entire sentence instead of move words around within that sentence. Also on Match madness it gets to a point after level 2 it become too difficult. I wish I could reset to accomplish my quests for the day but you can't. Overall it's decent but it's definitely geared towards you spending money on boost and things of that nature.

For a while, Duolingo was doing okay with how it ran things. I know many apps use many ads, like this one. But now the way it is now is even worse because you are unable to practice to obtain multiple hearts now; only 1. This isn't helpful if you want to progress in the lessons. If I'm struggling in a lesson and only have 1 heart and fail, now I have to practice again on something that doesn't have to do with the lesson. It's unfortunate to see this app getting worse as the months go by.

Welcome to capitalism. Duo has substantially changed, preventing ANY practicing unless you are completely out of hearts, and when you ARE fully out of hearts, can only practice once. I don't have a problem with the ads, I'm not paying, and the ads aren't obtrusive, just the standard garbage mobile ads. But the lack of practice at this point is a VERY CLEAR and OBVIOUS attempt to shove their super/Max subscriptions. 590 day streak, goodbye. I'm disappointed.

Now I have completed the Russian course and am doing the daily refresh. It seems like I get the same 20 things over and over. No sign of others. Everyone hates the new format. Admit it and go back! I used to go back and forth between new lessons and older ones, which helped me remember vocab and grammar. Now you have to go in order and have a bunch of lessons on the same thing that you forget 3 days later. I used to spend up to 2 or 3 hours on weekends, now I force myself to do a couple lessons.

Takes more than 24 hours to renew even ONE heart. And doesn't allow further feedback. I don't know about every language but in Spanish it doesn't give correct grammatical translations. And it mistranslates and expects students to mistranslate answers. For example it will have the English verb ending in "ing" when the word they want isn't the progressive form. "I am running" is actually translated as "I run." And it marks answers wrong that are correct for what they are actually asking.

Really easy to use. And using my phone means I can use it anywhere. The lessons are quick to do, so it's easier to fit into a busy day. The gamification helps make it fun, but the scoring fails a LOT of the time, which kind of defeats the whole advantage of learning this way. If they could ever get the scoring to work properly, I'd give it 4 stars.

While Duolingo is not the best method for learning a language out there, it has really helped me personally get much more familiar with reading and speaking several languages. Many of the updates for different activities have been helpful additions. However, the entire social aspect and the notifications have become so painfully twee and intrusive that it actually makes me want to leave- I would have canceled had I not already been billed for my supporter fee (which should be used better!)

Been playing for a year & I subscribe. Despite that I've noticed their move to grab more money since it's never enough. Bugs are piling up penalizing me in competitions. Tickets are pointless as they likely get so many they autoclose them. Gems used to be a couple a day & take a month+ to earn "free" time extensions. No more. They've taken away the am/pm chests while apple still has them and they had 3x xp weeks before android.Learning is great, but they want more and more money like rest, run!

After a year of having Super, I recently canceled it. I'm not interested in giving money to a company that uses AI for its content. That being said, the app is now unbearable. Not because of third-party ads, but because of Duolingo's own ads. There's an unskippable 30 second ad about Super after EVERY. SINGLE. LESSON.

Duolingo works to expand vocabulary, but it doesn't explain basic grammar until far into the course. Using trial-and-error to lean basic grammar is not fun. This is not helped by the many errors, with exercises only accepting one of two synonyms for no reason at all despite telling you that you can use either. The limited hearts make this incredibly frustrating, as does the recent removal of the ability to practice for hearts, which is a transparent cash grab that will probably lose them money.

It's was fun and made learning a game, but after 3 months, it became less fun. When playing some of the timed word match games, the matching words would take time to display, but the clock continues. Also, the words would shift places, making it seem less fair. Also, you need to by time or play 8 hours a day either, not practicel. Unlike other language learning tools, for this one, you end up paying more than it's worth.

Recent changes in the app have ruined the experience. Probably wasting my time at this point. I was earnestly trying to learn but the hearts system changes really downgraded the experience. Seriously thinking about quitting now. It's becoming a chore. Used to be very enjoyable, gave it 5 stars. Very disappointing.

I'm giving 3 stars solely for the timed exercises. It's impossible to meet the extremely short time requirements when you have to type answers out. Matching, selection and speaking work great but you can blow 10-15 seconds typing. If you run out of time you're prompted to buy time extensions. That's fine of you are playing the free version, but you get the same prompt with the buy ups as well. Poor showing. Otherwise good app; fun and educational.

Technically, this app is no longer a free app. With the changes to hearts/practicing, the app is now "pay to play." This app has also gone downhill over the year I've used it. Audio to text interpretation is terrible. Audio quality in sentences is spotty. Phrases are often repeated in the same lesson.

I am Learning Spanish, some Japanese at one point. Choose as many languages as you desire as long as you have the time. There are many great features to enhance your speaking, building grammar with the language and test you all throughout the way. The courses are self paced and a devotion of your time. Since the most current updates; as a nonsubscriber, I have no been able to use the practice to earn hearts. The options are to watch more AD's or wait a few hours for those refills. Fix please.

I USED to love Duolingo, but I'm taking off a star since their agenda has shifted more toward profit. And another for how bummed I am. There are now certain features only available with premium. You can no longer practice to earn hearts, or get explanations for mistakes. I haven't given up on duolingo yet, but I'm spending significantly less time with it and won't be referring folks. There are other options. Like, REAL people, with no subscription fees or hidden agendas 😀👍 ¡Adios!

This app is amazing for learning new languages, however I am giving it 3 stars instead of 5 for it's emphasis on using an AI tool to learn from mistakes in the premium membership. AI has been proven many times to be very inconsistent with giving accurate and correct awnsers and responses across many topics. This AI tool could slow down someone's progress in learning a language if not demotivate them entirely due to confusing and inconsistent assistance. It's far better to give out premade tips.

An overrated app that's prinarily dedicated to making money, not educating. You're better off looking into your local public library's language resources that are often free and much more in-depth and expansive. When the app first launched, it was a decent resource to learning language basics. Now, it's swamped with ads that include its own ads that market its latest tier system of learning where the paid plans offer more resources to simply practice. Disappointing.

Lessons are repetitive and try to re-teach me words I learnt in my childhood. A minor typo will cause the lesson to focus on the wrong aspect of training. I've been using this app for more than 9 years, participating daily, to help with a language I already speak, and I have yet to reach the end of the course! And now they want money from me, despite the creator of the app saying Duolingo would always be free!

12/16/24: I started having problems with Duolingo in Dec 2020 due to heart refills glitching. The app had no problem taking the gems that I had earned (10 times) without refilling hearts like it was supposed to. They also removed Elvish as a language, which is extremely disappointing. Now, every time they update, you lose your daily and nightly boosts (so you miss out on league points), and they move the lessons to different tiers so it's impossible to pick up where you left off. This app is sad

This app is impossible to use without their paid service. I get the need for ads, but I don't understand advertising your subscription after each lesson that takes less than two minutes. If you aren't going to make me learn at least stop spamming me with notification and emails it's scummy even if I can turn them off.

The sheer amount of ads make this app insufferable. The lessons are fine. But there's not only an ad after every single lesson, but there's a super duo promotion as well. Peer pressuring me into buying premium after every lesson makes me want to switch apps, not pay $80 - $100 for no ads. I don't mind watching an ad every lesson, but the super promo is what is most aggravating. If anything, promote it when I make a mistake, but leave me alone if I do a perfect lesson.

I started using Duolingo because I want to learn Italian in a structured and engaging way. While the app does a decent job of introducing vocabulary and sentence structure through gamified exercises, I found that some of the content felt overly focused on social and cultural messaging rather than just teaching the language itself.

The game-like attributes, scores, and leaderboards keep me invested day by day, which is useful. The way that the lessons are set up helps me retain information, too. However, a lot of grammar rules are left unexplained, so I'm left feeling confused and frustrated. On top of this, a handful of translations are either not widely accepted or do not mean what Duolingo teaches at all. This app is good when treated as practice for a language instead of a main teacher.

an add every 2 to 5 minutes is a far shot from the former no ads pledge. content is great. will probably be a lot more enjoyable once i can afford premium but as it stands now having to sit through the same dumb adds every 2 minutes serves as a significant barrier to wanting to continue to use the app.

The app doesn't let me speak. I've checked my phone's settings and the app's permissions. Pretty frustrating, especially when I have Super. As a result, I've been told by people fluent in the language I'm learning that I'm hard to understand sometimes since my cadence is so awkward. Otherwise, it's a really great app. I like the free version and I'm glad I can still learn even when I can't afford Super. I tried Super recently, and it definitely makes it easier to learn drastically faster!

Duolingo is a great way to learn new things like a new language, math and even music. There is one problem, the hearts. The hearts is a dumb idea and slows down your learning. Even though you can practice for more hearts which is great. It still feels like a chore. Overall, a great app. I may want the subscriptions a little lower and the hearts system removed, that's really it. Definitely recommend.

Duolingo is THE best way to learn a language. I'm learning 5 rn, and it makes it super fun! You can never get bored with this app. A few things I wish were included: Why were the motions for the characters deleted? Every time you'd get something right, they would do like a happy dance or something. I miss those. Also, I wish there was a follow button to click from a computer and also have access to the settings. I can't seem to, so I always have to use my pad. But all in all, I ADORE this app!😍

Duolingo is a good choice for language learning, offering practice conversations and grammar rules, learning is very easy. The few free for a day super trials are considerate, and the way it's designed is very user friendly. However, there are some few issues I run into. For the music courses get very laggy, and feel almost impossible sometimes to hit notes on time. Along with slow loads for Profiles and the News tab. Overall, this is a good choice if wanting to learn a new language for free.

Great app for learning a language. I am particularly frustrated with the super subscription. When signing up for the trial, you only have the option for the yearly price of 92 dollars. The trial says it will notify 2 days before the end, but it didn't. I'm so mad I want to delete the app. But I just paid 92 dollars so that would be dumb. Don't use the super trial unless you're ready to drop food for a week on it.

I've been using this app for 8+ years & I love it! There are so many language options & you can pursue multiple at once. I fully credit it for my high fluency in French. I use the paid version now because I want to support future efforts. One negative is they don't always communicate major changes in advance. I once downgraded my rating because I thought they'd wiped out my progress, but, in actuality, they'd just added a ton of new educational content, which made it look like I'd regressed.

Was fine while using Super trial subscription, but two ads after every lesson is so obnoxious. I spend more time watching ads than doing lessons. It's like if you're not paying, they'd rather annoy you into paying for the overpriced subscription than let you learn. Limiting your lessons with the hearts feature is enough on its own, but the ads on top of it are relentless. If this persists, I'll definately find a new app.

Duolingo is a great way to learn a language wrong. Sure it can teach you great things that you wouldn't have other wise, but it teaches the wrong words that aren't commonly used. It also doesn't explain grammer and just throws you new concepts without ANY explanation, so eventually you have to figure it out on your own, deeply confused. Duolingo isn't all bad but when speaking with someone who really knows the language will make you sound like a 5 year old ( Nothing against them, haha ).

Great app. I am learning French again after stopping in college. It's faster than traditional classroom lessons, as stated. I like how they use multiple ways of providing lessons and requesting answers. The app keeps getting better too; keep up the great work all! It would be great to see links to learn more (if wanted/needed) in the tips area. Also, pemium is better! It is worth the money to save time without the ads and to be able to fix my wrong answers during a lesson.

Just finished my free trial, and have came to the conclusion that Super is just too expensive for what you get out of this service, as well as they don't provide more than 3 pricing options. I was heavily invested in Duolingo up until this point, basically feeling required to pay in order to proceed is not how I want my learning experience to ultimately be. Back to the books I guess!

I have been using Duolingo on and off for years. I've been currently using it to learn Japanese for about 9 months, and I'm really liking it! Of course, you need to do it alongside some other resource (like Anki for example), but it really helped me learning hiragana and katakana, and it's a great way to force me to review things every day. I don't think I would have kept my streak without the gamification. I love the new features they are introducing (like listening practice in podcast form)!

More than once, while reviewing French, the app jumps ahead of the reading exercises. As in, I finish what comes before, and instead of having to do the reading exercises on my path, it just jumps them, and the next exercise is automatically open/available. The problem is that doing the reading exercises after they have been jumped gives you fewer points once you can only "review them." It has happened several times.

I have been using it awhile now. It is addictive for sure, but I feel like it's medium-shallow instruction. It needs more in-depth explanations of grammar and when you use certain words, verb forms, endings, etc. I am using it as a refresher, but I can't imagine trying to learn from scratch with this. You are just repeating things you don't understand. Very inefficient. And eventually, frustrating.

New update makes it so that, in the Japanese course whenever you need to enter text to write out the pronunciation of kanji, it automatically pulls up my English keyboard on mobile, but I don't want to write in Romanji and I have the Japanese 12 key keyboard enabled. It used to just pull up whatever keyboard was open last (like most applications function), and then it keep opening the 12 key if that's what I selected. Now I have to manually re-select it. Every. Single. Time.

I gave this 2 stars because of their sneaky billing practices!The actual lessons are great however they advertise that you can have an individual plan for $6.99 a month or a family plan for $9.99 a month but they will charge you for the entire year! They should not advertise it as a monthly fee if it is in fact a yearly fee! Also, the yearly fee is supposed to be $83.99 but I was charged $89.87!

There are some fun elements, and the system is pretty well made - but i find the system to be pretty frustrating when they doc you hearts for mistakes the app makes with the language. Ive lost a lot of hearts to words that sound the same when asked to pick, I've lost hearts to responding how I've been taught by Duolingo, only to be given new phrases never heard before and told that they were what I should have used. There are other issues with it but not enough space to type it out.

It's nice to learn basic phrases and pronunciations of other languages, but it doesn't teach you the alphabet of other languages and doesn't teach you basic grammar. You'll need more than this to learn a language. I also really dislike the leaderboard. I know there is a way to eliminate it, but I don't want to lose my friends/followers from making my account private. The leaderboards are really unfair because you're competing with other people who might have easier languages/subjects.

Plenty of errors in French and Spanish, would like to see Arabic, Japanese and Korean as well. Edit: much better than it was a decade ago when I first reviewed; although, the ads are sooooo much louder than the app. Please make the app louder so that I don't have to keep changing my volume. Also PLEASE add the option to opt-out of romanization of languages with their own alphabet, it's more distracting than helpful - let me use my ears and eyes on only the language I'm trying to learn.

I really love how much I've learned here but they've removed a LOT of the teaching from the app. It's difficult to understand rules, when to use what grammar, when it doesn't teach you. And the learning is very rigid and linear, with no way to venture out and learn what you want to learn. Plus, it would be really fun if Duo could incorporate a swear words section, because it's important for foreign learners to know if native speakers are talking badly to them.

There are some serious holes in the learning. The voice speaks quickly in the other language and there are no pronunciation guides to show you how letters, groups of letters, or words are pronounced so have no real idea if you're pronouncing anything correctly. After not missing a day, mostly twice a day, for 4.7 years, I still have no ability to hold even a basic conversation. Also, if you force stop the app, it will just turn itself back on, which is really bad.

It's an amazing app but it has its flaws. The pronunciation on some things just sound completely different even if you slow it down. And the characters show what it looks like but sometimes when it comes down to drawing it it'll show something different. And last thing, for some reason I cannot buy the 1 month plan or the other ones, it just says the play store is being forced to stop. I'd like to buy a plan so it'd make it easier for me to not lose my streak.

great app, has really helped me learn Spanish French and Japanese even without super duolingo, definitely recommend, 10/10 edit: i am doing the English course for fun, and all of a sudden I can't change the language I'm learning because the button doesn't work! I'm on the daily review and now I can't advance leagues because I can only get a small amount of xp every day! Until this is fixed, your rating is going down duo

Ive been studying Japanese for two years now and they aren't consistent. No two identical sentences are the same some times and that's frustrating when you arent paying for their "premium plan". They move the end of units further and further every time you finish a lesson and you think the unit is over. They ding you on the smallest mistyping. It just makes learning all the more frustrating. Update: What the hell is up with the icon?!?!?! It's disgusting to look at!!!!!!!!

Duolingo is a great app to learn new languages. On the free version, there are a limited amount of extra exercises you can practice, but I like that when your hearts run out, you can always gain more by practicing extra lessons to continue on the main courses. The ads for Super Duolingo get pretty annoying, but it doesn't interfere with the learning process. I love the widget option and have kept a 20 day streak in French thanks to it. Duolingo offers great recaps and new material all the same.

one of the animations at the end of a lesson is really disgusting (duo sheds his skin like a snake). I found a setting that turns off animations which means I'm not too grossed out to use the app (although i still see the end frame of the animation with the dead duo skin on the ground). But I'm bummed because I liked a lot of the other the other animations. I really wish I could just turn off the nasty ones

I'm still early but this feels way more adhd friendly than class ever did... EDIT: I've run into a bug that I cannot fix and has rendered the system all but unusable, and looking online, I can't find a solution that works. whenever I have a spoken part of a lesson, I click the button to record my voice and it doesn't give me any time to speak before canceling it and marking it as failed. I want to keep using the app, I was enjoying using Spanish, but I can't complete my lessons this way.

The Japanese lessons are great, and pretty much all-encompassing. The music lessons are decent, but the sensitivity for input is a bit too high. I don't even press a not on my screen and it registers it as input. The ads can be a bit annoying, but the annual plan of super is so cheap that I'm actually thinking of getting it! I've had a great time with duo, and the lore is *chefs kiss*

My review is about the free version. The approach to memorization is good. There needs to be more focus, IMO, on speaking what you're memorizing. I can get great scores on vocabulary without speaking more than an occasional phrase. Also, there is a bit too much focus on shortened expressions when beginners should be learning the full versions that the short forms are derived from. For example, I should learn, "Pleasure to meet you" instead of the short form, "My pleasure". 4 stars: not bad!

This app is great, except that I am hard of hearing and on some lessons the volume is painfully low, so I just skip them. Also, there are often surprise lessons out of nowhere. And it seems that I am expected to know the answers (like they are trying to trick you). And in the pull-down menus that should have the correct words, they are often missing. And when a discount is offered, it is not a true offer. Having said that, a big thank you to Duolingo for this free app! I have learned so much!

I do like learning with Duolingo. I rarely if ever give a "perfect" score, but I am giving my highest approval. The fact that I can go back and relearn a lesson that may not have been optimal is great, and I definitely work at my own pace, when I have time to focus. Over all, I don't have many suggestions for improvement, and those few are more personally-based, and not app-function-based.

I LOVE IT! It's the best way to learn a language!!! BUT, the music app HAS PROBLEMS!!! At times, the music app is great, but often, they make you attempt to read and play musical notes at breakneck speed with NO chance to slow down. This is an assinine (sp?) way to teach music to total beginners. They don't have this problem with the language app. If you're a beginner learning Spanish, they don't start you out reading classical Spanish poetry. So why not follow the same logic with music?

It's been a fun experience so far, there are a few aspects that drive my interest specifically; the leaderboard and "league promotion" platform is engaging and creates a bit of a competitive environment that aides in learning. Trying to get the high score for the week is a cheeky little way to keep people coming back. The lessons/mini games/varying challenges have been enjoyable as well. I also appreciate the addition of music/math to the course choices to supplement the language courses.

Great app, they gamify learning languages to keep it engaging and fun. That said, it is unnecessary to have that many ads on the free version. Every. Single. Lesson. you get an ad for the paid version and something else. I understand the need for advertising to keep the app free, but it's excessive to the point that now I actually don't want to pay for the full version.

Cool app. Some language programs are better than others, but you still learn a lot. My biggest complaint is that the ads are insane if you don't use the premium version. You get an ad for an outside product and an ad for the premium version after literally everything you do and some of the "ads" are full on chintzy mobile games you're forced into for a bit. I get that they need to make money but this level of ads is wildly inappropriate.

Very helpful and engaging while still being simple. There are a variety of different games and tasks to help you learn. The only thing I don't like is that there is very little in the way of explanation about things like verb conjugation, and what little there is is basically hidden (you have to click the book icon in the section header.) You do have to be committed to learning and it's necessary to also supplement with other methods (websites, videos, etc) but it definitely works!

I have been using Duolingo over the past year as a student (member of a classroom). It has been a great tool until this past month, when they overhauled the experience, adding ads and forcing the heart system. Now, the app is practically unusable. The ads are buggy and take over the whole experience, while the heart system feels like pure greed and extortion. I really hope this is just a bad update, but as it stands, can no longer recommend this app.

It's great to include some foreign words and expressions into the real life context. Also, it helps to overcome a new language barrier due to speaking practice. However, I wish I could turn off competing with other people: it feels tiresome, especially since there're constant bugs when I'm promised to get x2 points but I earn x1, or opening another chest gives me nothing. To cap it all, it doesn't explain grammar properly and there's no European Spanish.

Clicking on a word places it on the sentence line, then pops them back at times, causing errors. You can catch it going back & forth multiple times while you watch. The small girl voice is impossible to understand about 60% of the time. Also, the entire leaderboard concept is counter productive to actual material retention. That should be an optional feature not tied to the learning process. Finally, it appears some people cheat somehow. Evil new icon was the final straw. HATE IT! Uninstalled.

Teaches words from most languages fairly well. Honestly isn't great with conjugation, grammar, and proper sentence structure. I would say that if you wanted to visit another country and learn a few common phrases in their language, it's fine for that and may even be among the best. However, if you wanted to become fluent or even just conversational in that language, then you will at least need a secondary reference and study material, or even an additional app along with DuoLingo.

This app includes way too many interruptions to your learning. You will spend more time dealing with ads than learning. The constant ads are annoyingly loud relative to the rest of the app. The lessons are way too repetitive. The paid version doesn't have the ad issues, but the rest still holds true. The paid version is VERY expensive, especially given that the mission statement of the company is "to make learning free".

They have completely gamified this app, if you have tried it before in the past and didn't find it engaging, try it now. They have added all kinds of goals and things to collect, daily challenges, xp points, beautiful interface and design. I love the art style and sounds, as well as haptic feedback. Well worth paying for the subscription too. I find myself wanting to learn every day to keep my streak going! Excellent app. Thank you devs!

I'm very disappointed. I've used this app for years to learn Spanish. It has actually helped a lot, but lately, I have found that to earn hearts to do lessons, I have to watch ads instead of doing the practices to help me understand the language. Yes, I can have personalized practice if I want to spend a ridiculous amount of money per year (with no monthly option to break up the cost) for the super. Sadly, I think it may be time for me to start looking for a new app as my progress has stopped.

so many of these language companies just throw some "science base research" together and hope it works. then they say "we make learning fun" Duolingo is the only one that I actually want to keep going with. The main driver for me is the leagues. Just trying to stay in a league requires you to continue learning. if you want to advance you have to do extra work. Fantastic design too you can skip the voice recognition and listening parts if you are doing it in a busy setting. This thing is great.

After years of use, I have very good verbal skills. But in the last few months, Duolingo doesn't recognize my pronunciation of 4 simple phrases. These go into the error section, and, no matter how many times I try, it will not accept simple pronunciations that it accepted thousands of times before. I can't find anyone to fix this bug. I will NOT renew my subscription if this bug isn't fixed.

I've had duolingo on my phone since it came out practically. I've finally got the consistency to do it for 64 days now, learning Italian. I get that some people say it's too repetitive, yet I think that the whole point is that repetition is key to remembering, so I do enjoy that. I'd like to see a section where there's timetables for words and the (io, tu, lui/lei, noi, voi). I personally don't way for the premium plan, but my mom has it, and it's amazing!

TLDR: they are making changes to make you pay more. Duolingo as a language learning app is great. No issues there. However, in recent updates, options that used to be present previously are gone, in favor of something that will earn them money. The main issue is that you can no longer practice to earn hearts. You have to watch an ad. this is fine, except for the part where you can't extend your streak by practicing if you're out of hearts. Makes maintaining a streak way harder for no reason.

I do not get enough practice with the free version. The family plan is a good value. Bad news: contains a lot of ugly woke artwork throughout, and also some unpleasant bad attitudes in the characters in the Spanish course, totally unnecessary. Good news: It does seem helpful for practice, especially if you combine it with other language learning tools such as grammar lessons on YouTube, vocabulary practice, practicing with friends, etc. I really enjoy the Russian course and Spanish is ok.

I enjoy the app, and I am currently trying to learn Russian - however, there's an issue with the 'speak to answer' questions. You're meant to tap the screen to active the speech but it's only giving me half a second to speak a full, short sentence. it's incredibly frustrating considering I'm genuinely trying to learn, and I can fully do so without that feature working correctly. I would absolutely love for some feedback, if this is somehow my issue, or an issue as a whole.

March 2025 Update CONS: Ads. The biggest problem is too many and extremely repetitive ads. Have to watch ads for hearts. No respect for dark screen choice on android. Bring back the old characters. PROS This is mostly a good application. Love the stories, section-tips and mistake-tips/suggestions I like the new parts you added to each section such as present tense. Slow speed is a great feature.

Explaining errors shouId be included! We have the regular family plan. One thing I've always wished was that all answers could be read aloud by the app. The video game part, where you navigate "a player" to learn things, is annoying. That's a big fail as far as my learning style. I thought it was cool when I started seeing an option to "explain my error," only to be quickly disappointed. This was only for the Max which costs twice as much. No thanks. Explaining errors should be included.

So, recently I have been having mic problems when using the app. it does not seem to want to record my voice at all... which can be problematic when doing lessons where you need to repeat words or phrases. I know it isn't my phone because my mic works for literally anything else. I checked permissions and they are allowed to use mic for Duolingo. So it's not a hardware issue but a software one. So FIX IT!

My experience with this app was good to begin with. So I decided to get the subscription so I could practice more often without limitation. In the 3 years I've now had that subscription. The quality of the app is going down... I have made several suggestions, but nobody listens. So now I'm giving a negative review... Their examples are hard to hear and even when I say the as they sound they don't count... Even my Russian friend has said the examples don't sound clear enough to understand.

I joined Duolingo a few years back but couldn't get into it due to life. I have returned and have found the app has evolved into something even better than I remember. Visually appealing, interactive, mix of speaking and reading activities, some friendly competition for motivation, and good use of gamification. I am enjoying it thus far and will use it as a reliable tool to meet my language learning goals. Thanks for creating a great app.

would have rated it higher, but this app is starting to go downhill. don't get me wrong, its great if you know nothing about a language and want to learn easy for English speakers like Spanish, French or German, but between the blatant advertising at the end of every lesson, the lack of support for popular languages, the repetitive lesson structure ,the lack of teaching grammar structure, and most recently the removal of practice to earn hearts, i wouldn't recommend. I'll be quitting soon too.

Going downhill for money. When I started 3 years ago, Duolingo was great, and got better with updates. But starting 2025, they've changed for the worse, keep going downhill, and I'm about to leave, TBH. The gamificación has gotten too aggressive, the leagues aren't fun anymore, too much pressure to buy Max (which is pretty much garbage, from the trials I've had the misfortune of experiencing), NO helpful tips at the beginning of sections that makes learning WAY harder...make Duo fun again!

this app makes it fun to learn a new language. Unfortunately, they've removed some great functionality and added a ton of 30-second ads instead of the 10-second ads that used to me more common... Users can no longer practice to earn hearts. That was my favorite feature because those lessons reviewed a mix of prior lessons. I'm so disappointed they haven't replaced that ability...

Avid user, at its core it's a great app. But the push for monetization and buying Super Duolingo is absolutely ridiculous, not only are there ads everywhere (which are tolerable, to be fair) they removed the one feature that lets you keep using the app without paying or willingly watching more ads: practice for a heart. The whole defense of "it's faster for the user" is flawed because it's inherently forcing users to watch more ads. Put it in separately if you actually care Duolingo.

I have been using the free app for over a year. A recent change, where you can no longer practice (to earn hearts) seems to defeat the purpose of learning. Of course, by paying for Super Duolingo, you have unlimited practice time. However, I don't feel like this is helping me learn Spanish, so I am not going to upgrade to a monthly/annual fee to use.

Great for learning how to read Chinese characters and basic grammar. Decent for vocabulary acquisition. Terrible for learning how to pronounce the words. BUT, a lot of fun, motivating, and positive. I think I will learn my vocabulary and hanji characters here, and then take a real world class to learn conversation. A great way to get started quick. I've had excellent results!

Not good at learning languages. I'm trying to learn German, and it doesn't explain the grammatical rules that well. It throws you to the wolves and says good luck and bombards you with alot of words. This app also seems to be more focused on aesthetics and game play rather than teaching. It got me started, but I can't seem to learn more. Oh and the challenges are ridiculous, I keep having to do 50 every month while living a busy life and I can't do it anymore. Touch stress.

The app use to be good. I am on a 560 day streak, and I use to be able to practice whenever I wanted. Which really helped with learning and practicing what I was struggling with. Now, it's micro transactions out the whazoo for everything, and you can only do lessons now (to keep your streak going). Which is very annoying. I don't understand why they removed all that stuff, but I'm highly considering Uninstalling the app.

11/4/22: The update. Oh dear. Before, I had so many options for how to practice and learn: I could skip around, pick a different topic every day, or choose a story if I was feeling cozy. Now my path to learning is linear - if I get sick of the topic, too bad. And stories are locked away? I was a "Super" user and loved it. Today I cancelled. 4/8/25: Back to check it out. Oh dear. This is a high pressure environment now. Is there a lite version?

This app was great until they changed their ads. Now you have to sit through unstoppable ads, with deceptive tactics to make it impossible to "X" out (especially with fat fingers like mine. The ads pop up after every single exercise. Previously they were brief ads that didn't interrupt your flow, now you spend 20% of your time in the app watching stupid ads. I don't think I'll put up with it much longer if they don't change the format, and I'll just delete the app.

It's a fun learning app, but extremely unfriendly to free members. at first it seemed great, and I nearly made it all the way through the first section of Spanish, but as I got near the end it started bombarding me with ads for it's paid features. It's to the point where every single lesson is followed by a 30 second ad for either max or super. honestly without the ads I probably would have tried one or the other eventually, but now I find myself hesitant to even open the app and continue.

I was getting something out of this app but since they updated it so you couldn't practice for hearts anymore, I succumbed and bought a year of Super. It still doesn't explain why you made a mistake unless you pay even more money, and the daily goals in Super now include timed challenges, and if you run out of time, you can pay real money to get more time. For one lesson. The regular lessons are fun enough that I'll stay for the year I paid for but after that, I'll find another app.

The original goal of making language learning accessible has completely gone out the window in favor of corporate greed. If you want to watch constant advertising in your face, even when you're paying for a premium version, this is the app for you. If you would rather be a part of a cheap meme with language learning themes instead of spending time learning the language, this app is for you. I have been happily using Duolingo since around 2016, and it breaks my heart to see it like this.

Came back after years, waiting still for Slovak to be added but hasn't happened yet. Duolingo works well motivating you, it's intuitive, so I do recommend that. But I'm shocked after coming back and finding so intensely much advertising which wasn't there before. Especially for their own paid programme. Duolingo, I don't need reminding after every lesson, this is way overdone and to no avail.

Update: Started using this app again after not using it for many years, this time to learn Japanese (was practicing French before). This was the first time trying a language I hadn't had any learning exposure to previously. I can tell I'm picking up phrases and can read some things while I'm watching anime. The 3rd party ads aren't too bad, but the super duolingo 30s ads are really annoying and can't be skipped.

I currently have a family plan, and its a million times easier to learn on it. Once youre far enough in a language, there are stories, games, and talk show like fun to enjoy. But when I had the free version, if i got 5 questions wrong I was forced done for the day. This apps free version Id rate 2 stars, cuz new languages youll need to be wrong a lot more to learn.

it isn't bad, I am picking up words really well. It is just annoying when you have to type in a word and you type it correctly (accentuations and all) and it is somehow incorrect. It really makes it hard to progress in rank/level if the app is bugging out so bad you get an answer incorrect despite the correct spelling and accentuations.

Contains a lot of propganda. There are no clear instructions about how the app works, and what I have figured out doesn't seem very consistent. Things like when triple XP is available the next day, once you've earned it the day before. Or when exactly the triple XP expires. it's all very much a guessing game and quite annoying. Will probably cancel and try a different tool.

for the most part, this is a great program to brush up on vocabulary. You might even learn some languages. But there are bugs. I paid for the version where if you make mistakes, you don't have to wait a day to get back to learning. unfortunately, once I got past the baby steps, I found some bugs. In the module about talking about the weather, I ran into a problem where a sentence had two blanks, and you are supposed to fill in the two words. EXCEPT YOU CAN'T! So much for pay to play.

I have a 97-day streak! i love the app. it keeps me busy when I'm bored but also works my mind so I'm not just mindlessly scrolling on say "tiktok" or "insta" I love that it's free, but I also do love "super" duoling It's fun and gives you access to more things. but what I truly love about the app is it keeps up with all the languages you're learning, so you don't have to do one at a time. I and I would recommend it to anyone who wants to keep their minds busy while also learning a language!

The app is horrendous and constantly crashing on me, forcing me to use the website browser to keep up my lessons, and it's horribly uncomfy to use compared to the app. I don't know what they recently did, but this has only been happening in the late Feb/March months, and I'm at my wit's end with it. If I lose my streak in full as a result of the app crashing constantly, I will delete and never return.

When a heart is taken for an incorrect answer, it should be replaced if you get it right when you correct your mistakes. also, they shouldn't have silly things you'd never really use, like," My toes don't listen to me." And there have been a few times where my answer was auto corrected, and it made it wrong! Looking at it in context, what they changed it to didn't even make sense. There have been several times that I've been so fed up with the glitches that I quit, losing all my progress.

So Helpful! I've only been learning French for about 300 days but I can already speak comfortably in some areas! I will say, after having a conversation with some French friends of mine, I realized it's not entirely accurate, but it is a great start and I love how it makes it fun and competitive for ppl like me:) So if you're looking for some basic knowledge, go right ahead. But if you're looking for more fluency in a certain language I would try something else.

I will five star after the issue is addressed. Diamond league is swarming with bots/hackers. many in the top three will have tens of thousands of xp. There's no way to compete for the 1st place achievement with this particular problem. Also, the lack of screen sensitivity is infuriating when you hit the right combination, the continue on timer just to have a heart knocked off and to repeat the question at the end.

I've been using Duolingo daily for over 2 years and I used to love it. Over time they have made the free version less and less enjoyable. They have now taken away the option to do practice lessons, which helped me solidify my knowledge before moving on in my course. They've made it harder to get gems and they've made the features that require them cost more. They've lowered the XP you get from doing pretty much everything except moving through your course at a pace that's too fast for me.

I'm done with Duolingo. It was good when I started. I studied daily for a year, and recently subscribed for a second year. I wish now that I didn't. Since the latest "updates" it's glitchy, not giving credit for completed lessons, among other issues. The background is stark white (previously dark), which is IMPOSSIBLE for my eyes. There is ZERO customer support. They respond to nothing. There are many other language apps. I will choose another, and suggest all do the same. 🤮

I agree with many other reviews... Good at some of the basics but little explanation. Many of the exercises don't tell you the translation of the words. (When learning Japanese characters, you get the word right but there's no reinforcement or learning what the words are.)
