Also, the app icon is creepy looking.... apparently it changes if you miss a couple of days and now it's scary red. It has the opposite effect than I think it intends... I've removed it from my home screen.

Duolingo, not again. I bought 500 gems to repair my streak, but then the repair thing just vanished. I closed it and opened it again, but it still wouldn't appear. I want to fix my 400 streak, and I would appreciate that because I worked hard for that streak. But I will say this is a great app for other languange learning, music, and math!

Great app. The interface is organized sort of like a game which keeps you interested. And the lessons are based on contextual learning. So new words or phrases are usually introduced in sentences with other words that you are already familiar with. The app does rely heavily on push notifications to keep you on track. Which is a good thing for consistency. But it can be a little annoying at times. The best part of this app is the amount of free content. You can do almost everything for free.

Duolingo is pretty good but the streaks and ranking ruins it for me because I'm more focused on keeping my streaks and being high on the leader boards to even improve my language skill. I think there should be a feature to like turn it on or off. I know it can help you want to improve your language skills more but it just stresses me out in my opinion. If there is a feature that turn it off let me know please.

For some reason I am completely unable to speak in my exercises. I tap the option to speak and the app bugs out and counts the exercise as incorrect. This bug has persisted for over a month and Duolingo has done nothing to fix it. I have tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app and it appears to be a permanent feature of the app now, despite the fact that microphone permission is granted to the app.

After coming back after a few year hiatus, it's clear that the users are not the priority. There's no real incentive to keep learning past the multiplier timer. You can't practice to earn hearts, so it's harder to fully learn the concepts, which sometimes, aren't very clear in the first place. The push for AI content is atrocious and y'all should really be reading the room- nobody wants AI garbage. This used to be a really good app, but profit is clearly more important to you than your mission.

I enjoy parts of this app. But after providing feedback multiple times, including screenshots, regarding a bug affecting my scores in the app and getting no help or response, I'm getting frustrated and disappointed. I like using this app, but also would like my performance to be accurately recorded. Not sure what else to try at this point.

The app has recently been flooded with generic mobile advertising slop after every single activity, making it an actively unpleasant place to be. Additionally, the more I progress though the lessons, the more I suspect I'm not actually learning and am just getting better at pattern matching. Everything I "learn" goes right in one ear and out the other.

Loving it so far for me & my kids. A couple issues: lots of vocab is taught but no explanations about grammar or sentence structure. 4 stars because I do NOT like the characters they give. The first question in Spanish is "which one is the man?" w/ 3 pics. The woman has a partly shaved head, which is confusing for kids. There's also a moody teen & grumpy bear. Like...why. Just keep it positive & lose the agenda please!

I've had this app for about 10 years. During that time, I was able to gain unlimited hearts. Now, with the development of DuolingoMAX, the app has become subscription based. I would HAPPILY purchase the app for up to $40 if they gave the option. Oh, well. With the "MAX" option continuing to be pushed, the consistent annoyance of woke idealogy in the lessons, and the increased adds, I doubt this app will remain on my phone for much longer. Goodbye to what used to be a good thing.

I started with Spanish and loved it. I had to put Spanish on hold because I started nursing school and it started to feel like one more thing to add to my list. so In order to continue my streak I started the music lessons! 4 months later I can read music! I am still learning, but I have played music from a music sheet without the app. I am thrilled and thank you Duolingo for making my dream come true! I'll pick up Spanish again later!

it's fine for learning vocabulary and for exposure. but the actually interesting features are paywalled - those could ensure their dominance of the language learning apps but they don't do it. The rest of the program is fine for free but has so many bugs, that I can't imagine the paid tier would be worth it. It is somehow actively worse than when I began using it about 5 years ago, too.

It's cool I guess, like it helped me learn but I don't like how they have a leaderboard and they constantly compare your progress to other people's. They also have this thing where you start the day with 5 hearts (sometimes less for no reason) and if you run out by getting 5 answers wrong, then you have to either watch ads, wait for like 7 hours, or give them a monthly subscription to get more. I don't get this, because you have to have at least 1 heart to learn. DO THEY NOT WANT ME TO LEARN???

The way it's monetized is very predatory. I finally signed up for the paid, ad free version, only to THEN find out there are still sections of the app that are locked behind yet another, higher paid tier. If you want help figuring out why your mistakes were wrong, you would have to pay for this additional tier. When I first used this app years ago it was clear the primary intent was to help people learn new languages. That's changed. I would recommend another app, I won't be renewing my pro plan

I have been using this app for.ovwr one year. in the past month the a droid app only works well a few hours in the morning. After noon it crashes and does not work. Besides that, some brilliant minds decide to remove atractive incentives to study like 2x "chest" that could be unlocked depending on your activity. And the extra 5 points on Saturdays. before buying it: excellent app. the pricing is nowhere to be found. I am not taking the free trial without knowing the price.

I would have given Duolingo 4 stars, but recently, its ads are WAY TOO MUCH, and I have the paid version which isn't cheap!! I have been using this app for 2 yrs. I never rate an app right away just for this resson, as sometimes it takes a while before you get plowed with ads. Now they have the Super/max Duolingo and bombard you with ads MORE and MORE for the new version to up grade and also with ads for games. TOO ANNOYING!!

I've used the free version of this app for several years now for financial reasons. Up until the last year or so, that was fine. however, not only has the difficulty scale changed, the ads for super Duolingo have gotten very condescending, And they've completely stripped all of the assistant tools- like the ability to practice to re-earn your hearts- out of the free service. now, they just make you watch ads. that doesn't help anyone learn. I don't have fun learning on this app anymore.

Duolingo’s Mandarin course has significant flaws: the speech recognition AI is outdated and robotic, which is a huge problem for tone practice. It often freezes during speaking exercises, resetting progress. The app teaches unnatural phrases without context, and some words lack audio, with issues persisting despite reports. While it’s decent for vocabulary building, it fails at teaching real-world speaking. I’m on a 60-day streak, but the paid version isn’t worth it.

Super Duolingo has been great for learning and practicing Spanish. I find I retain words really well without needing to do a lot of vocab drilling. The only thing I dislike is that I feel Duolingo doesn't provide enough try/fail support. If I make 2-3 mistakes in a lesson, I would prefer to be required to try the whole lesson again. The heart system kind of does this, but because the hearts system is app-wide, you can't go and do a lesson in another language or do a math or music lesson.

Great in the beginning, but then forces you into a subscription to continue. For the first few units it does well in teaching you, then testing you. After that, it just tests you on every lesson without any instruction. You'll find yourself not learning any more and feeling pressured to purchase the subscription due to the errors you're making.

Great app. good at getting you hooked with the double, triple points etc once you've completed a lesson. Only deducted a star as years ago when I used it there were community boards and discussions on each question, so if you got something wrong you can learn why it was wrong. I think removing that was a mistake. still a good app for practice though and learning new words, sentence structures etc even if you won't learn the reasons why the language is constructed the way it is.

this app is great, and it very much helps with learning languages. however, it has an excessive amount of ads, with an ad shown every 2-3 lessons. it doesn't sound that bad, but lessons take 3 minutes tops, and i usually complete them in 1:30. that's an ad every 4 minutes and a half. insanity. edit: this is now a 3-star review after i switched to learning Russian. the Russian course is VERY secondary to their main ones, as it has no grammar tips. ads show after every lesson now. disappointing.

I first made an account on Duolingo in 2013. For a decade, I've used it off and on to maintain proficiency and learn, and I've also seen the service steadily degrade even as its offerings seemingly grew. There are frequent errors in content, the most basic practice features are locked behind a paid tier, and most of all, any other single language has vastly more features, better educational models, and isn't utterly strangled with ads. The paid tiers provide almost nothing beyond removing ads.

If I start a lesson at 11:56 and end 12:01 it uses a streak freeze. My lifestyle is very much oriented around night and I would essentially get punished for it, using all of freezes. Hence why I don't use the app anymore Some functionality is missing, such as in-app feedback (send separately through email, which has a link in app) ability to remove lenguages. I have 4 languages I added but don't have the time to learn on top of my 1st and I keep getting reminders for them.

My notifications stopped working, if I don't randomly remember to check the app for the morning/night chest, I lose my double points for the day. My biggest upset with this app is after about a year learning Swedish, I am unable to learn more and am stuck on a loop of reviewing lessons which do not change. It's just a loop of the same lessons over and over. There is absolutely more to learn for this language. C'mon Duolingo, use that marketing money to develop more lessons, we know you can.

I was very close to being a native speaker of the Turkish language some 20 years ago. I was planning to refresh my knowledge of it as I have not used it much in the past 20 years. Duolingo frequently misuses vocabulary, the sentence structure sounds very unnatural or wrong frequently as well. It is ok to pick up vocabulary, but don't expect more from this app

The education portion is great; but Dark Mode is no longer an option, which makes using the app painful. New additions of math and music are frustrating. Math does not introduce new concepts before quizzing on them. Music plays nearly a beat behind the displayed note. The problem with sound not syncing to visuals is a constant in regular speech.

Frequently freezes up while doing lessons. Doesn't always credit me with the Daily Quests I've completed (like reading the next lesson). Still freezes after installation on new phone. Occasionally doesn't give me the double or triple points for the full time once I've earned them. And the App icon keeps disappearing from my home screen each day as the change the icon's appearance. Utterly annoying!

Fantastic app for learning languages but I dislike the newest update with all of the super long ads. If I didn't have a streak going I'd stop using it. 3-11-25 update For most of February the long ads went away but recently they came back again. Very disheartening. Makes me not want to use the app at all but I've got a good streak going. update 4/6/25 I'm done. The ads are longer than my lessons are. It's just not worth it anymore.

It is only sort of acceptable as a learning app. It does not truly teach and is more of a practice drills sort of activity. But the reason I am making a review now after years of use is that recent changes are really unpleasant. The computer generated speech is grating and often incorrect. The newest lengthy animations are wasting my time waiting for them to end. And they've added a HUGE amount of vibrations to things that makes it actively irritating to hold your phone.

not sure it translates to actually speaking. all of the extra animations and game style stiff takes away from the learning. way more ads than there used to be. just not as enjoyable as it once was. Like seriously, does anyone feel like being randomly quizzed on spelling? Spelling. For words in a different language that you have seen a handful of times? Is that what anyone downloaded this app to do? I have to buy hearts because of the pop spelling quiz?

It's still a good app, but the updates have been slowly making it less and less worth using. Removing the ability to just practice was a horrible move, and the new ads for Super Duolingo are obnoxious. They used to be stunning animations with a voiceover talking about what offers were available, now they're just stationary characters with an AI voice reading out some horribly unfunny dialogue that also sounds like it was written with AI. It's starting to make me genuinely hate these characters.

App loads quickly now. Still only portrait mode (which makes typing challenging for me.) I strongly dislike all of the animations that were recently added! They are distracting and slow down the app. I am not sure of their pedagogical purprose. (But they can be turned off in the preferences menu.)

It's pretty clear that Duolingo has been generating lessons with AI and the quality of the lessons has been dropping as a result. The most egregious is the podcast type lessons they've introduced where nothing is said at all. They are some of the most unnatural conversations I've ever heard, so all you really get out of it is practicing hearing the words. There's some value in that, but I'd rather have a story that actually makes sense

I've used it for a year now, and I've learned a little bit of Spanish. It's definitely better with Super Duolingo because I could practice my weak areas. Should download if you like gamified learning. I do recommend that Duolingo developers increase the level of each language for English speakers, and after making it where it's one CEFR level per language, then do it for the non English speakers. I think there should also be a section in the practice hub where it focuses on grammar.

I was sad to discover features and services were given priority to iphone over Android. As an android user that subscribed to premium it took the wind out of my sails. Paid the annual fee, but once that is expired likely going to walk away. Not thrilled on paying to be a third rate user. Otherwise the experience was good, but the repeating lesson structure turned into a boring chore after a few months.

There are so many avenues and games that could be added to this for additional learning fun. For the price, I don't know that it is worth it, although I bought the family plan. I'm hoping they will expand the gaming universe of duolingo to keep things interesting. Charging extra after paying a big payment for explanations of your mistakes feels very predatory. It needs a dictionary. 4.3.25 Changing to 1 star. They have pop-ups that can steal your hard earned diamonds with one click. Scam.

Loving this game-ified style of learning! All the characters are wholesome and profile character customization has many cultures/races in mind (rarely see that in even mainline games). The ads are not at all heavy handed, although would be fun if they were in the language you're trying to learn. For a free experience this is a fantastic tool to add to your language learning toolbox!

Such a wonderful app! Even the free version gives you the option to practice for more hearts, so you can keep learning all day if you want! And the blogs are a great resource for bite sized learning. Just an amazing opportunity for people who want to learn on a budget. I do wish they would add an American Sign Language course! But other than that I'm impressed 👍🏻

Loved the older versions! Revising my review because there's no more practice (you know, where you're supposed to learn from your mistakes like the app claims)... I'm crushed for the kids trying to learn other languages with Duo, but can't anymore unless their parents hand over their wallet. This current version is a reversal of Duo's "Mission" to help everyone learn. Bring back practice.

Overall it's a decent language learning app. Definitely needs to be supplemented with other resources to actually learn a language otherwise it just turns into a game for people. Still disappointed by the removal of the basic, but necessary, "Practice to Earn Hearts" feature but at least you can get hearts back without wasting gems now, but I miss the practice. The app development is a bit of a rollercoaster so expect so expect some progress setbacks and popular features to disappear.

Really like this approach to learning another language--it starts small and lets you build up knowledge at your own best pace. Sometimes I want a little more scholarly approach, like in learning the letters of the Greek alphabet by finding out what sounds each letter covers and what its name is. In these cases, I supplement Duolingo with a little research of my own. But on the whole, Duolingo uses a non-scary, user friendly style of learning that feels like a game. I recommend it!

Duolingo is the best app to learn language. I am currently trying to learn Korean,and I have 2 other language apps downloaded. If you can manage the time to learn what each word means. That being looking at the other 3 choices and learning what they are instead of mindlessly completeing the lessons, you get a lot more out of this Superiorly designed app.

Very helpful and makes learning a new language fun, but I have to harshly dock it because I find the practice of advertising more expensive plans to users who are already paying for a plan to be highly tasteless. Super Duolingo is either ad-free or it isn't. Putting Duolingo Max features on the lesson "trail" like video calls only to say "sorry, you have to pay more to use this haha" is particularly gross and makes me less likely to continue paying for Duolingo at all.

it's great for getting started. it works a good deal differently across several devices. Phone, tablet and laptop. either way. I've found it will count your answers as wrong when they are actually correct just not the exact words posed by the app. So. it's pretty discouraging to use for beginners! Especially since It consistently uses ugly bullying practices to try to motivate users. What a Disgusting practice. I will need to use a different app with the children. This one is not appropriate! 😡

Dear devs, is this a known issue? The "music" lessons are driving me crazy. I have a top tier phone, and somehow this type of lesson makes the phone lag like crazy during them, making me either miss the notes, or push them earlier. I love the app (except the ads), please address this issue for a higher user experience and rating. Thank you in advance!

I love Duolingo. I'm a super Duolingo subscriber. BUT, recently my XP is still stuck at 3214points and has NOT increased at all. Even when I use an XP booster, the number doesn’t change, which is very frustrating. I'm not even able to get time booster set which needs 4800xp. I reported this issue twice, still nothing changed. That's a bummer 😕 PLEASE FIX THIS ISSUE DUOLINGO TEAM PLEASE 🥺🙏

I've studied Spanish through highschool and college. I didn't want to do the last 2 Spanish public speaking courses in college, otherwise I would have minored in Spanish. I approve of Duolingo as a great study tool when you already have a foundation. I can't give an absolute ground zero opinion though but this is a very well designed app and they have kept growing and developing a fun experience for years now. I'd give it a try, it might surprise you

As a free app it is pretty good, I don't mind the ads most of the time. One thing I'm noticing is almost every change they make is to force you to buy premium which is too costly for me to do. Not to mention about 50% of the most annoying ads are for premium. Today's experience is I have a 755 day streak, I did a normal training and because you have to pay for them to explain why your wrong I don't understand why letters are switching making me get it wrong. So pretty soon I'm going to be done.

I have been using Duolingo for over 3 years everyday. I have been using it to learn French and it's become apart of my routine to do a lesson. To be frank, the app does a bad job at it. Firstly, I've been through what feels like 5 curriculum changes since I've joined so I am constantly unsure of what my real progress is. The ads make using the free version impossible and the paid version doesn't offer enough value. Lastly, there's no way to learn a language with an app, u need someone to talk to

The lessons don't explain why a choice is being made anymore. instead you have to pay extra money to see an explanation. and several times the answers are wrong or the hints to help are wrong. I'm moving on to other apps. after 668 days off a streak I tested "level 2 newcomer" and each app literally started me at "hello" and "goodbye". For a lower price I get better lessons and explanations on other apps. To be fair, ANY explanation is better than the zero explanation from Duo.

They have completely ruined what was once a great app. Very glitchy to the point that it has become frustratingly unusable. Who ever took the app over has ruined it. I would even pay to use it, but not until they fix it. Stay away until then. You will know by the comments if that ever happens. Ok. I paid for and used the app for a year. That experience was much better. It is a good way to begin learning Spanish. You do have to supplement the app with grammar books etc .

The app is solid. Definitely leans towards a strong bias of reading vs writing and speaking. The bad side is that there is zero support. I've submitted two different tickets over the past month+ and followed up on each of them multiple times with a single response other than their automated response stating they received my message and updated my ticket.

8 year streak- Duolingo was a big part of my life, being able to take 5-10 minutes a day to better myself and learn 3 languages solidly. With a recent update the app is now PLASTERED with ads. Not just regular bargain basement ads, but unskippable 60 second videos or game demos- if you skip? You don't get your XP or daily quest points. I'm going to hit 2700 days and never touch this app again, another amazing thing ruined by profit-seeking and private investment.

I hate, like absolutely HATE that they changed Legendary from the distinguished, beatuiful purple color to the tacky gold color it now is. Now having my lessons be legendary doesn't even feel special anymore. Update: Also, I wish the listening challenges (like the radio ones) had an option to listen to it slowed down. I can't keep up with everything they say, and sometimes I barely can get the gist of the "episode." Wish it would go back to the old duolingo... not a fan of all these "upgrades".

this app owes me 15 minutes of 3x points. It froze in several ways mid-exercise. I checked if it needed an update - yes. So I let it update. Still frozen. No navigating within app - regular lesson wouldn't start, it wouldn't load time, nothing. I closed it, force closed it, nothing, finally uninstalled/reinstalled, since that was more feasible than restarting the whole device. IT WON'T EVEN LET ME LOG IN NOW by any means. It won't even let me reset my password to log in. Fix your ish, Duo.

I was able to quickly shake the dust off of the Spanish I learned in high school. I had originally given a 5 star review, but the recent push towards generative AI and fewer real people involved in creating the lessons often leaves typos or mistakes uncorrected or vice versa. I used to have a paid subscription but Duolingo will not get anymore money from me as long as they continue to abandon real speakers and teachers in favor of a deeply flawed AI model.

Had a temporary issue at the get started screen. Gave up un-installed and later tried again. I don't like that you can't redo lessons, only a short review. Repetition of lessons would be really helpful in how I learn. I don't like that there is no word and phrase full list of learned words. Overall the app is fun

This app used to be great, but is now terrible at speech exercises. Certain phrases will simply not work for literally no reason, even with perfect pronunciation. It feels like the service is trying to scam people out of hearts just to get more users to pay or make it impossible to get perfect scores on lessons by failing people during one of the most simple tasks.

Vietnamese lesson was very short and didn't go too in depth. Kind of disappointing. The music lessons don't always sync with the music and says you're out of tempo when you aren't or it doesn't like up with the music. It's irritating when I know I'm not off and the app deducts points. Okay overall but annoying sometimes.

Please bring back Dark Mode. This app is blindingly bright in light mode, and removing the option to toggle Dark Mode and instead locking it to the phone's settings is irritating to the point that I downloaded Babbel. It's a great learning app other than this surprising and distracting removal of a useful feature that improves user experience. I am excited to edit this review once the feature has been fixed!

This is by far my favorite language learning app. I have a 753 day streak learning German and everyday I am excited to learn my next lesson. It is great in motivating you to keep learning with the streak and ranked leaderboards. The art is really cute and well done and the classes are well designed to learn language naturally through immersion rather than trying to explain a set of rules. It definitely doesn't replace traditional language courses but it is a great supplemental learning source!

Been using it daily for 4+ years now and it used to be the absolute best app on the market. But now it's just throwing 2 ads at you after each lesson and they've removed the practice to earn hearts feature which has essentially made it impossible to learn anything on the app. Edit: well the ability to earn back hearts with ads is back so at least it's somewhat usable now.

Not a bad language app. Unfortunately there are so so many unwanted ads, both from third parties and from duolingo themselves. After literally every 2 or 3 minute lesson. This app would get at least 4 stars otherwise. It almost seems worth the investment to start a new paid account with one of the premium language companies.

I first used this after reading the Duolingo comic on webtoons, which was adorable, & loved it immediately. I always struggled with school but wanted to learn Italian. Duo made learning possible in such a fun way! The support and encouragement through the various exercises, badges, & daily streaks, help encourage me to keep learning. I've found so much joy in learning various languages & didn't realize how easy it can be! The only thing that would make this app better would be if they added Sign

If you want to learn a commonly spoken language, I find the courses are very strong and memorable. The approach to learning and playful animations Duolingo uses makes language learning feel like a game. My only complaint is that I find the course in Hebrew a bit buggy and isn't fully voiced like other courses like Spanish. I'm assuming it is this way for languages that aren't widely spoken. It'd be nice to see them eventually get around to improving the Hebrew course. Even so, I'm learning.

still a good app for learning vocab, but wow has it become bloated with ads and gamification gimmicks. Having to watch ads on a free app is totally understandable, but after almost every lesson now you get a 3rd party ad, then a Duo Max ad, then some friends streak thing, then an update on your 'league', then your daily quests, then.... on and on and on. I feel like I spend more time watching ads and animations after each lesson than I do actually working through the lesson.

Edit: 2025 Duolingo has gone down the drain since they became a public entity. Everything now requires payment in one way or another. There is an unskippable full screen ad after every lesson. mistakes are paid, legendary is paid. the entire app now forces you into an 8 dollar subscription to be like it was before they sold out original: 2016 Free, no bugs, It's perfect for anyone to learn almost any language

Best for Latin based languages, beware others. Mandarin lessons leave much to be desired. I would have rated higher if every other lesson weren't a writing lesson. Duolingo hardly ever explains the "why" which is foundational to writing Chinese characters. I really hate the writing lessons. I regret accidentally buying a year of Duolingo super! I'm finding more success with the Pimsleur app which focuses on learning new phrases in a practical way. They're comparable in price.

Best learning app! I'm currently learning Japanese, Music, and catching up on my Spanish! And don't worry, if you lose your streak, Duo will not take your family, they're just jokes! Love this! You could also have friends, and friend streaks! Meaning you could be doing lessons with your friends & family! So many other languages to choose from!! I totally recommend if you're traveling, or just want to learn for fun!!!

This is a great app fot learning a multitude of languages. From speaking, forming sentences all the way to expressing your thoughts in that language, it really is a great tool. Doulingo also helps you prepare for real world experiences like ordering food, calling a cab or many more situations in by simulating it. This is app is a must if you are new to a languagr or want to learn. For me, its far superior that Babble by a mile. The app is also very motivating towards the user to keep on learning

The app usually works great, but lately, it's been very buggy. They've been updating it with new/refreshed animations, but it's still buggy. They used to be a 5-star app for me, but over the years, they've dropped features that used to really help you to learn the language, and now they're charging you even more to add those features back. Pretty lame considering they claim to want to help the world learn. I used to love the company and app, but now they're just like any other company.

Duolingo has completely got rid of everything that I loved about it, cranked up the ads, and replaced it with a terrible AI subscription service scheme. It is worth nothing now.. The biggest thing is they removed the "Practice for Hearts" option in order to push people to pay for some (now) Artificial Intelligence slop. I used to like Duolingo, but way too many terrible decisions are being made on this app I feel.. I NO LONGER RECOMMEND THIS TO ANYONE LEARNING A LANGUAGE. FIND ANOTHER APP

This app is amazing. It's helping learn new languages I've always wanted to learn without having to leave my couch 😄. I am currently learning Japanese and it's very helpful. Though, with languages like that I'd recommend doing a curriculum on how to write in Japanese because it shows how it's written in English form above the actual word in Japanese so it's kind of trippy with actually learning how to recognize the unique characters in its actual language. Please fix this Duolingo!

I've been a regular duolingo user for years now but the continuous downgrading of features which were previously free to all users is making it unusable. you can't even practice to earn hearts back, meaning if you fail a lesson you're forced to watch an add or spend an in game currency which can be quite limiting. don't see how that's promoting learning. and that's not the only feature they've removed in recent years. heavily considering switching to another app.

They continue to lock any explanation of mistakes behind a pay wall. I find it a poor decision. You're supposed to be a free app. I can see charging for conversation practices and other advanced teaching methods, but to have the base app not explain anything is not great. This is even if you are paying for a subscription. The SUPER subscription is where you get explanations. The super subscription is twice the price of the basic subscription.

please just use another app. i have an 86 day streak and i dont feel like i've learned much. it mostly teaches you irrelevant sentences and when learning a new word, you have to guess it at most times. also i wish there was a feauture to turn off the leaderboard. and i feel like its forcing me to do a lesson, and its kimd of hard to stay consistent. also, the limited hearts really make it kind of hard to actually learn. especially when it puts a "duolingo super" ad after every lesson.

Paying for nothing! I have been using the paid version of this app for 4 years. Now, even though I pay, I still have to watch ads. And as many have mentioned no more just practicing. So, gone from 5 stars to 2. Tried the "Super" upgrade; nothing happened: no practice sessions, still have ads and only 4 lives. Free trial of nothing! Boo 👎

The free version is sufficient, but it really has a lot of ads. The writing is entertaining and the game-like approach does keep you engaged. Duolingo Max is kinda pricey, but it does help with why you make mistakes or why a conjugation works one way or another. The video calls need a wicked fast Internet connection though.

This is a good way to have fun while learning a language. I don't like the constant barrage of ads for buying more upgrades. I bought a time boost for $20, and it actually didn't give me a time boost. Rather, I've been stuck on the same challenge while it is asking me to buy more time boosts. Where is the first time boost that I paid for?

It was bad enough when they took away practicing to earn hearts past the first one - now I can't even practice to get them, I have to watch ads. And the best part is... it doesn't even work! This morning I watched two ads and earned two hearts - just now I watched 6 ads and didn't get a single heart. It's completely bugged out, and now I can't do anything but wait. It would be better if we had the OPTION to either practice or watch ads, and even better if it actually, you know, worked...

It's a good tool to use if you're going to be speaking primarily in class or on vacation, but the vocabulary is limited. The program also teaches reading and writing much better than it does speaking. It doesn't give you much of an opportunity to practice. I'm using it to brush up on verb conjugation in various tenses, it's helpful for that.

I've used Duolingo of and on now for over 12 years the lessons are fun and interactive , and I can honestly say they have gotten better over time . Update I've had the app for a while.and I've noticed some glitches , little annoyances like telling me a sentence is wrong when I have the exact sentence / word / spelling so I'll be penalized and then move on but its annoying , and there are Soo many adds in the free version . Thinking I might need to shop around

this app has gone downhill to trash. Upgrading to super is a waste of cash. Whatever you love about the app will be changed in a few weeks. There are so many reoccurring issues and new ones that come along. Stick with the free version or get super for 1 month, you'll see the glaring issues for yourself, and save a lot of money.

app glitches and will screw you over time and time again to try to ram super which costs too much down your throat. the app will make it so difficult when you use it trying to force you to buy super. so unless you are planning to pay for this overpriced app go find a more user friendly way of learning that doesn't try to suck all the coins out of your pockets.

Daily user of app and the new design has really taken a lot of the joy out of practicing. The "broken emblems" was a fun concept that kept you bouncing around previously completed lessons daily and rewarded you nicely while refreshing your knowledge. Now any past learning is almost discouraged with low rewards and a lot of scrolling. Would be nice to have option to go back for practice and have need ui for new learning.

Here's the problem... when you make mistakes, the option to continue practicing is taken away from you. Of course, I have the option to buy more opportunities. But being forced to watch ads should keep it a free app. Unfortunately, it's gradually moving away from educating to forcing paid subscriptions.

I tried learning Korean and was incredibly frustrated with Duolingo's approach. I haven't even finished the first Unit and they are asking me to repeat complex sentences with words they haven't taught yet? I would like to see them revisit these lessons and be more intentional about teaching proper pronunciation of the basic sounds and words. It is very demoralizing to fail the speaking lessons and examples over and over because you don't know how to correctly pronounce the words they are using.

I do like Doulingo.The number of death threats you can get from it is a very big amount. It's a very easy way to learn any language. Sometimes,I feel like it moves a bit fast with new words. The story part of it is annoying. They ALL have gay characters and not good things. If that wasn't the case, I would've given more stars. I at the moment am learning Chinese, I think it's a great way for anyone to learn! I just wish they could get rid of the stories. And keep the death threats to a minimum.

I love everything about this app and its special reminders😂 but by the time I updated the app, all my legendaries had gone, and the style of stories changed, which I don't like. It turned out to be a monologue. It's boring, and voices in stories don't sync anymore, which leads to misunderstanding the story.

Duolingo removed the option to practice mistakes as a way to earn hearts back. This makes learning the language more cumbersome and ultimately takes away the option to repetitively reinforce what the user gets wrong. This decreases the chance of extending your streak because you either get fed up watching 3 ads in a row (counting super duo ads) for one heart and close the app or you wait for the hearts to refill and forget entirely that you didn't complete the lesson and lose your streak.

The recent change to almost completely remove the functionality of the practice to earn hearts feature has made it an exercise in frustration. It's a shame because I've loved this app for almost two years, even after they added ads after most lessons. Edit: and it keeps getting worse as they ramp up the competitive aspect & remove functionality. Well, you can completely refill your hearts again now which is good but it's by watching multiple ads, not practice which is bad

Duolingo is a good learning app. However I downgraded them to 3 stars because I think they are losing an important element by eliminating the community help feature. The feedback others gave on why answers were wrong was not only helpful, but there was a connection through the language; essentially I believe Duolingo lost a portion of its soul with this change. Edit: downgraded to 2 stars. $95 for super, and $165 which contain elements that used to be free. Must watch ads for heart regen. Sad.

The app itself is great. I am giving two stars because I was NOT reminded about billing two days prior to trial ending as advertised, for which I was charged the ANNUAL amount rather than the monthly amount. Now I will continue using the app since I have paid, but users beware: there is a gatcha element to this. If you are ok being near-randomly charged $119, do the trial version.

I've been using Duolingo since around 2014 and it's gotten better with each year. I mostly use it to learn Japanese now, it's a great beginners app and the best part is it's free. The ads have increased a lot since I started but they aren't unbearable and the learning algorithm has gotten so much better. I originally used it to learn German and now the algorithm has progressed so far I need to start over in that language to be at the "appropriate level" to where I left off a few years ago.

The content seems decent, but the flashy get you hooked and hound you to pay isn't a good feeling overall. Gems, Ads, streaks, combos, competition, asking you to add friends, and worst of all limited lives so you have to either pay,wait,or watch ads? A good language app doesn't need to use freenium strategies to get people to pay.

used to be good, but now it feels like they're trying to actively push me off the app or pay money. like they took away the practice feature if you run out of hearts and made it harder to collect gems and more expensive to amend mistakes (buy hearts). there are also more and longer ads. I feel like in many ways they've lost their company vision "make learning accessible to everyone" and are actively making it less accessible and making learning harder than it was 3 years ago

glad to be learning a language. but there is way to many ads. theres one at tje end of each lesson. by the end of spanish section 1 i will have watched 181 ads. not to mention the super duo ads after each ad. so in total to compete one section. i woukd have to watch 262 ads........... why!? this isnt convincing me to get premium. i originally downloaded this because it was a free resource but nowadays this app is just annoying

I'm really enjoying this app. The only reason I did 4 stars instead of 5 is a few times I have gotten an answer wrong that was either not covered or was only mentioned one time so I don't remember it even being mentioned at all. However, it's thankfully not hard to get more hearts but it is kind of frustrating to lose a good roll/fail a whole lesson because of that. But I am really having a lot of fun with it. I've edited to reflect my feelings on current changes and glitches.

My review is partly my fault, because I caved to the constant nagging to try the free trial of Super Duolingo and didn't cancel it in time. One star because they assured me they would remind me before the trial ended, and they did not. They also failed to inform me that it was $99 for a year, so imagine my surprise when I opened my banking app today. And they don't do refunds. I better know Mandarin like I do English by the time this year is over 🙄

the app is great and I like doing the lessons mostly to keep my streak going. I have a 500+ streak right now. so when the app updated to where only practice lessons are for Super and not for people who don't have it I got disappointed. the amount of gems I have to spend to get full hearts is kinda outrageous. I like practicing for more hearts because it gave me more hearts and helped me practice. Duolingo becoming the new Spotify???

Magnificent learning platform. Highly recommend to anyone wanting to learn a new language including Music. Hopefully, they continue to expand the content to include more subjects. Super is a nice add on but I love that they don't force you to get it and you can still go through all the courses without it!

Everything is nice but IT'S REALLY FORCED to have the streak. Duolingo I know that the streak persuade the learners but in my opinion the streak is VERY overwhelming. How I think you should change is by adding a switch at settings that can stop the streak program. People can see this overwhelming is by the streak. For an example the streak can my people like me forced to do duolingo or I lose my /their streak. To continue, I am learning Korean and everytime I do the voice stage it can't hear me.

The app doesn't really help with learning languages all that much and now after every lesson I get 2 ads. I loved when it was just every few lessons you get 1 ad that Duolingo made, but now there is an extra ad for entirely different apps. Overall, it just doesn't feel the same and I'd recommend using a different app for learning languages.

I have really enjoyed using this app and am currently just over a 300 day streak. The only thing I can complain about is that the monthly quest meter doesn't seem to be working properly. I missed January's badge after doing plenty more exercises than it said I did. Now for February, I've been doing 2-3 lessons each day and as of Feb 9, it's showing I've only done 4 quests. I'm not sure what isn't working properly to count my lessons, but I'd appreciate if that could be reviewed and fixed.

update, I paid to get Max and it's really frustrating that it cost so much, but the video calls don't work half the time. Maybe work or the kinks before charging a ridiculous amount to use a broken product! Why can I no longer practice to earn hearts? I don't mind the ads, but features are being taken away from the free version. Duo, what happened to your mission to make learning a language fun and free? You are turning into a sellout!

I've been using Duo Lingo for four and a half years and have a paid subscription. I've enjoyed using Duo. The app used to have a very useful feature for users to discuss the answers of lessons. A while back this feature disappeared. Now, Duo Lingo has a new, much more expensive subscription tier with the aforementioned feature. Without the higher tier, there is no explanation of certain lessons if you make a mistake. Not a great way to do business, you evil owl!

It is difficult to follow along. Doesn't provide guidance or reason when a wrong answer was made UNLESS you purchase the higher version. I have to guess at answers because no instruction is given. I figured out on my own that if you make an error, you need to wait a day and retry your lesson. Eventually, you just memorize the right answers without learning what you're communicating. They're always pushing you to buy your way to more trials. Very frustrating lessons.

I find the Duolingo app really helpful for learning words and phrases, there is something in the multi-phase (speak, listen, write, repeat) that is more effective than other apps. The problem with this app is that I am more interested in speaking than writing. As a tourist or short term visitor, there is little reason to be able to write Japanese or Chinese characters - many native speakers are not good at this. An opt out option would be helpful, to allow focus on the things that matter.

I gave this app five stars ten years ago but I have to edit now because the obsession with the streaks has ruined the experience completely. I enjoy the lessons in the app but with no way to turn off the competitive elements I lose my motivation and get more stress than joy from it. I'd recommend finding any alternative way to learn a language.

The app could be more challenging at times. Sometimes you can get your way to the correct answer with elimination while thats not an option in real life. And another example is while pairing, trying to have words from similar categories. When I get words like Monday, Pants, Until, Five and Soccer it becomes too easy to realise. Rather have "Pants, dress, shirt" etc. Ps. Super Duolingo ads are too repetitive and long.

I am increasingly disappointed with Duolingo. It used to provide a clear series of lessons and encouraged me to practice my learnt vocabulary to recover lost hearts. This is no longer available. The inability to practice to recover lost hearts is motivating me to stop learning languages. I have tried the music lessons and I find the virtual keyboard lags and misses touches on my phone making it too difficult to get the one star to progress in the music playing lesson.

App is great to start a new language. However, it is too much gamified. XPs are tilted towards practicing at specified times for a specified period, which is unfair. Besides, they could have added tips for each chapter and full explanation after each answer so that it helps you learn more. The worst part of the app is such a loud beep sound after each answer that it won't let you use the app, even if you have lowered volume with someone sleeping around. Hope, they resolve these issues soon.

I used to love Duolingo. One used to be able to actually learn on the free version with enough focus and determination. In the last two years, they've made inhibative changes that make the free version downright frustrating to use. They no longer have grammar helps available on questions; they've taken away explaining mistakes and moved THAT to DuoMax. They've taken away the option to practice now, and replaced it with an option to watch ads for more hearts. Will the "upgrades" never end?

I enjoy using this app, but there are a few problems. It doesn't explain grammar or sentence structure, so you just hope you can pick it up. They have also taken away the ability to win back hearts by doing practice lessons, so you have to pay a high gem price or wait 24hrs to get full hearts again, and the monthly price is too high. If it was £6 or £7 a month, I'd be tempted to subscribe, but not ay £10! The adverts have gotten worse, they are very loud, and I'm beginning to want to leave.

Outstanding app. I've been learning Italian for just over 30 days, and I'm fairly confident with simple sentences. The app keeps on your case (if you enable it to) to keep your streak going. The pronunciation lessons are a bit of a pain as it doesn't pick up what I say, but I've only had one penalty due to that issue. The other gripe is that there's one feature you cannot access without a paid subscription. I understand why, but it's a pain none of the less.

It's decently effective and engaging, but it is extremely pushy with its marketing. I pay for super, and they still try to force me to use Max. I pay to remove ads, and I still get ads for Max. Add to that the fact that it's notorious for teaching grammar that sounds weird to native speakers and nonsensical sentences, and you have a product that leaves me feeling frustrated. This is the Blizzard of language learning apps. Money and shareholders first. You used to be so cool, Duo. What happened?

Very, very annoying adverts after literally every lesson unless you pay - and since you have to pay a year in advance that's not a small sum. Sometimes what they teach you isn't correct either (as per my OH who is a native speaker of one of the languages I'm learning). Very, very, very US based. App doesn't always recognise lessons that have been completed either. TL:DR I'm looking for a better language course.

Practice button has disappeared and seemingly been replaced with a button to upgrade to "Max". I'm already paying £71.88/year for Plus. Not happy that I now get pestered to pay even more for a feature I don't want (AI video calls) just to regain a feature I used to have (practice). If that doesn't get reverted this will be the last year of my 7 years subscription.

The latest update to the Japanese course has basically ruined it. I'm about half way through section 3, so reasonably far into the course, and it's giving me sentences like "I eat rice". This is stuff taught in section 1 and is just a waste of time this far into the course. Now listening lessons have also been removed entirely... Will be cancelling my subscription and finding an alternative if these changes stick. The course has gone from a really good learning supplement to completely useless.

Very good app for learning languages online. Love that maths and music learning have been added to the list, making it more fun to use. The presence of ads at times is quite disturbing, but not a big deal. And also, when you're taking a speech test in the German language, it doesn't work and wouldn't record your voice, making it impossible to move forward in the speech test or even do it to begin with. This is quite disappointing because you wouldn't know if your German accent is correct.

I was a bit skeptical to use Duolingo again after the whole revamp thing, and after i heard they got ads now. but turns out it's still great, the ads are only after lessons, and right after i finish my super free trial, they immediately offered me another week free??? i don't know if this is a mistake on their part but i'm glad i'm able to learn a new language on a budget.

Overall the application is fine. There are some considerations: Today's review section is VERY repetitive, you can fo the same exercises 10 times in a row. The speaking mechanic is one of it's weakness (even with native speakers), and hints are not always synchronized with the unit (meaning suggestions are useless). Grammar is inexistent. On every mayor update your progress is lost if you finished the course. The feedback you give takes months to be applied. The family plan is the best option.

I used to really love this app, however, it has progressively got worse. Learning has become too heavily gamified, and grammer is difficult to reiview without paying for a subscription to access templates or practice sessions. The 'hearts' system has also become a pain. If stuck on a difficult section, one has to repeatly alternate between a practice session that has no basis is current learning and the main event. There are better apps for each specific language, that I reccomend people look to

Great app for learning languages. But I hate the hearts stuff. If you make a mistake, you lose a heart, and you learn by making mistakes, and you can onlg make 5 mistakes. So it's basically punishing you for learning. It doesn't give me any motivation to keep on going. Please remove the hearts. It would make the app way better and actually help me learn faster.

Adding to the review below which I wrote a few weeks ago.... its still not showing the lessons you've done when one of the daily challenges is to complete a number of lessons. Thats very annoying and so is that they took away the option to practice to earn hearts unless you are down to zero. Previous review - I'm still doing daily lessons after nearly 4 years. Lately it hasn't been updating the challenges, like today it wanted me to do 3 lessons - I did 4 lessons and it only showed 1!

The WORST thing about duolingo is that they do not have an option for "vacation mode." I assume students use this app, and during the exam week, it's definitely difficult to keep up with the streak. First of all, the "streak" method to get us to learn is not effective at all. I find myself just mindlessly doing lessons just to get a streak. It's my exam time, and I have no idea what I have learned on duo recently. Please, duo, come up with a better strategyrather than pestering us all day long

They have removed the option to practice to earn hearts (as in more tries). Instead you are forced to watch and ad or wait 5 hours (it used to be 4). If you are punished to this extent for making a mistake how does this encourage learning. I feel that eventually this ap won't be free altogether. Already they made a more expensive subscription version as if paying a basic monthly amount wasn't enough...

Found the app to be quite useful. However, this last week, the app has stopped allowing you to submit speech when prompted. It submits your answer straight away, not allowing you a chance to speak. This is happening on my families iPhone's as well as Android's. Flagged every time in the app, yet no fix with the recent update. 17/03 - still no change.

I have been using it for a long time (with Duolingo Plus nearly 3 years), but now that I have the opportunity to use my knowledge seems like if I didn't knew anything, all the grammar explanation for small languages is almost forgotten in the app, the only thing that could change this point is a more detailed grammar explanation section, like the one that appears up top every path, besides this huge disadvantage the whole app is good for learning languages. Plus music course is buggy.

It is great that you can practice languages for free. However you get what you pay (or not pay) for. It is becoming increasingly frustrating. Hints are often wrong or missing. When you work towards challenges, rewards are repeatedly taken away from you. Eg I read same story twice and nothing! While I do not usually care about the games and points, it is disappointing that you miss out on rewards and acknowledgements for your efforts. CS are non-responsive. Please fix if you expect people to pay!

Duolingo desperately needs to sort out its word practice feature that only gives beginner words or just replace it with old-fashioned flash cards. The lessons are fine but I'm simply not remembering vocab in the long term. I know this because I do weekly lessons and meet native speakers to practice. I pay a family plan so I'd appreciate it to have all the features in working order. I use Anki too, which is fine, but I'd much rather a smart in-built vocab tool in Duolingo.

For regular users, Duolingo is a fun distraction that is also educational. It uses a hearts currency to incentivise less mistakes but comes at the cost of ads. If you become a super duolingo member, you no longer have to watch ads, you get infinite lives and unlimited legendary challenges. My only wish, is a word bank that you could look at without trying to match words, that allows you to browse and recall but otherwise Duolingo is top-notch.

I'm getting very frustrated after working for an hour. I see that NONE of my points earned have accumulated. In fact, they went down. It's happened many times in the past, making me drop down levels week to week. Looks like the double or tripple points calculation is not working - even though it is showing the amount before I watch all those ads. Got to fix this!

Decent app for learning basics of a language. The free version is unusable as they punish you for making mistakes so you can't continue as if the excessive ads weren't enough. And also it doesn't allow you to practice to earn hearts anymore. There also isn't much value in continuing the course after finishing as the daily refresh is always the same so you end up memorizing it all after a few tries. Old lessons only give 5 xp but at least it's a better veriety than the daily refresh.

I love this app, but there are a few key features that should be available in the free version that are essential to learning a language - specifically the option to explain incorrect answers. Fair enough to have the AI enabled functions and unlimited hearts locked behind the pay wall, but the premium subscription is way too expensive for me to afford.

DO NOT USE THE MUSIC LESSONS! Their timing and key detection is terrible!! Did anyone even test them?? App is buggy, with the talk exercises turning themselves off, keyboard automatically changing to another language and layout, and exercises submitting while you are still typing. It has so many ads, so what should be a 15mins experience turns into half an hour or more. Do not do any music lessons with a headset, it makes the tempo be half a beat off at random on top of everything else.

Literally useless once you've been through the exercises. Once you get to daily refresh it just repeats the identical lessons. After 1 year daily streak it's not really helping me speak Dutch or learn anything new. I'm pretty disappointed at how little it's developed as an application and tbh I'd be embarrassed to be a developer for this application, it'd be so easy to vary the content and when you're actually paying for it. Just rubbish!🤦🤷

duolingo is very easy to use and also very enjoyable with the games. the only downside would be the hearts, but that's why super duolingo is extra helpful. if you're trying to learn a language, I would recommend duolingo, or even if you want to do art, or even maths! it does take a long while, but that just makes your learning more thorough

The way that I love this app needs to be studied, I love how I can get to practice multiple things at once in a very short period of time, as I'm waiting in line for coffee or inside the bus. Thanks to Duolingo I am actually able to speak some Spanish so far and I am also very excited with the addition of music and math in the learning program, as I have always wanted to learn music and remember the mathematics that I have being taught in school. Great app overall!!

I love everything about this, even though sometimes it feels that in certain lessons and/or units there are only a handful of different phrases being used. I sometimes just memorize its structure, when translated (which isn't the goal of learning a language). also, i would love to start learning Thai, but haven't found a good enough site just yet. maybe it's possible to add it one day - i for one would love that a lot.

my German has gotten so much better since using this app. I love the way it helps you remember words. the little characters and their voices are memorable. I would love to see bigger interactive stories. I pay for super, which is good, I have German friends to speak to, so Max, for me, is not needed.

Good for basics but after that look elsewhere. App keeps getting worse. Audio quality diminishing, often initial consonant cut off. Used to have really thorough and clear notes for each lesson for you to keep, now really minimal ☹️ Doesn't Duo want to help me learn languages? Way more x2/3 XP potions than necessary, and not rewarded enough gems - trying to pressure you into paying for premium, and frequent video ads for premium between lessons is pretty annoying. Bring back old Duolingo!

Duolingo is a great app for learning Mandarin! It's user-friendly and easy to navigate, and the lessons are engaging and interactive. The app offers a variety of activities to help you practice your language skills, such as listening, speaking, reading, and writing. It also provides instant feedback on your progress and offers helpful tips and tricks to help you improve your language skills. With Duolingo, you can learn Mandarin quickly and effectively.

If you want to use this app for free you will be hit with ads after every lesson and if you make more than 5 mistakes you will not be able get full hearts to do more without spending in-app currency which costs real money. So this is more like a paid app that you can trial. For me in Canada their low Tier paid sub is almost $140/yr, or double that for the higher Tier. The price is almost double if you pay monthly. All to "keep education free" :eyeroll:

This app has gone downhill since I got it two years ago. They lock free features behind a paywall and push AI powered subscriptions onto you. And for an app that "uses ads to fund their teaching," they sure do love not teaching with how many times a right answer wasn't accepted and with giving users random words they haven't even been taught yet. It's good for starting a language and that's it. Don't even try the music lessons unless you want to get pissed.

Very frustrating price changes. Annual plan is advertised everywhere on the web at $59. Now it's called super (unlimited hearts) at almost $100. I spent way too much time trying to find the $59 annual plan to no avail. I much prefer transparency to vague marketing. Seems very sketchy.

I'm amazed it has such a good rating. The content is odd, some of the sentences are bizarre, points have gone missing on multiple occasions due to glitches, and the new advertising is annoying; particularly given I already pay for a premium version. I won't renew.

I paid for Duolingo super for one year and don't recommend it. There just isn't enough content to be worth it. I studied Hindi and it only does very beginner level. Once you complete their curriculum the practice sessions repeat the same questions endlessly it doesn't vary their previous content at all to keep you properly refreshed. When I've spotted errors there has been no follow up whatsoever and they may never correct their mistake. This was much better when they had Duolingo contributors.

Great experience and it's really helping me learn 2 languages Edit: the newer versions of duolingo aren't as good. Too much emphasis on match madness. Also the lightning rounds are endless due to a glitch. There is no way to beat the clock even if you answer or match quickly. Very sloppy programming or QC.

The amount of ads is genuinely ridiculous now. Often I have to click/wait through 3 or 4 ads. And now I've seen multiple ads for alcoholic beverages. As someone who chooses to be sober for very good reasons I find the ads extremely offensive. Booze ads are the last thing I want to see in the morning, and for an "educational" platform I was surprised to see that they don't care what the ad content is. God forbid there are underage people or folks who struggle with alcohol abuse using this app.

I really enjoy Duolingo, although some of the AI generated sentences are very strange. Human translators would be better. The app also does a pretty poor job of explaining grammar sometimes on the Spanish course. Used to be able to earn heart back by practicing, but that's been stopped now to make people pay for premium.

The language teaching is good. The whole experience is let down by an interface designed for 12 year olds. No i don't want to play games. No i don't want a free trial of Super. Why can you not publish a straight price for subscriptions? Instead you want people to take the "free trial" hoping they will forget to cancel and you can charge them. I would be happy to pay if you didn't hide behind this nonsense.

Amazing tool and good content for people interested in learning and exercising what they have learned. Now, regarding real feedback for developers: 1. Change the button "out of time, buy a time pack" out of the same place where the submit button is. Is ridiculous how many times I've tried to finish wrongly said purchase, without any intent. 2. Make the missions maybe swapable, 1 time each day, since sometimes I need to remember old stuff not learn new stuff. And lose the 3x

I loved it when I was paying for Super, but since having to cancel, the nonstop ads and activity limitations are absolutely *brutal*. It genuinely ruins the fun of learning through the app, at least for me. I almost want to give up on it entirely. Miss the way it used to be.

I use to love Duo but I now understand that for the conceptor IT'S A GAME NO A LEARNING TOOL I like the grammar rules but you can't learn be fluent unless you interact with somebody. With Duo it's repetition of where you are in the levels but they really should include word learned at earlier levels as Iforgot some of it. The streak and scores are annoying as they just want you to play as long possible because for them it's a GAME. I'm looking now for other app as I want to learn not play a game

After recent updates, I've noticed that certain xp boosts that I've been purposely saving have been automatically activated even though I specifically did not activate them. This is incredibly frustrating because the app shows that I have "triple xp" boost activated but when I fnish the lesson, im only getting regular xp and now my triple xp boost does not show in my boost inventory. FIX IT.

Spanish is not Castilian Spanish (despite the use of the Spanish national flag) but Spanish as spoken in Latin America! Given the many distinct differences that do apply both grammatically and in simple vocabulary, this should be made immediately obvious and it is not. I subscribed to learn how to communicate better with some extended family from Madrid, only to learn they were confused by my use of different words and structures. Great tool, but these differences matter and should be clear.

The one big mark against it is that they seem to want to get you to pay for the app. One way is you get 5 "hearts" a day, and if you lose one every time you get something wrong. If you run out, you either have to abandon the lesson or recharge, for a cost. But they steal hearts. They give you hints, that are wrong, then take a heart for putting what they suggested! I'm seriously considering deleting the app. it's not helpful.

A useful refresher on important vocabulary. Vocab is taught simply with visual and auditory supports so you learn in multiple ways. I like the short and simple lessons, and the assessments feel very comprehensive because of the variety of questions formats. On its own, duolingo can teach you a language, but I think you will learn MUCH faster if you also supplement with books and telenovelas. I often notice my duolingo focus skills pop up naturally while listening.

Be careful of which course you're taking, I'm doing Polish and tbh, it's incredibly boring and monotonous! The exercises are limited to typing and choice, there are no exercises to actually help speak, I'm on section 2, Unit 10 and there has been extremely little to do with introducing yourself, and general conversation! It's hardly interactive enough it's just all round tedious!

edit: Please bring back the "practice to earn hearts" option! I actually learn something in the process, it's a good review and allows you to not feel as forced to progress or redo lessons for practice. Plus you don't learn anything from ads. The only reason its not a 5 star is because there are missing features on android from iphone. The story formed option I find is so useful for comprehension and is not available on Android. If this changes, I'd be happy to give the app a 5 star.

I think Duolingo is overall a great app for people starting to learn a language and really any language it's just the fact that you remember literally everything that you learned I think it'll be helpful if you like go back to all your units that you've learned and try to relearn them if possible but I think it's a great app overall.

Seems to have intensified the gamification lately to the detriment of the learning. This is done with point multipliers making staying on the leader board all about knowing how to get the most points rather than about spending time learning. Despite this focus, the boosts often fail to apply which is intensely frustrating.

I'm really enjoying learning with Duolingo. I did French at school and really wanted to re-learn it as a family member lives in rural France and they don't speak much English in that region. Duolingo makes learning a new language (or brushing up on an old one) fun! I pay for the AI package and talk to Lily a fair amount. I'm already thinking about learning another language or doing math when I've finished French.

I like duolingo's learning method a lot. I feel it's more modern, natural & fluid those used by its competitors. That said, I've noticed a few errors with translations/sentences. A picture of a milk carton had the word for vegetable as translation and the picture of a tomato was supposedly a bar... Sometimes more than one option is correct, but doesn't count or when reviewing words, a recently learned word was ditched for a synonym that was never learned before. And the courses need to be longer

I like the app! I'm doing Chinese on duolingo. I do 1 writing lesson & 1 regular lesson in the app each day. Each lesson takes about 5min. I always make sure I have 1 in each day and am on a 400 day streak. It's not as good as a full class with a real teacher to correct your pronounciation or writing, but its better than writing books and flash cards & audio is comparable to other audio lessons. If there were some chinese/taiwanese tv drama/video shorts that use the same conversations it'd help

It's a good language learning app, but there are some bugs I would like them to fix. First, I got First in the diamond league, and most importantly, it said before I was about to get a first, top 10 qualify for the tournament, got First the next morning, and still in the diamond league. Another thing which, I really don't know if it's a bug or not, but when you go to the music course, it pulls up a woman with an electrical guitar, and just sits there forever...

I've been using this app since early last year and it's been really helpful in learning Spanish, practicing every day has really helped me learn. when i see people speaking it on TV or out in public, i understand some things they're saying which is a lot different than how it was before. i enjoy the new mini game feature they have in the store and such :)

It's a lot of fun and I actually do feel like I am learning a lot + have been able to hold conversations in Spanish much more than I used to after only reaching a Duolingo score of 30 out of 120 possible here right now. It is still buggy though and a lot of times I won't get the XP that I'm expecting or the conversation with Lily will freeze.

This app is amazing! If you want to learn a new language but don't know where to start? Duolingo is the best app in my opinion to learn new languages, I wanted to learn Portuguese to communicate with my friend that lives in Portugal and speaks Portuguese. This app has helped me so much!! It may be hard at first but once you get the hang of it it's really easy!

Overall, this is a great app - it makes learning new languages fun, and I'm definitely learning. However, it would be better if the app provided guidance when I get answers wrong. Often, I feel like I'm guessing the answers, rather than learning the rules behind verb conjugation, plural forms, etc.

I don't get excited about the points, and gems, because I have NO idea what they are used for. Even paying for a family membership they nag you to BUY more [something] whenever you run out of time on a quiz. I wish they EXPLAINED what new words are. I have to just guess randomly with my a, un, l', les, etc. I have written down a lot on a cheat sheet but it would be better if they just TOLD me what the word means and masculine, feminine, plural, tense, etc. The GOOD thing is that they use

I love it. It is so helpful. just one small thing: sometimes new words appear for the first time and it's asked to translate... A suggestion, too: please make a page with verbs and grammatical rules, which we can consult when we are in doubt. The stories are so nice and helpful. But they are always the same coming back at all the levels. And they are not related to the program of that moment. So, same stories, same speech speed, same grammar issues at level 5 or 35. That should be fixed. 🙏

I don't know if you guys use AI to practice the weak skills but if you do,its all wrong. I am currently doing Korean and Spanish and I already had a pretty good Spanish but I'm practicing it more now. I had one awful lesson with many mistakes but once I passed it,the next lesson gave me the exercises that I aced from that lesson and marked them as hard words which didn't make sense. Also sometimes I lose marks for spelling and sometimes I don't,it should be consistent throughout lessons.

Good for a quick dip, but not good for serious learning. I'm really surprised it doesn't offer a central place for all grammar learnt (you only get it at with each small section, and even then, one grammatical rule can be fragmented over several sections). Equally, there's no centralised list of words learnt anywhere.

The slider on the math is terrible. Just because I dont get close enough to their arbitrarily chosen point I fail. In German I can fail a whole speaking point just because I apparently cant say "Humbolt Universität" correct even if all other words are spoken perfectly. Also just noticed I was set halfway back a unit in my German? I had gotten to speaking about colleges. Now I have to redo half of the passport stuff!

I'm no longer able to practice for more than one heart. They also messed with multipliers running out and no more streak freezes from chests. The pop-up ads have become loud, long and intolerable. New exercises have you gambling on what the right answer is, even when there should be multiple possibilities. The free version is no longer what it once was. It's as if they're annoying you to upgrade to the paid version. I have a 3 year streak, yet with the new updates I am no longer a happy user.

With the new update they ruined the learning process completely. It is very unrewarding to complete lessons now. All units are in random order without a certain theme, so if you want to repeat vocabulary, you first have to search for that unit for 10 minutes. The path does nothing to improve our languages, instead it is a chaotic way to demotivate progress. Update 2024: They removed the possibility to practice lessons to earn hearts. It's not free anymore, but a paid app with a free trail.

I am deeply disappointed that Duolingo has removed the option to earn hearts by practicing. This change is unacceptable and counterproductive to the goal of language learning. The ability to practice older material not only helped reinforce my knowledge but also encouraged me to use the app more. With the added incentive of gaining hearts, I felt motivated to engage and learn consistently. Now, I find myself using the app far less because once the hearts are gone, so is my ability to continue

Over the last few months, the app has got worse. Considerably worse. Some of the issues that are making the user experience worse is the insistence of pushing American English, rather than actual English - so if you don't have to translate into American you get it wrong (this has got worse and worse. Constant reorganisation of the courses so you end up repeating lessons as they decide to move them around. Now more features added behind paywalls - including the ability to add more lives.

After using Duolingo for over a year to study German, the experience has been positive overall. The app is user-friendly, motivating with its streak system, and covers fundamental vocabulary and grammar well. Listening and speaking exercises help with pronunciation, and spaced repetition reinforces learning effectively. However, it lacks depth for advanced grammar and can feel repetitive over time. Speech recognition can be hit or miss.

Edit: Be aware that when using the free trial and it shows that the cost will then be £4.99/month £59.99 in total, they actually take £59.99 out of your account and not the £4.99 per month!!! So unhappy to have a huge chunk of money taken that i was not expecting. Only google play can refund, Duo dont deal with it! I am really enjoying it. Though I keep getting an error when I try the speaking exercises and despite trying every suggestion to fix it, I just can't fix it.

The latest update is full of bugs, makes the experience worse, and makes it harder to learn with the hearts system. It has bugged out multiple times, with the keyboard crashing so I had to exit out of a lesson, the app failing to track progress, not being able to start lessons, not being able to change settings etc. There are core lessons that are being blocked now and requiring the paid subscription to access. It's wild that they still advertise themselves as a 'free' language learning app.
