I loved using this app and half the fun was being able to practice multiple times. Yes I made mistakes but that's what happens when you're learning a new language. So I'd put in more practice to gain hearts to continue with my lessons. NOT anymore though, the Devs have become money grabbers and switched that facility off. Got to pay now to be able to keep going with lots of practice. I think that might be a bad move and turn more people away.

It used to be good and fun, but since the last update, the experience has changed. Unless you pay, or have experience of the language you're learning, you will be unlikely to learn much. You can't complete lessons to restore more than one life anymore. It's therefore very difficult to learn, as one mistake means that you can't learn. On the other side, I've just finished a trial of the paid version and it's so easy. You can make as many mistakes as you want and bluff your way into progress.

Amazing app. If you're not a beginner, you don't have to go through аll the lessons and just learn what you need to. You can practice talking, hearing and writing. It has a few ads with the free version but really they're not everywhere on your screen, neither are they unskippable. Just one very small thing : You can't learn several lenguages at a time. But I think that changes with Super Duolinguo. Overall, A. M. A. Z. I. N. G !

Edit: As time goes on this app is getting worse and worse. There's very little value in the paid plans with features being taken away regularly. No more forums was a massive blow for me as I used them to get insight into grammar rules etc. which Duolingo does a really poor job of teaching. [5* Hands down the best language learning tool out there. The hardest part about learning a new language is being intimidated too early on and Duolingo eases you into it and challenges you as you progress.]

Engaging and rewarding in a game-like way. The notifications can be a bit much - quantity and aggression. Some things are oversimplified imo - lack of grammatical explanation, translating tense incorrectly, constant multiple choice instead of typing - but it's still educational. (This is based on my experience in Spanish and Japanese.) I don't like how you automatically follow people when you're "invited" to someone's Premium. You had me following opps. Add Tagalog / Filipino please!!

I'm commited to a year of Spanish, but I'm getting annoyed with some of it - mainly its juvenile obsession with sport and schools/universities, which gets stale very quickly. It seems to be teaching Latin American Spanish. Sure, you'll probably be understood in Spain, but there are differences. And you need a brief course in American English to avoid confusion - Americans, most of the world doesn't call football 'soccer' or autumn 'fall'.

Generally a good app to learn languages with. Sadly the options for non-english speakers are very limited so if your english isn't good enough to learn a new language based on it, you might not be able to learn the language you want. The ads are okayish by now.

Decent language learning but intrusive monetisation. The lessons are good, but even with a monthly subscription (Plus) it won't give grammar explanations without an additional monthly cost (Max), and the module revision lessons are set up to encourage you to buy booster packs through microtransations. The main path of their course is decent, but a lot of the stuff around it is toxic and offputting.

They recently limited earning hearts by practicing to only one heart, only when you have 0 of them. It makes it extremely hard to use the app and actually learn the language if you don't use the premium. I have used the app for years, and have a 1780 days streak, but this might actually force me to stop using it, and move to another platform or a tutor, instead of paying for a premium. I really hope this is a big that will be fixed soon, and not a feature with a goal of forcing people to pay.

Scam! I used to love the app, Signed up for a auper duo lingo free trial, The app said I would be notified before the trial would end, I chose to only do the trial, because I simply can not afford to keep duolingo. I never received an email or in app notification my trial was about to end, and suddenly waa charged about €100 for a yearly subscription. I don not have the money to pay for this, whoch means I wont be able to pay groceries for the rest of the month now.

Really, Duolingo is that app for learning. I've been privileged to use a lot of learning apps, but Duolingo methods are really among the top tier, and they work. Simple things like streaks and the rewards really keep pushing. And the UI and total experience remains true to the app's policy of fun learning. Also really enjoying the new music and maths!

Duolingo used to be great. I'm on my second language right now, and I almost have a 1000 day streak. But whoever is running the business are a bunch of greedy scumbags. They keep changing the app, to try to force you to pay their ridiculous price for super Duolingo. For example you used to be able to do practice, that would regenerate hearts if you were low, but you can't do that anymore, and they took away my streak freezes fornko reason. I'm considering just stopping at this point... Very sad.

Reducing my score more due to recent changes to remove certain perks from free users. I was loving the app and am still trying to use it. But taking away the ability to practice without needing hearts means I'm unable to memorise things as well and it's moving too fast. Bring back the ability to practice to earn hearts and i'll give it 5 again as it was really helping with my vocab

Update2: Purposely making the UX for free users worse, Duolingo comes across as a cruddy company. Will probably give up soon. Update: worse still. Takes ages to load. Bugs. Lags when I'm fast on Match Madness so my taps don't register, making it impossible to complete unless I spend loads of gems. Sound on adverts is very loud and intrusive with dark patterns to disable it. Issues make the paid version not worth it. Original: The new interface/UX is unintuitive and time wasting in numerous ways.

I understand that this is a free service, and I am grateful for the opportunity to practice. However, I am disappointed that I have lost the chance to practice for free hearts, as I feel this would be beneficial to my learning. Please sort this out. May I add that this application appears to prioritize game-like elements, such as earning experience points and advancing through leagues, rather than genuinely facilitating language learning.

I don't see ads because I use ad blocker, but I could imagine the pain if experiencing it after every lesson. I hate that the radio lessons uses a synthetic (AI?) voice now. It no longer feels authentic. How are we going to learn authentic human-made languages if it is based on something artificial? Furthermore, I don't think this app helps a lot in certain languages for a good amount of people. Do search for alternatives just in case.

Had to delete some of my original review to fit this edit in; speech recognition is poor; "oh damn" does not sound like "Es ist ein Bär" and my German friend got the pronunciation wrong frequently when she tried it. I'm told by native speakers that there are errors. Also it's more useful for speakers of American English as other English speakers will loose lives for translating café as café and kino as cinema. English sentence construction is often odd too. No room to say more

1 star for stealing paid features and making a great application worse every day. It was an application that could teach you a language. I am on 998 day streak and learning Spanish for 60-80 minutes a day. And it works. But: 1. When I paid my annual subscription - I had a personalized practice. Now they moved it in new, more expensive plan, stealing it from me. Guys, I paid for it! 2. I also had a practice section. It is stolen too. Just deleted. 3. Nothing to do after finishing the course

Duolingo is going a bit downhill in my opinion. I pay for premium and now I'm constantly bombarded to get the "Max" for another ungodly money! Additionally after each lesson there are way too many click to actually complete it. It feels like it's turning into yet another "just keep your streak" app. Honestly after many years of using this app I'm not really sure it brings enough of value anymore to pay the premium.

Great app for learning new languages, but I have seen the difference in effort depending on the course. For example, I would like more types of exercises in my Polish course, like speaking, stories, etc. that others like Spanish get. I just finished my Polish course and I don't think I know enough Polish, PLEASE EXTEND THE COURSE. Besides, the reviews that I get daily are repetitive without touching nearly all the topics that I really need to reinforce.

I enjoyed Duolingo for a while but got tired of useful features being removed and changes to the gamification that meant rewards were withheld constantly. It's just not fun to play any more and the push to paying even more for the MAX (basically AI) version means that the language learning part is less useful now too, because functionality has been hidden or moved to the MAX tier. I'm going to find a more reliable app that actually teaches me how to use a language instead of this buggy one.

I'm editing my once positive review because the recent changes have made the app almost unrecognizable and the learning experience no longer satisfying or enjoyable. There used to be great grammar tips for each lesson, which are now almost completely gone. The grammar tips that are left are not helpful at all. Also, the rewarding system glitches too often and nobody ever responds to the complaints. I missed out on the daily chest rewards too many times and it's frustrating to say the least.

This is a very good app but I had to change my review because Duolingo has made learning with the app a bit more difficult for me and stressful also. I understand that you are trying to get your users to subscribe to your super plan but not everyone has that resource, is the best option for you to stress them about it?? why do I have to lose all my hearts before I can get from learning lessons. It's not cute, you're already doing so well but don't destroy your efforts with such a useless update.

Duolingo has become extremely hostile to free users; you can't even practice to earn hearts anymore. From Duolingo's website: "Learning a language is expensive and inaccessible to most. We created Duolingo so that everyone could have a chance. Free language education — no hidden fees, no premium content, just free." Duolingo has lost sight of their original mission statement. The app is very unfriendly unless you are paying for it. What a disappointment.

Sorry for last rude comment. I found the new update quite good, while the XP bonus potion won't get after each small topic finished, but I get the potion from daily task, and x3 XP Bonus, which makes me finally can play the main topic without worrying getting too much XP bonus, then felt wasted if I quit the game on halfway. Only things i don't like still the feedback page still loading when clicked from the setting menu. And I also lost some of my XP Bonus in the past weeks.

Generally a great free resource. Have been using Duolingo for 5+ years. Quality of the different language courses vary. Ads have become more intrusive recently, and are much louder than the lesson volume which is a particular problem. Often now multiple clicks are needed to dismiss the ads. Used to be tips on the android app but no longer available.

Free version is rubbish. No way to customise the learning experience. Not inclusive to different learning styles or goals. Notifications are very annoying. The sentences it uses to teach are totally irrelevant or nonsense and wouldn't actually help you start to communicate with anyone in that language about anything halfway normal. Halts your learning for hours if you make too many mistakes and the lives take hours and hours to refill. Will probably delete this app again.

Excellent app for learning languages and building confidence in speaking. I'm following the leaning path but love how I can jump backwards and forwards through levels to brush up and keep things challenging. It lost a star because when I'm traveling (without internet) I only have access to the next lesson or two before I need to go back online to download the next ones. Maybe they should add an option to download an entire unit before I board a plane?

This app could do better. If you market your map in the UK & Ireland use the correct spellings & words not American versions. The rewards you get for completing the daily quests are frequently not given. If I'm paying to use an app there is no excuse for it's inability to function probably. I'd give zero stars if a I could, I'm off to try a different app.

In my experience— Duolingo greatly helped me take the first step on learning the Japanese language. It made the lessons fun and immersive. The app also constantly reminded me to take my daily lessons in an interesting manner that really catches my attention. Overall, I could say that Duolingo is a great app to learn your desired language without being burnt out. Will continue on using it!

I've used duolingo for several years and have a 852 score streak. I enjoy using it, it's helped immerse me in my Greek heritage. But I don't think it's an effective teacher. The app emphasizes competition and simplicity over effective teaching. I didn't touch numbers in Greek until a year in, which is insane. As of 27/11/24 practice has been greyed out for free users (a free source of hearts) and xp reduced which is very greedy. I'll continue using, but there are legitimate issues.

I don't like being forced to pay so much money at once or watching this many ads otherwise. Finnish is ok, but that's probably because i don't speak it already. German is awful. Pronounciation is terrible, sounds like they wouldn't even be able to find germany on the map and voices are annoying and awful. Plus, its using a whole level to teach three different words and then ramps up to 30 new words the next lvl and then doesn't mention them again.

even with premium there is no way to practice hiragana at my own pace, it just keeps adding more before I've even learned the last lot which is overwhelming and I don't learn anything. Also, the maths is great BUT that one thing you have when you need to GUESS the answer with the sliding bar with no indication as to WHERE the correct answer might be, it shouldn't use a heart when you get it wrong. That blind answer bar is ridiculous and has nothing to do with actually getting the answer right.

Pros: - gamification: they nudge you to do your exercises daily. - baby steps: you can even learn a language with little daily investment. Helped me with Italian Cons: - German: strange word order in sentences, only one option is accepted. - small things, such as "auf Arbeit" sound like they want you to use eastern German slang, which sounds horrible for the rest of Germany - English they expect you to guess the gender of people or singular/ plural Annoying: end of week is time zone dependent

I love duolingo. This app is fun and engaging(even without pro). It doesn't beg you to buy it, and the app is perfectly usable without it! however, the only reason it is missing a star is because during the speaking lessons, it sometimes sounds really muffled and hard for me to understand. And I wish there were unlimited hearts in the free version as well. (For me, this would make it a lot better for everyone). Other than that, I definitely recommend this app!

Learning gamified, and we don't need to participate in the social aspects if we don't need to. Balanced use of ads, the free version works well enough. Learning a language with my 6 year old is fun! One complaint, and it's an edge case, but my daughter is terrified of the broken glass icon, so much so that she threw her phone away and broke it when she saw it. I've uninstalled the app from her phone now and she's practicing on my computer, but that's not ideal. I wish there was an option.

It's gotten worse. Two emails sent for support have gone unanswered. It's great if you're trying to learn a simple language like Spanish where they give you helpful tips to help you fix your mistakes. But try learning a harder language like German, no tips, you simply have to use rote memorization to master the language.

Duolingo is a really good language learning app BUT after every single lesson you are made to watch an ad on why you should get super and sometimes there's an extra ad. Duolingo's whole motto was supposed to be learning made free. also, they have taken away the option to practice to earn hearts. You can only practice when you have 0 hearts and you can only do 1 lesson then it asks you to watch an add to earn 1 extra heart. I don't love the app anymore and I'm sure you guys can find a better app

The app used to be fantastic, but is progressively becoming less usable (mostly after the AI integration). After the recent update, in the free version, I can no longer earn hearts until they become zero. Then I get just one heart for the next five hours. I get that it's a free version and can live with the ads but if you start making the free version unusable so folks shift to the paid version, most people will simply abandon it.

I've been a Duolingo member for over 11 years, and I've seen a lot of changes. I'm currently a paid member, but the changes to the app have me concerned. Earning gems is harder for all members, so I'm concerned that the app is getting to rely more on micro transactions, even for paid members. I'm also concerned that the loss of practice lessons will lead to fewer new players sticking around. Additionally, the difference in user experience between Android and iOS is really inexcusable.

Very thorough and gives you chance to speak and write as well as listen. Builds up slowly up is fun. The little owl is so cute!! The only criticism is that some of the story levels in the first few units use words you wont have learned yet eg the lady wanting a new jacket,yet they still count towards your average and passing the level Would recommend Duolingo to anyone wanting to learn a new language

This used to be a great app. Recent updates have made it nearly impossible to use with a free account. I have every badge back to January of 2023. So going on 2 years. I've gotten stuck. I dont know what I'm doing wrong. You only get an explanation with a paid account. You used to be able to practice to earn more hearts. That is severely limited. Many times it's not even available. I see more ads than ever before. I wouldn't have a problem with that if I was still able to learn effectively.

I'm not bothered by the leagues and double/triple xp, daily quests, and monthly badge - they do motivate me, but ultimately, I just want to learn. What really annoys me is when I am penalised for using English English. May not be the best standard, but better than nothing.

Removal of the practice feature is exceptionally frustrating. I don't care about the ads, whatever, but if you want me to spend more time in your app to see more ads, let me practice when I need to. Counter intuitive. Really? Now you've removed the night owl items??? C'mon.

我喜欢学习中文! (I like studying Chinese!) I never thought it was possible, as a lightly-educated rural American, and it's actually way easier than you'd think--Duolingo does a great job with that. The tough part is sticking with it--I'm only on a nine-day streak, but I've already learned a lot and I have no desire to give up or slow down. It's so much fun, and it's incredibly rewarding.

I really like the app and I'm using it almost daily, but it has some technical issues which are really annoying. Like it doesn't count double XPs sometimes even if I have active booster, or sometimes it doesn't count any XPs for the lesson made. It's frustrating. Also sometimes it takes a century to load the lesson or the results!

It's a good app, it's a little confusing and annoying at times. but overall it's pretty great. The ads aren't too much of an issue for me, even if there are after every lesson pretty much. It teaches basic things, and as for understanding little things, I have to research on my own. Doing it every day can we strange at times seeing as some days I don't have the time to do it, and there almost lose a large streak. Other than these issues, I think it's a great beginning to any language.

The experience with the app gets worse and worse over the time. Where should I start from? First you increase the "price" of league games. Over the course of 1 year, it went from 10 gems to 30, and then to 100. Now you are removing the option to gain heart with practice. You are basically forcing users to pay, even though they are paying with their time watching the ads. Pathetic!

Duolingo is a good app for learning languages and fun too, but the consistent ads is getting so annoying and unnecessary. I mean why do I need to be reminded after every other lesson about the premium version. I'm already aware of it. Now, after a lesson, you'll have to watch two ads, as if one wasn't enough. People will subscribe when they can, we don't have to be reminded every five minutes.

Dec 2024 update ruined the free version. And from what I'm reading in the reviews, it ruined the paid version as well as they added another Max pay level for features that were already paid the first time. Not being able to earn Hearts by practicing changed the dynamics completely in the wrong direction. After almost 2 years of daily use I am considering uninstalling.

Using this app to acquire some Portuguese vocabulary. Have been told, by native of Portugal, that I'm really learning Brazilian Portuguese, NOT Portuguese, as spoken in Portugal. Latest problem ... I appear to have lost the ability to earn hearts by practicing. These limitations explain the loss of two stars.

I love how I can learn every day and the motivation that duo gives me is awesome and I now know new people and I have improved in Italian non è fantastico? And u see people complaining about the ads and how its hard to keep up because of that and that the repeated lessons are too easy, yeah it is some how true, especially the repeated lessons, and to the person who said speaking is better than writing that's very true

When experiencing a particularly difficult section (irregular verbs, conjugating root words) more quidance and explanation to better understand would be nice. Since my weak spot is not being able to understand what others are saying, more storys, podcasts etc. would benefit me. Also more variation in voices.

Oct '24. It's taking away quests that I've completed; I come back in and I have 1 less than when I left. As for my wife's installation, she'll complete a lesson on the path and it won't enable the next lesson sometimes unless she stops the app and returns it, sometimes not until she tries to recover multiple times. Nov '24: happened again. The Sunday leaderboards were reset, and a quest I had done was replaced. I completed the new quest so I had 4 for sunday. Today the count was reduced by 1

Beginning levels are great for when you want to learn a little of the language of a country you are visiting, but after that I would not use this to learn a language from scratch. I use it everyday but only to supplement the formal classes I'm taking and to keep my vocab up now that I no longer live in the country I was learning the language for. You have to get Max in order to actually understand why you got a question wrong and there's no real explanation of grammar prior to doing a leason.

Duolingo is a really fun game style language learning app. But it's not perfect. I suggest getting a few other apps and juggling them I'm using it to improve my am Japanese. As I have a base, I don't know what the experience is for beginners. My biggest complaint about Duolingo is translating Japanese to English. I'll know exactly what it means, but there are too many words to arrange, it's worded funny,or it doesn't like the way I arranged words. There must be a better way to do this.

very easy to help bring back memory as well as to teach. only thing that would make it better is if it really was free access to the entire app. this would be a great app for teaching children at an earlier education in school as well as to teach people of all ages. it's fun and self rewarding!

Fun tool for building regular language learning habits, but definitely shouldn't be used as your ONLY language learning resource, since you are going to miss out on a lot of grammar side learning, at least from my experience with the Japanese course. But you do get a lot of repeated exposure to a lot of useful vocabulary so that makes Duolingo a beneficial routine-enhancing habit building tool, mainly for language studies, to be used ALONGSIDE other more in-depth tools and resources! :)

this app is a good way to learn a language it startsoff very easy and then helps you progress at your own pace. its very beginner friendly and if you already know a unit it allows you to skip. just saying I've learned more Spanish here than i have theough the public school system. Oneday i would like to see an option for sign language!

It's a good learning app, it helped me learn spanish and other languages a lot, but without super duolingo, it's VERY annoying, after every lesson you finish, there's an AD like they're forcing you to get super duolingo Overall I recommend downloading if you wanna learn a new language (you can also joke around with your friends about duo)

It used to be a good app for learning languages, but now it has gone down the toilet! I can't even earn prizes for completing my daily quests because intrusive ads run immediately after. I and you can't get rid of them, and they never end. I wouldn't mind them if they'd end at some point. You are forced to close the app and reopen so you can continue learning. But you lose out on the prize! Also, you can't practice anymore to get your hearts back up. It's made learning more frustrating.

The app focuses too much on spelling proper names. The list of all of the words you've learned so far doesn't work for many of the words, so you're out of luck if you struggle with specific words. Many languages have multiple words that mean the same thing. Duolingo teaches you the different words but fails to explain WHY you would use one over the other in any given situation. It's very confusing and frustrating.

I do recommend this app ヾ(･ω･`;)ﾉ Because it actually works unlike other apps and it really tries to teach you words you would use. It also makes everything as fun as possable! It really feels like a fun game! But some things I don't like. Like you streak. I just don't have enough time to do lessons. So I do kinda wish there was like a vacation mode or something I know it sounds stupid but just dont have enough time! Thank you for reading! :3 ♡＼(´▽ `)ノ♡

Not 5 stars because of the difference in the computer version and app version. Like I can't see my French score on the computer app but I can on my phone app? But so far it's been a pleasant experience. I'm impressed how polished it is, even on a Google phone it seems more polished than some Google apps. Keep it up.

Great app and it helps with expanding your vocabulary and helps you familiarize with basic phrases. It would be great if the explainations for the grammar rules were more detailed. I am currently studying japanese and my only problem is that words that I have not encountered before appears in the target practice in the practice hub. Idk if this is a bug but I finished studying hiragana and katakana (not including dakuon) before going to section 2 if that helps.

I've been in using the Duolingo app for two years now. Although I know I could be further than where I am if I spoke my chosen language more frequently, I enjoy learning at my own pace and seeing the milestones achieved. Duo cannot do this alone, so the other characters make it a more interesting (Lily is my favorite 🥰😂). I look forward to continuing to my learning.

It's a decent app but the timed tests are way too short. 1 minute and 30 seconds isn't enough time. I get down to the last 2 questions and run out of time no matter how fast I'm going. Edit to 1 star because even though I've paid for premium the star tests are timed with no where near enough time, I used to build up gems from doing the quests but they got rid of that, now they only give you a boost that I don't care about. I'm not spending any more money on this app

So far I love it, first time using it. I am trying to learn Japanese. My only gripe is the constant request to purchase Super Duolingo after every chapter and/or lesson. I understand the other ads on occasion but being bombarded with trying to buy the upgrade is super annoying.

Used to be a great app, but changes have made it WAY worse! Removing the help feature where other users could help explain why a solution is just bad! Now you can pay to get AI to do it (yuck). I understand pushing for more revenue, but do more ads! I don't want to pay for the super or max option because I want the heart system. I WANT to practice when I screw up. Give me a heart in exchange for an ad + practice lesson. Show ads to access help, I don't care, just be the app you used to be!

I was told about dualingo by a friend. So excited to see Hatian Creole! I went to Haiti on a Mission Trip when I was 9 and 11... and wanted to take it in high school... Needless to say, French was the closest. not quite close. I did also sign up for Spanish as I have forgotten it. I am struggling more with the grammar part of it. I felt the Spanish one is a little more organized to understand and implement that. I also got 2 days of the paid. As someone who is low income, that is incentive!

This app used to be really good but it's got progressively worse over time, first removing the discussion where there was valuable information about grammar and usage, then reorganising the lessons without acknowledging that you hadn't learnt some of the content that was moved to earlier in the tree, and finally giving far too many new words at once with not enough practice to learn them before the next set. Along with endless notifications, threats and gamification, its no longer worth my time.

If I had to rate the App, 10/10, but has a learning problem (don't know if it's me) it's feeling more like a game rather than a learning tool. I go into lessons and I prioritise getting it right rather learning about it, because the art style is good, but distracts me, also the answer section, I feel needs to be a little bigger upon completion of a challenge.

Explanations are back with Max, not as good as sentence discussions. You should put Max on correct answers as well, not just wrong ones. Sometimes you get lucky with a good answer but still don't understand. Loving the podcast style lessons, very challenging, would be nice to have translations on the transcript though. Overall an improvement of the disaster this app was for the past year. Still a long way to go to make it truly accessible and efficient.

Very frustrating. I've been doing the duolingo for several months. I have achieved many thousands of xp points. All of a sudden, some bug won't let me continue with my lessons. I can only recap, I can only practice the previous ones. I wrote several letters to support . No response. To my utmost pain I have to stop duoling. if you don't want to be disappointed you too should switch to another app. E.g. busuu. If they fix the bug I might come back.

There are a lot of bugs, duolingo support never gets back to you even if you're a Duolingo "super" member, and a lot of them can make using the app difficult or in some cases impossible. On multiple mobile devices and on desktop audio has trouble playing. Sometimes you can't select things so you have to get answers wrong. The button to disable certain types of practices (like listening or speaking) is right next to another one and if you press it disables that practice for 15 minutes.

Sure the app helps learn a new language, but if you're anything like me, it's not always great. Chasing Duo daily goals rather sets me back instead of helps. I get "Finish a unit" several days in a row and rush through units without learning, and get overwhelmed. With some "features" it seems they are only chasing user hours spent in the app, and not so much the value of learning. Room for improvement.

Used to be such a great app, but it's really gone downhill since I first started to use it back in 2016. There have been so many useless changes that only downgrade the user experience (getting rid of features like discussions, the ability to view how much XP you have in each course, removal of the tree, typing lessons, to name a few) and it becomes increasingly more obvious with each update that Duolingo doesn't actually care about the userbase, because they completely ignore the criticism.

Not terribly helpful. It used to be really good, but now its guidebooks are lacking and it no longer has the help button if you get something wrong. And now I've reduced it to one star, because we're paying £80 a year for no help at all, so much is guesswork, and they keep pushing to pay double that for their AI powered 'max'. I've had enough, time to part ways you greedy ********.

works, but could be better if the lessons contained grammatical rules (this might come later, I'm at lesson ~15). Currently, the lessons are vocabulary, with no explanation for conjugation, even though the different forms are part of the lesson. My second criticism is that all versions of a word count as a new word count, which quickly clutters the word list. It would be nice if the conjugations were listed under the basic form, which would make it easier to find the word you are looking for.

Overall, a very well-designed app that rarely crashes. The main issue is that English translations are in American English so phrases like trash can, vacation and store assistant are used instead of the English words. 8 Oct '24 - 2 ads now run after every lesson on free version, one advertises the Duo paid version and the volume of the ads is much higher than it should be. The free version is probably now not worth using, unfortunately due to the long ads, repeating the same message.

It's an overall good app if you really wanna LEARN a language. The courses are well designed and all that. I'll just suggest a feature that, in the section where we can see the words we've learnt until now, along with the meanings of the words, pls also add the pronunciation as they are in the lessons. Like along with the meanings of Japanese words, also add the romaji pronunciation of those words so we can revise anytime. Otherwise it's a fantastic app. Keep up the gud work guys 👍

Very useful, but when learning Russian, I've encountered a very annoying problem that has prevented me from continuing to learn the alphabet! The task asks me to translate Russian letters to English ones, and one of the letters is not on my mobile phone keyboard! The letter is an "i" with a breve. I've tried going to google just in case there was a keyboard I could just download that had the letter, but there are none. I've seen a few people talk about the same issue on social media. Plz fix it.

IT IS THE BEST APP FOR LEARNING LANGUAGES EVER, there cannot be any better app than this. And this is also attractive because it makes me wanna do more so I can beat my friend lol, but sometimes u are unable to hear, only hear 2 words in matching for some reason, you already thought that ahead and out an option, greattt, and I don't need to worry because I'm good at ready from sulolingo before this datretd happening. And the is no animation also for sometime but it's ok. I recommend to download.

Generally ok but weird American English and odd phrasing. The ads are too frequent and look really scammy. The voice volume is too low, the sound effect volume ear splittingly high. Some of the lessons are repetitive and unfairly count mistakes when you absolutely cannot tell whether they want you to use tu or vous (French) Edit. I just bothered to pay for premium to get top service and get rid of adverts... only for premium plus or whatever to be offered. And if I pay for that what next?

Ridden with AI, low quality voices with lots of mistakes, lazy sentences (also probably AI), and forgeting Android exists, just panders to its iOS users. Not to mention completely abandoning courses and not working on the new ones. It's just ok if you stick to popular languages, like Spanish or maybe French or German. Rating lowered from 5* to 2*, which is a bummer, since I used to like this app very much.

There is so many audio problems. Not only is the recording and sounds for the Korean lessons warped and crunched, but also there is just as much issue with the speaking lessons where you copy exactly what is said and it tell you it it isn't sure, so you end up losing all your hearts to an error on the apps end and you can't practice. Not to mention I want to know how I am ACTUALLY supposed to say these words without distortion.

Your Ads are crashing your App in between lessons. Kinda if annoying when you are on a roll and you have to watch Ads between lessons but your Ads don't play or freeze and you can't close them, so you have to exit and re-enter the App; losing your progress. Edit: Now when I select "Tap to Speak" for the speaking parts it tells me it will skip speaking for 15 minutes.

Learning spanish 3 years now. I have learned a lot but also seen many changes for the worse. I subscribe and so unlimited 'lives' which helps learning, and lots of little annoying things (like having to be a mind reader at times regarding masculine and feminine) however the latest new 'thing' is duolingo MAX. Double the subscription price to have the basic principles explained. The mistakes I'm making are because I'm having to GUESS the correct answer as I haven't learned it yet! Not ok!

I can't handle the Duolingo ads with the loud American voice. I have to shut the app. I wish they used the normal ads all the time, but 9/10 ads are the duolingo one and the abrupt loudness of the American voice stresses me out, so I just shut the app entirely. It disincentivises me from doing any extra lessons because that ad almosy always plays. That excessively noisy ad alone will be the reason I inevitably stop using this app. The app is good and ads fine, just not the Duolingo ad.

Profit-driven, lazy, barely useful anymore as a tool for learning languages, and pretty much everyone hates having terrible-looking avatars thrust upon them instead of custom profile pics (which let you express yourself as an individual) and we know the change is motivated purely by them wanting to have to do as little work in managing the app as possible (i.e. custom profile pictures mean an element of human moderation is required). Ruined

5 stars, it made me kinda half way to french 💝 But not my complaint !, I'd like this league/leaderboard board stuff out, not all users may agree to this but how about if so ..., then a feature of disagreeing to participating in leaderboards is set, so that some people like me can finally focus on learning and stop competing to be the best, it not about being the head it about learning, and if you're not seeing good progress like those achievement it's dissatisfying please help n put diz feature

Used to be a genuinely good app with tons of features and useful things. Now, half of those features have either been deleted or are behind a paywall. First forums, then profile pictures, and now practicing to earn hearts too??? This has gone too far. Duolingo has now just become a really inefficient way to learn any language, and is no longer any better than all the other big greedy corporations. I am seriously disappointed in you.

Duolingo has been thoroughly destroyed by greed, I'll happily watch the ads and even have downloaded apps and similar from them multiple times, though they have now removed the option to earn hearts by either watching ads or practice, which was one of the main ways to keep everyone not subscribed still learning,now it's nigh impossible to progress in a course. I'll be removing the app and find alternative avenues for my learning, don't support blind greed like this.

The ads have recently become so obtrusive that the app is unusable. After each completed lesson, I must close the app and then reopen in order to progress any further. After completing over a six year streak, it's hard to say goodbye. But, the app is no longer fulfilling what it had promised.

Okay. Despite all the negative comments let me keep it frank. It is true, I do not recommend using the free version, but the super version is very much worth it. And I am from a country where the currency exchange is bad so its relatively expensive for me. 60 dollars a year if youre serious about learning is really not that bad so I dont get the negative comments. I notice you also get more XP per lesson when you move up to B1 levels so it helps with leaderboard if you care about stuff like that

Pretty good for learning a language, breaks it down into manageable chunks. Sometimes it swamps you with new words when you could do with extra practice. The points system is broken. Sometimes says I'm earning triple, but only gives me 1.5x. Other times it says a lesson is worth 30 but only gives me 15. And the speaking element doesn't work properly. There's also an element of woke ideology, meaning it wants you to learn phrases that you won't need unless you're going on a woke holiday.

Duolingo’s simple design makes language learning fun and easy to follow. The gamified lessons are great for beginners, but the hearts mechanic is a major drawback. Running out of hearts after mistakes slows progress, and while Duolingo Plus removes this, it’s not worth the investment without grammar. The app focuses on trial and error without teaching grammar rules, making learning less effective. Duolingo would greatly improve by explaining grammar concepts instead of relying on repetition.

Update sept 2024. They added a a new nudge notification from friends with no option to disable. And if you turn off all notifications they keep pestering you to turn on.Review Sept 2022. Dislike the modified implementation of offline features, which feels more like removal. The main reason I paid for premium and this auto download feature almost never works. Selecting a course in poor coverage network area doesn't load. Also dislike them lockino and turning off the comments section for courses

The new version is just weak, I don't like it. And when I play match up, it's so slow that it actually messed up my results, because one word always shows up faster then the equivalent, and the words you already matched up correctly take too long to dissappear. I spend gemstones on that, it should be way better than this.

Have used Duolingo for a couple of years but the new hearts have spoilt it for me and mean that I am wasting a lot of time restoring hearts rather than making progress. Seems like just a ruse to force you to upgrade. Fair enough, but they are trying to sell it as a learning aid, which is crazy as the Pro upgade gives unlimited hearts. Now seeking alternatives.

Some languages don't have a good explanation. And when I answered with what I had wrote, it was wrong, even though the word or sentence is the same in English. Why don't Japanese lessons use kanji? Sometimes I get the wrong answer because of it. Kanji is one of the official Japanese writing system and is very important. Also, some questions are lack of context. E.g. ドリンクバー was translated as self-service drink bar. How can I know it was self-service? I just know it's a drink bar as it written.

Glad that they fixed the gross snot icon, but why did they randomly remove the dark mode feature? My app had been in dark mode for a long time, even though I didn't have dark mode enabled overall on my phone. I loved it. Now the app uses light mode even though I didn't change any of my phone settings. I can't enable dark mode overall because it messes up my phone's theme.

1 . Lessons loading too slowly to be useful. 2. Feedback option in application doesn't seem to work => Drop a star After a period of rapid changes and problems tracking learning progress the application seems to settled down. The problem with application memory usage rapidly increasing also seems to have been at least partially resolved. The application still seems to be relatively indeterminate in awarding double XP. Sometimes double XP is awarded but not applied.

Duolingo is the most ubiquitous language-learning software I know of, and for good reason. For beginners and intermediates, I can't think of a more accessible alternative, and its prominence means there are more than likely friends of mine I'm able to level up alongside. My only gripes are that I wish there were fewer ads for the premium version, more ways to interact with other users, and a greater variety amongst the kinds of challenges possible. Even without those changes made, it's amazing!!

The app was great at first when I was able to get the free trial subscription. But now that I have to pay or use the free version, I doubt I'll use it as much. If the app was worth paying nearly $100/year, I would but I don't think it's worth it. I get many says that make no sense "the bear didn't play the piano", well duh but who would say that in real life? There isn't enough real life scenarios so how can it be helpful? But hey, at least I can say the bear doesn't play piano in German 🤦‍♀️

The format of Duolingo really helps me learn languages quickly. I enjoy the ability to review any lesson anytime. My only wish is that there was some kind of anime or video that would play a short at the end of each unit using the language and words learned. This would greatly improve the understanding and learning of the spoken language. I would have given a 4.5 if that was an option because of this hole in the learning process. Gave a 5-star review because the app deserves more than a 4.

I've been using this app for a couple of years now, and I really enjoy using it. They've added MATH, which used to be my WORST subject as a child. I went back to college at age 28, and I suddenly understood it better. Math is LIFE. It makes you think and analyze. I LOVE that they added this because I'm 45 and need to sharpen my skills. I can also speak some Spanish. I never got past Spanish two in junior high. Now I can small talk. 😍 This app is relaxing, too. I look forward to it daily. ♥️

WORST thing about this app: the INSIPID 15 second ad for Super Duolingo after every. single. lesson. There is also an ad before it that you can skip after 5 seconds. I'd rather have 2 normal ads than the 15s Duolingo ad. I finish a lesson in ~100 seconds, so 20s of ad REALLY slow down my progress. Plus in the unpaid version, you get 5 hearts and if you make 5 mistakes, you're done for the day. Which seems self defeating cause then they can't feed you more ads. all that aside, it's effective

This is one of the best language apps I've ever seen! I'm usually bored after 5 minutes of learning, but this keeps me motivated to keep going. I know it will take a while for me to learn, but I'm committed to keep going. Duo keeps me motivated and I'm not frustrated for making a mistake, I can just try again. And the in app purchases a extremely fair, almost everything is free. And Super duolingo is completely optional. I'm able to get hearts super easy without it. Keep up the good work! 😄

The recent update is terrible. If it ain't broke don't fix it. This app was an effective resource for learning and now it has been turned into something of a trivialized game. 9/24 Edit: suddenly getting a lot of political messages inserted in my lessons. I just want to learn a language....

Absolutely fantastic app that helps you learn a language. It won't get you to fluency on its own, you have to actually practice and use what you've learned on it, but it's great. I will say I could do away with the creepy weird icons they sometimes change to... I guess they're trying to be quirky? Some of them are just disturbing.

It's not terrible, it's massively improved since the last time I tried it. I will say that I am a Brit and a lot of the English translations are in American English, it would be nice for British English to be an option because I get confused and answer wrong. Also I'd love to be able to move the 'selecting words' rather than having to get rid of them all and reselect to change the order, but overall, it's a fun way to learn vocab

Lately, when completing daily quests, it's not always rewarding you with appropriate credit toward the monthly quest... it's an insignificant bug but it's annoying. That said, forget some overpriced apps & other services... this is a great free/cheap way to learn languages.

Edit: TEAM DUO FIXED IT!!! :D I was really upset with the new Norwegian update because the exercises stopped working and the voices went weird and wrong sounding but they've fixed it and put the voices back to the proper sounding voices so now I'm really happy!!! ^.^ Thank you for fixing it Duo team!! I can understand what they are saying again now and the course sounds like my other Norwegian resources again instead of completely different/wrong pronunciation. 5* for fixing it! :)

I have been using Duolingo since the COVID shutdowns and have learned a lot. I really have my doubts about its effectiveness as a stand-alone method of learning language but it has been very useful for me. A quality of life improvement that would be easy for them to fix would be to PLEASE move the reward for finishing a friend quest beneath the night owl/early bird rewards since it has better longevity. I can't tell you how many times I accidentally wasted the friend quest reward.

Great app, has helped tremendously. Could use some work with accepting certain answers like "telephono" when its actually spelled "telefono". I would love to be able to give my 2x exp to people as i dont have the time to do 3p minutes of duolingo a day and hate to have them go to waste as you typically earn them after 10-20 minutes of duolingo.

duolingo's a good tool to be used in conjunction with in-person french learning :)) however, with regards to the interface itself, i hope duolingo can roll back its removal of profile pictures. i don't see why users should be forced to create avatars since anonymity is a priority for many. my suggestion is just that profile pictures should be brought back. thanks!

I've never been very good at learning languages but I seem to be getting on with this method used by this app so who knows, perhaps I'll be able to get by in Lisbon. EDIT IN 2024 I'm starting to wonder if Duolingo is really good...at getting you good at Duolingo. Let me put it another way. I don't think that, on its own, it will teach you true understanding of a language, nor how to SPEAK it. It's a good tool, but it can't be the only tool.

Been having a lot of issues lately with the exercises not being counted once I finish them. I've had to redo exercises 3 or 4 times over. Closing the app and coming back sometimes helps but it will usually just count one exercise if anything. So frustrating to keep doing the same exercises and not progress

I absolutely love it 🔥 I love how they fixed the leaderboard bug. I love the new features that have been added especially to the Japanese lessons content because it was only Kanji practice but now there is a mini podcast which makes learning Japanese even thrilling since we don't really have any stories to read like in all the other languages. I do wish the podcasts were a tad longer, but hey, it's better than just practising Kanji. Learning a language has never been this thrilling.

Currently on the free version and wow, my wife and I are learning Japanese. hands down best.. well... duolingo app ever ahaha :D had a demo of the paid version for 3 days, and it was also amazing 👏 really big round of applause to the team that made this and would also like to say thank you, you guys bring people together across languages. respected! :) :)

I've began using Duolingo since the last year to learn German and it's really effective and improving my learning. The daily tasks help to keep me determined. But, I've come across an error recently; there is another type of translation exercise and of course there are words to use. Although, when I try to do it, it doesn't work and I get stuck on that particular excercise. It frustrates me as I have to exit, and I lose my short progress. Please look into it. Overall, a fun learning app! :)

It's a great app overall, really fun. But, there's one thing really annoying. Two, actually. First of all, my animation settings are toggled on, and yet the motion is still stiff, no animations shown. I believe it's a bug. Secondly, the ads are SO annoying. It just keeps popping up almost every time after I finish a lesson. Please, Duo, I don't have the money to buy your trial yet, so stop giving me your ads. Still a great language learning app, though.

It's a fun easy way to learn a new language or brush up on your current one. Big complaint is the speech recognition in the app. It is TERRIBLE!!! I repeat a phrase and get it right, then I repeat the same phrase the same way and it marks all or half the words wrong. Then sometimes I say nothing correct and it marks it as perfect. This issue needs to be fixed ASAP, as it makes the speaking sections unplayable.

Amazing app! Really makes learning enjoyable. The paid version looks to have some really awesome benefits. If you're someone who is interested in languages or intends to travel, it would potentially be worth upgrading! But the free version is good too. It just limits your mistakes, you then have to wait to retry the quizes. The paid version gives you unlimited mistakes. Either way, super cool app!

I really love Duolingo! The app makes language learning fun and easy to understand. The lessons are engaging, and I’ve made a lot of progress with the language I’m learning. However, the advertisements are becoming too much. They pop up constantly, and I keep getting reminders to get Super Duolingo, even though I can't afford it. It’s starting to get frustrating. I understand the need for ads, but reducing their frequency would make the experience much better.

I've been using Duo for almost two years now, and on and off for four years before that. It's a great learning resource for those who want to learn the basics of another language. However, in a recent update, they removed the 'practice for hearts" option, which I have found to be incredibly discouraging. This wouldn't be that big of an issue if the hearts regenerated faster, but it takes a SINGLE heart 5 hours to regenerate. Extremely disappointing, this app used to be something great.

It's a good application to start learning multiple languages with different exercises such as writing, speaking and listening. However, it's getting increasingly difficult to use it as a non-super user (free). A few weeks ago it was still possible to replenish multiple hearts by doing special exercises. Then it got limited to one heart and now you have to either pay, get duolingo super or watch ads. It's quite unfortunate what the need to earn more and more money does to good things.

This program is incredibly engaging and makes learning really enjoyable . The way it teaches is fun and innovative, which keeps you motivated to learn more . There's just one minor thing, the ads in the program are a bit frequent, which can be a little distracting . But that won't stop us ! The solution is simple: Subscribing to Super Duolingo removes all the ads, and it makes the experience better. 😊😊

Subject: Issue with Listening Exercise in Unit 9 (Social Media) Dear Duolingo Support Team, I am experiencing an issue with the first listening exercise in Unit 9 (Social Media). The audio clips do not match the displayed words, making it impossible for me to complete the exercise and progress to the next stage. I have tried refreshing the page and restarting the app, but the problem persists. Could you please look into this issue and provide a solution? Thank you for your support.

The app has a lot of ads, which is very frustrating when trying to learn. However, if you think, "Okay, I'll spend some money for education," you quickly realize that it becomes outrageously expensive. Because even though you pay 15€ for Premium and no ads, you still can't access everything. For that, you need the affordable Duolingo Max for 30€. It leaves you speechless. However, the design and learning content are well done, and you can learn quickly if you want to.

Agree with a lot of the other reviews. As of spring 2025, functionality with a free account has become very limited. Repeating already learned content has become quite inconvenient, making it harder to do two languages at once, but also more difficult for me to remember everything from only one language course. I loved the option to be able to earn hearts by reviewing older lessons, but that seems to be completely gone. I learn less, have less fun with the app, and spend less time on it.

I've been using Duolingo for over two years to learn Japanese. I used to really enjoy it, but recently, I've been having an incredibly irritating ad appear, where one of my most used features used to be. I'm paying for Super, which is supposedly "ad-free". I wouldn't mind if I could still use the features, but having them replaced with an ad, when I'm promised an ad-free experience, it's not on, and if it continues, I shan't renew my subscription.

There's a lot I like about Duolingo and also a lot I don't. It's a great simple tool to help learning languages, but at times it's too simple - it doesn't explain the 'why'. I get that they need ads on the free version, but after every single lesson? It's infuriating. I don't like that you can't disable the streak feature - it actually makes me avoid the app so it doesn't make me feel bad for losing a streak. Nor do I want people congratulating me on 'coming back' every time I just open the app!

I have been using Duolingo for over a year to learn French. In the past, when I encountered a difficult level, I often lost all 5 hearts. But then I kept the streak by practicing. The new update requires watching ads to get 1 heart. This makes me stuck on a difficult level and spend all my diamonds to buy frozen items. I can't go to the next level, I can't keep my rank, I can't get experience if I don't complete any level.

It's a great app, and since im using it for free, i have no problem with the random ads that pop up after the lesson, but those super duolingo ads are just annoying. on average i finish a lesson every 2 minutes, and i don't need to be reminded of the free trial 30 times an hour it's distracting, and it just makes me want to quit studying and sometimes i don't get the reward from the quests due to some kind of glitch or something . over all it's a decent app to study new languages.

Super fun way to learn a new language but the amount of adds is kind of annoying especially for people with time limits on their devices. It also gets frustrating when you lose all your hearts and aren't able to continue on. I would definitely recommend this as a tool for learning because it helps you enjoy education without simple and pointless lessons! Unlike many games, I have found that doulingo is almost exactly as engaging as many adds and people say.

Duolingo is a great app for learning new languages in a fun and interactive way! The bite-sized lessons make it easy to stay consistent, and the gamified approach keeps learning engaging. I love the streak system and challenges, which motivate me to keep going. However, some advanced grammar explanations could be clearer, and the ads can be a bit much without premium. Overall, it's a fantastic tool for language learners!

whatever people say, it's still a perfect tool for long-term learning, when you better take it slow and easy to get used to a new language. I use it for learning Arabic and it's one of my main tools for the language right now. after all, when it comes to languages, you can't use only one tool and expect any kind of unbelievable results. duolingo works perfectly in the purposes it's made for: steady and easy learning of basic structures and words from zero, with listening and speaking exercises

It's a good app for learning vocabulary, but I think it needs more practice opportunities. I’m currently in Section 1, Unit 5 of Chinese, and many vocabulary words/characters from earlier units no longer appear in exercises. Without consistent review, it’s hard to retain them long-term, especially as new units introduce more words. It would also be great to add mini-stories for listening practice.

A great app that I've been using for almost 9 years and am currently on a 375 day streak. However, they have introduced a bug that does not let the app to be used in dark mode. It used to be synced with the phone options, but suddenly stopped working. And the app settings have no option to change it. I will be using another app until they manage to fix the issue.

I haven't been on Duolingo for so long but it's really good to learn a new language. I like that there's a language score that tells you how you would perform in the real world and it's pretty accurate for the most part. However, the app itself sometimes has performance issues that causes learners to lose progress in that lesson. Aside from the issues with the app hanging sometimes causing loss in progress I would generally recommend the app for learning a new language.

I love the app and it's a fun way to learn and there are also a lot of languages to choose from which is good. the hearts however are really frustrating especially for someone who is learning a harder language, To me the hearts are a hindrance to learning. But I am very glad that the standard version is fully usable without a premium subscription unlike other apps.

Recently, there's been a few bugs. Choosing your quest partner glitches out (it pops up and then almost immediately just disappears) which is insanely infuriating. Also, I wish we could turn off the voice that repeats every word you click. It's extremely frustrating and distracting. The challenges where you pick the words to copy a sentence, you can't go back to change 1 word without having to undo everything up until that word. All quite annoying. But fix the friend quest thing.

I ABSOLUTELY LOVE THE APP!:D I love Duolingo because it helps me learn and talk with my Japanese friend! It's really fun and engaging. But there's an issue… For the third time now, my streak froze even though I completed my lesson on time. I did my lesson at 9 PM, but when I checked my widget at midnight, it showed my streak as frozen, like I hadn’t done it. This is really frustrating because I’ve been consistent, yet the app doesn’t register my progress correctly. Please fix this bug!☹️

it seems from my first time using the app for a different language a few years ago that there is more focus on actual things not weird phrases you would never use (may come later). I am finding way too many ads now, I don't even remember seeing any or if there was, it was much less. Makes learning, not fun to see ads that you can't skip, and it takes you out of the learning part of it, to then becoming just keeping your streak and not actually learning.

There is an increasingly frustrating bug in the app that prevents choosing words from the word bank, with no way to progress beyond, resulting in a wrong answer each time, which then costs hearts. The bug persists now for months. As for the content of the app, it's very good for starting out, but once you get to late beginner or intermediate level on some languages, there is not enough explanation or demonstration of why certain grammar rules exist. That lessens the value of the app.

disappointing - at first this app is really quite good, particularly for introducing different verbs, tenses and vocabulary. Very quickly, however, the programme is repetitive to the point where you get something right because you had the same phrase or question only seconds ago. It becomes too boring to use. On the upside it's OK for the very basic foundations of a language and you can learn several languages at the same time. also watch out as payment is not monthly but one lump sum.

The last couple of updates are full of bugs. When I try to select a lesson, it shows 60 points, but after completing it, I only get 45. I activate boosters, but they don’t apply. In Math, when I try to match options, even if I pair them correctly, some still remain unselected. I complete a lesson, but it still warns me that I might lose my streak because Duolingo didn’t count it. The app is really buggy right now—what’s going on?

Psychologically, duolingo has absolutely the best approach in learning foreign languages, but it has a huge flaw which are the ads. If you don't pay for the "premium" version of the app, you end up with insufferable ads after every single lesson. Not only do you have to watch random ads, but after the random ad, you have to watch duolingo's as well. I get that they have to promote themselves, but does it have to be that extensive? It just makes me want to delete the app and give up.

Too repative and easy. You can guess most of the answers by process of elimination and it reinforces bad habits. The radio sections are very boring and you are unable to skip the intros and outros. Not good if you are in a rush. mine seems to be stuck in a loop and not teaching me anything new and repeating the same stuff again and again. Im paying and still getting adds for Duo max. Very annoying! every other level is a duo max level. The video game sections could be more exciting.

Absolutely zero customer service for problems, even if you're a long-term paying user. It's not good for trying to learn, ok for refreshing. They used to provide lessons, but you have to pay extra for that now on top of the pro membership. it's not worth it. I had an almost 3 year streak as well in the top league... it dropped off incorrectly, and I lost the whole streak. there was nobody I could talk to about it. that upset me, so now I'm cancelling my subscription and going elsewhere.

It's just not what it used to be. They're removing more and more free features to force you to pay for their premium subscription. Hearts take too long to recharge, there's no explanations of sentence structure or grammar, and some courses don't have much content (Spanish has a whopping 8 sections, while Finnish has only 2). The news about staff being let go to be replaced with AI is disappointing as well, and is not a good look for the company as a whole. I'll be looking for another app.

This app is truly outstanding in both design and functionality. It runs smoothly without any glitches, offering a seamless user experience. The features are practical, well thought out, and genuinely helpful in learning new languages. I appreciate how intuitive the interface is—it makes navigation effortless. Overall, it's one of the best apps I’ve ever used and I highly recommend it.

they're pushing ads out that you can't close. horrible UX. I have to close the app and reopen it to get out of the ad. I have more frustration with the UX than I I have joy of language learning. I'm considering other options, some premium and free with my Library (I suggest others do the same). it seems the focus has shifted completely to making more money for the app, at the expense of user experience.

It is so user friendly and it makes learning entertaining, with its bright colours and animations I think even kids couldn't stop paying attention! 💯. Edit: Lately it's been having the issue that it doesn't recognize when I finish a lesson and it sets me back to do the same thing several times, I would really appreciate if that was fixed...

I always wanted to learn other languages, and this app makes it fun and easy to do. The only improvement I would like would be more stats available in my profile. like time spent on a subject. I dislike the extra cash grab for time increase on challenges, the cost is high enough for the app already. You don't have to buy the extras if you don't want to, but if you are a slow reader or have dyslexia it's unlikely you will get them completed without buying more time.

As a former paid member, I love this app for the variety of languages available, but the way it teaches doesn't work for me at all, so I've stopped payment. For example, when I return to the first lesson (after doing many lessons), the lesson has completely changed and seems to expect much more advanced knowledge. I don't return to the first lesson to review more advanced stuff. I feel like I don't have any control over how I learn, as it seems to change frequently.

There are way too many ads. Some of the so-called challenges are impossible to beat unless you pay, and it's obvious they're made intentionally so. One example is the word matching game, where sometimes, I'd have 3 empty spaces on one side of the screen and 5 words on the other, on top of that at times I have to wait for the word to appear, it doesn't happen immediately, so my time runs out. You can't skip anything, so I have to watch the same short clips of the characters every time.

Duolingo is one of the most fun language learning apps I have ever used. Unfortunately, the app these days has been crawling with ads. Unlike the older version where people had an option to watch ads rather than now forcing them to watch an ad about something else and after that ad, it will give you another ad about their "super duolingo." It was much more beneficial that people had to work for learning the language by earning hearts through more practice but it seems now it caters monetization.

I think it is a great way to learn a language. my child who is 8 is even learning through it. however, there are things which are not appropriate in our culture. I subscribed in Duolingo max, but I think I will cancel it. explain my mistake feature is good, but the AI is not good at all. conversations are too short and are irrelevant to the topic of the unit.

Great free app but paying isn’t worth it. Android always lags behind iOS in features, and browser version even more so. Support is unresponsive to bugs and other issues. Negative feedback, during learning, feels overwhelming, and time pressure from XP boosters add unnecessary stress. I find myself avoiding consistent learning due to this. I would like to customize the learning experience to my liking.

The app really sucks nowadays. The XP of lessons shown before starting is 120 and once I complete the lesson, I only get 45 XP. This issue has been there since a long time. I am losing on XP without any reason. Also the audio lessons aren't upto the mark, the audio words and the words written in text in the English language are not at all matching, I have to skip them the whole time, as it makes no sense to complete it. Also manier times my 3X boost is lost without any reason, the next day.

I've been using this app for ages and I hope I will continue doing that. Though I am not happy about the recent changes in the app, for example, now you must pay 100 gems to play timed challenges, and that is frustrating coz gems are hard to earn and very often I can't do them (challenges) from first attempt... Besides, ads became a bit more annoying... Still, I really like the app, and I hope it will be changing for better. New languages are appreciated!

This is the best language learning app ever. They simplify the class in a way that even a language novice must understand it. I never had a background in Spanish, but now I see my self speaking some of the basic words. If you are seeking an app to help with ur language learning, I recommend Duolingo, it has been tested and trusted. The only problem with it is that very early in the morning it's like the system crashed, but after some hrs like around 10/11 am (WAT) everything is back to normal.

I genuinely don't understand how this game is supposed to help you learn a language. I've been taking many languages under my wing during my almost two years of 'learning' in total. here's my take on things. 1. Quite annoying. lessons are boring, and don't teach you a lot the communication in language itself. LARGELY missing the point 2. Skips many steps. while I was learning korean, I was having a hard time because they kept going over easy concepts intensively and skipping the harder ones.

This app is becoming more about profits and less about actual learning. They keep removing the option to practice - a huge component of strengthening launguage learning progress - for "hearts," effectively stopping free users in their tracks. What once was a tolerable amount of advertising is becoming overwhelmingly unpleasant. I was in the top 1% last year and was recommending it to my friends but I can't recommend it anymore. I'm now considering other resources instead. Sad to see it, duo.

this app is awesome, I'm learning German with it and it has helped me a lot and I have improved. but the last update they gave us made everything ruined. the exercises are now easier, the speaking part doesn't work right and it has some other bugs too which it didn't have before. It was very good for about 4 days ago, but since the update, it has really started to be annoying. But this app is a real masterpiece for learning a language.

For some reason I keep coming back to this app and this time I love it more than ever. I really like the silly little skits and characters, I might cringe once in a while but it makes learning fun and light-hearted, which is weirdly soothing in a way? I don't know how others feel but the French course has been fantastic so far. I'm saving some money to get Super now since I enjoyed the free trial. Only complaint: maybe calm down with the ads a little bit. Keep up the good work!

Loved this app when I first started using it, but, changes have significantly reduced my enjoyment. I would still prefer not to be in leagues. Not having the option to practice for hearts anymore is very 'money grabby' in my opinion. Moving features that were part of the free element away because they have added another level of subscription. Often not getting hearts back within the prescribed time. Rewards not being applied as expected. Don't think I will using it much longer.

when you click the words in the example, they don't consistently show you the translation in every exercise. Also when you successfully complete a translation, it sometimes just says congratulations, it doesn't confirm what the translation actually was. Plus if I pay an annual subscription, 'Explain my mistake' button should be included. Asking for MORE MONEY AGAIN for core learning functionality is not right. that's the whole purpose of the app!!

I really enjoy learning on Duolingo, but ever since signing up for the Super Duolingo version of the app, I'm not getting the XP boosts like before. Even though I'm completing the missions and it says I have an XP boost for so many minutes, it just doesn't happen. It is incredibly frustrating every time I have a session at the moment, which is why I am only giving 2 stars. Very disappointed!!

I've found the app highly effective at training me in the basics of several languages and building vocabulary in another I studied in school. The daily review seems to focus disproportionately on a few areas that were weakest when I completed the lessons. The ads for horrible games seem to be gone now, thankfully. But I'm uninstalling the app because I personally can't manage the "side effects." For me the gamified interface is too habit-forming. YMMV.

This was a great app. Now this update changes entirely the way you learn. You are forced down a path which i dont want to take. I was was learning so much more with the tree view. My progress has slowed down significantly with No improvement. Please bring back the old tree learning view. You can jump around and choose what you need. Update 2025. NOW the ads are relentless. 30 second video adsa after every lesson make this app totally unenjoyable. Duolingo has hit rock bottom. Don't waste ya time

Once upon a time I rated this four stars. The teaching is still passable, but all elements that used to teach theory (the community forums) were removed a year or two ago. Most frustrating, though, is Duolingo's approach to communications and notifications. At least the obnoxious characters can be switched off, but the notifications are still annoying and passive-aggressive. "You're scaring me, don't skip a day!" is tremendously snotty... particularly at 0620 in the morning.

It is a really great app. I am currently learning Arabic. The problem is that I already know how to read it so I think there should be an option if we only want to learn how to speak a language and not read it. I also think that there are too many unnecessary ads between every lesson about joining super duolingo.I think for the ones who are using it for free there should not be so much forcing.I get it if you put ads once in a while but every now and then is irritating. Everything else i amazing

Review is for the Irish language.. the audio is often clipped, meaning the start or end of a word is inaudible. At times the pronunciation (or recording) is very poor. I'm using this as a supplement to my live Irish classes with native speakers, so I do know what the words should sound like. This could be a lot better and if it was I would be willing to pay for it. However, the subscription model - pay for a year up front - is too expensive. I'd prefer to pay on a monthly basis

Has sadly become worse over time. Useful community features removed. Too much focus on AI degenerated content. Quality control is almost nonexistent for newer courses. I even noticed it in the Spanish course, it just gets worse the further I progress. And seriously, the Chinese course is terrible. Who came up with the idea to force new learners to memorise hanzi? Just leave it in the separate section and let users choose when they want to go there. No more super for me until I see improvement.

Not much has changed in the past two years. Lessons in Asian languages still a total disaster (sentences like "the wand chooses the wizard" instead of something useful). Ancient and ineffective learning methods - associative learning is only for beginner parts, later, you will waste your time endlessly translating absurd sentences, like above. Super is a waste of money. You are better off spending it on actual IRL lectures.

It seems the AI video call issues have been improved. This app is fantastic for Japanese! The lessons progress slowly to cement words and phrases. This app supports reading, listening and speaking skills. I paid for the top level, because that is still only a fraction of what I would pay for traditional textbooks/tutor. For those expecting miracles, this app alone cannot be your only source of learning. I use Google translate, free audio books and even movies I otherwise wouldn't watch.
