The App has been crashing a lot recently eg I complete a lesson and it won't record it which is incredibly annoying for a paid app. The App is basically a glorified phrase book. It doesn't give you any information about grammar so you do need to use alternative sources to help with grammar. The voice recognition part seems quite good without too many glitches as opposed to some Language Apps.

There's been a change to the free to use settings, where it's impossible to keep your streak if you fail- unless you want as many adverts as it takes for you to complete your session without mistakes or you pay for the unlimited upgrade. It no longer feels like you're learning, you're just punished for not remembering the correct sequence to their answers

Don't get me wrong, Duolingo is a good app. I've tried to learn numerous languages off Duolingo (Turkish & French) and for the most part, it's decent. However, I don't like how there are many advertisements and the hearts that get lost off of simple mistakes. whenever you lose a heart and have to watch many advertisements it makes me feel discouraged and not excited. i was doing my Turkish lesson and it told me to type in "Man" in Turkish which means "Adam", I typed in the correct answer but "❌"

After two years now: Gaming aspect motivates very well. Lessons are not there as it it is a hard-knocks learning system..but it works well. However, the gaming system is frustrating for androidcusers as iPad app users can score 20x the points without hardly looking at their screens...actual experience. Recommend this app for language learning! Careful...Spanish is easy at first but gets to be the most complicated language later in future and affirmative tenses. I just guess most of time.

TL;DR Was good; won't renew! No longer seems to be a way to turn off lessons such as Music or Math. Song progress is gone as is pretty much all previous music, even Hotcross Buns has a slower tempo along with new accompaniment. Wouldn't mind the new music if I could have kept the old. Math matching is broken: have to go super slowly to avoid false matches. Stories (German) keep being moved; can't tell which have been read. Finally, the constant pressure to buy the AI driven Max!

I really like this app as a whole, and I was going to leave 5 stars, but the speaking lessons are why im giving 4 stars. It doesn't give you a correct answer if even one word isn't recorded correctly by the microphone. Another reason is when I deleted the app, all my progress on my maths course got removed, and my french and music courses are fine.

I love this app. Unfortunately, it has not been working for me as of yesterday. I started to notice that the app will say you have double or triple xp for 10 or wtv min but then when I do the lessons it did not work. I also have a 463 day streak and I can't get access to the streak society app icon. I tried updating the app it did not work I installed and uninstalled it and now it won't even let me log back in. It's saying error please try again later. I'll be so upset if I lose my streak HELP!

I wish there was more context. they started off with how to order something in a coffee shop instead of explaining masculine and feminine concepts or starting with numbers and date and time, you know, the basics. The UI is very childish and it's annoying to constantly have to click on continue instead of the lesson advancing on its own once you provide an answer. I may stick with the free version until I find something better but I will never pay for this app.

Almost useless. It doesn't actually teach you how, as there are no actual lessons. Also, there is no button to turn off American words. Wastes so much time learning the wrong words, or making two translations per word in your head. Surely simple issues to address, ridiculously. The exercises are decent to keep things ticking over if you are already learning it properly but don't have time to study properly.

This app is great and has allowed me to learn a language fairly well after 1 year of use. However, after one year of being a super (paid) user, there is an advert after every exercise. I feel like if you've paid for that amount of time already, the app should remember you. Another area of improvement would be where the chosen word to translate (in my case for my selected language) is the same translated as if it were in English. Makes the exercise pointless and this happens often.

Doulingo is a fun way to learn a language and it's alphabets. It even has math and music, meaning you'll learn to read notes and play along with a "piano" which is just a few keys.. and math.. is math, but think of it like reviewing math in a fun way. Although it's far from learning a language through engaging in real life, Duolingo's still improving ... i think. Either way it's still a really fun app whether you take it seriously or play it as a game. Me? .. a little bit of both.

Edit: New update has somehow disabled dark mode, a very frustrating experience. They've also have launched a new video max purchase belittling the already perfect super. Super members have infinite lives so makes no sense in tournaments with unfair advantage. This is the most innovative approach to learn a new language. The best so far. Great ui, simple lessons, great challenges n many more. I hope to see more languages coming to be able to learn. Spoken and written modes would be more easy.

I like working with duolingo..it keeps you moving...but it makes it too competitive. I always feel rushed...some of us aren't so quick and just want to enjoy the learning experience. Let the competing be a choice. I personally am not bothered about coming first..I just want to learn. The good thing is by joining a subscription I can get plenty of practise....

I pay for this, stop constantly advertising the new more expensive plan to me. It's a good app to learn the base of a language before consuming it elsewhere. There's a large imbalance across the different languages though. Japanese is great with all the features and voices, but Swedish is not. Mandarin seems good, unless you learn it from a starting language other than English, then it's half the course content. Why?

Overall it's good but the way it teaches written language is frustrating and possibly flawed, it moves too fast. you'll get a guide for how to write a character once or twice and then it will give weak directional queues without any reference to the letter on the screen do you're forced to strain yourself trying to remember something you saw for. 3 seconds, two weeks ago. They also take way too long to integrate object associations so you can translate in your head faster

My streak on it is 1750 days, because it used to be a great platform! idk if they have new management or what, but the user experience has become absolutely horrible. it's so clear they're intentionally making the free experience more gruesome, so as to bug people into buying a membership. instead, they're likely going to lose users all-around. It used to be clear that the main objective was language learning, and that showed up in everything, but now it's clear it's just about making money. 🤮

2022: 5* App. Lessons clear, fun & effective. Been using it for years now and it's improved on earlier versions since using in 2015. 2025: 3* Sandards have dropped dramatically. Ads really aggressive, too often & too long. No 'quick lesson' anymore, as you spend longer waiting for ads than you do learning. Only one error and you're 'out of hearts' then have to wait hours or watch another ad to continue. Can't even upgrade as app won't connect to play store. Not what it used to be.

it is a very good app that is definitely helping with my GCSE and it is helping me make great progress. the design of the app is very good and it is usually very speedy when loading. it does have a few ads but it is only after you complete a lesson. it can be hard to remember to keep your streak up but you do get reminded by the app if you are about to lose it.

The start of my duolingo journey was fine. It descended once the amount of ad walls for each thing got added on or changed. They used to have "Practice to refill hearts". Now it's watch 30 seconds to get 1 single lousy heart. You fail the learning portion and forced to watch ads to refill for another attempt, from the beginning. It does not even account for the fact that you'e done attempts, just another money-focused function of the app. I recommend you uninstall the app.

improved graphics. love the animations. language lessons are still too repetitive but the addition of new game modes spiced things up a bit. the music lessons need to improve. it's very hard to be on beat because there's a slight delay between the actual beat and the note. also needs to consider reaction time. notes are very easy to miss if you're the tiniest bit late.

Gamified language learning. Lots of ways to motivate you & kookiness. Layout isn't as intuitive as it could be- information to help understand grammar rules isn't easy to find. Also there are sometimes glitches/bugs - my Word Collector achievement got stuck over a month ago, and I get regularly get announcements I have achieved a new level but it never actually updates and just reverts to my old word count & doesn't give gems. The free version can be overwhelming with the all the advertisements.

The app itself is great, I love using it and learning French is made massively easier. Having just moved from an iPhone, the quality level between the Android and iOS App versions microphone understanding is huge though, my S25 Ultra struggles to understand my speaking when I'm getting the simplest expression exact. Very frustrating not being able to progress because the phone thinks I'm getting it wrong. I'm blaming the Android software and not the phone though, maybe I'm wrong?

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Amazing Language Learning App! Duolingo is an incredible tool for learning a new language! The lessons are fun, engaging, and easy to follow, making it perfect for beginners and advanced learners alike. The gamified approach keeps motivation high, with streaks, rewards, and interactive exercises that make learning feel like a game rather than a chore. I love how it covers reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills in a way that feels natural. Plus, the bite-sized lessons fit i

Very buggy now. I frequently don't get credit for lessons I've done, or don't get points, or the app hangs. Not good when gamification is one of the main tenets. It keeps interrupting my lessons to prompt me to leave a review, even though I have already left a review. I thought, since I am now on a paid tier, that it would stop nagging me to pay. It just switched to nagging ne to upgrade to higher tier. It's still nagging.

In regards to the radio/podcast listening exercises, please add a skip feature for the pre- and post-ramble. It gets very grating being forced to listen to the same jokes over and over again. For example, with "Late Nights with Lin" I get so sick of hearing Lin talking about how much she loves naps. At least give an option to have these pointless rambles be in the course language. For example, I'm learning Japanese. So have the hosts speak in Japanese, not English, so at least its more practice.

The app is great while you are using the free trial or if you can afford to pay for super or max. If you can't afford it, it's very discouraging to use and extremely frustrating. Almost impossible to keep your streak alive without max and the option to practice to earn hearts has also disappeared. The challenges are not fair between free and paid users they should be seperated as the paid users get a TON of major advantages allowing them to pummel the ones who can't afford it into the dirt.

Good - but stories end! Good language learning app but the developers have been lax in that they've started up stories--which are a great way to pick up new vocab and grammar but I got to the end of them relatively quickly and no more are available, which I think is pretty lame. Also, there's only so much annoying cutesy language, ridiculous intonation and talk of kings, queens and dolls one can take.

The app does not allow for slower voice lessons - either superfast or super slow, neither of which are normal speaking speeds. No useful information for learning new words for new lessons, it just throws new words and sentences at you, which you will get wrong, which does not build confidence or an underatanding of what you are suppose to take from the lesson. If it weren't for the fact I already paid for a subscription and have other family members using it, I would have left.

Constantly advertises Super/Max, that's fine. Constantly shows the monthly price for Super/Max, no problem. Want to get Super/Max, and they will charge you the annual fee. I imagine they hope people will forget to cancel the free trial or forget to cancel when the annual charge renews. I find it quite an exploitative approach to subscriptions.

I like Duolingo because it helps you get a grasp on a new language you are learning. The only problem is that Duolingo is more of a vocabulary increaser. It can help increase your vocab and stuff but it can never teach you the way a school can teach, or perhaps even a teacher. I really like Duolingo otherwise. It's like a game, that's why it is fun then other apps. It makes learning fun!

I love duolingo but I'm getting done with the ads, it was alright before, but they're getting more and more annoying... It's slowing down my progress and I'm honestly losing motivation because each time I complete a lesson I get like 2 or 3 ads. Isn't learning supposed to be free? wasn't this the app's purpose from the start? It doesn't look so free anymore.. Idk.

Duolingo is a decent language-learning app, but it has a major flaw if you're tackling a language with a different alphabet. Instead of easing you in by teaching the new script, it jumps straight into words, expecting you to just pick up the letters as you go. This makes learning something like Russian way harder than it needs to be. If you don’t already know the alphabet, you’re stuck trying to memorize words without fully understanding how they’re written or pronounced. It would be much better

Overall a good app, but I had to wait for so long for a specific German story lesson, and it was so long that it might as well take forever. And also, some speaking practices won't get my pronunciations correctly despite it already being correct. But recently one issue is that some of the scores for my language courses are not appearing. Pls fix the issue, thanks.

I have been very happily using the free app for years. Has anyone else noticed the recent change in advertising. I'm sure the ads are longer, and they only show super duolingo ads. I'm finding the marketing ploy too aggressive for my tastes and am probably going to delete the app. Especially now that there are othe language apps on par with it.

Subscribed to German course about 4 weeks ago. Initially I enjoyed the gamiification, encouragement to learn, and animation. But the novelty has worn off. It has been teaching me the same simple set of words for the last 2 weeks and if I have to repeat them again I'm going to scream. In German even. Like a gambling machine, the app is rigged to force you spend more money to advance your learning even if you subscribe. Nicht beeindruckt (not impressed). Waste of a potentially great app.

I used to be dedicated to this app. It taught me Spanish, it's helped me quickly learn enough of multiple languages to get about, and I have a 1600+ day streak. However, with this new ad heavy update, which means the user has now lost the ability to earn new hearts by doing a review lesson (now you can only get them for watching an ad) I'm getting frustrated with the app (as I know other users are) and am quickly falling out of love with it. I may even have to give up my multi year streak

I'll give it 4 stars because it doesn't allow for you to save your streak, and there's been a few times where I've played and it just didn't register so I've lost my streak or had a "streak freeze" used. There have also been a few times where I did multiple lessons, and it didn't register, so I couldn't gain EXP or move on to the next lesson, which was frustrating. Other than that, I think it's great.

I have some issues with short term memory and doubted my ability to learn anything new, much less a new language. But after a year of not using this app I started to realise I was understanding bits and pieces of speech in Japanese shows I was watching. Then I started using the app again and I'm getting 90-100% on everything, where previously I was really struggling. Its given me a lot of confidence.

They took away the features and ease of use for teachers and students. I did not want to link my personal life (paid version) with my teaching (unpaid version). Now it's ridden with problems my students have 5 hearts again and their level is not there yet! This was a great tool to get students excited and motivated to learn a new language. Now it's dull and I have completely stopped using it for all my classes. So sad, duo please fix it.

Good for learning vocabulary and introducing sentence structure, although some people have told me that Japanese people don't actually speak like that. The grammar is non-existent. You really need to have sources other than Duolingo on the side because there's no explanation in the app of Japanese grammar, which is pretty complicated. The listening exercises are inadequate as well. Lots of bugs and reporting them is to no avail. Keep your money for better apps.

Good. Expansive. The subscription is completely optional, since everything can be done without any issues without it, and even the ads themselves are not that interuptive, since they only appear at the end of a lesson. However, one detail that doesn't sit well with me is the price of starting a legendary lesson, which costs 100 diamons without the subscription. This is very annoying and makes doing legendary lessons basically impossible, or at least not worth it without the subscription.

I have been using Duolingo for a few years and found it very useful and a good way to learn other languages. However it is now frustrating to use for two reasons. 1. There is an intermittent technical issue where on selecting a word from the choice of answers it puts the selected word below the answer line so you cannot complete and have to quit. 2. Duolingo has removed the option to practice which it important to consolidate learning.

I've been learning with Duolingo Max for about a month. Previously, it worked fine--I could chat with Lily. But for the past week, when I chat with Lily, the quest doesn't complete, and I don't get any points. The radio feature that was available before has also disappeared. I've reported the issue multiple times, but there hasn't been any fix at all. it's really awful.

Duolingo used to be an incredible app — fun, accessible, and genuinely motivating for language learners of all levels. I would’ve happily given it 5 stars not long ago. But unfortunately, everything that made it great is now locked behind an increasingly expensive paywall. The free version has been stripped down to the point where it feels more like a frustrating demo than a real learning tool.

I liked this app a lot. But not anymore. I was using it because it was really free and also simple to use but quality. It is free also now in theory, but there is a limited number of lives, making 5 mistakes forces you to wait very long until your next 5 lives refill (whole day long) and you also need to watch too long ads before starting the next lesson. So, after over 2000 days of daily using Duolingo, there are very big chances that I will quit using it. It became too annoying.

This app is great for learning different languages, math and music! I've been using it for a while now and I really like the app but there's one problem: Even though when I am on the last heart and had didn't make any mistakes, it just tells me to quit the course instead of letting it slide for ONE singular mistake. This has been annoying me lately and this why I can't give Duo a 5/5 stars.

I play Duolingo multiple times a day every single day for the past 1640 days, and the recent update is really annoying. The app gets a threatening icon when the day is almost over, and by changing the icon, it rearranges my app, making me have to drag it back to its place every time. Not only that, recently a lot of shaming is going on to make people do their lessons. I will do it, I have been doing it for the past 1640 days, so I really don't need this. If anything, this demotivated me a lot.

I am disappointment with the recent change in the heart refill system, which now forces users to watch ads to regain hearts. As a long-time user of Duolingo, I have appreciated the platform’s commitment to making language learning accessible and engaging. However, this new policy feels restrictive and negative impacts the learning experience.

Recently the advertising on Duolingo has been incredibly aggressive without a subscription. Some of the ads they push make it impossible to continue learning without restarting the app if you're unlucky to be come across them. Additionally they also have started to push their subscription service even more. I get a super ad after almost every completed lesson and/or exercise.

Apparently there is no one available to monitor the Greek lessons... I've been reporting errors on the "learn the letters" section for months, with no corrections being made. The AI audio voice mispronounces letters and letter combinations frequently. It would be nice if someone would look into this - let alone actually upgrade the Greek course to be even remotely comparable to the more popular French or Spanish courses, which have stories and varied voices, to say the least.

It definitely will get the job done for the most part and I enjoy the exercises. But when I tried it a few years ago, they had aoeaking exercises. Now I see threads of 100s of people who no longer have access to speaking, and they layouts don't look the same for everyone. They need to admit if there is an issue or if it's been removed. Speaking is kind of CRUCIAL when learning a new language.

Duolingo is a very good app. It has a very good, advanced and entertaining learning with a lot of languages you can learn. This app is very fun and they give you challenges, a daily streak, and even a leaderboard to compete with other players! This app is perfect if you want to learn any new languages that you find interesting. Duolingo is very kind and nice and they also compliment you. The best learning app ever, but you can buy super Duolingo to get a much better experience than the original!

App is inexplicably stuck in light mode and the setting to change it has been removed. Therefore it is now unusable because the bright background hurts my eyes. The option to change your daily goal in the app has also been removed. Have submitted two bug reports with no response. These were basic features and removing them makes absolutely no sense. Uninstalled, as the browser version now has better functionality, and no stupid incessant ads either.

worst technical support ever!!! The app became worse and worse with every update! It is not intended anymore for learning but to force you to pay for it! Learning means a lot of practice, but you cannot practice unless you pay for it, and even then, the practice is extremely limited. The free version allows you to learn new words but not practice the old words. Bad for learning!

It's good that it doesn't force you to pay any subscriptions but they don't really go into depth with sentence structures for any of the languages. For example, in Finnish you basically have to use common sense or do a Google search for answers to be able to tell difference in words with suffixes (there's a lot of these being introduced) and it makes no sense that I don't even get told why it does that or not. In short, good app but other smaller languages barely get any explanations.

I gave lower rating due to the constant reminders and continued price options, despite expectations of the app being free and wanting to learn at my own pace. I use Duolingo to learn German, and find the exercises very useful. But the daily targets and options to pay if you don't complete exercises correctly are not to my taste. I would recommend Duolingo if you can do the exercises daily, but go elsewhere if you prefer to learn at your own pace.

The app itself is great but the only problems I have is that in music there is a small delay when you press the C key and when it plays and no matter what i do I can't get the speed racer achievement. I get 40 XP in timed challenges and collect my gems but when I reopen the app it shows I didn't get the achievement and takes away my gems. I don't know if anyone else experiences these but these bugs are really annoying. Please fix them and thank you.

The teaching you give us is excellent but it's taking a lot of time. But we must say, maybe teaching and learning is a big progress. But one thing which is unacceptable in Duolingo is that even with our without super Duolingo there is no purpose in giving advertisement. That's not encouragable by everyone. We would really appreciate if you let us study with no distraction. We thought that the best teacher in the is Duolingo, teaching with no money, yes it is but with a lot of advertisements. Ty.

One complaint is that they do not provide European Portuguese. First lesson learning animal words such as armadillo or elephant, not essential! I think beginners would like to learn basics such as ordering food or finding out train times etc. That has yet to come up and I've been working on it for around five months. Although I appreciate that repetition is needed it's doesn't vary enough with the same key words cropping up time after time. Not worth paying for!

Duolingo is a fun and engaging language-learning app with bite-sized lessons, gamified features, and a wide range of languages. It's great for beginners and casual learners, but it may not be enough for advanced fluency. The free version has ads, and the AI-driven lessons can sometimes feel repetitive. Still, it's an excellent starting point for learning a new language...🤗

The range of languages you can learn on duolingo is brilliant and the short lessons are great, but it's definitely got some flaws. I'm fine with the ads but the constant pestering to try super duolingo is extremely annoying, lives take an absolute age to build up, and I get incorrect scores if I spell a name wrong which is silly. I've been going 20 days now and hopefully will be able to continue

Having been a Super user for a couple of years, I'm dissapointed that they created a new tier 'Max' that is 2.5 times as expensive as Super and includes features that used to be in Super. I stopped my subscription and found the free version to be completely unusable as there is no practice anymore and your punished heavily when you make mistakes. Practice makes perfect and mistakes help with learning but stunting the ability to practice when you make a mistake doesn't foster education or growth.

I've been using Duoling for a few years now and I love the app, the design, the games, and it keeps getting better. It is not for serious learning obviously, but I've been loving the Spanish (and many other languages) course and I've learned a lot despite not taking other classes. It keeps you engaged and the app itself doesn't take anything too seriously, it's just fun and that's the best way to keep going ☺️

This the best app for learning languages and maths and music. It is simple, very good, nice, and easy to use. the courses are well designed and and help to connect with people, travel and etc. I am learning hindi, and japanese. it's a very good app. but one thing that I don't like is, that this needs super. it's cheap, like in ₹2.00 but ehat about the -18s ?

Was enjoying my language learning experience until last week there was suddenly a heart system for me. I can gain more by watching ads but the app now also forces me to watch long ads without giving any hearts. Making mistakes is a large part of learning and this change makes the app a terrible experience as a free user. It would be fine-ish if I could just come back to it later that day but all the hearts take 25 hours to come back.

Would be 4, but super duolingo commercial after EVERY single lesson. No grammar definitions whatsoever. A LOT od drilling and repetition, but then when new words come no proper repetition. New words list is without gender (m/f/n), which for languages that have gender is barely usable. All in all, too big of a time investment for what youre getting back.

Used to be a decent enough app but good god the bugs have become atrocious over the last year. Every single day something breaks, xp boosts don't show up. missions will complete themselves and then randomly vanish, it's a complete gamble whether the XP boosts work. I've nearly lost my streak because it didn't register half an hour's worth of practice. I bought a year's subscription but I won't be renewing with how dysfunctional this app has become. I would give zero stars if I could.

Duolingo has really helped me with revisions of French and Dutch. My vocabulary has increased a lot. Please update the Dutch language as it is very limited. French is way better for options in learning. A few English translations aren't as correct for the French, but I understand it. Overall, I recommend DuoLingo for up to Pre-Intermediate French and for mostly Basic Dutch. Later, I'll try other languages. Thanks for all your hard work.

English is American English, so have to use American English words which is very frustrating and sometimes confusing. Spanish isn't European Spanish and you can't choose which version you learn. Can get very repetitive. it doesn't take the time to go through basis on how verbs work, or run through the numbers. just a different approach.

A great way to learn and build up vocabulary in your own time. Easy to follow, and helpful to repeat things you might struggle with (in my case the grammar). Can be slightly frustrating dealing with the Americanisms for English words (such as "purses" for bags, "pants" for trousers, "check" for bill, "soccer" for football) or I would have given it 5 stars!

This app used to be good! I used to like it but now with the heart system the hearts force you to be almost perfect when they say practice not perfection. They took away the option to practice to earn hearts which is more discouraging. The microphone function to practice speaking doesnt always work properly. The way this new learning path is working makes it difficult to remember and recognize new words, and add words the user hasnt learned yet, making it difficult to learn anything.

won't stop saying "You are offline" when i'm very clearly connected. have tried multiple different networks and none worked, Including 4G. even when i did manage to do my lesson, It didn't register it as done and didn't continue my streak. super glitchy, Please let me know how to fix this. update: I tried uninstalling and reinstalling, Worked for one day, stopped working. in anyways this app is filled with mispronounciation when it comes to japanese. i have better apps, like for example, renshuu

Scoring doesn't work sometimes despite what points it tells you that you can get and has been like this for a good while. Last night, my first lesson for the week, I did 3 triple XP lessons which should have been around 300 points. I looked on the scoreboard at the end and I have 80 points. Very very frustrating. As a positive, the improvement to watch ads to earn hearts makes the free app usable again but I already got into using another app because the recent lack of ability to earn them

Buggy, no algorithym to promote your learning & facilitate going faster and the gamification while possibly suitable for under 12s is annoying for an adult. Additionally, the "free" version tries to upsell you constantly, after each exercise; if the app wants to charge, then just charge, stop this stupid nonsense of offering it for free but constantly tapping the user to upgrade to pay. The content is good, just wish they'd separate out learning for adults vs. children.

I opened the app today and I don't like the changes. The screen swipes left when you respond to each lesson of screen (I'm getting a headache from seeing the screen move every time). And the lesson is slowly going through, it's like you want to waste my seconds with the limited time of the booster you give us. Revert the changes back.

it used to be perfect before they added the ads. instead of practicing to refill hearts you have to eat h an ad now!? I got 3 ad's one after another even though I had a perfect lesson and got everything right. I swear it's gonna get to a point where you have to watch an ad before a lesson, I used to love this app now it's just becoming like all the other apps that spam ads. Hope it doesn't get worse from here

It's been almost 2 weeks of me reporting that I'm getting the answer correct in the app. I finally progress in lessons that kept marking me wrong I mention on my review a few days ago. only to be stopped by the same word being marked wrong. Section 1 unit 3 lessons 5 of 5. I hope I'm able to progress my lessons soon instead of waiting for days. It's becoming frustrating. I even did a proper complaint with my email. It's the same word and I am doing it correctly. Now I'm missing out on rewards.

This app is great. I just with there were more languages coming such as tagalog. I also have suggestions. 1. You can choose the characters, meaning if you don't want to see or hear them when you do your lessons, you can go to settings to remove the characters temporarily. 2. new characters? especially during special events, which would be temporary. 3. characters actions. they could be sick, tired, or something like that to let you know what they sound like if that happens. 4. more events.

The app is good although the Spanish they teach is more from Latin America and not Spain. I am getting better using it but the amount of ads is absolutely mind-blowing. Click on this? Add! You finished watching the ad? Here is another ad for Max. I get it that they need to survive somehow to keep it free but it's insane how many ads you have to watch.

The team at Duolingo was always adamant about being a free language learning app. This is no longer the case, since they changed the way the hearts are replenished. you can only replenish them through watching ads or waiting a set time. Yes its still technically free but you are now paying by being the product. with learning a language nothing is more important than consistency. The moment im forced to watch an ad i turn the app off. I could pay but other apps give a much better service

Each update makes the app more unusable and the main goal of providing a tool to actually learn a language has apparently long been abandoned. Getting rid of the option to refill hearts by revising and replacing it with ads or monetary demands discourages the learning process. Not that it would make much sense now, given how many mistakes keep showing up in lessons.

I was enjoying this app with a Super Duo subscription for last year. Then, I decided to subscribe to Max to have speaking practice. It cost much more expensive and I am totally disappointed by this version. Only one option added to call with Lily and it really one sentence with no feedback. I tried a few different apps that offer speaking with AI and it much-much better. In my opinion, the cost of Duo Max doesn't make any sense. I am going to unsubscribe the Max.

Sometimes gives you words with no explanation and expects you to "just figure it out". What's wrong with having a button that opens a wall of text explaining everything that is being taught in the current section/unit. I Spanish, you learn words for "drink" near the start. But there is no explanation is to why "bebo" is different to "bebe". Explanations should never be locked behind a pay wall. Especially when the user is already paying for super.

In my opinion, Duolingo is an amazing app - it's a great way to learn another language without taking classes in real life. It offers a wide variety of languages, and helps you to remember what you've learnt by regularly revisiting previous topics. I thoroughly recommend this to anyone who is interested in learning a new language, especially if they don't have a person in the area who can teach them the language personally.

after 3+years it's getting worse and worse, I hate the owls personality change it's not motivating just irritating. I hate that there's no real tips or explanations in the free version, that its all based on American english is awful, so many ads, so much of it is all so gameified instead of actually about learning. I'm looking at alternatives as my learning has really slowed down & I'm not having fun any more.

what was once a great learning tool, is now almost entirely unusable. at the end of every exercise (that takes a minute or two to complete) you have to sit through 2 adverts (one for an upgrade and one for some random product) all the best tools for revising are locked behind upgrades and you can't even 'master' a lesson without paying 100 gems per attempt. and I had super for a year and instead of getting ads for super, they started giving me ads for max. not worth the time anymore.

It was an awesome app! It won't let me just practice to earn back hearts anymore, you have to do the lessons and if you are out of hearts you have to either watch an ad or buy them back. Super disappointed with these changes. I have been using it for 2 years and seriously thinking of ending my streak!

Been a user with super account for 15 months Good: Gamification keep you practising. Can track activity against friends Bad: Just if time using it features have been removed and placed behind, even high pay walls (£11.99 a month and still have to pay a higher subscription to he able to be told why my answer is wrong or to explain!!) Constant pushing of next tier (max) Bugs appearing more often with faulty or very specific choice of wording decisions on what counts as a correct answer.

My review is about the Japanese course: In short, its super long and tedious, you dont learn grammar or conjugation until youre a bit far into the course, and one thing that bothers me is that duolingo teaches verbs in one conjugated form instead of their dictionary form, making learning other forms or casual form difficult! I've abandoned duolingo, i learned conjugation and grammar through books and videos, i got Anki for vocab, i joined a japanese learning discord, and i got better and faster!

it's fun and actually works, I'm finding myself even early on into starting a new language to recognising characters, and that's just credit to the app teaching well, repetition where it makes sense and great positive reinforcement. the streak is addictive and motivational for sure, well played. most of all I love how funny, sarcastic and encouraging the characters are!

It's good for learning, but there are too many stupid rewards like gems and chests and unnecessy stuff like that. Maybe good for kids, but I just want to learn. And I paid for a full year subscription that I thought would give full access to the app, but then found out you have to pay more for "the full version" which makes it seem like I got ripped off.

I can't write as much as I want to. 500 characters isn't enough. The xp competition ruins the fun of learning, but turning it off can only be done by having a private profile. No friends or family. No daily events. Can't get the numbers you need for monthly badges. The constant upselling is horrible too. Have to pay for a year to get any kind of value, and get spammed with adverts to buy max. Great. Bugs are a pain to. My app has now gotten stuck in light mode. Cant fix it. Support ignores me.

Freezes dont perpetually keep your streak. There is no point in anyone doing a single "class" just to keep the streak, this is not making learning fun, instead its a chore you do it once per day and end up hating it. People sometimes make time to invest and do over 10 "classes" per day but that does not justify as higher commitment to a streak. Complete brainrot streak system makes me hate this more than it should.

A few days ago I was unable to select one of the languages I normally practise, then when I closed the app to reopen it, I was stuck on the green start up screen. 7 hours later it was still the same situation, so I uninstalled and reinstalled, logged in and then was stuck again on the green screen...did a lesson on the browser version for now. I've since restarted my phone, no luck, and also updated the software on my phone...also no luck.

I never took Duolingo seriously. It's a fun way to know a language to an extent where I can watch a movie in that language and have a basic idea about the conversation. But, this app is slowly losing its credibility. You will lose a streak out of nowhere, you will be pushed down to the below league just like that even though you finished at a position as per their requirement to be at a certain league level. And the worst part is that we cannot reach them to rectify it. Just send "FEEDBACK".

I've been using this app for over 2 years now, and I really enjoy this app for the languages I'm learning + reviewing (🇨🇳+🇳🇴,🇬🇧,🇳🇱). It is easy to use, and the exercises are short and fun. I especially like the cute little characters. They make it feel like I'm learning together with someone by reminding me (without being too pushy), and they make the learning process less boring.

Okay, but cannot brush off the feeling that it's more some kind of typical mobile game with dailies, ingame purchases, subscription to get rid of constant ads and then you kind of just mindlessly breeze through the neverending repetitive lessons which leaves a bitter taste while going nowhere, not learning anything really in the end only leaving bored after some time. Buying practice/grammar books and actually *write* anything with your own hands will do the trick way better, seriously.

i love the app, but please make "big" purchases reversable. i accidentally bought something i definitely didn't need or want, and it took about 80% of my gems 🥺 edit: something relatively smaller (100 gems) happened to me again. i know this may not be the most common problem, but it's annoying when you purchase something by mistake and the app doesn't even give you a short grace period to undo that action.

Here’s a well-rounded feedback on the Duolingo app: Pros: 1. User-Friendly Interface: Duolingo has a fun and engaging interface with gamified elements like streaks, XP points, and leaderboards, which keep users motivated. 2. Wide Variety of Languages: Offers a large selection of languages, including popular ones (Spanish, French) and less common ones (Welsh, Hawaiian). 3. Bite-Sized Lessons: The short and interactive lessons make it easy to learn on the go, especially for busy people

I made a bad review before, hoping that the app would be better, but it is getting worse. The amount of bugs, specially in the music section, are horrendous (hitting the correct note, but given as wrong, so you lose hearts and can't go on). It is like there is no testing done at all. And then they keep harrashing you with a paid plan, which is way too overpriced for this buggy app. Please, if you are serious about learning a language, just pay a credited teacher. This app is at best a game.

My chosen language (Finnish) is excessively limited and doesn't offer much variety in learning ie No Stories. I've also been waiting since the big UI change (from TREE to PATH) for Duolingo to add Grammar Tips, because it's a necessary element for understanding what the heck is going on when you progress through the course. My rating will NEVER exceed a generous Two Stars unless Grammer Tips, at the very least, are implemented.

I like Duolingo and all it helped make learning foreign language fun. It really helps us learn a lot. The things I don't like about Duolingo are the math and music learning. The math is just not what I expected because even when I pressed the high school math lesson it's only multiplication and the music so far is only piano. I haven't really gone through those all the way but I'm just saying those are my experiences the first time I tried them and I haven't really continued them.

Started pretty good and nicely. UI designs are friendly and useful. Exercises really make you learn. My only suggestion is: I would really prefer if Russian also had written readings in Latin alphabet at tooltips too, would help a lot. *Update: It was really good and I gave 5 stars, but after today's frustration I'm on the edge of my 2nd uninstall. You ruined a properly working app and destroyed its learning trees and infested it with intentional error traps that "mishears" everything. 1/5

Duolingo became worse it was really good and now it keeps making me lose lives. For example when I press one of the words to go to the sentence it doesn't go and I don't realise and then I get it wrong. I also keep going down in the leaderboard and in the league. Yes, it does help u with learning a new language but at the same time u can barely learn it bc of these things. Personally, I used Duolingo all the time and now I barely use it bc I keep losing all my lives and streak.

Duolingo is the best language learning app I have ever tried. It's free and user friendly it has all language options like if I speak English then I have to look in the English speaking ppl. I have selected French and I have a 76 day streak on it for now. There practice points as well like if u do a mistake in the lesson then you can practice it. You can practice listening and speaking as well. Thank you. I have a great experience of it and I am having it.

The free version is extremely boring with exercise repetitions over and over even though you already received the highest score the very first time... This app doesn't let you move forward. Constant advertisement interruptions and endless attempts to make you pay for another version. There are better alternatives on the Internet.

I love the game but I hate the competition aspect of it. I would love to turn off the leagues aspect of it. The notifications for this particular thing like this person has taken your spot etc. turns me off like anything. I had 400 days of streak and I just couldn't handle the league thing anymore. I just want to learn and talk hypothetically with people.

Great app! It helps a lot to learn languages. The only thing is that the "practice to earn hearts" is gone unless you have no heart left, then you can do 1 lesson to get 1 out of 5 hearts. It's more difficult now to finish the lessons with only 1 heart and no way to get more. I wish they would bring back the option to get all of your hearts back.

Good app when it wants to be. Way too excessive with the ads. It turns into nearly every minute. My biggest dislike is the way you get questions wrong supposedly if there spelt wrong. As someone who's always struggled with grammar and spelling since childhood doing it in a different language is not as easy to remember. While at the same time I understand if a words completely off that's fair but one letter off is way too over the top. I'm not doing the Spelling Bee.

No support from Duolingo at all despite me paying for an expensive subscription. App stopped working 1 day after the Refund policy from google expires, so bye bye money for the rest of the 9.5 months I paid for. Speak practise has stopped working suddenly (immediate 'incorrect' before I even can start talking). All their general troubleshooting doesn't work, bug reports etc are not responded to. Would not recommend the paid version, since you do not get what you pay for.

Everything was good about the app, obviously you had ads to deal with whilst using for free which is totally understandable, but now if you run out of hearts/attempts to get the correct answers you can no longer practice to gain hearts or qualify as finishing a lesson of the day. It's just a ploy and another way to try and force people into paying for subscriptions. As usual in this world, people will never have enough money, they're always wanting more more more. Stop with the greed!!!

Duolingo used to be great 10+ years ago. Absolutely horrible now. If you've got 20 mins to practice, half of it will be spent watching annoying ads for the premium version and the other half with be constantly interrupted for no reason by that stupid owl and/or offers for gems, prizes, streaks, lingots, or other desperate attempts at unnecessarily gamifying the experience. Personally, I don't appreciate obnoxious interruptions when trying to learn. I doubt Duolingo exists in 10 years.

I cannot in good conscience rate a learning app that prioritizes profit over accuracy. I'm using it to refresh my Japanese, and the amount of mistakes is disturbing. E.g., differentiating between lunch box and lunchbox to fit their format, or using ai-voices that don't use the correct pronunciation (which in Japanese can be the difference between 'oo' and 'tai'). If they would only read the reports and check with someone competent....

the app keeps getting worse and worse over time. it looks to become only about earning the money. Even though the app can be great,the heart system is not fun to continue the path of learning.. It pushes you down to do low level learning in order to try again.. Reported it, and many other things, and it hasn't been picked up. The basic structure need to be improved, especially the Spanish is very full of basic mistakes, and my family has even a different version while both are up to date!

Good, but sometimes when I have a 3x xp boost, and I get a 2x xp boost, it glitches and just gives me 2x xp boost, even when I want to claim in shop. There also other numerous bugs and glitches like one where it set me back an entire unit, and another witch reset my streak even when I had a streak freeze. Pls fix these annoying bugs so people can enjoy the game more.

When you spend money for something, you expect to get something out of it. For me, it was no ads and I also enyoyed the training hub's daily lessons. Now I get ads for a new tier and my personal lesson has been replaced by an ad banner for said new tier. Whichs feature set I don't even care about. This is not pushing me to get the higher tier. It's pushing me to cancel my current subscription and go somewhere I feel more valued as a customer.

It's today my 365 day streak in Duolingo, and I'm very much disappointed that a lot of features that were always free, are now paywalled. You have to either pay for the subscription or pay a lot of crystals. I understand making new features like ai studying help or whatever accessible only with the Super subscription, but not the old features... It's even more dumb, considering there is no method to buy the subscription in my country

Very unclear pricing. I've been trying to find out how much Super cost and Max but I have no idea. I've searched the entire app. It is also not listed on the adverts for Super that appear after every other lesson. They try to get you to sign up for the free trial but they don't even tell you how much you'll be billed after the trial ends!

Duolingo is hands down one of the best language-learning apps out there! The gamified approach keeps learning fun and engaging, making it easy to stay consistent. The bite-sized lessons are perfect for busy schedules, and the interactive exercises help reinforce vocabulary. Whether you're a complete beginner or looking to brush up on a language, Duolingo is an excellent tool to build fluency in a fun and stress-free way. Highly recommended for anyone serious about learning a new language!

at first i really love this app. it's cool i can learn a lot of languages, i can even learn math & music BUT the super/max ads this app gives everytime i finished learning is too much seriously. it's so annoying, give it but don't everytime i finished. okay i know the super thing is cool like we can even do training, but NOT ALL ppl have it..so the ads is definitely too much. better if this ads thing is fixed to less ads, i hope u understand the ppl that don't have this super/max pack.

Duolingo has truly revolutionized language learning, making it accessible and engaging for millions worldwide. What I appreciate most is its gamified approach; it transforms what could be a tedious task into an enjoyable daily habit. The bite-sized lessons are perfect for fitting into even the busiest schedules, and the constant reinforcement through repetition and quizzes really helps solidify new vocabulary and grammar concepts.

Decent but as a long time user this feels like a sinking ship. App is getting worse with every update, regarding performance, motivation and general design. Mostly just adding more opportunities for advertising or the spending of Premium currency while removing free features. Seems like the good times are over and they're now trying to get as much money out of it as possible. Update: Now seemingly very limited possibilities to do free lessons so hard to call this free anymore.

It gives you a game like feeling when literally learning a new language. It makes learning languages so easy and accessible. I enjoy the various personalities and little details added to the characters. Overall, it is a good experience. I must say that the ads are becoming a bit out of hand lately 😂

it's okay, but it focuses way too much on random spelling, and the phrases are not super useful. it's fine for gamification of learning, as long as you aren't hoping to do it for a month and actually have vacation- ready (language of your choice). I did it for a month and can only tell you about what nationality people are, and eating pizza.

Good to start but: 1) not enough explanation of the rules 2) They have introduced a higher tier for AI explanations - which I refuse to upgrade as I see it as moving the goal posts in a cheeky way 3) the time based rewards encourage people to not spend enough time learning properly but to rush through it to get points. i liked it initially but now I start to see its limitations when trying to seriously learn a language.

The app is great, but it should teach the rule or words BEFORE asking questions. For example, it should first explain the lesson’s grammar/vocabulary, THEN test us. Right now, it feels like guessing instead of learning. Please improve this. Love the app! But please show grammar rules/vocab FIRST, then quiz us. It’s hard to learn by guessing. Thanks!

Kinda useful overall for vocabulary - would be nicer if they had more extensive theory cards. Daily Quests are not appropriate for people who already finished all the lessons as they often require unachievable goals for Daily Refresh (notably the speaking ones and the 15min one which is way too long for anyone doing a decent job). Lastly, their add strategy is all over the place. One week I have small square ads, the next I have unskippable 10+s ads, the next one the close button changes...

Aggressive nagware constantly trying to upsell you on an even more premium plan than the one you're paying for. Linguistically it keeps getting worse too. Intuitive learning exercises sacrificed on the altar of flashy animations and rigid scoring systems. So after years of use, no thanks. I'm drawing the line.

Duolingo is a good app for learning simpler more domesticated languages such as French or Spanish, but I tried to learn Korean on Duo and it was going by way too quick. It doesn't provide enough information for the more difficult languages with more rules, but that's really all. Also it tells me to do my lesson at like 3 in the morning when I'm still sleeping which is annoying how my phones goes off. But that's all, 6/10 app overall.

Everytime I open the app, it says "Duolingo wants to send you practice reminders". I have used the app every day for several years without ever using practice reminders, without using the widget, without allowing notifications. I have always had notification features off and yet this banner keeps showing up everytime I open the app. Please remove the banner. Not everybody needs constant marketing in order to learn a language. If this is a permanent feature then I'll find a new app.

it is good if you want to properly learn a language. But if you just want a casual experience, like learning essential phrases and words for traveling, then it is not great. I'm also not sure how it handles other languages, but for Korean, AI voices pronounced some sounds and words inconsistently.

I like Duolingo, and really appreciate that the free version is usable - unlike a lot of services. It's a great app that 100% does "upgrade to premium" the right way. I love that it makes learning languages sociable. I'd really recommend it to anyone who's trying to learn a new language. However (and I don't know what this is like for other languages) the differences between American and British English can be irritating, particularly as someone from England, as the app favours American English.

I would put 4 or 5 stars but the newest update locking me out of practicing to earn hearts entirely is a big downgrade It went from (years ago you can use this as many times as you want) to You can use the feature once to not existing at all Upside is the program is still basically the same and will give you a very interactive way of learning new words and exposure for languages you want to learn, and the voice over for the words is very neutral. In short: great program practically unusable now.

They're removing the icon if I don't practice as early as they think I should. This is annoying beyond what I can express here. I haven't uninstalled it only because I am using it with family and I am still learning. It's a fun way to learn languages. I'm more consistent and enjoying the process more with Duo and friends. The free version is what I used for over two months before we got a family plan.

This is just the best language app. I am not suprised that it recieved so much love and is the first thing that comes to mind when we think about learning a language as well as maths and music. The only thing which bothers me is the repetitive questions that irritate me every single time. I bet there are so many questions that just repeat for about 10 times. I wish you can fix the bug. Thanks for your time!! Have a nice day ahead 👍👍

I'm paying for Duolingo Family plan. I don't want to see ads for Duolingo Max after I finish my lessons... It's an unacceptable industry standard where apps somehow think it's ok to advertise their own products within already paid products. If I want Max I'll consider my options when I have to renew my plan. Remove the ads or I won't renew my plan, you have a lot of time to coordinate this extremely difficult task of pressing the remove button.

Dont like the app comparing me with beyonce. Dont like leaderboards. Exercises repeat with too much frequency. Edit: units just teach like 1 or 2 things. I often can skip 80% of the unit because it's all repeat repeat repeat. It's like the app thinks you are dumb or something. Translation using the word bank doesn't teach you at all because you can guess the whole sentence. After a 600+ days streak I can tell you that you will learn much better Japanese with Renshuu.

I'm frustrated with the bonus exp features, as they are incredibly buggy. Every other day it seems it's not applied correctly. Just today, my 20 minute timer ran out 10 mins early. Other times, the bonus is not applied at all. The frequent unpredictable behavior of this so-called 'reward' leads me to rate this app poorly.

Great app but the customer care is absolutely terrible. I contacted them for help over a month ago to regain access to an account and they haven't got back to me at all. I've followed up repeatedly, and still nothing. Now I've cancelled my family plan because I'm not paying for an app and service that can't be used. Don't waste your time with this. Any other app must have better customer care than these guys. It's extremely frustrating.

Great app, the fact that I can choose the length of my lessons is a nice plus. I feel like the pace at which I learn things is good and i can always go back and review things if I forget. Edit: Lowering the stars because they removed the "practise for hearts" feature which was one of the things I liked most.

i am using this app since 2019. i am really disappointed how you have lowered the quality of this app. the free version has becoming really annoying and frustrating. you are not following your motto but just desperately showing super Duolingo ad literally after every lesson. you need to fix it. many people worldwide use your app for free learning. don't make it hard or annoying for them.

I'm on one of the first lessons to an introduction. I didn't understand the previous lesson, which left me with nothing but guesses, and the app will not let me redo earlier ones to actually learn what I don't understand. So now I'm just tapping random answers cause I don't know them anyway, hoping to pass before my 'lives' run out and it asks me to pay them for a refill. I was excited to try the app, but it's straight up bad.

it's a really good app. learning is fun and comprehensive. although, I have an issue with the Korean courses. I know how to read the Korean symbols but understanding is a little bit difficult. imagine learning a completely different language with completely different symbols and pronunciations unlike Spanish that still uses English letters and pronunciations. learning a language as tedious as that needs to be handled with more care especially for people who learnt English as their first language

Duolingo started out as a fun way to learn a language but have increasingly made it worse with each update. I'm assuming as a way to push people into paying for a subscription. You get served two ads every lesson, and they've removed the ability for you to do some generic practice which was really useful for strengthening words you've already learned.

The 'practice to earn more hearts' option has been removed. Now, I find it much harder for me to extend my streak. Before, I'd just practice, which helped me with learning, gave me a heart, and extended my streak. Now I have to spend gems or watch lots of ads, especially if I fail lessons, just to do the same thing. This makes me spend more time watching ads than learning, makes me feel less motivated, and also makes me spend the gems I'm saving for other things. Duo, please fix this. 🙏 🙏

This app become more useless each year in the last 5 years. now you can't earn Heart points doing an exercise or studying some basics again, u have to spend at least 30s watching ads. that's pointless. this should be an app for language learning. Now, it's an app that wants to generate money trying to steal its users with a low quality product and useless tricks.

Duolingo is an amazing app that has helped me improve my English skills significantly! The interactive lessons, quizzes, and games make learning fun and engaging. The app is user-friendly, and the daily reminders keep me motivated. I've seen a huge improvement in my language skills, and I couldn't be more grateful. Highly recommend Duolingo to anyone looking to learn or improve a new language!"

Two points: 1. No grammar explanation in some cases. In terms of functionality of the app it's nothing, you just need to add more explanations for every section. 2. Explain to me the point of 2 Match exercises. I understand why the app forces you to pass this exercise, you need more engagements, but why do you think that this exercise teaches anything? Absolutely useless exercise and I hate when the app forces you to pass it.

This was once a 5 star app, but after recent updates and changes, like erasing 3-star progress on music lessons (what's the point mastering them?), I don't enjoy this app anymore. But I bought Super Duo in December, so I'm screwed. The new pictures are disgusting and a learning deterent. How is Duo melting out of his skin or turning into a revoltingly ugly unicorn condusive to learning? It's not. Thanks Duo, for ruining a perfectly good app. Been playing since 2016, but this is too much.

It's good, although the hearts system is quite punishing and practice to earn hearts has changed significantly. But it is a free app, and as annoying as running out of hearts can be, I can't really complain. Since the first part of my review, Duolingo has updated the hearts system, so you can earn hearts by watching ads. This has made it easier to advance and I'm really quite pleased with this, so I 'm adding another star.🤗👏👍

Useful, but quite often the English translations of foreign phrases are a bit weird. The "English" is very much American so strange stuff like "He studies math" instead of "maths" is to be expected, but there's also a lot of strange phrases that I've never heard from an American either .... Some of the recorded voices seem to mumble and are hard to follow - but that's probably a good thing as (for example), very few real French people enunciate like De Gaulle.

Duolingo is great as an app for learning languages, but the free version is frustrating after running out of lives. It would be better if lives reset after each lesson in the free version, so that if a student uses all 5 of their lives in one lesson, they lose the progress for that single lesson, but can try again without having to watch a bunch of ads or waiting to do so. Running out of lives and having to wait a day to keep learning really makes the learning process frustrating.

It's very very useful and funny app.I liked The characters' interaction when you answer right or wrong 🤣🤣. And I also liked the widget of your daily active,it makes me go to the app and take a lesson to not lose my stroke😂 Third thing is the multiple languages,there is a lot of languages that makes me training on more than one language. THANK YOU "DUOLINGO"

Deleted, streak of 2805 days. Very buggy. Amount of ads increased drastically. Made unusable at high levels unless you sub. Fun has been taken out of it. I have used it for about 8 years and it helped me learning Swedish. I enjoyed it but it has become very repetitive. I refuse going for the paid version as it is overpriced. If I would go for a paid language course, I would take a different, far better and less expensive one. Duolingo is now doing all it can to get you to sub.

A sudden inflation wiped out all my virtual currency, making even basic items like a streak freeze unaffordable. This aggressive monetization push has made the app far less enjoyable. Time-based exercises are now nearly impossible to complete within the limit, seemingly designed to drain soft currency and push users toward spending real money. On top of that, the app is buggy — sometimes it doesn't register completed lessons, forcing you to repeat them for progress to count.

Overall Duolingo has been really helpful for me learning German, the little games are fun and it's really improved in terms of teaching method since I last used it. However, I am consistently having issues with the speech exercises... they are usually fine when in a general unit, but when you have to repeat the same sentence over and over, certain words will not be accepted. This is kind of weird because I have had sloppy pronunciation be acceptable and perfect pronunciation be unacceptable 🤔

This app is amazing! It’s so interactive and engaging that learning a new language feels exciting and rewarding. The challenges keep me motivated, and the achievements make progress even more satisfying. Learning in person can be intimidating, but this app makes it fun, stress-free, and effective. I’m so glad it exists—it has truly helped me improve!

Now worse than ever before. They have taken away the option to practise and changed the way that the leagues work ( if they are real). I'm not interested in competition, I just want learn at my own pace. Guess I'm heading for the bottom league. Totally de motivated by it now. Impossible to make progress with free version now that you can't gain hearts by practising. Don't appear to get 5 hearts top up either. Making more progress now using Busuu.

The app itself is great, but the ads are a big No-No for me, (respectively) , hence the 4 starts. There's been some flaws to the app popping up, in one of the practice modes, whenever I'm learning Italian it gets stuck on Finnish, and when I'm learning Finnish, it gets stuck on Italian so it's hard to get in alot of each practice modes...

I'm currently learning Chinese, and it's going well. You can do a lesson a day, or spend more time if you can manage. The only complain I have, is that after you get an ad, you get another Duolingo Super ad right away. I understand that the service is free, but I don't need to be reminded of Super after every ad. I'm more than happy to sit through the ads that help support the service, but it's a bit much after a while.

it's terrible. i get a duolingo ad after EVERY lesson i do. it's really discouraging to do a lesson and get hit with the same thing right after. also, i hate that you can't turn off listening or speaking exercises in the settings anymore. if you're looking for an app to learn a language, duolingo is NOT the choice for that. it's just some money grab app. it shouldn't hide pretty much half the features behind a paywall. i learnt english faster by watching content in that language rather than duo.

(On Android phone) In the "Your collections" option where I can see the words which I have learned up until now is acting all crazy since the last update. Words have appeared for which I've not even completed relevant lessons. My learned words are disappearing and reappearing randomly. Please fix this issue. I'm unable to practice my words. Please fix this issue!

I have been using Duolingo for 400+ days, always praising it to other people because it's a good way to learn some words/ease into a language. Some lessons are frustrating because the smallest autocorrect or spelling will register wrong when it's just like capatilisation (on a phone, mistakes happen) but nonetheless... I use it a lot. Now, my friends and I woke up to find out you can no longer do practise lessons to rewin one heart, meaning you cannot continue your streak without paying. This i

Good for learning some language like English and Japanese, but in Arabic lessons, I noticed there are fewer variations to the modules, and they even include local dialects such as egyptian to it. Also, recently there's a bug in Japanese that won't allow me to check my answer and proceed even when I have arranged the words correctly. Overall okay and helpful, but if you want to learn the languages seriously, take external courses and don't solely rely on Duolingo.

I have a streak of over 650 days in Spanish. Duolingo has been great and fun. But the recent removal of "practice to earn hearts" is ridiculous. If you don't complete your lesson on your first attempt, you're forced to pay or watch ads just to keep your streak going. It makes it so much harder to get a quick lesson in on the go or before uni. For an app that puts such a focus on maintaining your streak, removing an option to keep learning to get your streak is unfair and greedy.

I've been a user for 4 years, and app changes up all the time. Generally it is great. My language is slowly getting better, the app is fun, the stories are cute and funny. The AI is *not* worth getting as there is so much lag that it misses what you are saying or talks over the top of you. I've tried it in 3 countries now. The Apple version is better than the Android version - fewer bugs. E.g. On android I've had the same 5-10 words appear in my unit word review for the last 6 units.

So much worse than it used to be. Decent way to start learning a language, but... After a few mistakes you can't do any more lessons unless you pay. Ads are for annoying time wasting apps, easy to click on by mistake and inconsistent where the skip button is. After all the talk they gave 10 or so years ago about their aim being free language learning for all, not making money, I'm not happy.

I'm happy to try and learn new languages, and I do appreciate that this app is free and easy to use. However, while I understand the need for ads, this is far too much. I get an ad + a "get Duolingo super" ad after EVERY. SINGLE. LESSON. Truly, I understand that since I don't pay the subscription that ads are expected. I understand that. But this is too much. You're not making me want to get super, you're making me want to delete the app or do as little lessons per day as possible.

I am so frustrated, I love this app but as from Thurs last week I cannot complete my unit because of a fault in the program that disallows me to continue. I am an 850 day veteran, I have contacted duolingo via email, I have told them when I attempt to insert the particular verb, there is 3 user insert points for the different me, you , they ,us etc, when i click on any of them, one answer disappears and the continue button is greyed out making it unable to continue, I await an answer.....

For me it is very enjoyable the way the learning is made. I agree with some comments that say its bad because of some mistakes or weird sounding words, sometimes its hard to tell what it actually says. I would love if there could be added more content to the rest of the languages like there is for German and such (reading books, podcasts, games), to Arabic especially, the difference between German and Arabic content is beyond me haha. It would be nice if Serbian could be added soon too!!

The app itself, design of UI and lessons are simply amazing. it's 5 stars from me. However, the whole taste of quality product gets ruined when ads start to pop up. But I guess that's the small price to pay for the array of truly amazing and intuitive features Duolingo has. This is still a 5 from me.

This app doesn't provide the most important things needed for language learning and it also gives false information for the language you learn. The app also gives notifications as if your friend wrote them while your friend never "nudged you", it just presents the notification as if he did. The messages are awful, sometimes written in red colour and anxiety producing. Duolingo gives notifications not caring about the time of the day, often at midnight, one a.m., two a.m. etc.

So I've been with this app since 2020 as a passive way to learn a language. Recently it's absolutely overrun with ads. >900 day streak here: Duolingo gets worse with every update. It was never the best way to learn a language, but was a fun way to build a habit. The new addition of largely AI-generated activities has also led to a slew of low-effort, often incorrect roadblocks that pad your time on the app. Don't bother with Duolingo in 2025.

it's a nice learning app! funny and diverse characters ask you the questions. but not full stars because in several languages the words you learn in the first levels are not useful for daily life, and if you don't have the paid version there is a lot of annoying advertisement that you have to watch. in my opinion the paid version isn't good enough for what you get, and there is no option to have a monthly subscription.

"Try free" ads after every single round, it's just ridiculous. And if you try to skip it by closing the app, it doesn't count towards your daily quests. Helpful app for learning languages worldwide.. This app makes us learn words and pronunciation.. Currently I'm practicing French along with, I'm also increasing the brain power by solving basic maths.. It's a fun app and while it won't teach you a language all on its own, in my opinion, it's a good place to start. If it's not fixed soon, I may lower my rating.

great learning content and I've been using for years now. Ads have got a little bit pushy, some even try to trick you into going to the website with little fake X's in the corners and fake battery low notifications (shein). now the bear character says he doesn't understand why humans have multiple other apps and streaming services but won't get duolingo premium service.. the reason for this is that it's a large one time payment instead of monthly. change this and see how many people upgrade?

I've been using Duolingo for a few different languages over the years, and it's great. It's a perfect addition to my weekly 1 hour formal lesson that I currently do. Just a little bit of practice each day has helped me advance further in my language learning. Thanks for continuously trying to make it fun and interesting!

It's a good app for learning new languages but the app is weird. It pressures you to much to do lessons and the ads for super duo is ridiculous. After every lesson it happens. just be normal and helpful and I'll rate it better. I still use this app but just fix your stupidity. all I'm trying to do it learn French!

Can be fun but talking exercises are sometimes broken and I really dislike the speed challenges and way too many notifications (can be disabled though). Update: Got much worse with the last update messing up my progress. Some translations don't make sense at all in my native language. I guess they are AI generated garbage

Its really amazing how this app helps us to learn a new language. Its system of learning is really great and it compels us to learn .I like their thos feature the best so we cant be lazy ,..... Really loved this and having good experience Most of all i am a student so its really important for us to learn new languages so we can work abroad and experience the most... A big thanks to Duolingo and its team ..❤️❤️❤️😍💗

I recommend this app because Duolingo really helps me with German. Like, I learned so much from him and I feel like literally going to Germany! It says that it's an all approaches but it isn't like it's really helpful you can get it for free and it barely takes any of your memory he's your life helper like he's the best you could even get super Duolingo and Max Duolingo which is like a better process by using AI and it makes you to learn for free!😊😍🙂😁😃

Does a good job of helping you through the difficulties of starting to learn a language. Isn't overbearing and only takes a few minutes a day. EDIT: They have changed the method of earning more hearts from practicing the language to watching ads. Find another solution for your language learning. There are plenty of other systems available.

How in the world do I still not get the right amount of xp half of the time? It's such a simple bug, and you just refuse to fix it. This has been an issue for YEARS. Deliberately ruining your app and sucking all the joy out of it is certainly one of the strategies of all time. Great job devs... Let's also straight up remove dark mode, because why not. That's the big change this app needed. Absolutely infuriating

I've been tolerating the gradual removal of free features, but a recent update rolled out the complete removal of practicing to earn hearts, to be replaced with watching ads to regain hearts, allegedly because the practice wasn't great for learning or user retention.
