So instead of improving practice, they've removed YET MORE features to force people to buy Super. I only intend to keep using the app only because of my 2000+ day streak- anyone else, I highly recommend using another app instead. 🥲

Such a very interesting and motivating app for ppl those who like exploring new languages. 😀😀😁 . At first I thought such apps would be so boring but not at all. Hope this helps u. There are many many languages you can learn like English, Japanese, French and many many more. I have never seen such a useful app. Thx Duolingo for giving ppl opportunity to learn new languages and also for ur motivations. Currently I am in my unit 2 chapter 4 ( describing bout ppl in Japanese) Byee!! 😀

used to love this app but they've gotten too greedy for paid subscriptions. you now can't practice to earn hearts and if you don't fully complete a lesson, it doesn't count towards your streak any more whereas it used to. they used to go on about how they took so much pride being a free language learning app but now, yes they have a free version, but realistically, if you make mistakes and manage to complete a lesson a day, it'll take literal decades to complete the course

I decided to learn a new language and this app is helping a lot. Is easy to use and the plus version allows you to study even offline. Perfect for me, since i work in a place with no internet connection. But it doesn't make any distinction from português and Brazilian, which are similar but different. Also some of the phrases that teaches you make no sense and are bit random. One thing I really don't like are the extremely passive aggressive tones of notifications to "motivate" you learning.

i love this app, a very fun way to learn new languages, you don't even get bored. i am enjoying learning German right now! the problem is there are just too many ads. i did three lessons today and literally had to watch an ad after every lesson which was quite annoying. it's a request please spare this fun, learning app atleast. otherwise i'm fully satisfied with this app :)

Learning a language was recommended by my neurologist as a way to keep my brain sharp due to a neurological illness. I didn't think I'd last long but I do it every morning and I love it, Ive been doing it for over 2 years now. If I ever go to Italy I think I could get by quite nicely with what I've learnt. Its great to have the lessons broken down and have the help at the top of screen. I like to see how Im progressing in the tables. Thank you.

The app has nothing to do with effective language learning. This is a competitive game based on repetition of useless language patterns and phrases. Wasteful time killer. Less popular languages are even dangerous to play with as it gives you incorrect or inappropriate phrase versions. You've been warned.

Duolingo is a fun and easy-to-use app for learning languages, especially for beginners. Its gamified features keep users motivated, but the heart system in the free version can be frustrating. While it helps with vocabulary and basic sentences, it may not be enough for fluency. Overall, it's a great tool for casual learning.

I used to like the game but, they changed the way hearts are earned, people who subscribed only had small benefits but now it's literally pay to win. Besides this the game also doesn't run smoothly on my phone and has a lot of bugs which can make it impossible to finish an exercise or claim a reward.

I downloaded Duolingo because I wanted to learn some Mandarin. However, I had to give up because Duolingo kept using different speakers of different sexes as pronunciation guides, and they all seem to be pronouncing the words slightly differently. I need an app that lets me define one speaker, preferably of my own sex, for all the pronunciations so that I have a solid foundation for my own pronunciation improvements.

Edit:My last report has been fixed but there is another major problem with the app. the widget disappears and the widget is the one key reminder that I have to increase my streak and if I wanna add the widget manually, I won't find Duolingo on the list of widget apps please fix this ASAP and this problem began after updating to the recent version

somehow lost my super duo lingo after an update and everytime i try to redeem it, it says "can't connect to google playstore". i have cleared the caches of both apps and still cant even repair this broken app. more people seem to have this issue and there does not appear to be a fix. i am not enjoying being bombarded with ads.

It is a great app to learn languages.And u can learn multiple languages instead of just one and there are loads of rewards like potions which can make u get more XP and there are leaderboards u can compete in.And when u reach a certain streak each time u get super duolingo for like three days. So overall its really good use if u want to learn any language.But the only bad thing is that not every language comes but its alr.

Duolingo is one of the best language-learning apps I've been using. A great feature they have developed for this app is the gamification of the learning materials. However, there are some requests I really deem essential both for the app and for us learners that need to be included to make it more interesting: Please add more games to make the process of language acquisition more enjoyable and less tiresome so we can stay longer on the app to learn more. please add more leagues.

This application is so amazing but it repeat the same thing over and over and sometimes that's quite annoying. But after all this Duolingo is an awesome way to learn new language And I am glad that I had tried Duolingo. I liked it so much. I think it can be much more better on every side. Thank you for wasting your precious time reading my opinion.

very good but some characters don't pronounce connecting words like and or this is in Russian and I often have to slow down to here the connecting words wich I guess is to help in actual conversations but it would be nice if there was an option to have them talk slower for people who can't here very well. overall very nice app would recommend.

Best app if you want to learn or improve your vocabulary on a certain language and it is also free and it has a super tool l that helps you to learn and achieve more XP point of more learning capacity as it has many activities like conversation speaking listening and so on you can also review your mistakes and over all it is a great app for starting at the bottom or just improving your capacity of learning it also have many games and you have to continue a streak or duo will be 😡😡 angry......

One of my favorite apps ever! I've been learning French for the past 7 months with 3 perfect months, very fun and very helpful! I do have a request tho, I'd appreciate it a lot if Duolingo could add a Coptic course for English speakers, I've been wanting to learn Coptic for years but it's been tricky for me, I'd love it if I could finally do it through Duolingo!

l love duolingo so much . lt always is the go to app if you want/ need to learn a language . I love how it has all the features a person could think of . You can even practice to earn hearts without getting super duolingo. The only thing I suggest is that maybe duolingo doesn't need hearts . We can just play without hearts and so we can keep playing infinitely. Thank you.

I love duolingo. I do french classes at school, and it helps remembering stuff for next year. i love the free premium features too, but it isn’t fantastic for quickly learning a language, though. it could use more helpful sentences. Great, accessible tool. love the marketing team

Excellent app to learn. Though it doesn't teaches what we want at that exact time, it is excellent. It uses questions to improve speaking, writing, understanding and hearing other languages. It also sets up daily streak for learning every day which makes children complete and wanting to learn .

The Super version helps with learning. I like the listening practice and the vocab practice. The free version is frustrating as you are allowed no more than 5 mistakes, then a long wait to reset, so not enough practice to learn a difficult language. It's a shame they got rid of the grammar tips they used to have. Now you really need a book as well for reference, or a person who can explain the rules a bit. Otherwise it's frustrating trying to guess why an answer is marked wrong.

good app...great learning expericne...it offers you Duolingo premium every now and then...the leaderboard makes it even more engaging...try to introduce more leagues in the app...and maybe introduce a feature of chatting with other people of same course...great app... scientifically designed methods...best for learning a language...do give it a try ..all the best on your learning journey 🫡😊

Overall the app is great. But of course with learning any new language, other resources are needed (podcasts/movies/books etc). There is a bug where it sometimes won't pick up what you're speaking. It's definitely a bug because it won't even pick up my native French wife's speaking. I would love to see more stats and be able to categorise the words you've learnt by verb, adjective, noun etc. I would also love to see more explanations/theory rather than just game-like exercises.

It's simple to use. The way that the lessons are structured also amazing. Yet I'm a bit disappointed with one of the learning method in which we need to write down the exact translation. For example in Indonesia: "Saya ingin mencari petunjuk arahnya" apparently need to be translated to "I want to look the directions up" which doesn't make any sense. "I want to look for the direction" is just fine yet the system sent an error mark.

Really liked the app, even decided to pay a subscription and used it happily for 4 months. Today, I found out that Duo skipped 8 (!!) units in my German course, and there is no way of redoing those normally, only reviewing. Now, all my progress is messed up, and I am left with a huge gap in my knowledge. As a result, I had to delete the course and start all over again because the course has also changed, and units I learned before are all different. This is very demotivating.

It is a very useful and fun way to learn a language for many reasons: for business, for travel and even for fun! However, without either a Duolingo plan (costs money) or some other form of spending, it is difficult to learn a lot of a language quickly if you are going to the another country in a short amount of time. I really recommend getting the app because personally, it has been helping with my Spanish fluency a lot for my upcoming visit to Barcelona.

It's fun to use for the first ten minutes. Then ads appear and they stick around. Could not get back to the app even after waiting ten minutes. I purchased "Hearts" from the app, but that didn't help the issue. The style of learning a language is not well organized. Verb conjugation is never explained. You are just told that you got it "wrong"...how can you learn from that?

I love the app in general, I think it's really good. However, the 1 ad takes longer to watch than completing 1 lesson, and it happens after every single lesson. It basically encourages you to pay for premium otherwise you just get demotivated by such long ads. Please do something about it, either reduce the amount of ads or reduce the time of these ads.

It's a good app and quite helpful. If you already know the language or wish to perfect it, this is a great tool. It's a convenient tool to expand your vocabulary. I didn't give it 5 stars cause it seems to have a microphone issue lately, at least to me. It doesn't detect my microphone when I speak and immediately tells me I said things wrong. It's a shame cause practising orally is essential in learning a language.

Lessons rely solely on context clues, many of which have multiple correct or plausible answers. Full of ads. If you want us to pay, just put a price tag on it. I also hate the streak system because it destroys your motivation to learn. I used to spend hours a few days a week learning lessons, but now I just do the one lesson a day to keep my streak. It just gets annoying, and I'm not motivated to take that initiative in my leisure anymore.

Really great app... Except for the fact they recently lost their mission to make it free and accessible for everyone. After every single excercise (which lasts about 2 minutes) you get 2 ads, both lasting about 30 seconds each... great way to prioritize learning! Worst thing is the second is always the unskipabble add for super duolingo. I'm fine with a normal ad, but stop being greedy please and stop pestering people into paying 15 euros a month for the version without the super duolingo adds.

Great app for practicing languages, thank you!🧠 Would like to mention something that could be improved: In the music course when you need to tap the rhythm (especially double notes), it would be so much better if there were two buttons, because sometimes you can't catch the length of the note and rhythm at the same time using only one button

It gets you learning. I wish I could turn off streaks though, it drives me nuts. I am more likely to do a long session every few days, rather than a little every day. But when you do that, the app bombards you with messages about losing your streak, which is very counter motivational. I know how Duo would respond, but I know quite a few people with a 200 plus day streak, because they do one minimum lesson a day. They can't functionally use the language they are studying though!

There's a technical problem with the App. (Spanish from German). I was a beginner when I started learning Spanish. First I was placed too high in the learning path abd had to learn grammar that belongs to a much higher level (B1-B2). I did levels 1-3 and suddenly was thrown back in a different level 3, which was more appropriate for beginners. I did this level too 2 months ago and I can't proceed to level 4. I wrote Duo 3 times, but no one cared to solve the issue.I am totally disappointed.

The app is really good overall. It is interactive, fun, engaging and game-like, as the advertisement once stated. I recommend that the Korean course is reviewed, as it seems a bit less visual compared to the courses in other languages. Another suggestion I have is for the app to maybe include some more languages, like widely spoken West African languages. Aside from these, the app is one of the best when it comes to learning new languages.

This app use to be useful. But suddenly it thinks it's my first time using it, lost all my progress for some reason. Then to top it off, it doesn't hear me verbalise the tasks it asks, I have a brand new phone that works perfectly for everything else, but anytime I try to repeat the sound for words/sentences it always cannot hear me. I'm constantly set back due to how the app works now. Before, you could choose skip verbal until you wanted to. But now it's mixed. So you must pay to avoid this !

I have been using Duolingo for almost 10 years. As a user, the experience is getting worse and worse. It is ok to sell extra features to help the study experience but it is not ok to put previously standard features behind a paywall. (ex. unlimited hearts) The push towards the payed version is getting stronger. The ads are long and super disruptive during a study session. Other pay to learn language apps are better. This one is just the most famous one but it is falling behind.

Tasks where you need to construct a sentence out of words is bugged. Half of the times it refuses to insert any word (its font changes colour to red). The only fix is going to main screen and then back in the app. It makes time-restricted tasks nearly impossible to complete. Extremely annoying. Everything else is fine, but I'm a perfectionist. Inability to complete all tasks drives me crazy.

Duolingo is a fun and interactive language-learning app. With its gamified approach, learning feels more enjoyable. It offers a variety of languages and exercises, including reading, writing, listening, and speaking. However, grammar explanations are limited, and some features require an internet connection. It's great for beginners who want to learn in a casual way!

It's a good app to learn languages. But it does have bugs. The most irritating bug is when you loose gems on missing a streak. And it's not possible to contact support even with a paid subscription. Duolingo should provide some avenue to report issues, at least for subscriptions. After the launch of Duolingo Max, the explanations are also very limited, so the app is not as good as it used to do earlier.

The free version is basically useless. There's an ad break after every lesson (Lesson. Not chapter) and wait for it... it gets worse. After the ad, there's another ad begging you to get the premium version of the app. Basically, 1 lesson... 2 ads. Every. Damn. Time. The ads take longer than the lesson! I know ads are a way of monetization, but this is too much

There needs to be a warning in the app about jumping ahead in the course because if you do this, then you will lose access to the tutorial material from the earlier sections, you'll only be able to practice them, for which the content does not give you the assistance that the tutorial material does. Aside from this one thing, this is a great app. The way out is to delete the course, re-add it, and then skip forward to where you were, but accumulated XP for this language will be lost.

I love Duolingo, really. Its a well thought out app and it works. I don't mind the ads in the free version. But over the past few months, there have been ads that you cannot exit out of! That is absolutely not ok. I close an ad and there's another one running in the background and then I have to quit the app to escape it?! Unacceptable for this caliber of app.

I've been using this for some time and can get past a few annoying glitches from time to time, but this last update removed the option (at least on Android) to turn on dark mode. I checked each setting, rebooted the app and took to the web to no avail. Now the screen is so bright it's annoying and makes me not want to use it. Especially when I'm in a dim lit room and now my screen is illuminating everything. If I've missed something, please let me know where to find the toggle.

I really enjoy the app, it has a really easy way to progressively learn new things. But it is sad to see the greedy way the company is taking... Constantly pushing the paid version down your throat, is super hard to replenish your hearts now so you can keep learning. It's a couple of small things but it really is starting to take away from the experience

We enjoy duolingo app, especially now that my son has started learning how to play music on the app. But the music has changed. My son enjoys the piano music and the synthetic keyboard updated version is very distracting and does not help the player to follow the tune. He struggles to focus on the notes and it is playing faster than what it was before. Please can a option be made where one can choose the plain keyboard accompaniment. Thanks you.

1. It would be helpful to have some actual grammar and pronounciation teaching instead of leaving us to figure them out by the vocabulary presented. 2. Tips are occasionally shown but are on the screen so briefly I usually miss them. 3. Otherwise, I am really enjoying learning a new language via Duolingo. The lessons are fun and mistakes are reviewed at the end of each lesson. 4. For the best experience I would advise upgrading to paid so you can review mistakes and vocabulary.

Recently the reliability of doublers has been really bad. When you make the effort to send one to a friend or do the tasks to turn one on, it's really demotivating when it breaks. XP will also just disappear or not get counted in the first place. It's especially bad when it happens on a Sunday and it gets you demoted. Would really like to see developers work on making this more reliable.

The overall app is good and its user friendly interface makes it engaging. But the major cons are :- 1. Lessons feel way too repitative which is unnecessary. It's been a long time since I see no progress in my learning due to this. This needs to be fixed anyhow. 2. Exact meaning of each and every word is not given I don't think I'm going to use this app anymore bcs I see no progress at all due to repitative lessons and it is boring.

It's a very accessible, well put together app. Two main recommendations: 1. Translating sentences into your own language is somewhat useful. However, would it be perhaps more prudent to get the learner thinking in the foreign language more often? This isn't as easy, but it provides a learning curve which may be more beneficial in the long-term? I may be wrong on this one. It will be interesting to see what research says on the matter. 2. There's no explanation of the grammar. Word limit :(....

I downloaded this app and took the free trial for a month and paid for the yearly subscription, When it was the end of the trial month i had paid for it with no warning email sent to me, this was upsetting as i was planning on using the money for my weeks groceries. I emailed them with everyone telling me it was a waste of time, but still did and was quickly refunded and had my subscription cancelled. Great customer support.

Entertaning and fun app. Quite addictive. It's mainly a game and hardly a way to lear a language, though. I'm doing the Japanese course. There is no theory at all, and sometimes I think it would be very useful. Lessons are quite repetitive too. Especially kanji lessons are always about reviewing the same few kanjis over and over to a point where it becomes too boring. Furthermore, kanji lessons are full of errors and seem to be getting worse with updates.

I really like the app and use it daily - although progression can feel slow, I personally like the repetition for the most part. Unfortunately the ads recently have been getting more egregious, and I understand that's the cost of a free version, but when Duolingo's own ad for it's premium version openly mocks the quality of the other ads the app shows, that leave a bit of a bad taste

Update: There is now a dedicated section to practice learned words with the frequency and success rate accounted for. Nice one Duolingo! My main gripe now is ads for 'MAX' despite paying for 'Super' already. 2019: Not without its issues but super fun and addictive. I wish there was a list of words learned available in each section. sometimes the random generator will end up giving you the same phrase 5 times in a row and that tricky one you want to learn you never see again.

Duolingo has helped me learn French very well. It makes me excited to learn more. I would give it five stars, but there are some downfalls. Firstly, the ads. Its not that big of a deal, but they are annoying. Secondly, the whole hearts thing makes you feel pressured and when you lose them all, its really discouraging. Otherwise, I do recommend using duolingo.

The app is serviceable but extremely buggy, both in terms of learning (pronunciations are accepted as long as you make similar sounds without saying a word and any stroke will be corrected regardless of whether you learned the correct character as long as it's in the right order so you really don't learn properly from it) and their reward system (which fails to retain data when you refresh the app). As best as could be expected from AI learning, good for practice but don't pay for it.

Uninstalled it, now that it's no longer dark theme. Loved the idea of learning how to read musical notes but the amount of notes and speed of playing increases way too quickly. It's just frustrating. The threats imposed by the owl are disturbing. I thought this app was supposed to be fun? What even are the pictures at the end of a lesson? They are nothing short of creepy. Guess that fits their strange image. There are way better learning apps out there.

The app is really good, interactive and help us to learn English in a light way. The app makes any translations incorrectly for Portuguese, like: coffee shop, in Portuguese is not café, it is Cafeteria, and have others words that can be better translated. So other problem that I have is with speak exercises, a lot of time I speak as the app but it don't recognize it. And the last one is about progress, as we progress the app needs to introduce more words in English on the bottons. Thank you ☺️

Humbly I must say that it's not the beat choice for learning a language. Old text book style is even better. However it is BRILLIANT to expose people to something. Opening a book can be overwhelming, duo gamifies learning which is always great. It also help you understand the basics of how hard a language is / how to speak or write it so that you can decide whether or not it's for you. Personally don't think you can perfect a language from it as someone who's living abroad and learning with duo

One can always complain, but this app is golden. I have learned the basics for communication in other languages with it, and although it can always be better, it is understandable how good it is for something we don't have to pay for. I have two requests though: 1. Please eliminate the "no errors challenges", they are very frustrating and do not encourage participation, on the contrary, it makes people abandon the friend challenges. 2. Please bring back the "practice to earn lives"🙏🏻

I've been using this app to learn Hindi for a week, even without the Super Duolingo mode, and I absolutely love it! I highly recommend it to all beginners—the learning method is simple and effective, the graphics are engaging, and the reminders are helpful without being annoying. The overall experience makes language learning enjoyable. Looking forward to completing a full course!

It's good tool to learn languages. It can be fun but its shotcomings are sometimes very frustrating. Voice recognition is still weak. Also it teaches some useless stuff and it's missing proper structure. But I guess with leagues and achievements, they try to make it more a game than serious learning tool. It also cheats you out of XP multipliers if you seem too good or gaining too much. Multipliers time out quicker than advertised or they are never applied at all.

A fantastic app, an easy way to learn a language. I love all the different characters, they are very funny. You can practise the words or phrases you struggle with, there is no limit on the number of times you need to hear something repeated, plus it has a slow down button which really helps when the phrases are longer or a bit tricky. If you want to learn a language stress free then this is the app for you, I absolutely love it .

Duolingo is engaging and fun app, but some changes are frustrating. They removed the ability to earn back all hearts through practice—now it's just one, and the wait time to get it back is longer, pushing users toward an overpriced membership. There's also no real grammar learning, which feels like a missed opportunity. Plus, the notifications are borderline gaslighting, making you feel guilty for not playing. While it's fun for vocabulary, these issues make it less enjoyable.

I had this app back in 2019, when it was actually helpful for learning a language. I rejoined in 2023, and since then it's been getting steadily worse and is now unusable. They keep trying to force you to buy Super Duolingo by making the free version increasingly rubbish. It's filled with loud, annoying ads, it offers no explanations for errors unless you upgrade (therefore not allowing you to learn), and no longer lets you practice to earn hearts. I'm out.

App has continuously gotten worse over time by making free features paid, adding AI where it is unnecessary (such as to explaining the meaning of a sentence, which should be a basic feature and not AI) and having a very limited free experience. They recently changed it so that you can not practice to earn hearts, but instead you have to watch an ad. Importantly, even when Duolingo was much better, there are far better ways to learn languages. Only use Duolingo if you find it fun.

The learning is mostly slow. Many repetitions support memorizing on the one hand but can also be a drag. Some meanings are only contextual so it is almost impossible to actually grasp the core meaning of some words for which it is not obvious. Explanations are fewand they are "hidden" behind notebook icons for each section. The friend features aren't fleshed out. If don't mind competing with random people, having friends use the app too makes little difference. Overall 3/5

Great quality app and animations. The bad: the amount of repetition is mind-numbing and kills my spirit. I learned the Japanese alphabet from another app and it took me a month, and I enjoyed it... I'm in my fourth month now in Duolingo and I'm about midway with the alphabet. And I hate it. I do one lesson a day just to keep my steak. The app asks me to answer the same questions way too many times. If there was a setting to reduce the amount of repetition, I'd do it by 50%.

Welsh course seemed great at first but don't don't waste your time. After spending over 18 months with this it decided to switch to a "daily refresh" which just repeats the same 20 words or so for ever. Seems like there's nothing left in the course, but there's no indication that the course is over. It has just "broken" but keeps prompting you to come back anyway. After talking to first language speakers I have realised that it inappropriately mixes dialects without any explanation.

Bought the paid version, it's ok. Some serious inaccuracies and inconsistencies with the material. It has encouraged my otherwise very reluctant offspring to take a consistent interest in languages. The "words" section has been stuck on a very small selection of words. In Spanish I find the stories somewhat infuriating, it relies on reading between the lines rather than actual language skills, not good for autistic learners. The pointless new animations are annoying and distracting

I would give a higher review, but the most recent update is destroying my joy for this app. Ive been using Duolingo consistently for almost a full year, but recently I felt a big change. For one, dark mode just doesn't work anymore (probably something that they will patch), but the biggest thing is the ads. You would usually get one ad for the premium version of Duolingo a day (after the first lesson you do). But the new update shows an ad for Every. Single. Lesson. (So an ad every 2 minutes)

Love this app, but I find one of the recent changes irritating. The "5 in a row" or "10 in a row" pop-up in the middle of my lessons is super distracting. Please remove it!!! Other than that, I'm loving the app. It's absolutely usable even in the free version. Yes, there are ads and a heart limit, but honestly 5 hearts is all you need. I love the new lessons where it's a listening activity with a 'guest speaker' discussing their day. I do wish the friends quests were easier to complete though!

I personally benefited a lot from Duolingo for German, but many people find it repetitive and not functional, I think one should be attentive and careful with the tasks, so that you don't just do "correct" and "proceed" , rather you pay real attention to every word, how it is written etc and learn actively from it. then you can benefit good from Duolingo

After using it for more than several years with 589-day streak, I want to say that this app is useless. It does not teach you a language - its gamified model tries to keep you involved and answer as fast as you can. While running in pursuit of these achievements, I forgot to learn the language. Finally, I decided to completely uninstall it, remove my account, and get back to the classical, time-proven method, which gives better results. Using it with traditional learning did not work either.

I love playing & learning using duolingo! It's been 2 years since then. Really good app. But lately the ads is annoying me. While having ads is good in free version but 2 ads everytime I finish a lesson? That's a waste of time of waiting to get to the next lesson. If possible, please reduce the ads portion time. Like every 5 lessons or so. Thank you!

It's amazing! I am learning Korean and German right now. it is so simple and easy to use. plans are offered but not needed to learn, and there are little ads. There are also streaks and goals that motivate you to learn at least once a day, and my progress is amazing. You can also skip too different categories if you are visiting a country and need basics. 5 Star app, great experiences, and never-ending opportunities. 10/10 would 100% recommend for anyone wanting to try to learn a new language.♡

I think Duolingo is a great app for learning the basics, but it could really improve by adding more real-life English phrases. Many of the sentences feel unnatural or too formal, which isn’t how native speakers talk. It would be awesome if they added common phrases, slang, and idioms to help learners sound more natural. Another feature I’d love to see is an online chatting option, where users can practice with real people. It would make the learning experience more interactive and practical.

This used to be one of my favourite apps of all time. Faultless. The courses were brilliant and fun, and it was easy and compelling to use. Well, not any more. The ads, the heart system, and the forced new layout (the old learning tree was perfect!?) Have ruined the experience of using it. I know you have to make money, but gee, wasn't selling user data and an optional premium service enough? I held on with the duolingo schools loophole, but now you've ended that too. I will miss you Duo 😔

I love Duolingo, except for one thing. I find it extremely discouraging that, if you are having a bad day and use all of your lives before passing a lesson, it doesn't count towards your streak. Yes, I know you can practice to earn 1 heart back (and if you don't pay it takes forever for tour lives to refill), and in that practice, you can make many mistakes until you finish, but by then, you're so frustrated you only finish that one lesson for the day. Failed practice should still count!!

For Japanese is very far away from being more than 2 starts. Pros: Excellent for vocabulary. Hence the 2 stars and not 0 Cons: Pronunciation isn't of any use at all so you can't trust it at all. The audios with the characters voices are often incomprehensible. It reads the kanjis sometimes with a different reading and doesn't take into account context. Most translations of sentences , yes MOST, are unnatural or/and Google translated it seems...

Good for helping beginners, but there are way too many ads, and they push the streaks so hard. I understand the ads, but I wish they would limit how often they show and being able to quickly skip them. I really wish I could have the option to turn off features, like streaks, as I don't need the extrinsic motivations. I know it is beneficial for some others, so I'd rather it be opt-out.

For some reason I haven't been been able to do the speaking exercises for a few months now. The app doesn't register when I'm speaking and the recording just shuts off a few seconds in. I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app but that didn't help. Other than skipping speaking exercises, not sure what to do. (I have a Samsung phone).

They implement a "pay-to-win" strategy on their minigames which I'm totally against, it's just a ploy to get you to buy more in-game currency. There is not enough variation in the lessons, I've been on section 2 of Japanese for ages because I'm so bored of it. There's almost 40 sections! It's as if it tries to keep you there for as long as possible so they can can get another year's subscription out of you. I'm only learning new words, hardly ever learning new grammar.

Been using this app daily (paid subscription) learning French for 3 years. Last week the app lost my position in the course, and moved me to the very last lesson making it impossible to continue learning. Their support people are completely unresponsive - you get an automated brush-off email, but no assistance whatsoever. They are clearly not interested in helping their users. Summary: a good app, until it goes wrong, at which point you're on your own 🙁

It's fine for absolute beginners; for those who have little to no time; for those who just want to maintain some contact with a language. Don't expect Duo to teach you any language on its own - it simply can't. It's far too slow (feel free to skip units when able), and the endless repetitions can drive one crazy! It's English, Spanish and other major languages are ok. If you're trying to learn a less popular one, the AI generated sentences are really weird. I mean, seriously, PINK COFFEE?

I've been using this app for nearly 10 yrs, but it is time to give it up. It is clear Duolingo is spending more money on marketing, than on developing its language courses. And no, using AI does not count. It has been dumbed down a lot in the past years, and focuses on the competitive factor rather than on teaching you anything substantial. There are no grammar or sentence structure explanations, so you kinda just have to guess what is right

Duolingo is a great app. Very useful and efficient. However, there are some bugs. Pls check Mathematics Section 2 Unit 9 Match Madness. Eg: Say 2 is the correct answer; there are multiple options stating 2; now it keeps giving me incorrect until I reach the option (answer 2) that is paired up with the question of interest. Pls look into this. Thank you.

This review is for the Danish language. The Danish courses could really learn from the German courses. German uses animations that make it more engaging, the interface feels more polished, there are stories and hearing comprehension lessons, and they use real voices instead of Google translate. Danish sounds just like Google translate, and the words are often cut off. It talks way too quickly too, so you can't really hear the pronunciation well.

IMHO, the app is great. After a year of studies, I can tell that a lot of work has been invested in it. Texts are well thought, stories are funny, the characters are cute, and their voices (in English lessons) match them perfectly. A major drawback for me is that there are very few rules. I believe we, human beings, aren't an AI, and we need both rules and examples. Otherwise the efficacy becomes suboptimal. It would be nice to have a scrollbar in past lessons to easily find an excercise, too.

I gave in and bought 'Super' and actually I get good value from it. Not limited by hearts or ads I get hours of sessions. I would say, having started Italian from scratch and re-acquainting myself with French (which I previously studied for over a decade); it is not a great way to learn from scratch, there is not enough explanation of grammar and vocabulary. As an additional resource there is none better. The gamifying and competitive elements really encourage repeat interaction.

I paid for super a year upfront and had found it pretty helpful and fun, but recently they've taken away some of the functionality from the super subscription e.g. explaing your mistakes (kind of crucial for learning!) and locked it behind an extra AI subscription that costs even more. From the other recent reviews it seems like they've done a similar thing to the free version, more and more ads. I'll be deleting the app at the end of subscription period.

This language learning app is the greatest I have ever seen. One major problem is that although it will show you what the price for super is per month but only paying for a year is available. This could prevent many people from purchasing, including myself. Please add a monthly subscription option for family plans etc. and I will jump on that immediately. Thank you.

Great app for learning overall. I love the games and radio lessons. More of these please!! However, disappointed that only some languages seem to offer these (hoping that changes). I could also do without so many notifications. I could turn them off but I don't want NO notifications- just one or two a day would be enough of a reminder. I also have the widget so maybe take that into account. In my notification history I have 8 from duolingo in the past 24 hours reminding me to learn.

It is great as a basic introduction to a language, but quickly does everything in its power to get you to go premium. If you get stuck on a particular phrase or grammar, you may use all your hearts within the same lesson, and be forced to use the freemium currency just to finish one lesson you struggled with. The app also does a horrible job of introducing grammar and throws you into challenges that you've never done before so you'll fail, just to pressure you into the premium model.

The app itself is fun, but I'm not sure how efficient it is for learning a new languge. Mango Languages app is a great alternative to Duo and I got free access to it through my public library. There's also an ad after every lesson now. Each lesson is only about 1-2 minutes, so it's a lot of ads, and really slows down my progress and motivation. The ads are now long videos with sound. Uses too much data.

I updated the review, because I consider that the app has changed a lot, and for the worse. The content is good, but if you're not paying, get ready for the slowest performance ever, sometimes a simple lesson takes me excessive amounts of time, because the app makes my keyboard disappear. Just so you know, I only have issues with this app. Also, get ready for a million Temu ads...they are ridiculous and/or super loud (even shrill), and when not, well you're being advertised Temu products ?

Unfortunately, I had to change my 5 stars rating from 2023, to a 3 stars rating for 2024-2025. There were some major changes to the app, that almost make me want to quit the app, altogether. I love learning languages through the app but all the changes make it really hard. There were used to have 15mn booster at the end of each chapter. Now, if we lose our 5 hearts, we can only get one back, instead of 5. RIDICULOUS!!! Please correct this & go back to the previous version.

The lessons are useful, which is why I have purchased the app. The constant ads for family plan are distressing as I have no family. The constant insistence I commit to a learning streak is infuriating and demotivating. It is not healthy, ime, to do anything 7 days a week, without respite. I am learning through choice, no-one has forced me to do this. Nagging is demoralising for me, NOT encouraging or supportive. At least provide an option to turn this off!

I have used it for 3.5 months almost everyday. There is no grammar explanation even on a paid version. You pay for the "super" and they ask you to pay more to get explanation on your mistake. A very passive style of learning that does not help with speaking or understanding grammar but just repeating a lot of simple conversations. It's a great gaming style of getting acquainted to a new language but the actual learning is minimal. Switching to Languatalk.

loads of ads. over the year they really increased the amount of them. Recently, the biggest annoying bug is uneven sound levels, from super quiet to extra loud, in the exercises in which you are unable to report bugs. it became so annoying, I'm looking to switch over. It's completely unacceptable to have such technical issues in a product like this

What has happened to Duolingo? I no longer have unlimited hearts, the layout has changed and not in a good way. Almost impossible to compete now in the leaderboards. Ads after every lesson which cut into your time bonuses. I understand the need to make money but this is not the way to go. Having read the reviews to pay for the premium Duolingo I definitely will not bother. I have been playing for almost 2 years but am now looking for another app. 😔

The success criteria to meet daily chance is to do the ramp up challenge. I never do these as with my slow reading speed (dyslexia) I find it too hard to scan words to match. So I have to buy the very expensive "row blaster" to make it easier, but it still is too hard for me. This means I can't complete the daily quest, can't get the bonus multiplier for the next day, and so staying in my league harder. I am a max purchaser, the ramp is a greedy money grab taking advantage of my disability.

Predatory, uses really bad methodology to teach you when you're not a premium user. You can easily get stuck on a chapter with no easy way to learn from your mistake because, again, every basic feature is stuck behind a paywall. Once you've learned enough to get started the app is a set back on many aspects killing the fun of learning a new language. First few weeks/months don't suffer much from these issues as the content is generally easier, enjoy it while it lasts

I'm about to complete a year using the app everyday and meanwhile I cannot deny that it has helped me, it has a lot of problems and room to improvement. 1. If you don't pay you're gonna last forever to complete one single course. 2. Its learning process is slow and repetitive because it wants you to keep using the app forever. If you learn quickly you'll let the app sooner. 3. It has a lack of grammar lessons. The app is over-focused on vocabulary and you learn almost nothing of grammar.

It's great fun and you can chose any language. They rarely put ads in and everything is so easy to find. The issue with this is when it say 'Free trial for super duolingo' you have to put your details in but it's free? Why do we need to put that in? What if I forgot my password or something like that? Plus it says free ot doesn't charge anything so why do I have to put my details in? All in All. Great game.

While this is a usefull resource to learn new languages with. It's super restrictive unless you pay for Duo plus. And if you do TOO well, it will either change the question, or the answer you've input to force an arbitrary failure as you press the confirm button. Which I've seen happen on multiple occassions now! And to top it off, the English translation answers will have grammatical errors noticebly often in addition to needing to know American culture to understand! Like what's a sophomore?!

Once upon a time Duolingo was a 5-star app. I paid for it for years and it was a great way to keep engaging with the language. Unfortunately over that time, despite a few interesting features that were added, so much of the best stuff has been taken away. Most egregious is the rampant push of "AI"-powered content, which is thoroughly unreliable and - at worst - they make you pay even more for. I miss what we used to have, Duo. You're techno-capitalist garbage now. RIP.

Helps out with practicing and not forgetting the language, but I wouldn't rely solely on it. It needs to improve the quality for Japanese language too. The Japanese lessons seem to be behind all other languages. Despite having many lessons, most only cover N5, which is the bare basics. Also, but very importantly, customer support is terrible! There's no email to directly contact them despite being a paid customer!

This is truly excellent for me, I subscribed for brain training. But then I got into Japanese and since this is a particularly challenging language, I'd definitely recommend adding a section where you can find and practice ALL the words and phrases you've already been taught, not just a tiny fraction of them. You do tend to forget the older lessons and it's a shame that you can't revise more comprehensively.

Nice in the beginning. However, I find the topics they teach silly (my opinion). When learning a language, you want to be able to get your point across. Learning the names of wild animals should be a choice, not a must to progress. Learning to greet, find your way, buy food, etc. is fine to have to learn to progress. Also, an explanation on how/why grammar/words change when using singular/plural etc. would be nice, instead of just having to learn it because it's what they showing you. Too basic.

Free user here. Was better when you could practice for hearts to get as many as needed. Now it's only for one when you completely run out, so lessons are at a stop unless you pay or wait hours/day for it to refill (which is inconvenient and makes your motivation die). Also, goal posts keep moving. Only get Duolingo if you can pay. Otherwise, it's not worth your effort as things for free users keep getting worse + It's impossible to compete in leagues with paying users that have unlimited hearts.

Duolingo music keeps lagging on every phone I had. I can't learn any songs on normal tempo because the metronome either stays behind, or too far. Then there is the input lag on the notes, that begins with each start of a measure, resulting in many mistakes in return from the learner, Wich could he avoided if they can fix it. I don't know what to do, and I don't find any fixes online...

Moved to Max version which is more expensive but it is very unsteady. Most times the video calls with Lily freeze or I cannot hear what she is saying. It is not good quality. There are many other bugs it seems, where the app freezes, especially when it is trying to explain your mistake or answer and also when it's checking the summary of the story that you write. I would expect better. Other than that I am using it a lot and learning quite a bit!

This is a great app for learning, but my main problem with this.. Is the VOICE parts of language lessons, because it doesn't pick up any of my voice AT ALL, no matter how loud or close I am, it's always gonna take a dozen tries before it works. I just wonder if you could replace it with something more capable of functioning properly its role as to PICK UP YOUR VOICE.

Great app for learning 👌 I've been learning Russian for months to communicate with most of my family and understand them. Sometimes, they teach me some words, but when they leave, I always use this app. The reason I did a 4-star review is that I didn't really like the new update. I loved the old duolingo with stories and some characters moved. But now nothing moves. I was hoping to find the stories somewhere but it was gone. Please bring it back

At the end of some lessons, when youve got everything right, it challenges you with harder questions, usually three questions which can either be typed or spoken. I find these ones frustrating as they are usually the same question just worded differently and are hard to get right which then messes up your score. Why not just give one question rather than three that are similar that make you feel punished for doing well.

Great interface, luv the new radio stations and video games in the trail. Also new Music and Maths courses. Great! CONS: Only 5 hearts in the App for mistakes! Not enough when you don't know a language or music. App has too many repetitive phrases at times back to back. Chests open 12 hours later when your likely to be going to bed or middle of night. XP count always wrong. UPDATE Fri 2nd Sept 2022 The desktop version has been screwed by the new 'changes' by someone who doesnt use it!

Great app, doesn't just only help learn languages easily, but helps my attention with everything. It seems to make it easier to learn multiple languages, probably because of the cognitive stuff. Only downside is the amount of ads. I don't pay for premium and after pretty much every lesson it gives me an ad, usually temp and then a duolingo premium ad

My experience was fantastic. Learning in the Duolingo app is actually very nice and worth the time spending. Although like other apps, it needs some adjustments. Some words like 'Ella' in Spanish are sometimes pronounced differently. Ads arent really a problem, they are pretty short and can be cancelled in just a few seconds or are optional for hearts. Over all, I'd absolutely recommend it.

"I'm blown away by DuolingoThis app helped me improve my calculation skills and learn a new language in just 5-10 minutes a day. Engaging, interactive, and fun - Duolingo 's got it all! I've seen significant progress and I'm hooked! Kudos to the Duolingo team for creating an exceptional learning platform! Highly recommended!"

Great way to learn a new language for free or just spend downtime productively. I wish some of the stories came sooner for the Japanese course, and maybe a lite version of the talking with AI in the language you're learning existed in the free with ads option. The exercises where you listen to a conversation and pick out the topics being discussed are nice but I wish there were more of them to mix things up a bit more. Otherwise, an awesome app.

Since the new changes on Duolingo is has been a terrible experience! The algorithm of being in leagues is so messed up that no matter how many lessons you do, you are always put in the demotion zone. I have been using this app for years and this is the first time I am so annoyed at it. If this continues I will not be renewing my subscription ever again!! Very disappointed!

I enjoy this ap a lot, most of the time. I don't mind that you have to pay to get more hearts or that there are ads, that's to be expected with any free ap. But suddenly the ads don't have a close button no matter how long I wait. Nothing to do but quit the ap. And there goes the lesson - no credit, have to do it again, and no hearts refunded. This is a pretty awful bug :-(

This app is full of some of the most aggressive advertising I have ever seen. Back in the day it was actually about helping you learn a language, you had choices and there was a community. Today it's a gamified AI mess with the sole purpose of making money, pushing subscription on you left and right so the free tier is barely usable. And when it comes to subscription based apps, there are far more mature and effective options out there. Before going super duolingo, make sure you check those out.

Was great, mostly free language app, now too many ads, (some really long) they have eroded away key features unless you pay their subscription. It's no longer a level playing field. You can no longer practice to earn all the stars. When you are empty, you can get one star only. I've been on here for years, but I'm close to uninstalling it. Also, when you complete a lesson, you have to watch the ad, but also go through all the button stages or it doesn't count your score. Next day it

while I do enjoy the app, the ads are a killer, and the 5 heart system is really harsh. I would be tempted if the monthly prices were a bit cheaper or even as advertised. However, they do the classic is 4.99 a month, or 7.99 family plan... until you click to subscribe and find out they want the full year up front! other wise it's 9.99 a month... kinda ridiculous to be honest. Still a good app though in smaller doses. But I'm not paying £89.99 a year.

I chose Irish. I have some basic irish from school. The app is frustrating at times. The regional accent and dialect used for Irish is difficult to follow. The audio is not great, beginning/end of some words dropped. Mis-spelling is accepted for some words but not for others... why? Might try a different language and see how that works out. And maybe a different app for Irish.

Wow, how this app has changed. Not useful, advanced lessons only focus on a handful of words that repeat in the exact same sentences each week. The app's focus is on gimmicky animations, trying to stay relevant with kids, and trying to scam you into getting a paid subscription that doesn't actually add any value. This could be helpful for a short period of time if you just want something -- anything -- to jump start your language learning. But you're better off getting a specalised app.

The competition style learning does have its benefits as it pushes you to stay on top of the leaderboard, but the fact that you're dependant on hearts pisses me off lmao. Love the streaks, but kinda lame that you could be spending 10 minutes trying to finish 1 three-minute-lesson bc you have to watch videos to get hearts. So keep in mind that this is still a game that, regardless of whether or not it helps you with learning a language, has a PTW system.

I did say "A very good app, I really feel like I'm learning, it's also like a game, which helps" , I've got a long way in and they are giving me Czech sayings, but rather than translate the words, they translate the meaning, which is so stupid, I can work out the meaning of a saying, what I'm trying to learn is grammar and vocabulary. Also, after 3 years of using Duolingo every day, I can barely form a sentence. Typically arrogant app development that never listens to users.

I love the learning, it's improved a lot over the last decade BUT s this is a huge BUT it seems extremely difficult to manage a subscription!!!! They tried to auto renew, but I have a different cc number, so they couldn't. I tried really hard to subscribe to a single but was unable to. Now they've renewed my family subscription WHICH I DON'T WANT!!! REALLY REALLY REALLY tempted to switch to another platform at this point.

It's fine, but the free version is just too tedious at this point. And there's no way to practice for hearts anymore? You can only watch ads for a heart now. I'm sick of Temu ads. The paid version removes them and you have unlimited hearts but like... the paid version ain't really worth the money. If I'm paying I'd rather pay for an actual course or program. Anyway yeah ads have ruined it completely and I'm uninstalling it. I'd only recommend this if you just want an easy start to learning.

Dear Duolingo Team, I just wanted to take a moment to express how much I absolutely love this app! I’ve recently started learning Korean, and it’s been such a fun and creative journey. The app makes learning feel effortless and enjoyable, and I find myself eagerly looking forward to each lesson. The design is intuitive, and the bite-sized lessons fit perfectly into my busy schedule, making it easy to stay on track. The gamified approach keeps me motivated, and I can see progress

I paid for duolingo plus because of the extra features. I paid for a full year. Now they have a higher level "dulingo super" and all the things I paid for are behind that pay wall. Don't waste your money on this app. They'll just make a third paywall later. I want my money back. What I bought doesn't exist. It was false advertising. If I could give zero stars, I would.

Overall the app can improve because they don't teach you the words and they expect you to know what it means. I find it very hard to try and learn a language if I have to guess to get the word to get it correct. The good things are that it improves every part of a language like speaking, listening and others. In the game I would rate the game 3/5 stars but there is definitely room for improvement.

It is an enjoyable app to use, but to be honest I don't feel like I really make that much useful progress with it. It absolutely needs to be paired with other forms of learning which, after a short while, become much better tools for learning and make this experience feel somewhat redundant. I would love to see new, different and more immersive learning opportunities within the app. And whilst it can be used for free, I do think the paid options are a little expensive for the benefits I get.

Professional linguists meet mediocre software engineers. The core functionality of this so is really good. I've pepped up my rusty Swedish and started to learn Italian at an unexpectedly high pace. Can recommend! Then why only 3 stars? The gamification part of language learning (where you get XP and badges for practicing and participate in tournaments) is full of bugs that didn't get resolved in months. Customer service is practically non-existent!

I am rating with this not because Duolingo isn't good - I actually find it very helpful. Going from a novice in Spanish to being able to converse with people feels very amazing. However , the ads are becoming too much. Two ads after each lesson is excessive, and its frustrating sometimes that I leave the app before finishing a lesson. You have done us a great help by making this app free, but pls reduce the ads. Thank you!

good for building vocabulary and getting used to grammar, but I wouldn't be able to learn chinese from scratch using duolingo alone. It works for me because I knew the basics and the leaderboards keep me hooked. The most efficient way to compete is the play morning and night each day, which is a good habit, but the rewards for the main lessons are not competitive xp/hr when lessons get trickier. If you want to focus on learning, you need to use methods that reward less xp.

I have used Duolingo for almost 3 years but it no longer provides me with the learning which I got at the beginning. The app has been tweaked so many times and now the free version is rubbish as many of the revision lessons are unavailable. The paid version (what Duolingo calls 'Super') is okay but you'll have to contend with not getting all your points, which kind of negates a game/points based learning platform.

Has quite a few glitches where you lose progress. This is quite annoying as it is a lot of effort to regain the progress, and as you've already done the lessons it is very boring and demoralising. Invasive ads you cannot escape becoming more frequent now. App is rapidly becoming more predatory and lower quality lessons with frequent errors. Was a good app several years ago

The lessons are an easy, helpful and educational way to learn Spanish. Just the right pace to understand and speak this beautiful language. The quests are great! They're challenging enough to get your brain thinking but not overwhelming. I actually look forward to my daily lessons! I'm 60 years old and my brain doesn't always retain the lessons. I find the best way to remember is to write down the words and sentences. So far, so good!

Fun way to learn, and you do feel positive about your progress with all the little rewards along the way. Revisiting French, which I did to 'O' level,many moons ago, plus picking up Spanish for travel purposes. I'd like a bit more background to the grammar rules sometimes. Find the challenges frustrating because I know the answers, but I am slow. This means paying extra for timer boosts. I'm now trying not to get sucked into doing this!

Too much advertising already, making everything just to buy the subscription, I was ok when there were adds to keep the learning free, now there are too much, and the lessons are made already just to keep you longer in the app, not to teach you things faster. Hundreds of lessons of just repeating the same things, just waste of time now. Once it was a very nice app teaching you a language fast. Now it's not anymore. I'm just deleting it.

27.02.25 I've stopped playing as the levels are few. Completed the last level 3 times. For me, there is no incentive to climb to the top! Anymore. It's such a shame. 27th October 24 Awfully at the moment keeps freezing. Having to stop my game yo get the app to work properly. S23. Come on, sort it out. The latest update is awful. Unable to type on some of the pages, it freezes. Why release when it's got such huge problems. I'm enjoying learning French. Still need to practice speaking.

Great app, but impossible to contact support when something goes wrong. I did a progress test for Japanese, and after passing it suddenly the entire course was autocompleted, even though I've only ever done section 1. Now I can't do any of the lessons that I actually need to progress my language study. No support function or way to contact anyone who can help. :(

Duolingo is a very popular language-learning app, and its ratings and reviews tend to be quite positive overall. Here's a breakdown of some key points to consider: * Strengths: * It's user-friendly and gamified, making language learning fun and accessible. * It offers a wide range of languages. * The basic version is free, which is a significant advantage. * It's effective for building foundational vocabulary and grammar skills. * It has expanded to include math and music courses

Easy lessons, I like doing it and keeping the streak with my daughter. Giving it a 1 for the insufferable amount of ads. I completely understand the need for ads if you are using the free version but 5 min of ads after a lesson I can finish in a minute is beyond annoying. I don't want a subscription, just using the app for fun, I will watch your ads but making them longer and making me watch 3 of them is not going to make anyone move to the subscription, you are just downgrading the experience.

I do think the approach in which they try to teach languages is great and it did work for me to a certain point. BUT they're locking more and more features behind a paywall which makes it increasingly more frustrating to continue to learn. There's A LOT of ads, half of them just for giving them money so you can continue to learn. The app would be perfect if their main goal wouldn't just be to get you to spend money and if they'd lie a little less.

I experience glitches with my Pixel 8. Even though the application has microphone permission, and I make sure to use the phone in a quiet room, it fails to recognize any word and will often stop recording while I am talking. I have used the app on older phones with no issue. Also, for some reason, I am unable to add or follow friends. No idea why, I have Google it up and nothing helped. Beside that, the content is great and the application is easy to use.

Not a bad way to have some meagre practice in a language you're learning, but the gameified approach quickly becomes frustrating. It's a shame they started using AI for translation and voices — there's been a sincere drop in quality of audio and prompts since this started happening. Keeping a streak up is basically just an addiction at this point (I have over 850 days) and frankly I'd rather lose it so I can give this app up.

the lessons seem to have improved in quality since i first started using Duolingo 10 years ago. more diverse and immersive lessons. the amount of ads now is a major detractor though. every couple of lessons sure, but they have up to 2 ads after each lesson that can be long and awkward to exit. the ad escape buttons are often hidden, small enough that tapping them can be a challenge, and even come with layers of additional ads to exit one after the other to move on to the next lesson.

Using this to learn French. I've only been practising for a few days so I can review it on its long term language learning, but I do love how simple this app makes things. Very visual and I can track my progress, as well as earning points and taking tests. Will review again after a few months, but I love it this far! UPDATE: I found that I am unable to recall the language beyond the app.

Had to come back and edit this to change my review after using it for a while. It's awful for learning a language, it's just example after example after example, you're never taught anything, they just show you different sentences and you have to figure out for yourself why a word you learned two weeks ago is now different in what seems like the same context.

Edit: Removed 2 stars because app is glitchy. Hard to move to next lesson because the screen blanks out. Leaderboard results blank out and when pressing a word in Word Bank, the word doesn't go to the top line, it falls by the green Checked button. Please fix asap. Easy to use. Fun to learn. Keeps you motivated to learn and practice each day with interactive game like features to keep you interested in learning. Love it. Gracias!

Duolingo is not the best way to learn a language, but it's the best that's easily available. The biggest problem is that it focuses too much on translating and intertwining the new language with your native language, rather than independently building it, which is a better approach. Also, the translating part usually is from the learning language into your native language, and if you're like me, it's hard to translate stuff into English while you're in headspace of the learning language.

Have duo over a year with steak, missed a day here and there. U get freezes that allow to keep streak. Which is good. But, lately they don't let me practice to complete streak have to watch ads Which turn into 5 for 5 stars. Thus bushing premium and they style u learn isn't in every day talking only phases here and there and no context, so I'm very frustrated with this app rn!

Not gonna lie, I have taken up languages and left them over the years but this time I tried the app again and even got the super duo. it has been absolutely amazing experience. I am learning more than ever and even able to apply it in some places within 6 days of using it. It could be more detailed when explaining the grammar but it still provides a good exercise of figuring that out on your own with enough practice provided in the app. Would be great if it dosent send reminder email at 3 am.

Something it doesn't calculate times when a get extra points correctly. For example I have 5 mins when my points for the task is multiply I finish task after wich I should get +10 extra minutes bonus. It shows that this 10 mins are added but in fact when I go to setting only 2 mins are left (instead of 12). It's really annoying please check it and fix. For now just 3 stars until it not fixed. Update1. And still not fixed so 2 stars.

I get why the ads can be forceful. It's understandable. However, I HATE it when my ads started playing with a much larger audio volume than the learning audio itself. At times, I'm in a quiet place and needed to adjust with my surroundings. It's a good app, but I despise these little details they don't seem to care about.

I've been using the Duolingo app since 2022, and it's been a fundamental tool for improving my language skills. The variety of lessons and exercises has allowed me to learn effectively and enjoyably. I love the ability to customize my lessons and the gamification features that make learning fun. The app is easy to use, and the content is of high quality, I highly recommend trying Duolingo.

Language is fluid, imprecise and about conveying ideas. Everything about Duolingo is the opposite. No flexibility or variance in understanding. I have an uncommon language which regularly just looks like it has been pulled off Google translate. It's meant to help you learn but rather just gets you frustrated. Even when paying. Sadly for my language it's the only online tool to support my offline learning. It does a job. But could be so much better.

This product still gave me adverts after paying for no adverts. It gave me an option to "click here to get more gems" and then subsequently showed me a 30-second ad. It didn't tell me it was going to show me an ad before clicking. This was not an optional extra - it was a treasure chest reward and part of the Sections and Units for learning. I'm a little disappointed by this. It has also forced my profile irreversibly private, and I can not add friends. It is, however, good for beginners.

Using Duolingo is a great way to cultivate my love of languages. I just wish that the Android app would get the same priority as the iOS version. The app has gotten worse over time. It was better when stories had its own section, and when you could choose between different new lessons instead of them being arranged linearly. Ads have become more annoying too for the unpaid version.

Makes error by not pronouncing last short words, at normal speed. Can only be heard in slow mode. Marks you down for not detecting this glitch ! Misprounses sé as Spanish, even if not Spanish. Asks material not taught by the app, which is not fair. There is alot of unnecessary repetition after competence is reached repeatedly. Some of the non-traditional cartoon animation characters are a bit culturally inappropriate, and could be seen as offensive.

It's better than nothing, but almost every course is fundamentally flawed in one way or another. Once you finish a course, the daily refresh will keep you relearning the same unit over and over again to the detriment of all other lessons from the course. There are many simple fixes that could be made that the development team has neglected. If you can, get a different app or take a course.

They keep running this app into the ground. More and more AI muck, poorly thought out lessons with little explanation and more and more features are gated behind the subscription. I feel like I'm not learning much and I'm just wasting my time while I could learn from other much better sources. Not to mention relying on AI so much introduces a lot of mistakes in their lessons, as I've noticed when I checked out my the course for my native language. Waste of time, I'm done.

I'm a long time paying subscriber of Duolingo and love most of the features...but find the "game" feature that has recently been included in most levels to be an absolute waste of time and incredibly frustrating. Having to tap on random parts of a scene on screen to get a character to buy a present (a sandwich for her niece on her birthday in one I did today??) is so utterly ridiculous. I emailed this to Duolingo but heard nothing back. Overall an excellent app however & well worth it.

Seems your app has a bug in it. I tried starting a lesson; clicking the start button, but nothing happened. I tried to switch languages, but the anomaly remained. At first, it did as though it switched but then gave me the other language modules. On subsequent attempts , it refused to switch altogether. Kindly help revive this and fix the bug. Other than that, it's the best app to learn almost any language in the world.

Decent app. You learn by repetition and by extracting grammar rules from examples however I don't think the latter really works unless you are immersed in the language or unless the examples are provided sequentially. For example, I am not going to learn from extraction when to use che, cosa, quello che, or quale unless you explain it to me or unless you provide tons of examples one after the other so I can pick up on patterns/rules.

The app is not that bad, A lot of it is actually useful. I'd recommend to absolute beginners to get to know the language and see if you like learning it. For intermediate it's less useful as sentences make no sense as well as responses you choose. It's like 'I bought a new car.' And the right responses to it is like ' My cat likes my car too' I just figure the right one as the other one makes absolutely no sense. The stories and listenings are all fine, though.

Too much gamification, learning no longer seems to be a goal. Just tried the recently added Math learning and it is seriously lacking, which is ironinc because you would expect that it could do basic calculations correctly. One simple example is to multiply 4cents by 2. Obviously the answer is 8cents, however the only available options to choose from are 1, 5 and 10. Math should have the Report function for each answer....or are they not interested in improving?

An amazing app, have been able to make a few simple sentences within a month or less of learning Russian, along with a bit of additional extra learning. However, the challenge of being forced into different leagues is quite tiring and annoying- can you make it optional? Because it kind of demotivates me into learning any more Russian and encourages me to skip my lessons...other than that, a superb app at it's finest!

A great way to learn the basics. Very annoying to keep getting XP boosts when you want to quickly do one lesson. I understand the incentive but these boosts should be available to use within 24h not instantly... Would be much better! Edit: getting really annoyed now... I'm a Paying member and now instead of my daily target specific practice it has been replaced with an ad to upgrade to max.... I've emailed twice the developer and no response....

On the positive side, I am finding the app a really good way to learn. However, I am finding it unnecessarily hard as the English is written in American English. With no option for British English. As a Brit, there are American words we do not use, and spellings are sometimes different, too. This impacts the timed responses greatly, as my older brain is having to double-check the options. It's also not great for children learning a language or picking up on American spellings and using at school

First of all, Duolingo is a very nice learning app with efficient teaching skills. It teaches us as we are kids , and that is what I like the most about it. Secondly, it's notifications are extremely cute , convincing me to take up lessons. Last but not least , Duolingo offers us great understanding for our language or music or math, which is rare to find in other free app. Great efforts are clearly visible behind all of this. Literally, my hands are down for the struggles and untiring effort

The App has seriously degraded since they started. A lot of features have been removed over the years. Every opportunity to get you to pay something is taken. The app itself is good/used to be good for vocabulary and learning to read a language. If you are going to use it, it is at best a secondary or tertiary learning tool. I hope they return features they used to have, it was a nicer place to be and much more effective at language comprehension.

i had 800+ days streak, but decided to delete the app today, since they keep removing features from free version. Now i cannot earn hearts by repeating old lessons and have to watch 5 ads to get back to full health. To be honest I was not learning much from it anyway, it was more like a game, but since it's no longer "free to play" I will move on to something else.

It used to be better. It has always been a bit limited as there are no grammar explanations, and you just go one and one. Sometimes, you end up spelling words like peoples names, and simple typos are considered mistakes. But now, not being able to win back hearts, and they do not fully come back even after 24h, it is a big discouragement in using the app. There is basically no way to progress as you are stuck in the loop of 2 hearts, failing less,gaining hearts, and so on again

super fun and helpful app! the gamification makes it easy for me to stay engaged and learn, and there is SOOO much content to work through. my only sticky point is it hasn't really taught the grammar as consistently and robustly as the vocab, and I'm now not able to progress much - am trying to find some alternative method to learn the vocab. but will definitely also stick with Duo because it's FUN!

I like tak app a lot but! The are missing articles in German course. Articles are crucial in German and they should be shown everywhere (pair excesses, hints, Vocabulary with images, etc). You can't practice your Vocabulary not learning Artikels the same time. Grammar is still not in focus after so many years with Duo. Please takie it into consideration.

I am currently using the Duolingo app to improve my English skills. However, the excessive number of ads is significantly disrupting my learning experience. Ads appear between each chapter, making the process frustrating and reducing engagement. This interruption is affecting my ability to focus and maintain consistency in learning.

This is an AWESOME application.
