I love it so much and it helped me a lot learning a new language. 🤩 But in the new update, all the rewards from quests are exp boost, even tho it's great, but i would like to choose between gems or exp boost, or even letting me choose when to activate the boost during the 24h after claiming it pls!! 🥹 Great work creators!! ❤‍🔥

I've lost all my data, milestones and badges one fine day as I logged in as usual. I've sent many mails to Duolingo support but haven't gotten any reply so far. It's been more than a year. unfortunately as I still want to continue to learn I'm starting over again from the beginning. Also now they have annoying ads in-between!! It's very frustrating!!

Its a pity to see this app being run into the ground. This was a great tool for students to work on their language in a fun way and the introduction of Maths and Music boosted that only for them to completely ruin the for schools feature by essentially making it useless. Hearts are back to limited with no mercy for the slightest of mistake (part of learning is making mistakes!!) And ridiculously long ads. Its clear Duo's goal shifted to only making high profits.. Its time to find new tools..

I've been using the app (with plus subscription) for quite some time now, but the intense chasing and constant reminders are becoming quite annoying and border-line abusive. I'm in fact considering deleting the app for this reason alone. I understand you want to keep your audience engaged, but please don't add unnecessary stress to people mental balance.

Great for sentence structure. Wish they'd also use British English and not just American English. Unfortunately,other learners and I have discovered a lot of mistakes,and I have sent feedback,but nothing happens. Several bugs and always the same vocabulary for repetition - boring. I'm going to leave. Rather than investing in more ways how to earn money (Max,...), correct all the mistakes! I'm going to leave

I find the 'game' side of this so annoying and confusing. I just want to learn a language, not collect meaningless XP and hearts that you are punished with losing if you make an error. Excess amount of ads on free version. I selected the option of learning for travelling version so can't see why I need to learn to spell Philadelphia (in Italian) repeatedly (I don't even live in the USA) as it has zero context for learning some Italian for travel. So many things are annoying- very disappointed.

677 days I've been doing Duolingo. Some days when I was short on time (or kept failing a level), I would use the 'practice to earn hearts' feature to do a quick lesson and continue my streak...until suddenly I could only use this option when I had lost ALL my hearts. Then last night I discovered this option has been removed completely. If your goal was to demotivate, then mission successful.

Hey duo, everything about the app is great. I love using it daily to learn Japanese when I can. If possible, could you please update when it asks you a question and you have to click on the words in the other language to form another sentence. What I'm trying to say is, like on the PC version, your able to move these blocks around and alter their order without having to take them away and remake the entire sentence from the beginning. Many thanks and very great app.

Duo for schools doesn't give you unlimited hearts anymore, and we have video ads everywhere now. this was the final straw for me. will be deleting and finding other apps for a language refresher. thank you for making this decision easier — i've been meaning to do it for some time now, but the unlimited hearts feature kept me here. with it gone, using Duo for teaching has become unviable, too. i could take ads, even if they're annoying, but not losing the hearts. Atp, just make this a paid app.

The new "awards" are a serious let down 😞 !!! 1) I was looking forward to finishing the old ones and I hate having to start over. But much more importantly 2) You get almost no feedback to tell you how you are doing. For example I have no idea how close I am to the next "Mistake Mechanic" level! There really needs to be a counter on the badge so you can tell! Please fix this!

Hi, so far I am at beginner level Chinese. I learned how to speak and recognize characters at level 1 food and drinks. Things are going well but when it comes to picking just the Chinese characters to form a sentence, I cannot tap the other remaining characters on the selection. I try to tap repeatedly and only sometimes it works.

Making language learning into a game is the only way to get me to do it. Thanks so much! I'm learning Spanish, principally, but I've checked out Vietnamese and Ukrainian on here and they aren't as finished in terms of audio. It's a real pity because I need the audio a lot more for these languages. Edit: I've enjoyed playing music in your new course but now the music all sounds like Nintendo so I've stopped using it. Pls add an option to use the old music & an alert to let us know of the change

Fun for learning another language. I'm finally learning French and am spending more time at it than I would've if it wasn't fun. The free version does have a LOT of ads which is why I don't give it 5 stars. However, the paid Super version is inexpensive, about $10/month when on sale. The Max version doesn't seem worth the extra cost but I haven't tried it yet (no trial).

I have started using this alongside a paid language app. DuoLingo's style is motivating almost to the point of addiction, which makes for rapid progress. Some of the phases are a little odd and unnatural in the early to mid stages, but it seems that is to gradually add grammar while letting you make yourself understood early on. It is forgiving of small errors, but insists on detail when it is important for the meaning. Update: not fluent after many years. Aggressively pushes paid version.

An ok app, I woukd give it 4 stars, but: - it's riddled with publicity - dark theme has been disabled on the last update (I have just been flashbanged) - the learning process is more of a guessing game, and no grammar theory is provided - there are not enough exercises focussed on consolidating the vocabulary - the only form of retention is based on not losing your streak, the so-called "sunken cost fallacy" I'll give it another bit of time, but if this is the direction, I'll uninstall.

Thiss application is so good,i was learning Japanese, this is my day 11 and i like duolingo so much! Its almost like a game while learning, good job devs👍 Edit: 63 days streak, there's a problem. The animations are really cool, but... Even if they're on at the settings they doesn't show and it's an issue please fix it, I will give back the 5 stars when u fix it, and I will give 4 stars Edit 2: I got a new phone and it's been fixed I think it was a problem cuz my android was old, 5 stars again

Started out really great, but while learning French in later units, it's supposed to be teaching 'talking about hobbies', but it's started introducing 'work' and 'study' from MANY units ago as new words - so it's covering old ground, not introducing new content and getting VERY repetitive (and no, it's not a revision module). Also the new perfect score pictures/animations are creepy, right?

You used to be able to turn off speaking exercises in the settings, then they disabled that, but at least you could skip the exercises when you came across them. Now, at least in the German course, they have enforced speaking practice that you cannot skip. The app will often not accept my response, even if my speech is OK, so I cannot progress any further. It's very frustrating! Duolingo, please also consider that people with speech difficulties may want to use your app!

It's perfect for starting any language, also for learning vocabulary and practicing hearing. I have been learning german for some time and the only complaint I have is that it doesn't explain grammar explicitly, instead it implicity teaches things by repetition almost magically. I am a native spanish speaker that also knows english, so that would explain my personal experience.

When I first downloaded this it was great, I was learning french and was properly learning it fast. But now, it's hard to learn any language because the one thing that clearly helped me the most was the heart practice. I could practice all the way to five hearts and I would actually learn my mistake cause I could have a proper lesson, but unfortunately they've taken that feature away and can only practice to one heart making it hard to learn. You go back and forth, making mistakes and practicing

It was good some years before! But now update after update and having their main focus on money it got to be tedious, unproductive and quite rubbish. Once they just doubled their lessons in each section (without new content) just to have more interaction and ads revenue. And speaking of repetition. Spaced repetition is for language learning. Regular boring repetition is only for duolingo to make money. Now it takes much more time to learn it from duo rather than just sit and study it

When playing in the music lessons, there are certain tracks that do not slow down if you struggle with them. Also, regardless of what you are trying to play, if you pause it to take a look at what it is asking you to play, it doesn't pause. It goes back to where it wants you to restart. Please fix it so pause means pause so we can look at part of the stave where we struggle. Obviously, that is a likely reason for wanting to pause at that point. If you want to skip back to restart, so be it.

It's a very good practical little app to have in your inventory, especially if you want to brush up on languages you're familiar with, but you require a more fun and creative approach to re-learning! I do, however, wish more African languages were part of the curriculum, but with an added Music and Math course, there definitely seems to be more potential to add to this app!

I've been learning German for a while now. I've learned a lot, and it's really easy to learn using Duolingo. The app itself is good, but my main issue is when I'm doing my lessons and I got it wrong due to not knowing if the animal is a female or male. I sometimes lose hearts rapidly due to the translation just going from "a cat" to "eine Katze" as if I'm supposed to know the cat was a girl. The same goes with using "die" and "der". I still really like using the app

Edit: you can't seem to practice to refill hearts anymore. The button is still there but it doesn't work. Cannot overstate just how much this app sucks. It has actually gotten harder to learn with this app. Aside from their frustrating "bang your head against a wall" teaching style, with the way they have structured things now you don't actually know what topics are tripping you up. Which means you can't look things up elsewhere as easily because you don't know what to look up.

Great concept and I was enjoying the app however the ridiculous amount of ad spamming and inability to access certain lessons without using gems is too much and takes the enjoyment out of it. I ended up buying a japanese to english phrase book and I am learning way faster with that not having to watch 2 x 30 second ads after every single lesson.

Decent program to lean the basics. However, the app have many bugs. When practicing pronouncation the program fails to pick up my voice, app teach you sentences and phrases that are incorrect or not used in the language u are learning and also doesn't allow you to pause ur sessions. Definitely will not be renewing my subscription.

It's the best application to learn language According to Google Play, and yes I agree with you that it's pleasant to learn new language in this condition but I have a problem when I want to learn music the interface is laggy and the tiles are just teleporting and I just lost streak / heart because of this. Please just fix this I want to continue my music learnings sessions...

I've encountered 2 major issues whilst using the app: 1). Sometimes the exercise is formatted wrong. For example, you can only use one translation of the word is accepted sometimes, whilst there are multiple valid ways to translate. This problem is much more frequent if you use a foreign language as the base language (e.g. you're using italian to learn german) 2). Crashing. So. Much. Crashing. Worst part? It tends to lag and then crash at the END of a lesson. So you lose all progress.

The learning is quite slow, and there's an ad after every every 2 minute section and the app adds their own even longer ads after those ads. With on average of 20 seconds of ads, you spend over 15% of your time watching ads instead of learning. Not to mention if you run out of hearts, you need to watch an unskippable ad to get ONE heart back, so that's another 20 seconds of ads. So if you are trying to learn fast, like me, you are spending 20-30% of your time looking at ads instead of learning.

Good to learn basics, doesn't really teach about grammar or sentence structure though. Confusing user interface. Riddled with ads and memberships. The heart system is terrible. Part of learning a language is making mistakes, it's gonna happen ALOT, you shouldn't have to stop learning for a day or so just to get back on track.

the app itself is good. but the Japanese course still has lots of mistakes. The translation sometimes don't match at all. plus the XP is not counted at all. I went through 10+ lessons but the XP only went up by 25. Reached out to duoling several times, but the problem isn't being fixed. What is the point of those rankings if more than half of your earned XP is not being counted.

It's great for beginners. But, I have a few suggestions: 1)add more reading without the help of pronunciation guide, because I ended up memorising that instead of the actual letters. 2) to add an option where you'd be able to notify yuor friends on Duolingo or to save the streak when you can't get on your phone for a certain amount of period (like me, since i go to a boarding school, I don't have my phone with me to study)

As a starting point, this can be useful, however as you progress the app expects you to know new words, without the hints that you received earlier on. The voices saying the words are also inconsistent in their pronunciation, which is confusing and not helpful, clearly AI generated. There are also alot of annoying adverts if you choose the free version.

The old duolingo basic course provided explanations regarding grammar. The current duolingo basic courses do not. It is required to buy the most expensive AI package to have comprehensive explanations regarding grammar and It's very expensive. I think that should be included in the basic course the way it was originally. Most people need to understand what the different rules are for different languages to comprehend the patterns in sentence structure. The old duolingo was better!

Disgraceful, scheming behaviour. They advertised free trial of the subscription and quote a monthly price. They also say risk free we will notify you 2 days before your trial ends. I normally cancel free trials but thought I'd rely on that. The notifications never came through any channel at all. I noticed a day after expiry I'd been charged $270 for the annual subscription. I immediately contacted the play store to ask it to be reversed and that was declined, no response from Duolingo at all.

The concept used to be great, but now it's more of a money-making advertising platform based upon a game. There are virtually no useful grammatical explanations, the practice sentences are randomly generated nonsense ("Your cat never helps his brother." Really?!) and it feels that the purpose isn't to actually learn, but just to keep a streak. One final thing to the devs: if you intrusively insert ads (especially loud, shouty ones) into an activity I'm undertaking, I will NEVER buy that product

CONs: -lessons are inconsistent and repetetive at the same time -free trial turns into (expensive!!) paid subscription without notice -free version has way too many ads and punishes you for mistakes -the new character animations are cringe and creepy. PROs: -Interface and UI are quite good -leaderboard competition is fun -many different activities to try -gamified incentives to keep coming back to learn

Has become pretty much unusable: have to practise to earn hearts, but can only take you from 0-1, so still can't make mistakes. Can't even pay for a subscription (overpriced) as I get the message 'Can't connect to Google Play Store'. A real shame as Duolingo used to be a useful tool for language learning, but all the various money-grubbing changes have completely ruined the app. As long as the quarterly numbers look good, who cares about user experience, right?

Very buggy. Slow. Very little content in some courses (i.e., Ukrainian). No content updates. No corrections, even when pointed out. Stupid useless phrases. Useless exercises. No grammatical explanations whatsoever. Horrible review questions algorithm. Weird and unfair "rewards". Rewards XP instead of actual progress. Terrible speech recognition algorithm (recognises badly spoken words and doesn't recognises well pronounced words). Childish content. Terribly frustrating. Bad user experience. Etc.

This app isn't worth the subscription price. I tried the free trial, and I learned the same amount as when I was using the free version. Unfortunately, Google didn't wait a full 7 days to charge my card, so I was charged for a full year of this app that I'm not going to use. It also uses creepy and threatening tactics to try to motivate you to keep up your streak.

Fun, easy to use, go at your own pace. The auI could go on, budio is a bit hit and miss, and English translations are often judged as wrong, because they differ from American English. Certain English expressions are simply wrong. O 'clock instead of O' clock is a case in point. I tried the Maths course, but there were too many mistakes: examples covering choices of answers, images impossibly small, absence of correct answers, etc.

Good fun and quite addictive. It really makes you want to continue learning your chosen language. It is divided into bite size chunks, practical skills, repetition, audio and a little written work; so you learn without feeling pressured. I would however, like to see a little more gramatical explanation. The weekly tables that you occasionally compete in stop you from lapsing too long and encourage you to keep your learning going.

All useful features became 2 tier paywalled AI barf. Everything runs on 2 paid currencies. There's no real structure: Half a lesson will focus on a single word you know & no new ones and explanations of mistakes are paywalled AI slop. You're constantly bombarded with pointless pop-up animations, ads & in-app menus if you want to play for over 10 mins. The voice recognition has no consistency. Duolingo was only good for memorising basic words, but at this point flashcards will perform better.

I love duolingo. However, since they added the pay wall, that is the 5 lives mechanic I have found that it makes failing a lesson stressful as you'll almost be guaranteed to lose your streak. After all, you learn more from making mistakes when learning a language, why punish users for it. I'd recommend getting rid of the 5 lives mechanic to make people want to keep learning after making mistakes within lessons. Without it, I would rate this app 5 stars and recommend it to anyone.

Honestly, the language learning is ok, so long as you don't need to have any conversations within the first few hundred days of beginning. It's absolutely non conversational. While it's great for learning individual words, which can be helpful, if you needed to rely on solely your Duolingo experience, especially early on in your language journey, you'd probably have a bad time. And really, I wouldn't mind using it, and would probably continue if it weren't for an absolute bombardment of ads.

Really love the app and the daily opportunity to learn Spanish (or many other languages). My only gripe is that each heart previously refreshed after one hour. Now it takes 5 hours to refresh a single heart, significantly slowing down learning and the willingness to make mistakes. Please go back to the previous version of hearts.

It's a very engaging, educational app. However, it would be more helpful if the lessons started off with how to say the basic greetings before diving into random words such as water, coffee, etc., let alone before learning how to write in Chinese. I find that it's been hard as a beginner in learning Chinese and if I learned how to verbalise greetings and others basic general words first, that would be more helpful.

Really good to get the basics of a language. Very accessible and can be fun. The worst part for me is the fact that if you make a mistake Duolingo Super will not explain your answer. For that you need to upgrade your subscription and then it becomes very expensive. Also for this reason i would probably look elsewhere if you really want to become good at a language. Duolingo is fine if you want to brush up on some language for a holiday.

Amazing, Ive been learning spanish on this app and it is amazing, the lessons are short and frankly, quite fun! Fun enough to make me excited to do them every morning ans keep my streak going, they make it look like a quest with gems that you can use to buy power ups and hearts that you lose every time you make a mistake, if you run out of hearts, you can watch an ad or do a practise lesson, the only annoying thing are the adds after every lesson, but anyways, I would highly recomend duolingo

well, it is a very brilliant app. But the only thing i don't like is that like all other apps, we have to subscribe to super to get unlimited lives. well, I can expect that as it is the case with every app, but each life takes 5 hours to regenerate! even when I spend all my lives in one day, and sleep, I more or less only one or two lives the next morning. we have to wait for 25 hours for the next 5 lives!

As of tonight, the app has randomly switched to light mode! It will no longer run in dark mode. It has been fine since I first installed it three weeks ago, but now it will no longer run in dark mode no matter what settings I adjust on my phone. This is really frustrating. I suffer from various eye conditions im being forced to use the app n this bright colour is causing me a lot of discomfort! Having paid so much for something I am no longer able to use feels like daylight robbery.

Edit: Have a 800+ streak now so still positive in general however, a warning. They give you super trials and a warning to end it, but after a lot of succesfull ones. I cancelled the trial a day before it ended, got confirmation in the app, but got charged 89.99eur anyway. Duo, Google and PayPal won't refund. So beware of thieving. Nearly on a streak of a year learning Japanese. Duolingo is nice to use and thought I've experienced it being annoying sometimes, the reminders do help to keep at it.

I'm grateful that even using the free version as someone who is already fluent in 2 languages beyond my native tongue, that the app is helping me improve. I definitely recommend this app as a primer, light supplement or addition. But for the record, I really don't like the new horror expression on Duo's face for entering the app. Can we just leave Duo's expression as neutral/ light?!

I love using Duolingo, I find myself learning for an hour a day, it's alot of fun, competitive and interactive. I would have given five stars If it wasn't for the fact the customer service is so poor. There's been serval occasions where I've emailed them a specific question and not been given the right answer and many emails have been completely ignored. I also cannot use the apps full features, speaking exercises as it relies on Google which isn't available in China even with VPN

The voice recognition software is soo bad, they should just remove the speaking exercises until it works properly. The speed challanges also can't count. In the third phase the question count is always wrong. Reach 7, then try reaching 10. 6 questions later and you are only halfway. Writing exercises only accept a particular stroke direction and order, regardless of if you are correct. It is a fun app but it needs alot more work.

Too invasive. With the memes, the "gamification" and the widget. It's all about NUMBERS (leagues, streak, friends, XP…), so you commit to nonsense stats and forget about actual priorities. You can just learn your native language and get so much XP… It's addictive: You're supposed to learn for about 5 or 10 minutes a day, but you end up with days farming XP for whole HOURS of lessons straight, because they give you 3 trial days of Super or a 15 min XP boost, while you have something else to do.

I liked this app, but couldn't skip one of the levels with homosexual reference, even though it made me very uncomfortable. I honestly think they should give us the option, when creating our Duolingo account, to select whether we're comfortable with this type of content. I mean no offense, but I do have my personal preferences. Thank you.

Terrible UI and System. I had some recent issues accessing the app (was updated to new version) every time I loaded to start a lesson, and it crashed. Uninstalled, reinstalled, issue still existed. 3 days later, it worked, but I lost my streak, pop up asked me to repair it with 500 diamonds. I didn't have enough. So I purchased some. Surprise, surprise. It didn't remember that I was purchasing diamonds to fix the streak and it didn't fix it. I have to restart all over after spending money.

I'm editing my original review because the app suddenly changed to light mode even though my phone is permanently in dark mode! I don't know what kind of ridiculous update caused it but it was truly ill advised! I don't see myself continuing to use it if this problem persists. Amazing app! Easy to keep track my progress and fun to learn with! I love it!

I used to give this app 5 stars, but after 3 years of using it (and 1 full paid year) I switched back to a free account. They push their paid subscription every time you complete a lesson! Finally gave in to a free trial, which I swear I set to cancel immediately. Turns out I was wrong because I was charged $120 a few days later. Good luck getting a refund on the subscription - they divert all requests through Google Play so you never even speak with a rep. Can't even upgrade to a family plan.

I have used duolingo for years and it was great. I picked it up again about 3 years ago (1000+ day streak) and it was still good until recently. They have purposefully downgraded everything in the free version, it now has intrusive and deceptive ads and the subscription price is ridiculous. I wonder if the business has been sold to a scammer or is publicly traded now. They seem to be looking for short term gains, always a bad idea. Sadly I am leaving the platform.

Great for learning a variety of languages, and amazing at correcting mistakes. There is one problem, the listening and responding task sometimes doesn't hear what you are saying at all, and therefore marks it as wrong. It might be a problem with my phone but I'm sure others have had this problem too.

I'm learning French (pt-BR -> French) and it's frustrating how much errors and inconsistencies there are in the lessons. It was all good until they changed the course and pushed me to the 2nd level. Sometimes I wonder if it's worth to pay that much in Duolingo, the learning quality isn't the same, at least for French.

Great app for basic Italian. I've been on for approximately one month already with no issues to worry about until this review. Speaking option and ability has disappeared completely from italian lessons. I've checked and rechecked permissions, reinstalled the app, read for an hour on forums, restarted my phone a jillion times, cleared cache, tested my microphone on other apps and general messages. Pretty disappointing. Great potential, poor app support. Fix ya bugs Duolingo.

I absolutely love Duolingo! It’s one of the best language-learning apps out there. The lessons are short, engaging, and easy to fit into my daily routine. The gamification aspect—like earning XP, maintaining streaks, and leveling up—keeps me motivated and makes learning feel like a fun challenge rather than a chore. The app covers a wide range of languages, and the exercises are well-structured, focusing on vocabulary, grammar, listening, and speaking. I also appreciate the variety .

I have an on again off again relationship with Duo, and like any toxic ex, every time I try to get back together, it has somehow gotten worse. This time, I can't practice for more hearts on the app, but adding "/practice" to the url on browser works. Not giving it 1 star, because it could just be a bug that gets fixed. But then again, who am I kidding?

Duolingo is a really fun way to learn a language! I really recommend for those who want to learn a bit of a language. I have a small problem with the app, It's probably because my phone is old, sometimes when I press continue a few times, the delay makes me accidentally submit my answer to the next question, causing me to get an incorrect answer. Overall, It's still really fun!

My experience is based on the Japanese course. Duolingo is okay. However, it doesn't really teach you the 'basics' of a language from the ground up. Like, if you start a new course, it throws you into a lesson where it asks you to translate a few words you've never heard of, resulting in you losing hearts. Also, there is an major innacuracy in the Japanese course. 'Desu' means 'to be/it is'. It is NOT 'he/she'. Please fix this immediately, as it is incorrect and makes sentences confusing.

This app is great! I've been learning Japanese on this app for just over 50 days and I have got very confident at saying and writing quite a few Japanese words. It's fun and easy to do, but I think it needs to be slightly less strict about practicing everyday. I recently went on holiday, and the camping site I went to had no internet, so I couldn't practice for four days, and when I got back home it made me complete five lessons in one go to keep my streak going! But apart from that, great app!

I've been using Duolingo for quiet a while, i've been practicing norwegian so i can speak it when i travel there, or have a language that i could speak with friends with, so we don't have people spying our convo's. Commorcials are a bit annoying, and did cause me to not get my streak in time sometimes, but that happens rarely. It's a nice app for learning languages at a moderate pace. I do recomend it for learning new words and forming simple sentences.

So far I'm enjoying the german language course. unfortunately, I feel harassed by the application. If you purchase Duolingo Pro, instead of seeing ads for other things they force you to see ads for Duolingo Max and it's just as annoying. Also there's no way of turning on the streak tracking and the league scoring which are extremely annoying to have to sit through. Will not repurchase the Pro in future years.

The app is good but recently there is a problem with the listening lesson in Section 2, Unit 5. The matching pair task is giving voices and matching words as completely different ones, means I can't even get to the next task in that practice unless I connect them rightly which is ofc, impossible. So please fix this problem. I did 'do it later' but the 2nd listening lesson in the unit 5 was also having the same issue.

Duolingo’s awesome for picking up new words, and I’m really committed to it! The only downside? Every time you get something wrong, you lose a heart. If you run out of hearts, you can’t keep going until you’ve waited a bit super frustrating when all I want to do is learn, not play a game. The good news is you can avoid this by subscribing, which gives you extra features. 🎉 I’m not going for it, but there’s a free trial if you’re interested! 🤔

It's doing what it actually said it would do... Flawlessly, I might add. I would upgrade to "Super duolingo" immediately if I didn't have pending Internet banking issues, but without it, somehow, I personally find it better to a degree cause it provides more challenges. But for those that have a busy schedule and no patience whatsoever, 'Super duolingo' is the best way to go! The reason I'm going to upgrade it when I'll have the opportunity is solely to support them.

Not a good learning app, it will show the same identical question 3 or more times in a row, doesn't accept the correct pronunciation even when spoken by someone in the language being learned, and jumps into new topics/words without any explanation of what they mean. Technical support is just an AI bot and you never get a reply. I used it for almost 40 consecutive days and didn't learn much at all. Moved to another app and it's miles better. Don't waste your money on Duolingo!

I'm absolutely loving my language learning duo. It's amazing that I'm highly motivated to complete each lesson, driven by genuine interest. The platform's features, such as leagues, XP, and leaderboards, help me stay focused on my goal of ranking first.However, I do have one suggestion for improvement: Isn't fantastic to have a chat feature that allows me to connect with my international friends and followers??? Please create programming for the communication too😭😭👀

Is Sunday the first day of the week? No. So it shouldn't be shown as the first. Secondly, it's extremely annoying how your app constantly spams users and forces them to do exercises on a daily basis. I don't want to do exercises every day—I don't even have time for it. When I do have time, I'll do them, but your app keeps bothering me and trying to make me feel bad for not keeping up. This is not a good approach. I really like the learning style of the app, but everything else is poorly designe

Great way to begin to learn a language for free. It is great for those who want to give it a try withoit throwing money at something that tbey may only dipninto every now and then, or find that it's just not for them. The paid option would definatly benefit those who are wanting to learn a language and are wanting to commit, as it does have more to offer, with less restrictions. Either way, great app. 9 days in and loving it.

okay so I just began using it like a week or so back and I was very pleased with it all. the lessons, the practices and the difference pronunciations were all very helpful and innovative. but now, after every lesson or practice or anything for that matter you do on the app you get an ad after it which is, to put it mildly, annoying. it's the same on web as well, which is lowkey making the whole publication super pathetic and unwanted.

Learning with Duolingo was once fun, but now even with the paid version the constant push towards gem sales and tier upgrades is annoying and tiring. Also, course quality declined with each course 'update'. There's so many mistakes in now (that are not corrected even weeks after reporting them), that I've stopped trusting them. Cancelled my subscription and will never go back.

The app is good,but my main problem is how long it takes for the heart to refil. Another of my pproblemis it had technical issues when a I laying the game and was told to listen to the sound and put the words I heard. That's when the problem occurs. The sound will no longer be able to play making me unable to tell what sound it's making and there for either I have to fail by picking any word or wait for about twenty minutes first before the sound plays again. I hope you fix it quickly. Than you

Great app to learn any language. I have well progressed in Dutch. However 2 points should be looked at. First there is no explanation on your mistakes. A small explanation would be helpful. I have to explore other websites to find out. Second is the pairing match exercices issue. It is always the same words which comes out. I know now approximately 800 words and I would like more variety in the pairing word selection. In short DUO is great but you must have ither source of learning to excel

The new math section needs some work. Lots of it are hard to see on mobile, like when you're trying to see if the 100 block is 60% full or 70%. Hard to count how many columns there are on mobile. As well as there are a few exercises that ask you to give a number, and you're supposed to use a slider to put in your answer, but the only two numbers are at the start (0) and the end. So you give your best approximation of where it should be and you get it wrong because it was a touch too far

Its a great app which is used for years, but now if you struggle with languages they now penalize you by either stopping you from using the app when you run out of hearts or make you pay. If you're good with languages you don't need to do this and can practice as much as you like for free. This is rather back to front and rather contrary to the learning model it was originally built on. Also, it's insistence on US English words and grammar has been a constant problem and further penalises others

I’ve been using Duolingo to explore their music lessons, and I must say, it’s been an incredible experience! Learning music has never been this easy. The app introduces notes like 🎵🎶 in such a fun and interactive way that it keeps me engaged throughout. What I love most is how Duolingo encourages me to participate more, making the process enjoyable rather than overwhelming. Plus, the reward system with diamonds is a great . which encourages me more to participate.

The first months after starting I would have rated it higher, but there are a number of key issues: 1) It's significantly over-gamified - which often forces you to learn in suboptimal ways 2) Arbitrary changes to UI, xp gains etc all the time. But no improvements to content 3) Too little content for Russian (which I am studying) 4) the repeat feature when you've completed all courses is a mess/useless. You end up repeating the same 50 sentences while other bits get never covered

almost perfect. I love learning with this app, and I can now write in Japanese after a pretty short time, but there are a few annoying bugs (for example in the japanese typing exercises, I write the exact same as the result but get a mistake). also the listening exercises (specifically the radio-like ones) are quite annoying. they don't sound like actual conversation and take too much time. also, I tried the math and music but they were absolutely terrible. unusable.

it is one of the best application I have came across in my whole life. it has meticulously planned lesson plus every details are created for the learners to learn other language with the help of English. plus it gives you progress report with stipulated time period. if you are planning for learning a language than duolingo is a great option. it seems it is like a teacher only. which will guide you through every corner of the learning process. not only listening but writing and speaking. all 3.

This application is everything that one requires to learn a language. I had been using "teach yourself" books for years and was sceptical about Duo, even though I had been given positive feedbacks on it. Once I downloaded Duo, right at the start when I was about to begin to learn Spanish just to pick up the pronunciation and two and a half years after I can actually speak Spanish (I am near the end of the couse now)

I was doing German lesson by tapping a reminder, and when I was done with the lesson, it won't change to other languages as I tapped the flag icon, and when it changed to the other language (in my case Spanish), the screen turned bright, the dark mode disappeared. I use dark mode due to sensitive eyes so it's really annoying. I clicked my profile but there was no option for dark mode set up. Is this another bug after the most recent update?

App is good but shows always the same sentences/exercises (for Dutch course). The level should be increased and the vocabulary should pass 2.6k words. I would like to learn around 5k words in Dutch. Also, i find it not nice that for Voice Call option, there is an extra membership required.. Why is Duolingo Super not enough? We are already paying a lot of money!!

Well, the lessons kinda feel like they were put together by AI sometimes. The voices do too. Otherwise it's an alright app. It can teach vocabulary and some basic grammar but you'll probably want to do work outside of the app if you want to actually be proficient in the language you're studying. Also the stars are this low because they did this weird.. thing where they killed their mascot off and then put a dead bird as their icon. Creepy. I'm not a fan.

Edit: 2 stars to 1 stars. They've added MORE ads. For a while now, more and more of the app's features have been moved behind a paywall, and with the latest update, it seems the app is nearing the end of its product cycle: The learning experience is being sidelined in favour of making profit. As even the paid version of Duolingo unfortunately doesn't hold its own against the likes of Babbel and Lingodeer, I'm afraid I can no longer recommend it.

Don't waste your money on Duolingo Max, the AI video call is rubbish and it's the same conversation every time. It's glitchy and sometimes have to try 3 times to get it working. If you try to ask "Lili" any questions or have a different conversation it doesn’t work. It's no good for learning a language to travel. I'm racing through the lessons and we have only just learnt numbers up to 10? and very few useful phrases that you would actually need travelling.

There used to be a time when I could've rated this app with 5 stars, but then Duolingo was ruined with progress tree redesign couple of years ago, and now music course – not perfect, but still wonderful course – my beloved course on Duolingo since they don't care about languages such as Greek or Irish – my music course got butchered with "reinvented" songs. "Spring ‐ Oscar's version" was the first one I tried, and it broke my heart. Ntm Zari's, Lily's and Junior's versions are pure travesty

I've paid for language learning apps in the past (ConjuGato). But the push to monetization is horrible with this one. You can only make 5 mistakes per day, while mistakes are the way to learn. But even worse, when learning new words, it's not telling you first. You just have to guess, and thus lose one of your 5 hearts. Absolutely terrible. Uninstalling.

Great way to playfully learn a new language. easy, rewarding, playful, every day a little bit (or more if you get addicted), alone or with friends, uses all the little psychological tricks to keep you going (but in a good way), much rather have my kids spend time on Duolingo than any game or social network!

Making learning interactive and game-like is so engaging and makes me want to come back and learn more Since I got the Loog piano to do the music lessons, I do not know what fingers to play with and where to put them, especially moving up the keyboard and I only have 5 fingers, and I don't know where to put them!! It's really frustrating

Generally an ok app, but quite often the "slow" version of the listening excercises just do nothing, the continual change of the demotion positions in diamond is pretty annoying. As is the fact it will happily deduct multiple lives for the same question for pedantic (and sometimes outright incorrect) reasons Also ridiculous that your competitions, boosts and similar change significantly to accomodate paying users. Either have fair competition or get rid of it completely.

Having used this app for over 2 years, it's been great. A few quick ads started to appear which were fine. And today out of the blue, ridiculously long adverts have started to appear unless you sign up and pay a hefty price. Very disappointed and don't think I'll be using it much longer if the app continues to go this way. It's like being on a gaming app now. I'm not that desperate to learn a new language to pay £90 a year. Sorry Duo but this is one follower you'll be loosing.

I had a great time using Duo with a streak of over 1100 days, and a couple of years before that. Tried the paid version, but the limitations of the free version helped me to learn. A recent change now means that as a free user, you no longer receive explanations of why your answer is wrong, nor do you have the ability to freely practice previously learned grammar and vocabulary. Duo is no longer a free learning app. The free version has been reduced to being a trial for the paid version

With the current gamification system you need at least 30 minutes, but better more. You cannot just do a quick lessen, else you lose all the bonuses. Also, the heart system without paying is extremely bad. I am also not satisfied with getting the same sentences all over again in finished courses review, when the course has many more words and sentences.

I CANNOT DISABLE LIGHT MODE. IT HAS NEVER BEEN ON BEFORE AND NOW I CAN'T TURN IT OFF. THERE'S NO OPTIONS IN THE SETTINGS, AND IF THERE IS, THEN YOU SURE DID A GREAT JOB OF HIDING IT, BECAUSE I ABSOLUTELY CANNOT FIND IT. I CANNOT WORK WITH THIS LEVEL OF BRIGHTNESS. Update: I've managed to enable dark mode manually. And guess what? It's even WORSE! Some things COMPLETELY break in dark mode, such as matchup questions, and the whole app just generally looks deep-fried!

Yeah, Duolingo's heading downhill and fast. It was pretty mid from the beginning, but it was tolerable. I mean, this is the stupidest/worst thing a learning app — that advertises itself as the world's best for language — can do: locking the learning experience behind a stupid life/heart system. Not just that, but if you don't want to be restricted — which is such a obvious flaw — you have to pay an overpriced subscription fee just to learn properly!? This is atrocious, deplorable, and greedy!

I highly recommend Duolingo as your number one language learning app. Over the years I have learned to speak six languages the hard way. Back in the day 1970's and early 80's there wasn't anything like Duolingo to help, I certainly wish there had been. I love the way the lessons are structured, easy to follow, and great fun. Absolutely brilliant!

Took off two stars . One because they eliminated the practice to earn hearts feature for free users, which was so motivating and also such a great review. Eliminating this feature is a great way to keep users OFF your app. Another because I'm getting tired of being emotionally manipulated by Duo. The histrionics they go through to constantly make us feel bad and engender competitiveness has made this app a chore. I used to think this was a fun way to learn a new language. That luster is gone.

Getting even worse. Now it doesn't give the score bonuses that it says it has given you. You can only recover lives when you have none left, rather than topping them up by doing practice rounds. Also, it will only explain how you've gone wrong if you're on the paid for tier now. Useful for brushing up your language skills but the talk pages are gone too. Going down hill.

I think duolingo is great. its an awesome way to learn a new language easily but just a few things..... please can the everyday speaking be further forward. I don't need to learn about fruit or animals before I learn to say hello or how are you. I love the rewards such as the free unlimited for some days. I think it would be fun to have some video lessons to watch to help with your learning. interactive if possible. also some different adverts even if they r still made by duolingo. just anything

It's like a normal ad at first, then boom... Struck with like a 20 second ad you can't skip. I'm not sure if the double ads are intentional, since the app kind of glitches after closing the first ad, but if they are leaving it intentionally, then what a bunch of greedy corporate fellas. They really replaced the entire 'practice to earn hearts' feature with 'watch an ad to gain hearts'. Becoming more corporate as the days go by. Are they really lacking the neseccery funds?

I kind of love it!! I learned Russian through it. I mean I already knew Russian but I could not have the confident conversation but now I think it's quite easy. Well, well done Duolingo!! I think you're a pretty good language guider But the really irritating thing is: Duolingo sends me messages the all time saying "you need to practice" even though I don't have time to. But that's fine. 😊

Duolingo did me dirty. Long story short: Be cautious buying from Duolingo. Before the new year they had a promo on to get 60% off. They were deceptive & didn't have prices displayed properly. They also didn't confirm the purchase once the credit card info was entered, showing what final price would be. After complaining, they refunded my super Duolingo without my consent when I stated I will keep using it. Now I get ads all the time for Super Duolingo.

I do believe the app helps actually learn the language, but because of the limited hearts being so few and the inability to easily choose which exercises you need and want and not enough free in depth explanations to each section can make it difficult for someone who cannot afford to buy the unlimited version to be able to progress and learn. Making mistakes is essential so at the very least having deeper lesson plans can allow them to prepare better and learn more

I used to love Duolingo, but unless you are willing to spend a fortune on a paid subscription, it's really not worth bothering with anymore. It used to be amazing, and I was hoping to upgrade to paid soon, but they've cut so much from the free version that I'm not upgrading on principal now. The app tile is really annoying too - if you miss a day, it changes to a crying or screaming Duo face, which doesn't change back for weeks. This has become a terrible app, and I would no longer recommend.

this is a good app to test yourself with, but it doesn't really teach you - for that you need your own resources for example, sometimes the voice recognition is rubbish and frustratingly doesn't tell you what it hasn't liked. maybe it could highlight the word you are struggling with and help with the pronunciation? instead it just simply lets you struggle on regardless

Learning spanish (slowly). It is easy to use and totally free! 2025 update: far from fluent probably because I struggle with language. They have changed the app with a lot more ads - this is the price for the free version. I am still totally happy and am learning on a daily basis for the last 4 years.

A good app for learning languages. Even it has Esperanto. Also the day streak is really wonderful about how many days you have did your lesson. As well as achievements i don't learn any languages, but i prefer intermediate english to enhance my English skills, but obviously it has some problems, other languages like Japanese, this app lack focus on grammar. But this is good app though, and I have a suggestion, Add the chat, so we can talk too. It will make this app even more better.

Pretty underwhelming nowadays. The changes they made over the years are there to stop you from learning at a decent pace, unless you pay. I know apps are a business and they need to make some money to finance their work, but the type of monetization they opted for goes against their supposed aim of being a learning platform. No thank you, better go over to the babel app.

Good for something that is free. The ads are getting bad. I liked the first version MUCH better than what has developed. Sometimes, they don't translate the answer, it just says whether it's correct or not. There's not much learning in that. One of the guys voices irritates me so bad I have to turn the volume down when he talks. Taking away the practice to earn hearts function was a mistake, how are we meant to learn if we can't make mistakes? Lose five hearts and you're done? Stupid.

the translations are far too simplified. You need to know the what the phrases mean in the other language so you can structure your sentences like a Native speaker. However duolingo only tells you the equivalent in your language which doesn't help you understand the language you are learning. The app can be quite glitchy which is quite frustrating when it makes you loose your streak. It also often puts vocabulary into sentences you haven't learnt yet and doesn't explain your mistakes.

I've been using Duolingo for almost a decade, I have a 1971 day streak and completed every single monthly event since inception. In the past few years I've watched them close down projects (e.g. the best flashcard app on the market), the forums, user comments in app. The app now is full of very long unskippable ads & their own unwanted AI premium. I'm often marked right/wrong in appropriately. It's practically unusable. It's buggy and constantly changing. Goodbye Duo, you completely suck

It is a great app to learn the basics of new languages. However, I do not understand why their own advertisement (no revenue from that unless you subscribe app) pops up after each lesson completion. It is just irritating rather making me subscribe the app. I understand the adds they get money from that, which is bearable.

I've been using the free version of the app for almost 3 years and it's definitely gone downhill in that time. The actual course content seems to have gotten worse, while pushing the paid version in more and more obnoxious ways. I don't want to give up my streak, but I feel like I'm not actually learning anything anymore and am currently looking for better alternatives.

RETURN ABILITY TO LEARN!!!! I've spent a lot of time learning in the app and it's great. But recently, they moved off the ability to learn to get more hearts. Now, I have to spend my time looking through advertising instead of gaining skills. Guys, you are going the wrong way. RETURN ABILITY TO LEARN!!!! Please 🌹

This app used to be fun and educative; for the people. Now it's filled with ads every 9/10 lessons, and the learning structure is designed to stagnate your learning via hearts. Memberships are forced upon learners via heart system or excutiating ads. This is what happens when greed takes over. If I could give it a 0, I would. The app was so much better 3 years ago before the "path" system

The app has many mistakes whether translation is incorrect or listening ignores you sometimes, also there's no adjustments for difficulty levels (for instance when you choose hard it adds some serious grammar, complicated tenses or the amount of answers to choose is bigger something like that). tbf the app is getting progressively worse for me.. i get an ad for their subscription service after each lesson and on top of that, the icon changes at a certain time to remind me to do my lesson - this unpins the app from the start menu of the launcher I use (I use a launcher that replicates the feel of Windows mobile)

App is complete rubbish now... total garbage. Removing the ability to practice to earn hearts just barely scratches the surface of just how egregious this app has become. also, the branding has become absolutely atrocious and is just annoying and makes me want to use the app even less than I already did. do yourself a favor and spend money on a different language education app.

unfortunately this app does not offer any disagreement with the week's winners, as often I get more points, but still get different results. I often lose interest in playing when it gives me hard sentences to translate... I never translate these sentences Recently I have started to skip the chapter for no reason. Does anyone have the same issues?

A really creative and effective learning app. The listening exercises and stories takes the learning to the next level and makes it really fun! I think the curriculum is well thought out and the ad free lesson rates are p reasonable compared to what a foreign language learning institution would cost!

Many people complain about losing themselves in the game or trying to get to the top of the leaderboard and forgetting the actual learning, the solution is actually simple, turn off the friends/leaderboard option, I did it many months ago and now I enjoy the app and learn at my own pace without any pressure.

It was fun at the start but it was really hard to keep a long streak, Like when I was busy that day or just couldn't get to do it, it would fail me and bring me to a zero streak, it also started to get boring and like a job and the lessons would force you to have the sound up so if u badly needed to do it in a public space it would have to have it on loud to hear it otherwise you just couldn't do it

Love spending time each day learning through visual, auditory, and verbal methods. Lessons are layered and varied to keep on reinforcing vocabulary already learned. Learning hiragana, katakana, and kanji along the way is helping me to build confidence.150 days in. Am definitely going to continue. A great escape from doomscrolling and the news of the day. Teaming up with friends for challenges makes it more fun and I feel part of a language learning community. Love my Japanese Duolingo lesso

cool app but takes forever to convince the algorithm to give you a question about something other than wanting an apple. further, I find AI disgusting and wish that the premium I paid for would get rid of ads for it instead of having this app shill a premium+ subscription that's right you can pay 13$ a month to still get ads now. love en****ification

You can't know a word that you haven't been taught ! the teaching format is inadequate and kind of backwards. You're supposed to introduce a word or term , define it conjugate and teach proper usage. Duolingo throws in new words makes user guess the definition and grades them. Not bad but it could be a lot better

incorrect Point amounts awarded I have submitted six bug reports on the same issue and it is not been corrected. incorrect point amounts are being awarded, particularly in the 2x bonus rounds. this incorrect calculation has prevented me from advancing to higher levels. it has been going on for weeks and no response received from Duolingo regarding my bug reports

Quite a good app. Last unit (revision unit) greatly improved! Very good for beginners, to get some basic knowledge of the language. After a while though you don't really feel you are making any progress. I keep doing my lessons just to keep a regular contact with the language but might have to consider other options to actually learn Greek properly.

Edited 02/02: Old account has been reinstated, so my hearts are back and the ads have reduced. Thank you!! Previous review: I used to love Duolingo. I have a 1,172 day streak, so I've been invested in learning through the app for over 3 years, but I'm considering leaving along with others that I know. The new insane amount of ads, the paywall for hearts - only being allowed 5 mistakes (mistakes are so important for learning and development!) it''s ruined my entire experience.

My early experience studying here is good but not anymore. As the sentence gets more complicated, i found that the English translation starting to feel unnatural and won't allow user to suggest otherwise. Its frustrating and not exactly a good selling point. And the option to gain heart by reviewing past mistake is also blocked. A shame.

I think this is a super game for family, friends and others to learn languages and have the best learning experience with duolingo but hou can also practice math, music and more enjoyable things on this app. They have a 7 day trial for super duolingo max to upgrade your learning but either are great and I would definitely recommend this splendid app!

It is nice. Sadly, for me it gets repetitive in a few days. And that streak reminder is SO TERRIBLY FRUSTRATING(!!!), that I already had deleted the app few times. I come back after few months feeling that I have to make some effort to learn the language. But soon again it becomes more frustration, then a habit...

I use DuoLingo for Spanish and love it. Vocabulary, grammar and conjugation are all improving slowly. There are reading, speaking and listening exercises. Only downside is the amount of ads with the standard app. DuoPro is much better and you can get into a better flow.

I have the paid version and there are a lot of options to learn a language, the app is really useful and I like it. The only downside is that they use english as a second language and the translations are not the most correct. And another thing that I don't like is that many sentences are very strangely constructed, about animals that are like humans, a lot about same sex couples and many other examples that don't make much sense.

I haven't put in the time required until the past few days. I do know I understand much more than when I started with Duolingo. That said, when I hear Spanish being spoken it's so fast! With Duolingo I'm learning the words so I can pick them out when hearing spoken Spanish. I'm using premium, I might go to Max, we will see after I ensure I'm committed. I've tried a couple of the AI Spanish coach style apps but I was frustrated within 10 minutes. Duolingo is much 'friendlier'.

it is excellent for reading and listening comprehension, as well as building vocabulary and grammar. Its limitation is that there is no really strong method for improving spoken proficiency. However, the gamification structure is very relaxing.

It's a great app to learn other languajes. But there are two issues that made me rate this 2 stars. -1 star is due to the missleading extra gems texts. If a user already has 15 gems, and you guys add a pop up stating "Claim 30 more gems" that means the total should be 45, not 30. Not fun Duolingo. -2 stars is due to the recharge of the hearts. Last night I ended up with 2 hearts. 11 hours later i still have 2 hearts and neet to wait 30 minutes to get one extra. Not the first time it happened.

Used to be a great app but I'm worried about the direction. You used to be able to practice to earn hearts which was a very good system but this has recently been removed. Most recently, however, they removed accessibility settings including dark mode - the reasons why, I cannot fathom. I'm honestly quite worried this will start of trend of duolingo removing features for no reason at the cost of a worse user experience

The new animations are extremely slow and irritating. Even for paid subscription you end up wasting a lot of time. this is a big deterrent to use the app. most people want to come on use their time efficiently and learn something new. But now we end up wasting time watching slow animations which do not add value. Duolingo is losing out and taking a step in the wrong direction.

Most recently it's very bad. XP-Boosts are shown at first but not active. It skipped 6 Units (like I knew them already) , and put them in a different order, so I don't really know what to repeat and can't learn it properly. Also the new lesson themes and the way of interacting with modules within the lesson feels weird. Big sad

Horrible Changes year over year. The latest update has taken a lot of the fun out of it. I don't enjoy it anymore & most days i don't even want to open the app. The new layout is unfriendly an makes it seem like a chore. Constant app lags. Does not register my data at the cost of falling behind in leagues and having to pay diamonds for streak freezes even when I am active! Happens frequently! Doesn't award chests that you earn either. This is unacceptable.A slap in the face to dedicated learners

App has been good, except now I'm getting ads with fake "x" buttons! When you click the fake "x" it takes you to more ads that you can't close out of, instead you have to backtrack to the original ad. Completely ridiculous. I understand why the non paid version has ads, but Duolingo should vet the ads and not allow those with fake/deceptive buttons.

I was using this app for a lonb time (almost 3 years). There are some annoying/diappointing issues. 1) XPs are bookes randomly. I have reported the issue, but so far no response. Counting total number of XPs shouldn't be that complicated. 2) When you rate from the app it gives a 5 star without any option/confirmation. I will change this to 4 star when they fix these. In general: exercises are a bit repetitive, sometimes too simple. Very minimal grammatical rules/explanations are available.

I used to love the app but now that the practice to earn hearts feature is gone I will probably be deleting. used to be I'd run out of hearts and practice to earn em back, getting much needed review in the process and I would proceed to taking more lessons afterwards. Now it's just frustrating when I can't get the dang lesson multiple times in a row and have to watch a bunch of ads and learn nothing.

Please make use of the triple XP bonus optional. If I'm opening the app to do one lesson, I don't want to use it then. I may want to use it later, but it keeps applying automatically and most of it is wasted. I'll change my review when that feature is improved. Sorry to give feedback this way, but I've already reached out to support about this.

Was great until they started really pushing you to pay. A significant ampunt of ads as well as only giving you one free practice heart at a time are majow downfalls now. It now costs more than an amazon prime membership, but certainly doesn't bring that much value. If you could learn to properly speak a language with just this app it might be worth it, but it is not since you will need this among other resources to be fluent.

The words seem quite random... and not conversationally all that useful... but only up to 2nd or 3rd band... so maybe it will change. I don't like that sometimes it introduces new words without teaching us what they are.. and no explanation on them or how to use them.. which usually results in using up a bunch of hearts. if you are on the free plan, this is a problem. Also .. I don't like some of their 'common phrases' like .."we are big and rich" ,. That's a bad thing to say.

This game is fun and all not gonna lie but it takes you an ENTIRE week to move up in the leagues! and besides the friends quests are SO easy! besides your buddy that you partner up with doesn't get any notifications about you choosing them! and you have to buy freeze streaks! so frustrating!!!!!!!

It's great. I really love the UI and everything. And the most important, it's really efficient and you can really learn vocab, grammar and etc. But the only problem is for the phonetic part, it's kind of weak in hearing the word. e.g(you need to shout, otherwise it doesn't hear you.)

It's a very fun learning game for language,music and maths. It's events,side lessons and leaderboards are very competitive. You can review your mistakes from your most recent lessons. It makes you learn the words you are weak at. The creativity of learning in Duolingo is fascinating and fun. It's not like other learning apps where you spend more time watching ads than actually learning. Its almost a perfect learning app but I want them to add a trading system so we can trade gems and other stuff

Duolingo is absolutely amazing! I can still learn languages without a subscription, and it's very friendly. I love how it gets angry if we lose our streak because it wants us to be dedicated to what we're learning! The learning process is amazing, and I had memorized my first lesson in only a day! I'm so glad I get to learn my language with Duolingo!

This is my pov. I started learning Finnish around 2 years ago, my exp is absolute trash. First few units were fine, but then, the lessons became so fast and hard, the app doesn't even have the logic. It literally became Impossible for me to continue without google translate. Then just for streak, I started doing maths lessons, but nowadays, the lessons are so hard, you literally need a paper and pen to solve it. Like I just need to do quick little tricky maths lessons, not graduate in the app.

I love this app for learning. However, my subscription used to include explanations and user questions and answers for learning. Now, the plans have changed, and I'd have to pay an additional fee for those features I used to have and loved utilizing. At least I still have a family account, and my app is ad-free.

Bring back old Duolingo, please! I actually bought the premium version to use it offline and now they removed this feature. Why the frick would you do that? And also: I very much dislike the new linear path. It so confusing and unclear compared to the older system. I cancelled my subscription since it doesn't have any real benefits anymore. Bring back offline lessons :( Edit: It's even worse now and they continue to shittify it Before the new linear path it was such a brilliant app. Now shite

I don't think the learning method is effective and efficient. I started learning Japanese (I have around 25k experience points which translates to days spent learning), and the time it takes you to get to conversational sentences is just too long. Duolingo is motivating but wastes your time. It doesn't follow any sensible can-do approach, it teaches you "lawyer" before "hello". Other learning apps immediately equip you with conversational basics. Ultimately a waste of your time and motivation.

Deceptive and hidden pricing I have scoured both their website and their app, and as far as I can tell the only way to find out what the subscription cost is for their premium content is to sign up for a free trial. But to do that U 1st have to divulge payment info. I will NEVER give out my payment info without 1st knowing the full cost of what I am purchasing/ subscribing to. Even if U want to gift a subscription U must first enter your payment info before U R shown a price.

It was so much fun and addictive. Besides, it's free as long as you're satisfied enough. I love this app so much. No costs, free learning, (improvement on vocabulary, and grammar), improved speech day by day. @DUOLINGO , please keep this app free. It's useful and effective for people like me. Thousands of people learning languages are relying on you. Thank you for making this app.

It's a really good app, I used it about half a year. BUT I got a problem with the Listening part , recently when listening to conversations, there is a difference between the text that is displayed and the audio. I hope the App creators see these comments and fix it🙏🏻

I've definitely learnt a lot from this app however I've hit a wall. It doesn't explain certain grammar rules clearly so I feel like I'm just guessing half the time which is unfortunate. There are times where I feel I'm making loads of progress and then it becomes repetitive. It's a great alternative to doom scrolling though. Worth trying

Came back after 1 year because I've heard Duolingo died. I'm one of the suspects of not coming back. So now, since there are new courses ( Math and Music ) I decided to ditch my Japanese and Spanish and try on Math! Nice course by the way, I can subtract and add easily. Thanks to the moderators and developers for this awesome app! Loving it when I started my relationship with Duo in 2023. Love, Yana.

Looks like this Max transition is such a mess. I see completely different options on different platforms now. On iOS different, on android sometimes Max partially available sometimes not like A/B testing but price is same with the full iOS version. Browser nothing which I use generally when I use my laptop. Expecting a smooth transition from Duolingo team actually. So now we have to pick platform according to exercise we do.

the app is really good, I've been using it for months. I must say, there's one thing that gets me to break my streak. See, I'm learning Chinese but I just wanna learn how to speak, not to read or write hanzi. If there was a way to modify learning paths, it would be great...I just get bored every time I have to move on to hanzi and I ignore the app for months. I just wanna speak the language

I really enjoy this app, it's helped me a lot- but I'm not in the position to pay the kind of fees that are expected in order to truly progress with my learning. I just think it's a little expensive, and more things seem to be becoming restricted, but understand not everyone is in the same financial position, and some people may find this subscription manageable.

Duolingo has been good ever since i started using it. today i did a question right and it said i did it wrong.when i left the lesson to regain hearts,it said to watch a ad instead of doing a lesson.when i pressed the add and finished it i got no hearts.pressed it like 7 times and watched multiple ads but still got no hearts.tryed to look at the leaderboard and it said you're on maintenance break.tgen it said try to access a lesson on my home screen.tryed that and it didn't work. thx a lot duo😤
