"I'm thoroughly enjoying Duolingo! This language learning app is engaging, interactive, and incredibly effective. With its gamified approach and bite-sized lessons, I'm making progress faster than I thought possible. 5/5 stars - highly recommended for anyone looking to learn a new language!"

Decent app, teaches you some pretty stupid sentences that would never be said like 'my dog wears pants' and doesn't really do a very good job of explaining why things are the way they are. got pretty annoyed when it started making me play a short game as one of the lessons. To me they are not helpful. Teaches a lot of very formal language and well randomly start introducing another word for the same word later on, with no explanation to which situations/ countries it suits.

I'm updating the review and not changingthe rating because nothing has changed, practically even after flagging major bugs on the Japanese course it still hasn't been solved sent even an email to support and nothing. Duolingo is really dead and the devs don't care about the app. Also why can't android users have the simple feature to drag words like the ios ones can? Definitely trash!

The app recently removed dark mode from Android users making it difficult to use. This feature is not just a cosmetic preference but a necessary accessibility setting for users with vision issues. I've contacted customer service and was given a very generic response that avoided addressing my concerns. I am unable to use the app even at the lowest brightness settings on my phone. My inquiries for cancellation/refund are being ignored. 0/5

The app is very good, it makes it very easy to learn other languages etc, but the design is a little too childish, and not having super is so annoying, I have to watch an add everytime I complete a lesson, I can't make more than 4 mistakes at a time, getting gems is so difficult but they get spent in an instant, and someone not having super can't compete in leagues(practically) Otherwise, I think this is an amazing app

Okay, I like that I am actually learning a language. But it drives me crazy when we have competitions where we need to earn a certain amount of XP, you get a boost where you "Earn Double XP for 10 Minutes" and then it doesn't give you your double XP! This had happened multiple times, please fix it! I want my XP!

If I could give this app 0 stars I would. Sure it LOOKS helpful but it's really just another app that deserves to sit and rot in your phone. I've been on since March 2022 or so, practiced multiple languages through the years, and nothing has stuck. I don't know why I keep using the app when it's not actually helping me learn anything. I can do a language I already know how to speak and still manage to fail. and not no mention the fact the app isn't even free at this point. 👎🔥

This refers to German learning from English, and covers first 2 sections. Translations are loose, mapping idiomatic expressions from one language to another, making it hard to understand constructs and meanings of single words. Etymological knowledge should be used, in particular for derived words. Cultural aspects are confounding: e.g., mapping 5 o'clock to siebzehn (17) Uhr. Choice of words to teach is questionable (e.g., schnecke). Speech recognition always fails on some words like "Euro"

Hello Duolingo I am very impressed by your learning app and i practice on it daily but it is a humble request from me that please stop sending me emails every day. You send mail every day and the same notification is also sent to me from the Duolingo app. I like the fact that you notify me about my learning skills but you don't have to send me the same notification twice.

Unfair! I am a subscriber and when I purchased the app there were explanations of mistakes available, and now during my paid subscription you have removed that and have replaced it with an additionally priced option, taking part of what I had paid for away. Also! Why aren't there more languages, I would like to learn Maltese for example.. You developed the Klingon and Valyrian but not other actual languages instead? You should be ashamed!

it is a very helping app. I am recently learning Spanish in it and it is really helping me to learn it faster. there are 50 + languages in which you can learn easily anywhere also there are very fun challenges that 200 + people are working on it. it is also a thing named Streak, it basically marks your regular learning goals which motivates US and forces brain to do during everyday. although it's a very helpful app and if you download the super ,there are free hearts and no adds.

amazing I'm in love. most of the kids In my school speak Spanish specifically 1 of my classmates who says stuff in Spanish so the teacher doesn't understand,but now I can tell the teacher what she's staying. long story short barely any adds amazing tech and overall a good app. I recommend this if you're trying to learn any language. BTW there are a lot of languages to learn.

I paid a large sum of money for Super Duolingo primarily to "remove ads" which is listed as a feature. But then they just prompt you to upgrade to Duolingo Max between every lesson. I classify this as an advert. I contacted them and they said that the prompts cannot be disabled so I refunded the purchase through Google.

What I love about it is that it lets me practice a lot. Even without grammar-explaining, (which I can just look up on Google) I can memorize the words easily, contrary to many language-learning apps. However, I would definitely love for Korean to get some improvements (bigger chapters; stories) as it has become the 5th most learned language on here. EDIT: Thanks Duo for adding more to Korean. I'm very happy with the new voice practices. Also, the pronounciations are now better than they were :)

Let's see. Since my last review the functional quality has improved, no doubt. It's become fun again to use the app and come back everyday. However: Today I was greeted by a blindingly white background. Why? The dark theme that I saw and loved until yesterday, was great. There doesn't appear to be a setting to change this. The app is no longer easy on the eyes. That deserves giving it a couple of stars less.

I love the app BUT my account now seems to be broken. No matter how many new words I learn, the progress bar on my "Scholar" Achievement does not change.. It seems to be stuck at "256 words (Level 5)" despite the fact I have learnt well over 700 words in Greek language alone. I will upgrade my Google Play review rating once this error has been fixed on my account. (I have also sent an in-app "bug report" feedback message)

I am a paying user with the Super subscription. Ads for more expensive Max are everywhere, it's madness.. they are included in learning paths in the form of lessons you cannot do as Super (and paying) user, they are also pushed when finishing chapters/lessons and now even when going to the practice hub... Duolingo please, please, please stop pushing these ads to your paying users!!! Till then I would not recommended the super subscription.

When it works properly the app is great and I was making good progess with learning French. However, after six weeks a glitch occurred that changed my French learning score from about 9 to 129. In practical terms this meant I was suddenly launched into a high level of French language which I hadn't the skills to cope with. I sent two messages to the Duolingo help section on the app and these were ignored. This is despite this being a subscription account. So I am now unable to progress 😒

there's a huge glitch with the scoring they haven't fixed and the way they expect you to know certain things, like conjunctions without explanation isn't helpful in learning. but the way of helping you memorize is better than other programs.

so good so far, easy to learn and understand the language, beginner friendly. If you want more output you have to practice listening to speech, conversation and start talking. and the redesigned version just made me stop using it. It kindof creeped me out, and somehow my motivation just ended.

Duolingo is great for learning different languages. But a little detail here demotivates ig not only me but everyone. they give you 5 chances if you make more than 5 mistakes then you have to quit for that day which really demotivates me. and very frequently they offers you to get super Duolingo with ads which is really annoying. without these details Duolingo is a super app for learning languages I think if you want learn quick language this is the best app for you.

(Learning finnish) Useful for learning some vocabulary but not much else. The teaching of grammar is very bad and - at least for the language I am learning - the lesson types are incredibly limited. The app's free-user experience also goz significantly worse recently. Heart recharge time changed from 4 to 5 hours, unable to practise to earn hearts back unless you already are empty, etc. The app is mid at best, and a useless timesink at worse.

Duolingo app is a crazy good app for language learning. The best part about it is the way of teaching a language. I'm learning English over 1 year and now I am a people who can fluently speak English and the app increases my confidence to speak the language. Duolingo is a very good app for language learning and understanding. Please go through the app for availing yourself in the vast world of different language. And and now it latitude maths and music which is very good.

the app seems to be more interactive and effective of terms of learning a new language. whenever I come across a new word, it's algorithm seems to make us repeat it a couple more times until we know how it is used. Overall, the app is really interactive and even reminds us when we forget to practise for a day. It kinda makes us want to continue our streak which is really helpful to learn.

I have used Duo for 5+ years and am a top 2% learner and I am very disappointed in this latest version on Android. The last version made it harder to earn hearts with practice, now in this version makes it impossible. Your only two options are pay for unlimited or watch a ton of commercials. I'm just done. Thank you for making a supposed free app so painful that I quit altogether.

I used to use this app all the time back in 2023, and now I've come back to it to try and learn a language they have made the free version completely impossible to use (practice hearts), and the ads are completely in your face at all times. I wouldn't recommend installing this and just using a different language app as this one has failed

Hi. I've been using Duolingo for 760 days for free. The thing is, I'm pissed off by the amount of ads for buying super Duolingo that's been popping up on my account for over a month. The worst part is that I have to give 100 gems to play challenges which is the main thing I play to earn XP. That's ridiculous... Please fix it.

Duolingo is such a great app!💯 I've learnt a lot for the past week's, I'm getting so good at Spanish and I love it. on top of that, I would suggest more engagements with users where as they're supposed to communicate with other people in the languages chosen. but to add to that I love Duolingo and I hope other users find it helpful and fun.😄

before I rated this 5 stars but I have changed my mind for voice exercise where we are asked to speak, I speak exactly how it is pronounced but yet Duolingo marks me wrong and makes me lose heart and the point of the hearts don't even make sense we are here to learn not to write an exam. I get people can make mistakes but deprive them for learning because they make 5 mistakes in one day and they refuse to pay for super Duolingo so I suggest you fix the voice excersice stuff.😡

it's a great app , and spending time on doulingu makes you feel like you are in a competition, u want to get promoted in the next rank and keep your streak up for obvious reasons orr.rrr.........you know what happens... i really like the story mode and game mode used for learning, at the end i want to tell that this is the best language app ever made

it's an amazing app to any language l am also learning Chinese but the bad thing is that there are limited hearts and ads Which stops the progress because if you do any mistake you lose one heart and in learning language you will do mistake but if you lose your all hearts you can't practice again you will have to wait for a long time and everyone don't have this much time so your progress will be slow or stop and if you will go to buy the super Duolingo the price is high but it's a good app.

It's good but the monetisation has become a joke. 20second adds every minute of excersizes. There's only so many times you can say no to a subscription in 10 minutes without it being intensely irritating. I think there are better language app options that don't shove their product down your throat quite as violently.

I used to like it, but now it gets worse with every update. It's less fun to learn, since I get exactly the same sentences in the Daily refresh OVER AND OVER AGAIN. I notice I had no progress, haven't learned anything new for weeks now. It's disappointing. Every day I open the app hoping to learn something new, but get a lot of SSDD. Very disappointed.

I've had Duolingo for a week - big fan. but the effin emails man! I've unsubscribed from 4 different categories of email already - good job, you resched a new experience level. Oh hey, your friend achieved a new level! oh hey, don't forget to do duolingo today. Do you like Duolingo? Add a friend! The app's great. But stop blowing up my email. I already get notifications from the app ♡

I am currently learning French, and i garuntee this app is the best for learning a language. The streak system and candy crush like UI always keep u addicted . But the reason I gave it 4 stars is cuz it starts showing ads after some time, though they are small ones you can't skip them and they appear after every lesson completed.But if u ignore them this app is perfect in almost all aspects . 10/10

I'm so frustrated with the update. If I ran out of hearts I could practice to earn a heart and this counted as practice for the day. Now I have to watch adverts and retry the level. I'm on day 722 and I'm struggling with a level, I've watched so many videos, I keep failing and I'm overwhelmed. I need to take a break from this level but I don't want to lose my 722 day streak. Amzing. very fun and interactive. I'd like to suggest more grammar and morphology rules of French, Spanish, and Italian through Portuguese medium since it is easier to learn Spanish , French and Italian through Portuguese medium and it's easier to learn any of the 4 latter languages using any of the 4 as a medium to learn the other and not only English medium. All ads shown are now horrible spam links originating from China. There's so many ads it degrades the app to basically an ad spamming engine. They took away the ability to do a practice lesson to earn back lost hearts so you're getting punished for making mistakes.. Any features that're actually helpful for learning are behind a paywall as a premium feature. For an app that says it's making learning accessible, it sure is inaccessible...

too much in app purchase, way too much even in the case you already pay for a plan. complicated options which make the app very hard to understand, prize or specials you earn can't be used later (or can't be kept) complicated point and league promotion. poor presentation, no verbs, ne grammar. regretting now to have paid the premium. Very good app. Good for learning languages. I just have one nitpick. Oftentimes, and I mean VERY often, the music keys in the music course do not work. One time, I had a ×50 combo, but the F key did not work, and BOOM! There goes the combo. Please fix this problem. Other than that, it is good, and I recommend you try Super. still a poor learning tool that used to offer explanations of your mistakes but removed that to push you to pay for the premium plan well after getting the plan, they removed the explanations so you're only learning by repeating the same mistakes and memorizing the correct version without understanding it. Two years ago I placed a low review, I regret to inform that it has only been going down hill from there. Give me back duolingo from 3 years ago and I will pay for it. I think this app Duolingo is very good for me because alphabets are much better than before so I would like to try and get some of the vocabularies, grammars and the sentence structures more. Duolingo app is a highly useful and enjoyable language-learning app that makes acquiring new languages accessible to everyone. The interactive lessons are engaging, and the gamified approach keeps motivation high. While the free version provides a great foundation, maintaining a high level of discipline is key to making real progress. The app is excellent for building vocabulary, improving reading and listening skills, and reinforcing grammar through daily practice.

The move to 'AI ALL THE THINGS!' and the usage of dark pattern UX to try and force further upgrades means I'm done. I've paid for a subscription for years, and it's very disappointing to see greed get in the way of what was a fun if not incredibly robust language learning experience. Save your money and go with another app.

Duolingo should add manual dark mode button rather than following phone's theme. My phone was on dark mode but duolingo's theme is on light mode. I tried to find the solution but theres nothing to fix it. It was not like this before and it changed to lightmode itself after my 416 streak. It's just buggy and it hurts my eyes when I'm using duolingo at night. I don't like this app anymore and my motivation is fading away because of this.

I have used Duolingo for years. The new format update has completely removed the ability for me to learn in my own way. Everyone learns differently, but this new format only allows for a "single path of learning" option. It has honestly made me give up on 3 years of language learning through this platform. Additionally, I pay $180 a year for this app, and suddenly they have introduced an 'extra cash grab' called "MAX, where they ask for more money for new features. This should be included anyway

Many of the sentences in the more advanced stages are too long, and a small error requires you to type out the whole sentence instead. Also the prompts during errors are not helpful! Instead of zooming in on the areas with the errors, duolingo just marks the whole sentence as wrong and I have to zoom in on each word to find the error.

Used to be a great app, but have become increasingly tedious to use with frequent and intrusive ads after every single lesson. The heart system essentially punishes learners for making mistakes, which is literally the absolute worst way to learn anything. To anyone considering using duolingo - you will be much better off with a another app that doesn't punish you for making mistakes nor takes up 25+% of your time dedicated to learning with intrusive ads

Inconsistent in application of rules for translation, will sometimes accept vernscular, other times direct translation only and rarely only accepts the spirit of the translation (ie for proverbs or idioms). You will not be told why your answer is rejected, only given the sentence they want. If you have other resources this is nice practice, and great considering it's a free resource, but don't expect it to actively teach you edit: -1☆, agressive ads ads between lessons, errors repaired via ads

I've been using Duolingo to learn Spanish, but unfortunately, it's been a slow and unchallenging experience. The app doesn't teach you much beyond basic phrases and vocabulary, and the pace is far too slow. I wish there was a customizable pace option to suit different learning styles. The repetitive exercises get boring, and I feel like I'm wasting time. If you're looking for a challenge and want to make meaningful progress in learning a language, I'm not sure Duolingo is the best choice.

Duolingo is great! I've been learning Indonesian for the past 431 days. It's a good program which definitely teaches and encourages you,and also trains your listening skills as well as reading. I have the paid membership which is wonderful, but you can definitely get by with the free version. Doing a few Indonesian lessons is now a daily habit and it helps my brain to stay awake.

Reading my previous review from nine years ago is depressing. This was once a great app for learning languages. Now it's probably the worst of the bunch. They keep adding in-app purchases to try to get you to spend more money. They've gotten rid of the community aspect that made it easier to understand how the languages work. The characters they spent all that money on are super annoying. Want to understand your mistakes? Gotta pay extra for that. It's just about making as much money now. Boo.

Great app for learning vocab. Useless for learning grammar. More effort seems to be put into sounds and animations than improving the language courses.

I love Duolingo, it's perfect!! It has been only a day, and Duolingo helped me so much in learning Italian! In fact, it orginizes everything you have to learn in units. If your hearts are done, you can't play and have to wait 2 hours or watch an ad.. However, that won't change my rating. I love Duolingo so much!!🥰🥰❤️

removing 'practice for more hearts' is just rude. I almost have a year streak, but if this is how it's gonna be, I can find other, better, ways of learning a new language. I guess duo really is 💀 (also the new character based ads are supremely annoying. at least duo was cute)

Great app, lots of ways to learn the language. Not 5 stars because the phone call part where you have a phone conversation with a character (Lily) does not work at all and it won't let you leave. Volume cuts in and out and it freezes up.

this is actually terrible. I'm changing my review to be lower as the state of the app has become significantly worse. the addition of a life bar (similar to energy meters used in most mobile games) is possibly one of the scummiest things this company has done. they are timegating your progression in the language you're learning for the sole purpose of making you either watch ads, or upgrade. speaking of. the free user experience is clearly made worse.

I don't know what the deal is with this app but it does not listen to your voice correctly no matter how well you say things. It constantly misses the first part of sentences as well as repeatedly not pick up other words when you're supposed to speak into the challenge no matter how well you do.

I've been using the free version of Duolingo for a while. a very useful and nice app to learn a language. I used to love Duo! Recently restarted using it and I can't stand it. Why remove the option to practice for more hearts? Just adds even more anxiety than was already present through forced streaks. Thanks for getting me this far, but if you're going to pay for an app, there are much better options that will get you much further.

Wonderful, i think this is a masterpiece... You only need to take a few minutes, then boom~ you're just like knowing all of new language. Edited: The newest update it's the worse. Features of Duolingo : Wide range of languages, Gamification, Interactive Characters and Exercises, Adaptive learning, Free and Premium options, Events and many more.

The app is "ok." Good: Lots of different topics and words etc. easy to follow along. Bad: Pressure to compete. Pressure to have streaks. Duolingo is an amazing app for learning English. Its user-friendly interface and well-organized lessons make it easy to follow. dark mode is completely disappeared and it was beyond distracting and no longer fun to use because I'm either learning extremely early in the morning or very late at night and a dark room and my entire phone's in dark mode minus this app is the only one somehow so now I just do one lesson today just enough to keep my streak until they bring it the option for dark mode back

Terrible (non-existent) customer service. I'm reading Russian, Chinese, and Japanese at a first grade level in only three weeks each for free. This may or may not be the best tool to learn a language at a natively fluent level, but to learn the basics for the cost of sitting through a comparative handful of skippable ads? Amazing.

After having paid for duo lingo and used the free version I can say that you should save your money and time and pay for lessons. Great app! It makes learning feel like a fun game, turning education into an engaging experience. The interactive elements keep you motivated, and it's easy to learn at your own pace while enjoying the process. Highly recommend it for anyone who wants to learn in a fun and dynamic way!

There could be more explanations. and better ways to go back and look things up. Multiple choice questions often too easy, so you just go through the motions instead of really learning. What on earth am I to do with thousands of gems? Why does the app function differently on different gadgets? The app does more on my phone than on my desktop.

just returned from Germany and this app helped me gain an experience I was missing on previous visits, talking to people. Best App ever to learn a language. Easy to follow, engaging and actually helpful than just teaching a thing or too. It's a fun, and really interesting way to learn a new language! 5 stars, minimal ads, and not every feature is locked behind a pay wall. they give you plenty of reminders after a trial to cancel, to make sure they don't bill you for something you don't want.

the app is amazing the lessons are correct and great. Duolingo is getting progressively worse over the years. Great features for learning such as the discussion section of each example are now hidden behind pay walls. I am now thinking of moving away to other free resources since I don't learn much on this anymore. Greed destroys everything.

Duolingo is a very great app with a user-friendly interface, and the experience is top-notch. It would be great if they could begin with basics, like the alphabet, and numeracy, then vocabulary before the lexis and structure, it is the best I've ever used so far

Duoling is a great app and totally recommend, and it's so cool you can learn as many languages as you want at at time, plus be able to do music and math. The only thing is those rare occasions when you get adds recommending duo pro, but once you get past those, everything else is amazing !

Loving this app. Educational and addictive. Using it with my kids and went for the paid version to have unlimited hearts and more songs in the music lessons. Apart from one story in the English lessons that I considered inappropriate for kids, I have no compliants.

Actually one of the best options if you want to learn any language, specifically if you don't have the time to take a class like me. Duolingo is an excellent language-learning app with a fun, gamified experience. It offers bite-sized lessons, making it easy to practice daily. With interactive exercises, it’s ideal for beginners. Duolingo is a fun, gamified app for learning languages with bite-sized lessons and a user-friendly interface. It offers a wide variety of languages and is free, with optional premium features. While great for beginners, it lacks depth for advanced learners. Ideal as a supplementary tool for vocabulary and grammar practice.

Duolingo's limitations: Limited Depth: Duolingo primarily focuses on basic grammar and vocabulary, which might not be enough for achieving fluency or conversational proficiency. Oversimplification: Some users find Duolingo's simplified approach to grammar and pronunciation to be inaccurate or misleading. Repetition: The repetitive nature of the exercises can become tedious for some learners. Lack of Cultural Context: Duolingo doesn't provide much cultural context, which is crucial for understan

This app is super helpful and does a good job at keeping you motivated. So much better than Rosetta Stone, and free too!

Duolingo, is an excellent support to deeper study of a language. Quick fun exercises that are easy to fit into a day, plus it's possible to do for free, just watch a few ads. Good to revise GCSE level language, and prompt deeper study.

For years I've used this app & listened to the mantra, we want to make learning free for everyone. This company doesn't appear interested in that, which is especially frustrating as so much is now AI generated content and ads. Long gone are the user forums with language learners and native speakers interacting together. Everything is directed towards paid subscriptions and frankly AI generated content isn't worth it when there's more productive learning tools on the market. Bye 2060 day steak.

I am lowering the rating from 5 to 2 bcoz after the recent update they have removed the practice session with watch ads to earn hearts. I don't mind watching ads but removing the practice session has made the app almost unusable to the point that I almost broke my 1200+ days streak. Two stars only bcoz this app helped me for years before jumping the shark

Duolingo is a very good app if you want to learn a language you can learn any language you want and can even learn multiple languages at the same time.if you buy super Duolingo you will get unlimited hearts,practice your mistakes,ai phone call with Lily to practice speaking and more so I would definitely recommend Duolingo for u guys 5/5 stars

it was a lovely time for the most part, and highly encourages people learning together but the more I hear about it's obsession with the unnecessary addition of ai services, and hearing that it impacts the user's to the point that native speakers are being judged by said ai to be sent to the very beginning the less I can condone it's usage. really hope they go back to using real people and knowledge for these things, not something they just bought the rights to online

Bottom line is Duolingo won't really teach you a language because if they did you would stop giving them money. Also why when learning katakana can you not type in the hiragana directly from the Japanese keyboard, why does it make you type in the romanji and then translate it to hiragana.

this is a much lower quality app now that you can't practice to get free hearts. it used to be an educational service that could be utilized for free while only engaging with learning materials. kinda cool, but the turtle speed does not work, and several of the activities return an error when I click on them. I'd expect the app to be better if I'm paying $120 for it. Also, the text on many of the activities is very small and hard to read.

Everything is good I guess, but, there's one thing that I think they could change. The spanish speakers of all Latin America only have the option of learn English, Swedish, Portuguese, Russian, German, French, Italian, and Catalan, if you want to learn something like Japanese, Korean, etc, you need to know english first, what if people that doesn't know english wanna learn japanese or korean?, they can't.

this app is fit for kids and you can also do this for free and you can get a new super Duolingo for $ 0. 0 plus you can also learn a math and music not only coins of country languages but you can also learn the music and mouth which is better than just language so this is probably a good app

it's an amazing app , you can learn any language just by simply playing 😉, amazing And it's easy and there are many features which make it easy and efficient. amazing please try but there are also things that need to be improved 5 star from me . 🤛

this app tanked. used to be 5 star. they make certain things difficult to find like the shop. and removed helpful tools like practicing mistakes and matching words. It's disgusting, demanding money for knowledge is the height of cynicism. I like this app, BUT i understood when they added the option to only gain one heart when reaching zero and having to practice to get it BUT NOW, there is a thing where you have to watch an add, i wish i could just farm inf exp using the hearts, so you guys bring back infinite heart practices or else...

When I started it I thought it was boring but when I got involved I have now improved my French. I believe with time I will be able to speak it fluently. I thank the inventors of Duolingo as this will really help all people across the globe 🌍

Duolingo is amazing I was learning Chinese and I am doing so well. I have been making a lot of perfect lessons, it helps you want to study and they make you Streak so to make you more excited. I love Duolingo so much and hope other people want to learn from it 💗

I love this app over all cuz it's much more easy to learn a language on this app. Yet I really wish that the Duolingo could add the languages like Bengali and Telugu. Cuz my friend speaks Bengali and it would be great if Duolingo could add these languages.
Worst for speaking exercise, the apps does not recognize speaking even from native speaker. The new animation takes a lot of time. Worst for time sensitive lessons.

This app is really good for new language practice, but the reason I gave 4 stars is because a lot of ads keep popping up, and one more thing, please provide more creativity,because of this, the usage will be very enjoyable.

this is one of the best learning apps around! it's been four-five days and I am already memorizing some words! the start on the language you pick is also really accurate! even if you don't know a word of the language, duolingo will teach you everything from beginning to end as long as you stick with it! I would recommend this for anybody, no matter the language! 10/10.

I really loved this app, but I gave 4 stars because I noticed that in a lesson 'Falstaff guide to humans' the 🔴red fish 🐟 is on table and it's struggling to breathe, can you make a bowl of water and put it in that, I really feel bad for that fish, whenever I get that lesson I just keep on looking at that fish, if you do I'll sure give you 5 stars 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 Thank you so much 🫂 I hope you will read this, that fish really needs your help 🆘

DuoLingo is basically ok. but it is frustrating as hell. The number of mistakes is ridiculous. And unless you get the paid version, you only get so many errors a day. But it's basic flaw is that it does not teach grammar, you only learn by yourself piecing together what worked and what didn't. There is no way to learn a language ONLY via an app like this. It can, at best. augment other sources. Several years ago, there used to be a forum to discuss questions. DL dumped that. Ugh

Does not teach comprehension of any language's rules. Great learning experience but it doesn't give any guidance on pronunciations leading to repeated failed attempts at saying phrases. Doesn't explain anything, randomly introduces new vocabulary without any indication of how the words are supposed to be used. Spoken a loud exercises regularly drop words and syllables and don't explain why they are dropped. Fails to teach verbs and how to conjugate.
it was good but when it comes to the speaking parts, it doesn't listen for squat. I've done everything to fix my mic so this would work but it doesn't want to work.
I've been a user since 2017, but for some reason, the developers have recently decided to disable dark mode. This is a bizarre move and annoying enough to warrant a single star rating.

Yo aprendido mucho en español. Muchas gracias, Duolingo! This app is easy to use, the images are fun and seek to hold you accountable (Duolingo) in practicing daily. The rewards are real and the active community of learners encourage your progress as you learn. You can learn multiple languages at the same time. Kudoes for Duolingo!

There's no longer an option to practice to earn hearts. You now can only watch ads (unhelpful to learning), pay for premium, or wait it out for additional hearts. The learning experience used to be better before this change. They also got rid of grammar explanations on wrong answers for non-premium users

it's a very good opportunity to those who want to learn new language without spending a penny . and I really really appreciate your work duolingo . you are doing a very great job by helping people in their journey of learning a new language . a very heartfelt Thank-you to your team for providing such a effective course without charging . you are really a change maker for many person. I am very glad to use this app .

I give duolingo 5 stars because it makes learning fun like a game and easy to understand . When I don't know about duolingo, I think that the Japanese language is very hard, but after I got news that duolingo is like a game, I start learning in it. Duolingo is a free language learning app and I suggest it if you want to learn a new language.

Fantastic app! I keep running out of Gems though. You guys should introduce a black jack mini game where you can gamble your gems. ...Hold on, I know this sounds crazy, but as I inevitably lose more gems than I gain, it will encourage me to play more Duolingo!

To be completely honest : this is the ultimate best language learning app ever; However I needed to contact the developers but I didn't find the contact option : I don't know why the App froze couple of days in my calendar even though I am absolutely sure I practiced on them (frozen days) ? Can anyone please explain Although I do have the "unfreeze charm" I'm afraid to lose my streek for some technical issues;

Subscribed for a year's super Duolingo course in Japanese for my young child. He was really enthusiastic and determined to complete the course. He managed to maintain a 23-day streak, but the account got locked out. After trying to get the account unlocked by contacting customer service, I was ignored on numerous occasions for 2 months. My child was in tears for the first few days 😢, however, after the long delays of emails being ignored, he has now lost interest. Still no reply, avoid!!!
Just amazing. Has made learning of multiple languages so easy and efficient. But speech recognition is terrible. Please do something about this.
Absolutely amazing! Duolingo has so many languages that you can choose from! I recommend Duolingo for everyone who wants to learn a different language above any other learning app!
The ad after EVERY lesson with the latest update is INCREDIBLY off putting. It takes away the enjoyment that the app had. While perhaps useful, I don't see the fun in Duolingo anymore.

Good for learning foreign language. While learning Arabic I found one problem i. e. it doesn't teach or introduce new words in advance and hence we have to gaze the meaning of that word; this is not appropriate. Other than that the teaching process is good. Thanks Duolingo for giving me the opportunity to learn language free of the coast.
I'm learning japanese so fast and duolingo makes it so easy to stay on track and keep the learning really fun and entertaining at the same time. I really enjoy learning languages now👍👍👍.

I think best bang for buck is the family plan, it allows you to learn with people around you and keep up with it. Great app so far and I haven't had any issues. if you're struggling to learn, I have found sitting down and writing out the sounds and letters help alot.

This app is just perfect! ads are limited that is the thing I love, you can learn the best from duolingo, It deserves more than just 5 stars, I highly recommend to download this app if anyone wants to learn a new language, it focuses to make the base of the language clear making you learn it very easily, I loved it!

This is a wonderful game because now I can speak in other languages. There are two things I can say badly about and they are: I could always see an animation when I finished a lesson but now I can't. Also I would like if there would be more African languages for a better variety.

Duolingo is fantastic! The gamified learning makes language acquisition fun and engaging. I've been making real progress with my Japanese and Spanish skills. Plus, I just finished my lesson streak, so everything's great! (Please let my family out of the basement. 😰)

Duolingo teaches you so many languages and math and music. in Duolingo im learning Chinese. Though it's teaching me simplified Chinese rather than traditional, which I'm hoping there's no difference. If there's a big difference that might be a problem. Other than that problem, five star.
I really enjoyed this app very interactive and look and feel is awesome and provided a nice content for learning. If Its possible can u add kannada language for both sides learning rest is very good.
Dear developers, there is this bug in listening tasks, like "ask lucy" and so on, where the audios of the word do not match the words given. I kindly request you to fix this bug.

The order of teaching makes no sense and the exercises where I have to speak, despite repeating exactly as is said, it gives me wrong. but eventually I say it differently than is said and is right??? makes no sense. The fact that some key languages are not offered to non-english speakers (such as Chinese) is disappointing. And the fact that the leagues are formed not from learners of the same language. i am learning Chinese and I am in league with people learning Italian, German, English...

Great for language learning, but stand by to be inundated with nagging emails if you dare to miss a lesson! I've unsubscribed from emails 6 times in the last 10 days & I'm STILL getting emails, very, very, VERY annoying. I ended up blocking their emails, you really shouldn't have to do that.

GIVE BACK THE PRACTICE TO EARN HEARTS. This app used to be great I had given a 5 star review in the past it used to be a real tool to learn a language for free. Now its completely suffocating for the free user making it an extremely frustrating experience if you dont pay. Its a shame, this app had a great potential and during years it was the best option to learn languages.

I've used the app for years and never found a better one. The repetitive exercises make it highly effective, and its method for teaching letters and hieroglyphs is the best. BUT I’m disappointed to witness how you were adding more and more languages and never included Bengali, the 5th most spoken language. Its exclusion feels unfair. I hope you consider adding it so more people can benefit from your excellent teaching method.

It's a good way to get the bare minimum in the early lessons, i haven't made it to the more advanced lessons but still far its very bare bones and repetitive. which isn't bad but it would probably be better to also do your own studying on the side

excellent app, for new users , and the practice session is so clear in every aspect so , there is little chance of missing . regular use with good challenge will make a person extremely fluent in language.

A failure of a language learning app due to the lack of explanation about language syntax rules. On too many occasions, the learned sentence structure will yield incorrect answers, later on. It has reached the point where a wrong answer causes me to scream at the app "WHY? WHY IS THAT WRONG?". Best to have a real textbook to use in tandem with the app, because Duolingo will not teach you the rules of the language.

I love this app it's such a fun way to learn,such a cute duolingo logo,the day streak keeps me coming back everyday so i dont forget good touch duolingo,I definitely remember the words because the app keeps me intrigued,But! I put 4 stars cause I find the hearts annoy me even though that's why there's a pass but for people who just want to learn this app I'd recommend getting rid of them,plus all the adds are basically just the duolingo pass anyway,Definitely recommend getting this app!
This has been a great way to re-learn my old highschool French (buried from 30 years ago). I like the ability to pick up the lessons in short sessions.

This app used to be good, but has been absolutely ruined by the recent change that stops you practicing to regain hearts. I didn’t mind watching a few adds to use it, but no way am I subscribing to get unlimited hearts so after 565 days and stuck on one exercise for 5 days, I'm done with it.

I've tried the app to improve in my wife's language: she says no one speaks what's trained in the app. It looks like a very old/obsolete version of Korean that don't exist anymore. According to her, I should not use this app to learn Korean.

I have not tried other programs, so I do not know how this compares. However, I am learning the basics of a new language and starting to pick things up in real world conversations. I only use the free version, I imagine you can do a lot more with super duolingo or Max.

Giving a low rating because of their change to how you get hearts. You can no longer practice to earn hearts and keep your streak. You have to pay or watch really annoying and long ads. The app was so much better before this change.

this app is great! I'm learning 3 different languages, music and math! although sometimes it automatically corrects to a different word, and I get it wrong, or it reads me as pressing something else rather than what I selected. All in all, a aMEOWzing app!

I am very obessed with this app,I really like the way it teaches languages and its characters are amazing I like zari,lily and junior.I love this app as its teaches us in a fun . and we can make friends and start quests and friend quests.According to me this app is very amazing.I am learning many languages like english,french,russian,italian,japanese, spanish and extra courses also maths and music.I am learning languages from duolingo for 6 months and its very amazing and easy to learn language.

Very nice...There are no ads...They ask you whether you want to see the ads or not! That's amazing...Totally recommend 👌.....Its really fun, because there are story stages, listening stages etc...Really 👏 🤩 Amazing...9/10

I'm really enjoying the micro learning, but the explanations regarding grammatical rules are completely lacking, and some of the pronunciation modules are buggy. If you don't pay for the subscription for unlimited lives, it gets frustrating very quickly.
I recommend subscribing to super duolingo. The free app gets frustrating when you run out of hearts and the ads are annoying.

Duo provides great value in education for moments in between real life. Relaxing game-style learning with real world benefits and applications. I find the badges and milestones reward system motivational. A beautifully crafted app. I wish more jobs were set up with such clear guidelines!

Duolingo is a good, interactive way of learning a new language however it could be a lot better by explaining how/why phrases are formed in a particular way. Also to display the correct meaning when getting an answer right rather than just saying "Excellent".

They are consistently unable to have the audio match the correct reading of kanji in the Japanese course. Even the audio for the question and answer to the same problem won't be consistent. Like listening problems will constantly have different audio for the answer.
the super free trial is the most annoying thing in the world. if the free version was limiting my practice or if I were to pay, I'd be using drops, not duolingo
Duolingo is a great (and free!) way to learn a language. It provides a way to practice and retain information due to repetition, repetition, repetition.

I didn't think much of Duolingo as a good tool for learning Japanese at first, however, I've learned that as a supplemental tool combined with other things such as the Kaishi 1.5 Anki and Cure Dolly's YouTube channel for Grammer. that combined with regular Japanese immersion has helped me greatly in progressing toward my goal of learning Japanese and I'm very happy with it. The only downside I can think of is that Super is kind of required to make real progress. But it's a commitment so I get it

I have been learning korean from this app.I have improvement but even After I complete my levels It repeats it and doesn't allow me to play another level.It keeps doing it again and again even I do all levels correctly

I liked this app a lot, but after a while, I came back to use it again for learning German. it has changed in the wrong way, too many adds, there is no practice part anymore it has got unfamiliar and I don't like it

Watching creative Ads to earn hearts is definitely positive change from the versions before... More importantly, in my honest opinion, having more control in the app, like it was 3 years ago, was insanely better experience...🥺

Very poor, with multiple translation mistakes being taught to those that do not know better. And now it's virtually impossible to use for free users, as they have removed the ability to practice your language for "hearts".

Duolingo is an excellent language-learning app that makes mastering a new language fun and engaging. The interactive lessons, gamified approach, and bite-sized exercises help learners stay motivated and consistent. With a wide variety of languages available, Duolingo caters to beginners and advanced learners alike. Overall, Duolingo is a fantastic tool for anyone looking to learn a new language in an enjoyable and effective way. Highly recommended!
This is so fun, no ads longer than 5 seconds, and educational! no lag and alot of choices, makes it even more fun! I'm currently learning japanese, mizu means water.

I have had a hard time in the past trying to learn other languages. I've tried a few but nothing ever seemed to stick. Duolingo is probably the first time in a while that I have attempted and I'm starting to get the hang of it. so far it's broken down the language Into bite size chunks that are understandable and easy to remember. I plan to continue using the app and I hope to learn a lot more as time goes on.

I loved dulingo when I first started using it you could consistently learn at your own pace no heart system now you can barely get through a session bc not one but two ads pop up after each completion not to mention it's ad happy now .. subscription..money driven and over the top with ads update it's worse you can't even practice for hearts you have to watch ads or pay for their expensive subscription

Update 12 February 2025: This app gets worse and worse. As an early user (2016), I recall all the things that made duolingo excellent; forums, human speakers, volunteer mods, lots of lessons, reasonable ads, practice for hearts. That's all gone. At least there is still the languages themselves left. They used to claim their goal was to make learning languages fun and free hence why they had ads. Whoever bought this (I assume) from the original developers only cares about their Capitalist venture

I'll give it 3 stars... My reasoning is that it really is a good application to learn, but I do not need the notifications. The notifications are quite unnecessary, stating that I am failing or that I have been demoted. This isn't a competition! As everyone learns at their own pace. I really do not need that application telling me these things. Instead, it would be better to have a more encouraging approach to those using your app.

I'm having trouble getting stuck when entering a new topic, so it is difficult for me to continue my learning progress. I was going to subscribe, but after experiencing this I'll think twice. Please fix this issue soon because I love Duolingo so much. Thank you.

New version is horrendous. Previous one had a flow to it and it was more clearly structured as to what i was learning. New one is all over the place. Edit: speaking sections are mind numbing, having you repeat the same sentence 10 times.
lost the option for a randomised lesson, which i used alot, when I didn't want MORE words overloading me. also lost user interaction and real-life mods which were so much better than the rubbish AI.

Easy to learn. Love that you hear, read and speak !!! Unfortunately the customer service is bad. There are problems with getting the promised points when doing exercises. Contacted customer service many times, informing them about a problem but the only confirm getting the report but not doing anything about ir. Problem is now for over 2 month and nothing happens. Not an individuell answer to the problem, not fixing it at all. This shows how much they care about there customers. Bad attitude!!!

Holy... I've been doing Chinese courses for 3 days now, and I already feel confident enough to wanna talk to other Chinese speaking people. Every lesson is fun and engaging, and lastly unlike other apps the premium is actually useful, but you can still learn just as effectively without it!

I don't like the hearts it has.i know they are trying to make money but I'm trying to learn and with hearts I almost cried because I had one more to do put I ran out of hearts and had to restart.its a good app and doesn't lag. but when it comes to the part where you speak I might say it right and it will say it's wrong.but a good app mostly.

For Japanese. Duolingo should teach Kanji separately so that a person can learn it and understand it properly, not teaching it in one lesson and confusing the learners. Teach kanji the same way hiragana and katakana were taught. Separately. One at a time clearly, please. It's hard to learn it the way it is currently. Let's learn all the alphabet one at a time (I mean Kanji) , then learn the speech when we're done.

30 second ads after every lesson, plus asking again to double for chests and stuff is just crazy. Then you run out of hearts and have to use too many gems to buy them back. The free experience for Duo has become really bad on mobile unfortunately. Can't recommend it in its current state.

I'm learning french academically and I often find it difficult to go back and forth through lessons and comprehension. This app has helped me immensely in my studies and whenever I feel frustated with seeing it on paper, Duolingo helps me refreshes everytime. I have seen changes with my academic performace after I just regularly learned it on Duolingo. I'm very grateful and thankful to the developers for making my journey beautiful. Much love and wishes to the team✨😌💛

content: Great app, you can learn at your own peace, some bugs though lately, some lessons or exercices can't be follow because the original pronunciation sound just doesn't come out of the app, I mean, I have to find the right answer based on what duo is telling, guess what, duo doesn't say anything!! I need help with that, I love duolingo!!

I am so frustrated because this app could be good. But it's not. Arbitrary percentages on the lessons that do not add up, a progress bar that is a joke. Repetition of the sound and spelling but not the meaning of a word, I need to associate them all together. Where is the actual glossary? Why can't I look up a word? Why do you give me a new word like I should know it? Match the word you haven't introduced yet, and hope you get it right. And you still won't even know the meaning lmao 🤣

Duolingo used to be a good app. Developers are flooding it with unskippable ads lasting up to five minutes. That's completely unsatisfactory. The most recent update has exacerbated the ad problem exponentially to wherethe app is simply a blatant cash grab with no regard for customer experience. There are several better options out there, don't waste your time with this app until devs pull their head out of their...

I bought it for a year in 2023. I have buyer's remorse. You can only get a certain amount of XP in a day now, even if you're paying! Went from 2X XP to 1.5XP. Leagues: Demotion Zone?! Negative reinforcement. No opt out of league competition. You can only earn a certain amount of extra XP no matter how much work you complete or how high you score! Massive amts of stupid sentences you're not going to use IRL. Nothing to look forward to here. DEMOTIVATIONAL.

it has , easily, help me retain my native language, I was born in North carolina, however, all my father's but very fluent Italian with very Sicilian dialect.. I never thought I would be able to speak fluently again, I'm hardly able to complete sentences, lol, but it is making it so easy to learn, without making you feel less intelligent.. lol.. I would highly recommend!

Hi Duolingo Team, I’m a Vietnamese learner studying Chinese, and I really wish the Vietnamese-Chinese course had writing practice. Right now, I have to use the English-based course, which isn’t ideal. Writing is super important for learning Chinese characters, so I hope Duolingo can add this feature for Vietnamese learners in the future. Thank you!

Some of the learning is fun, but things like the Adventures, are very annoying and not helpful. Progress is slow and I am learning lots of things that I don't think I will ever use, but it seems necessary to get through these exercises to get to the next step

I'm learning arabic, but nowadays I'm getting frustrated to the fact that some important symbols which are found above arabic letters are not well visible,some are sliced a half, and others being hidden totally. so i don't know whether it's my phone's problem or bugs.

Changing my 5 star feedback. The app still works fine, but the company stiffed me with the family plan subscription. It didn't withdraw the money for my subscription, didn't notify my about their failure, and just cancelled my family plan. Now they want me to subscribe again for a larger sum. Checked with my bank: Duolingo's glitch. An unreliable company.

Previous design was much user friendlier. Do not like this at all. Posted a review a while back for two stars, came back to adjust to one, now you can't even do a lesson to earn a heart. It's like they don't want you to learn without losing an arm and a leg in the process.

I like learning on this app, but for me there are two major flaws which is why I've deducted 2 stars. 1. There are now double the amount of ads per lesson. 2. You cannot choose which voice actors you want/don't want. For me, I cannot stand Zari's voice, it makes learning Japanese painful.

No doubt it's a superb app, I've been using this app since last year and I must say I am enjoying my learning but recently I'm experiencing some kind of problems with the speaking questions. It is that even after saying the correct answer it's showing wrong answer. So my only request to the makers is to fix the problem because it's making me lose my hearts. Thank you

I have been using Duolingo for nine years now - so that tells you something! However, my Spanish is still not competent and the childish presentation and voices used by Duolingo really annoy me! I wish there was a version for serious learners and grown-ups. I do still use Duo but my main Spanish learning is done via another app and by practice in regular meetings with a small group of like-minded learners.

I have been learning Japanese on the app for a long time. It was good but the Kanji script lessons have been ruined. In one of the questions, we are asked to type a kanji character on screen, i.e., how it's spelled in English, like 'naka' for '中' but the input AI converts the input into Japanese characters itself, this way, the lesson becomes incompletable. Please, if any person from the developer team or any other person with power to resolve the issue sees this, kindly or cruelly, please do

Amazing app perfect for learning new languages and other things. I love it, but there's one thing.. when you start learning the thing is it doesn't teach you what the words mean in english, so you just stuck, you know? Well, that all great app though!

I really like the app, but I don't like how they don't explain things. I have the paid version. If I could get my money back I would. I've been doing the math part of this app for a few months now. The main issue I'm having right now is the app doesn't explain how to solve certain problems. It just gives you a problem. If you can't solve it, it just keeps repeating the same problem. This is a very good app if you want to learn a language. The only problem is that I advertises the paid version a lot. So much that I have memorized I but other than that it is great. You can even start streaks with your friends

I love Duolingo but it is getting to the point I'm wasting my time I am here trying to learn and get a couple mistakes wrong and I can't get the full experience if I'm running out of hearts and cant do it again and have to watch 5 ads just to lose it again and I'm done with it all all they need to do is take away the hearts so that I can learn and make it better we make mistakes sometimes and if I make a mistake then no more lesson I am done with it all

Give me Dark Mode back! While I like it on the whole, I've set all my devices to dark mode, especially for night use. Giving me a bright white screen is awful! There should be a toggle option for light / dark mode. Those that like it brighter can have it that way. Not happy with this update.

the app for my Android tablet (pixel) is very flawed. when you get something wrong, it doesn't show what the answer is. I hope this is a bug because that is no way to learn a language! how can you learn from your mistakes if they don't go corrected? thank God I usually use Duolingo on my iPad or iPhone. downloaded to use one time and am immediately deleting.

I used to like it. So I signed up for super. which is not cheap. shortly after signing up, they evidently rolled out another price tier called "max" or something. so when I get an answer wrong, if I want the app THAT I PAID MONEY FOR to explain to me why my answer was wrong, I have to pay even more. this apps entire ethos is horrible. good premise. destroyed by capitalism. like everything else. well done.

It is no longer possible to fill up hearts with practice. Only when out of hearts it is possible to do one practice. That is soooo anoying. Also bugs dont get fixed and new ones apear frequently. Very bad experience, this app is sadly declining a lot.

January 2025: I can not recommend this app to learn Japanese. Without prior experience in the language, I would be pronouncing words such as 着ました as ちゃくました vice the correct way きました. Also, the Japanese IME is not working correctly; e.g., tping utta should be うった and not うっtあ. All issues are being reported, but the company appears to be more focused on adding features vice, ensuring that the product is right.

it's a good app, however the video call feature freezes more often than not. Update: I hate it! If you make a mistake, it forces you to pay for more time. I paid almost $500 for the basic and necessary "add-ons". Some VC firm is out to rip you off! Adios!

5 STARSSS everyone should get duolingo. 1.hes just too cute when he does the "wabing" when you finish 5 or 10 in a row. 2. This app helps you communicate to others that dont speak your language and the new update they now teach music and math and you can work with friends. Would very recommend!

Duolingo is an amazing app/game I mean it helped learn quite a lot of Spanish but problem is that my I give my to my dad on Sundays and get it back on Friday so I'll have to miss out on 4 days of work but on breaks like summer or winter or long weekends I get my iPad until the long weekend or break ends but none of that matters because I can still improve my Spanish so thank you for making duolingo it's an amazing app and a easy way to learn new languages.

tbh I love duolingo. amazing learning game for over 9 year old!! Super Duolingo ads drive me crazy. Why should I watch a repressive ad after each lesson completes? One of the things that I dislike about this app is showing Super Duolingo ads after each lesson these days. I spent about one-hour learning before, but now I don't. It is very annoying to show the Family members and Super Duolingo ads after finishing each lesson.

userName: Brandon Harris - Keller Williams Community Partners
content: I think it's one of the best designed apps on the market place, but it's also expensive and getting more expensive with features behind rather large pay walls. I wish more types of education had formats like this. Makes it fun to learn.

I have been using Duolingo for 2 years now and my experience is really nice with Duolingo because it is a very fun and interactive language learning app and I have learnt Chinese now because I've been taking lessons everyday and now I am currently learning Japanese and Korean from doulingo I like this app very much and I am going to continue using Duolingo to learn more languages I really appreciate the hard work of Duolingo team and I hope they keep making more useful features. Thank you💕

Duolingo wasn't satisfied with the amount of people on their paid program so they took a good app with some bugs and started stripping the free version down. This is no longer enjoyable at all. Don't download if you don't plan on paying for it.

Sometimes in the multiple choice questions Duolingo gives two answers that don't match the question that is asked and tells you that your answer is wrong. Apart from this, the app is a fun way to learn another language in a short amount of time.

The ads have gotten quite bad, with fake 'close' symbols and the like. Also the content of some of the ads has gotten quite borderline (I often do the lessons with my kids with me). Would be great if Duolingo could do a review of what advertisers are using their platform to peddle.

Really great app, it makes learning so much more fun than traditionally learning. Also, the ability to follow your friends and have friend quests is really motivating. But some updates are questionable.

Saw I had an old five-star review while going through my app reviews and had to correct that. Since Duolingo started pushing AI, the actual lesson content has been increasingly unreliable, with correct answers not accepted, incorrect answers accepted, and miscellaneous other errors or confusions.

I am enjoying Duolingo again but I am very close to leaving because of the damn LinkedIn ad. I don't want to have a premium membership with them. Please add an 'x' to close it out instead of having to close our the app entirely to get rid of it.

A really great application to learn languages in a simple way yet so entertaining, especially for daily purposes. As an English tutor, I highly recommend this to anyone who has a passion to learn language. I was also hoping Duolingo could update more specific idioms of the languages.

It's good but if you select a different age just for keeping your identity safe they give you questions of that age.....I choosed a small age and turns out they gave me that age 🥲 Also the quiz is too long should reduce the amount for math as I feel you have to like be a bit energetic which I'm not at all🥲😅🤣🤣 overall good experience!

I've used Duolingo off and on for a couple years now. and I have to say they've got it figured out! (I'm learning Spanish) the conversations that you can listen to and then it will ask you in English what they were talking about to make sure you are connecting and comprehending is AWESOME! I don't normally post app reviews but that aspect alone blew my mind!

This application is highly beneficial; I regret not utilizing it sooner. 👍😊 It is arguably the most exceptional language-learning application and the best available on the Play Store, although the advertisements are somewhat intrusive. ⭐️💯 I recommend you consider trying Duolingo. 🤗🔥

Hugely addictive. I'm at level b1 in Spanish and then i will buy one level up to Duolingo max. I find that i can understand a lot of magazine and news articles. Nowhere near fluent because it takes me too long to form daily sentences in my head on the fly. Hope level c is added eventually. Can someone please revive the owl! And make his merchandise furrier. Better than phone games. Distract yourself with an educational app

I don't even so much mind the ads in the free version, it's the endless videos asking if I want to sign up for a subscription at a discount that are annoying. If I didn't want to sign up the first time, why would I want to the 17 other times you made me last night when I was trying to learn.

It was good at first but after a few days I discovered the 2 week free trial of super,so took the trial but still ads were coming.Another problem is,I am a bengalee so I want to learn english,but here in duo my translations were correct but still it takes lives and some time he gives same 2 options to choose and if I choose any of them,then again it takes lives.The pronounciations are not so accurate that's a problem again.The lives are must need to practice but it takes upto 5hr to charge one.

I feel like the app is easier when you know a few words, for example, I tried to learn Hindu. It was a struggle, but I know some common words in French. And so it was way easier to learn French. So I suggest to learn a few words before coming here, but overall, 5 stars!

It's all good but lives take an eternity to refill and are quite expensive... It's becoming a problem, specially when you're dealing with a difficult unit. I get the app needs something for paying and stuff, but aren't the adds enough? Or maybe just don't make us wait FIVE hours for just a heart...

the app is nice but sometimes it shows glitch like when our internet is in then also it shows that we are not connected. I hope it will be ok after sometime because I trust in this app and children should try this surely by learning new languages

I like the lessons, but hate the "Game" aspect of it. The microphone also doesn't work with the app, so l have to type that every time it asks you to to speak. I also hate all the messages it sends. It's like the Social Engineering apps in China, so I'm uninstalling it.

This is really one of my favorite apps, it's so easy to explore, you learn the desired language super quickly, and you'll have fun all at the same time! Edit: Still a great app, but PLEASE bring profile pictures back! :(

I took six years of Spanish and still couldn't remember anything but basic words. Maybe a few conversation pieces I had picked up over the years, but thanks to duolingo I'm actually learning and remembering. Great app even for free.

Every single update made makes the app worse and worse. I feel so bad for all these new people downloading the app and giving it 5 stars not knowing they're getting the worst, most money grubbing version of it. No more forums for discussing mistakes, you pay to have a computer (Ai) guess what you did wrong now (it's usually wrong). No more practice for lives, you pay for that now. Double the ads, pay up for just one ad, or pay double for none. They're more interested in stunts than their own app

I have always struggled with learning a new language until I tried Duolingo, it makes learning fun and understandable, I am committed to learning but don't have alot of time during the day, with Duolingo I have been able to do a little bit every day and it only takes 90 seconds to complete a Corse (if you know what the Corse is about) and the hard ones take 10 minutes, I love this app.

This app is amazing,it gives you access to learn your preferred language and also it helps you to get access to not only one language but two or more languages at the same time and also there's a voice speech that says the language for you so it's really nice.download and try and see

I have been with Duo since the very beginning. It now SUCKS. The company is greedy and doesn't honor past purchases. The language learning has been a downward snowball of suck for the past few years. Time to move on to better and brighter Language platforms!

I started with Duolingo ~4 years ago. And after the last update when you complete a lesson an add will start followed by a thing for Super Doulingo. After. Every. Single. Lesson... It is so ridiculous. I really wish that I hadn't updated this last time.

I was baraged into doing a trial of super. I got a chrge automatically renewing for a year subscription. I canceled within minutes. They have denied me a refund. I should be able to get a refund on a year subcription that I haven't used 1 day of. I don't have 100 dollars to spend on this purchase.

when it comes to mastering the hangul, this app is the best. but when it comes to other features, they should improve the listening and speaking features to help the learners even faster. overall, it helped me a lot so give it a try.

Hello! I finished Swedish course, in the end I was provided by everyday new tasks. But they are the same every day. Only few phraces which repeats every time. Could you update the practice for those who finished the course? It would be good to have new phrases every day

It's fun to learn a little vocab, but simple tools in the app do not work. For instance, you cannot hear auditory practices slower, and you can't practice words you've missed. I'm not going to renew my subscription.

Used to be the best app, simple and efficient. Now it is too complicated with NO OPTIONS for us to adjust the course (e.g I don't want to practice writing. Why forcing me to so that??). Also how on earth you teach beginners names of food before you teach them basic words like Hello, How Are You, Bye etc..

The lag in the new music lessons make them extremely frustrating to try and keep time. The ads that have been introduced for accounts in school are an appalling money grab and Duolingo should be embarrassed. How is it ok to be showing ads for games that include actual gameplay to students?!

The Ads are crazy, they actually advertise after every completed unit or section. I actually think that it hinders users time to learn. 😭 Other than that, great app. Cute characters and very efficient with teaching!

Ever since I found this app, I've always wondered; how should I improve on learning other languages? Welp, when I saw Duolingo, I decided, why not try it out? And I had a BLAST! This app is so fun to play! Even if it's not that type of game, but this? It's ten times better than other games! So, I'm here to say, thank you Duolingo for helping me with my Spanish lessons!

Very useful app that keeps improving every day. It would be nice to have the option to learn a language for 'vacation mode' for example. And for what I learn, I wish there were exercises for speaking Greek. Keep up the good work! 💚

I am really getting a lot out of this and have learnt a huge amount in a short time. I think it would be improved if they chose to include a fuller explanation of errors to save having to leave the app to look things up.

This is an awesome app , I have used it for learning French and Spanish. It's only an app which I have used for learning a foreign language and this is like not described in words . Anti-learning (was 5 stars) I have a 3+ year streak on Duolingo, used it every day and practiced a lot. Now though out of nowhere they have removed my unlimited hearts, unless I pay for a subscription. This means I am punished for making mistakes, and it will force me to quit a lesson I am halfway through if I run out of hearts. You can earn one heart back for free, but that means your next lesson has to be flawless - meaning I can't progress at all as if I make one mistake it will kick me out.

*A Game-Changing Language Learning Experience: Duolingo Review* In the vast world of language learning apps, Duolingo stands out as a shining star. With its innovative approach, engaging interface, and comprehensive lessons, Duolingo has revolutionized the way we learn languages. As a long-time user, I'm excited to share my enthusiasm for this incredible platform. *Interactive and Fun* Duolingo's gamification approach makes learning a language an enjoyable experience. The app's bite-sized les

A great app for learning languages. The method used is effective (for me at least) in causing actual word recognition and immediate association , rather than having to think to myself "what English word does this represent?". When I'm in German mode, and I hear "brot" I think of bread, rather than thinking "oh that's the word for bread."

I really love this app but they keep making it harder to use for free. as of the most recent update I can't play the match madness or other game for 10 gems anymore, now it's 30. I can't afford to pay for it so I guess I just won't play it as much as I used to. Update: now you can't practice for hearts either. But you can watch an ad in it's place. Lame.

The machine translated sentences are sometimes horrible. The old version does not have the same courses and functions with the new one. That brings inconsistency between my ipad and phone, which is not good for my child.

The main issue for learning Mandarin in this app that it hardly listen to my pronunciation. When tap to speak, before I try it is already conclude that I pronounce it incorrectly. I always had to skip the speaking part. Quite annoying. Considering not to renew my family super account.

The most useful features are becoming incresingly paywalled and I'm not sure about how the AI voices sound sometimes. But it's still been a really useful app for trying to get the basics down and keep my learning up to date.

I'm honestly learning French pretty fast. But I wish they would change the streak society app icon from time to time just like the normal green app icon. It's been an orange fiery icon for quite some time and I think it'll be more fun if it changed expressions.

This is the best app of all time. I believe you would get a lot more users if you included the level of benefits up to the max level, and only charged for the super level. you would get a bunch of people enjoying the benefits of Max and then seeing how much more they could get with super and taking the plunge.

This is a really neat app for learning languages,math and music,but there's one thing I don't get,when your given the three days for trial after a few lessons,your not able to access the premium features unless you use your one week free then pay later, which is not fair to be exact, either way,it's the best app around

I think duolingo is a very fun app that makes learning new languages fun! I've started learning Español (yeah I know some Spanish now) and im started to get good at it with my 2 day streak. I can't begin to thank you enough (in English or French) for the many advantages of this app. Duolingo offers an amazing program for free. Duolingo is versatile & consistently supportive. Tiny push, big recognition & a pretty cute Owl (or is that a frog? lol) too. Angel 😇

this app is an way to learn languages I've always wanted to learn. the daily challenges make the lessons fun and engaging and thereby helps make things easier to absorb and retain, unlike the tedious monotony of classroom or textbook instructions.

When a person finishes the course, there should be a larger variety of sentences presented in the daily refresh exercises. It's always the same sentences over and over again. otherwise, I have enjoyed Duolingo.

It's a very good app for learning language with fun but I get so annoyed for the advertises, and most of the opportunities are available for the super Duolingo users . So I think they should work on it so that we , who do not have super Duolingo can make more progress
