duolingo is the best language ap because you hvpave many things to learn and I love the animations mostly of which emotions each character is showing and it teaches music and math which can also help with my singing,piano learning and so much more I would highly recommend duolingo it's just perfect for children and adults that's is what I'm loving and you apcan take more courses than one and you can learn mathematics duoling highly recommended in my opinion try it out yourself that's why 5 ⭐

Nasty little manipulative app. Fun at first, and has potential, but continually tries to push you onto the next day's lesson, regardless of whether you've truly mastered the previous day's content. And of course, got to keep the shareholders happy, so it tries evey possible means of pushing you to pay money. Duolingo are a corporation with no genuine interest in teaching language. Don't bother installing.

Terrible app, doesn't teach you sentence structure and holds you back from ever actually fully learning a language. Whoever made this clearly has no brain. I've used this app for multiple years and it's obvious they would prefer to make money than to actually educate others, it's literally a video game.

The entire experience is ruined by getting the duolingo ad between EVERY quick lesson. I already got stalked by the damn owl on tiktok and now it feels like its beating me with a bat

Really good if your starting to learn a language fluently, they make this like a game to keep you in your learning. If your going to learn a new language, choose this app, although if you stop learning for a bit it sends you a lot of notifications.

Duolingo makes learning a new language fun and easy with its gamified approach. The lessons are short and convenient, which makes it easy to practice daily. I love the variety of languages available, and the app’s rewards and streaks keep me motivated. Great job! 👌

great app, sure you might not be able to speak it fluently but you'll get pointed in the right direction concerning how to structure sentences, how to use words properly and you'll be pushed to broaden your language horizon with new words. I'm a French learner and I've found it useful

I think the app is formated really well and the lessons are easy to understand, but I wish there were more languages. I really want to learn Bengali, but they do not have it. I already pay for the year subscription and now you want more money to be able to learn properly and have to explain why I got an answer wrong. That's just messed up. Best app I could find so I can learn different languages you should really tried this app out it's good it's an appropriate for smaller kids under 18 and it's perfect four people they're trying to learn different languages that's the way I rated 4 Stars you should try it out it changed my life

Hey lf u are a person who is attractive to learning new language then this app is for you. There are many options of language you can learn. It starts nicely as you can choose that how advance you are in the language you have choose. And l liked one thing that there are no adds. Highly recommend 😎

A nice self-paced language learning app. Learn as fast or as slow as you like. I would like more explanation of the rules of grammar at times, but can look those up online. The pace of new content with review is perfect for me.

Don't bother installing the app. It now only allows you to earn one heart, making new learning and practise impossible. Duolingo is trying to force us to pay, but paid duolingo is also horrendous to use. If it wasn't for the fact that I have a six year streak including a whole year in diamond league, I would be giving up now.

This was a great app and then it started penalizing me for speech errors and I got verbal apraxia which make it a lot harder to produce correct speech. I need more grace for speech speaking practice and an option to slow down the example speech so I can better figure out how to coordinate my mouth

I like this app very much This is very good for our learning, please also tell your children to read it, with the help of this app we can learn the language of all countries .This app helps us a lot in studying, writing and progressing.It is very, very good for teaching Bhaiya Sahab, however much praise we give for it, it is less. great for helping to learn language, only duolingo related ads-skippable after like 5/10 seconds-rarely get ads though! takes things slow, has reminders of past things you learned, i find it amazing, far better than other language apps, free! turns what would be boring, hard work into a fun game with stories and interactable games in between. has daily streaks-friend streaks too! help s with speech aswell as words/word order!!

I think it's very annoying that every app now has a subscription plan that reduces access to very useful things. However, this app has been very useful for learning a language and especially if you have already built to foundation for this language

As a free user this is a terrible experience. Imagine a learning oriented app penalizing you for making mistakes. You get a question wrong? You get kicked out from the classroom, no explanation given! Super users can make unlimited mistakes but explanations for why your answers are incorrect are only for max users (additional cost). The app is designed to get you addicted to playing every day. The emphasis of the app is unfortunately not on learning but user engagement and money first.

Highly recommend. this app will not make you fluent but O have perfected my Orosh grammar and I am learning hundreds of new words and where they are placed in a sentence. it is a very game like form of learning. you can compete against others users. I bought a monthly subscription offer for over €5. Well worth it.

Unfortunately there appears to be a lot of people who cheat In the competitions mode by downloading the mod version form some websites. Extremely unfair to others. According to Meta AI,I was informed that Its impossible to score 30 000 points ànd 433 perfect lessons In one day but this reflects on people's profiles and these points are scored in less then two hrs on the app. Let's be honest in the usage of the competition mode,so that those who compete can enjoy the competition mode too.

OKish app, but I find having to write and spell correctly somewhat difficult and unnecessary, when all I want to do is have a simple conversation in Polish. It's a very hard way to learn a language. The hearts feature is the worst thing added to an app. If you went to a Spanish class and after five mistakes they kicked you out. In the grand story of human learning, few figures have loomed as omnipresent as Duo. He was not just a mascot; he was a guardian of discipline, a symbol of persistence, and a motivator in the face of procrastination. He watched over millions, urging them to complete their lessons through notifications and, when necessary, giving threats. But now, he is gone. Duo’s death is more than a tragedy—it is a call to action. We depended on him to learn; now we must learn to depend on ourselves.

I do wish that you'd be taught the alphabet first then the words so you know what the heck you're reading 80% of the time and can develop the habit of reading it mentally. I also seem to have a problem with listening lessons, none of them play the audio, the only sound happens to be sound effects and the ding noise when you get something right or wrong.

Tinkering- I've been using the app for several years. It's fun, but it's so frustrating when the developers insist on changing how you interact. I know that it's to force you to subscribe, but after this time, I'm clearly not going to. Now, when you make a mistake, you can't simply practice to gain hearts as before. For me, this may end it as it will mean slipping down the leagues as you can't earn enough points. Stupid to force people who have very long streaks to give up the app altogether

It's a fun way to improve a new language you're already learning using more traditional methods like books, with grammar, spelling, sentences. It helps you learn new words. Another feature that motivates me are achievements, especially most XP earned, but it's incredibly difficult to beat an achievement if I can't keep track of my daily XP! I want that graph back!!!

Duolingo has turned into a cash grab and is no longer the 'free learning app' it claims to be. Over the last couple years, almost every helpful feature has been paywalled, restructured to AI, or both. The most egregious example is the removal of the forums. The explanations for mistakes have always been lacking, but at least in the past, you could get an explanation from an actual human through the forum. Now you get an AI explanation, only if you subscribe, not just to Super but to Max.

It is great if you like gamefied language learning. However, ads are much more frequent nowadays, they are reducing the amount of resources (hearts) you can get through practice so you pony up for their premium (super) plan that has no regional pricing. They have an ultra premium plan that has very few interesting features and is "AI" based, which at this point is more of a buzzword than anything really useful, and nothing you can't get for free with chatgpt TLDR it's good but declining quality
Great app, and have great potential, but lately became too toxic with the amount of ads. previously you had to get hearts via practices. now you need to watch ads...

Having used Duolingo for over 8 years, I've always been very enthusiastic about the app. However, the latest changes in the app make it so that you can no longer properly practice what you've already learned - you are forced to move forward in the course. This gives the learner no opportunity to consolidate learned knowledge and forces them to venture into new learning material even if they don't yet feel comfortable with the current material. I feel this causes a very problematic situation.

It's a great app and I've been using it for 10 years almost. Unfortunately I can't recommend it anymore. They have become so focused on money than nothing else matters. Adverts are constant, the plan prices are unbelievably high, and many of the courses are not even maintained - some having no updates since 2016 (check Reddit). I'd recommend Babbel which seems to be doing better and significantly cheaper with less adverts.

App is great, nothing to say. It's amazing how I manage to have fun and learn at the same time, but the Super Duolingo ad at the end of EVERY SINGLE lesson is really starting to frustrate me. I understand having one every few lesson, but like this it really demotivates me and keeps me away from the app. I barely kept my streak these days, knowing that ad annoys me. This is a great way to annoy people away! I get ads are necessary to improve the services, but this is getting a bit out of hand.

I was really enjoying using this app. I have a 115 day streak using it. I don't think that will last too much longer as they have now removed the ability to practice, which used to help build hearts back up when needed. They have effectively made the free version obsolete, unless you're a master at learning new languages and rarely make mistakes! Also adding, they are currently running a 60% off offer for Super, which I've tried claiming numerous times now, but it won't apply the discount 🙄

Long-time user, probably around 6 years by now, and I've really found the app helpful for developing day to day vocab in languages I already had a sound base in. I've also seen the erosion in features, but don't find the ads as they are too offensive. But just today I've noticed the inability to practice unless you've reached zero hearts, with an ability to only earn one back. If intentional, which from feedback it seems to be, this is a big mistake making it close to unusable as a free app.

duolingo is the best learning app. I am Tanmay Chawra from India. I am learning a lot from it. It is also telling me about my mistakes. Now I am not friends with those kids who used to do my work But later if I tell them to do my work then they make excuses but duolingo is my true friend, I want to say something else also, if there is an AI in duolingo with whom we can talk then it would be great. thanks Duolingo my dear friend. 😊😊😊😊🙂

I've been learning Duolingo off and on for about five years now. The program has changed a lot in that time. Added a lot of material to practice. Not everything that has changed is good. After changing to score, I can't go back to older exercises. The program won't let me go back to repeating older exercises like before... I know that the program offers a paid version with repetition. But why pay for something that looks like a great idea on the outside but doesn't work for me at all...

It's a fantastic app. I love learning on it. But I am genuinely tweaking over the constant Duo Lingo plus ads. It goes for so damn long, AND it runs after every single lesson. I've already tried the free trial. I do not want ANOTHER free trial, so it's another chance for me to forget to cancel and pay for it. It's too much, it's greedy, and frankly has seriously put me off duo.

Obnoxiously repetitive, frequent, loud, bright, animated, long, and forced ads, including ai nonsense. You're bombarded with ads, until you give in and buy a subscription, or move on. The app is only free if you don't make more than five mistakes per day. That pressure is antithetical to learning. And at a certain point in your progress it becomes impossible not to make (frequent) mistakes; the whole thing becomes a memorization exercise. For the price, you're better off buying a real course.

Lately I am very disappointed about Duolingo, with more and more features being put behind a paywall (specially the explaining why you did something wrong one, which in the end was the community's effort). Also, the monthly challenges seem to be buggy, the one "Complete your next 2 lessons" doesn't count them, doesn't matter how many of them I do.

i like this app. Even tho i don't think it's the best way to learn a language but it only takes 15 minutes so i guess it's good and it's enjoyable 😌 But there is a problem! Every night the DUO bird gives me nightmare 💀

I rarely ever run out of hearts, but when I do, I just practiced. It was a decent system. Now if I'm forced to watch an ad to refill a heart, I'd just close the app instead. That's way better for my mental peace than some ads. If you're so hellbent on showing us ads, why not show ads in our target language? Instead of coming up with cringe tik tok grade trash, maybe it's time the team behind Duolingo actually spent some time thinking about making the app better because it's in freefall.

Was a good app, now it's not worth trying. I have been using duolingo for a while, and this past week, I have found that my practice to earn hearts has gone! This was an invaluable part of my learning and reminding me of earlier lessons. The whole point of the free app was that it was funded by the adverts. Now I have adverts but can not be on the app anywhere near as much time. I'm losing out on learning and your advertisers are losing viewers. Please fix this terrible decision.

Was once an amazing app. But since the music and math update, there have been some problems. For example: in the maths course, they explain NOTHING to you. You just get some exercises and nothing else. For the music lessons, the keys keep getting smaller when you unlock new notes. How am I sopoased to press them!? Also my friends who also use this app have problems with learning languages. The units are too short and not everyone can learn so fast... I hope you can do something about this duo.

One of the best learning moments of this app was the ability to restore hearts by reviewing previously learned materials. This feature is not just a free way to get infinite hearts but a fair way to punish a user for their mistakes. Now, the PRACTICE TO EARN HEARTS button is locked unless all lives are depleted, and even then, you can't get more than 2 hearts. This is ridiculous! And, I do have a free account, but I watch a ton of commercials. Isn't that enough?

I used to love duolingo, it was simple and a fun way to get into a new language. But nowadays they're really leaning into the tactic where they make the free version so frustrating that you pay for the subsciption.. Limited hearts a day that you can only pay for, more and more long ads, and a lot of other essential tools locked behind the paywall (for example, "explain my mistake"). As soon as I find another alternative I'll delete this app

the bird has charm. a lot of it. it's a bit easy at first but it makes it more difficult when it goes from the third lesson to the fifth lesson. it makes learning easier than doing it without duolingo

I have used this app for 5 years, 3 of which I was a member. It's a good, user-friendly app and makes learning fun. I am grateful they finally added Math and Music courses to the Android version. However, after not renewing membership and continuing to use the app, the overall experience has gotten worse. The ads have increased almost twofold, it seems, and sometimes, the language targeted in lessons is hardly relevant. As such, I am using the app less and less and looking to other resources.

The program is great for incentivising a lazy teen like me to learn, but it struggles to teach effectively. Most importantly, they lay hardly any emphisis at all on grammar. I understand that they're trying to provide a more immersive, natural learning experience, but they're online, so they effectively can't. Consequently, if you're looking to be able to order something in a coffee shop, this is your thing, but if you're looking to be able to discuss philosophy, get a real french textbook.

Free, which is always good but no "improvement" I've ever seen has actually been an improvement and having started a second language I've discovered that the quality of each course varies wildly. The German course is quite consistent and develops in a sensible way. By contrast the Welsh course doesn't even have enough words recorded with the same voice to complete an exercise without jumping between genders and accents. Worth the price if you get the free version.

I'm really enjoying the app and find it very helpful for language learning. However, there is one issue that interrupts the experience. After each lesson, an advertisement pops up, which is understandable, but there's no option to close it directly. This requires me to close the entire app and restart it to continue my lessons, which disrupts the flow of learning. It would be great if you could add a close button for the advertisements or provide an option to skip them.

duolingo is a great way to learn new languages in a fun way, similar to playing regular games that don’t teach you anything. there's so many different classes, and it feels almost random. yet, it's super fun😁

This app, certainly for the language I'm learning is not worth paying for. The exercises are constantly repeated, with no new content. I had previously subscribed to the Super version, but it's too poor at the moment to make it value for money. I've been using it for almost 3 years, but I've decided today to give up!!! I considered rejoining Super, with the discounted subscription- but on requesting the family membership, it's the same price as always!!!

I don't recommend it for adults, because the learning curve is too slow (painfully slow). And I don't recommend it for children because of how aggressive and relentless the app is in pushing the premium version, at every step of the way, including continuous non-skippable ads. But even if we want to ignore all of this, you just don't learn much with duolingo, it will get you, at best, to an A2 level.

They keep changing it for the worse, punishing you for making mistakes (mistakes are how we learn right?), not fixing glitches, the new layout is awful. bring back the old duo lingo, it was far more interesting to be able to choose lessons to focus on. and there was more reward for revising. also they only give you 5 chances for mistakes, this limits learning ability and discourages from trying .

Loved it! Great combination of reading, listening, writing and speaking. Thanks. A month and a half in now and painfully slow. Updated but still terribly tedious waiting for it to load, content still good when you eventually get it. Another year in and updated to almost completely stop free practice. Not really a free app any more.

Love the app, ads are unbearable. Recently had to cancel subscription, and oh my god, it's impossible to play. After every lesson they make you watch their ad for subscription. I emphasize: after every lesson. I understand the need to advertise once in a while, but not so frequently. It doesn't make me want subscribe, it's irritating and steals my time. As a result I don't want to spend time in the app and consider switching! P.s. I don't have a problem with regular adds, they need to make $.

I've been using the app for a few years now. Initially it was great, lots of varied challenges & rewards which kept interest up and were effective in learning & practicing languages. Gradually lots of these features have been removed without any explanation, resulting in a massively reduced effectiveness. The ability to practice to earn hearts was axed recently, now the reward chests have gone, both of which were really useful tools to encourage & improve learning. Sadly it may be time to leave.

I am learning Chinese. Overall it is quite good, and I would give it 5 stars except for a VERY annoying issue. Since I started a year ago they have updated the Chinese program about 5 times. Every time they do so they somehow believe I have already learned words I have never seen before, since they added new words into sections I have already done. This is frustrating, and each time this happens it really slows down my progress.

2024 Since the 'Practice to Earn Hearts' option has been limited to only one heart, the app has been made virtually useless! Mistakes are part of learning and if only one mistake is permitted, no progress will ever be made as it's not worth trying. This little change has destroyed the useful of the app and I'm giving up despite a 7 year streak. 2015 Works really well and integates well across platforms and devices. Great for learning on the go.

I've spent more time in ads than in lessons this week. Another thing that bugs me is the leagues, make the English learners fight each other and not have them always dominate. Depends on the course, but my course is completely useless for listening and pronunciation, because it's an inaccurate ai voice. At this point I should've just bought some textbooks. The only good thing I'll give this app is the learning on the go, in the sense of repeating your vocabulary

This is somewhat a decent starting point for learners, showing a fun and accessible introduction but it feels like a game more than a serious learning tool. Personally I dislike the features (leaderboards and streaks) because they're unsuitable for those who want to learn at their own pace. The app also lacks cultural context, has limited grammar, repetition over depth etc. It's great for casual learners but less ideal for serious study but it's still a good app!

Love it. Makes learning a language so easy. But whywhyWHY did you introduce the ugly, off-putting logo? (Sick owl with snot) Ewwww! 😫 Update: the experience of learning Irish is disappointing. A bare-bones course, you only learn words and short sentences. No context or texts that are a wee bit longer. No dialogues. I'll stop using it (after 5 months of learning).

It was all good and manageable, not until they decided to limit the 'Practice to earn hearts' to one. I wouldn't be patient enough to repeat a level and "practice" just to earn one heart, over and over again. There won't be progress with that. This makes me not bother to be demoted from Diamond League, because it's just annoying me and wasting more of my time just to progress on the League. This isn't about learning a language anymore, it's more of a competition good for subscribed users.

Not perfect, but I have mostly enjoyed my time with Duolingo, and felt I was making progress, but a recent change made that only allows the user to to refill a single heart by practising, instead of multiple times to get all 5 hearts, has made the free version much more difficult. I get that they want us to pay more for Super Duolingo or but it just makes me want to leave the app, not give them more money

Great app to keep you learning, though it gets a bit repetitive sometimes! But since the latest update it has changed. It no longer gives you double points for 15 minutes when you complete a series of lessons. This means that if you want to keep up on the boards you have to do twice as much, and I don't have that much time. Why do app developers ruin perfectly good apps? I'm looking around for something better. Unless they change the system, I'll probably cancel my subscription
A third edit! I am pleased to see that they've finally allowed the free version to again earn hearts back. Granted, it's through watching ads as opposed to practicing, but I'll take it!

My progress keeps moving back and forth and I have had to repeat many story lessons. This is very frustrating and a waste of time. Sometimes there are questions that require knowledge about grammar that hasn't been previously covered. Lessons have also become very repetitive, requiring you to translate the same two words (often easy words) over and over. - German, 700+ days.

I had a lot of issues with Duolingo, when I was earning double XPs, but not getting it. I have raised a ticket about this issue several times, but it was never really resolved. The standard troubleshooting process didn't fix the issue. Also I would appreciate an option to be able to practice offline as well. Meanwhile Duolingo is making a lot of changes, which I think are not very user-friendly solutions. I would appreciate if they would focus on the basics working well instead.

I really enjoy this as a simple learning tool, however the lack of a British English option is pretty annoying.. I've never played soccer, I've never experienced this thing called "fall" in Autumn. Always catching me out! Edit: I've lowered my rating by 2 stars due to the diminishing quality of the app. The new radio lessons were fine when they were skippable as they seem to teach nothing. Now they're mandatory, plus this new "Max" subscription is being regularly shoved down my throat.

Made it pay-to-play. You can't refill hearts back to full anymore with extra practice lessons (only one heart if you run completely out so if you make one mistake in the following lesson, you're out of luck). Really goes against the idea of making learning accessible and easy for everyone. To add insult to injury, I tried contacting their support team about this issue to determine if this was a bug or a purposeful change multiple times with no response. Message received, adios!

I love the mission behind this company. I especially love their music course. It's massively improved my note reading. It will be even more amazing if we can play alongside our own keyboard. Plus more aspects of music theory like chords, scales, and intervals. I'll be more than happy to pay extra for that. For a free education app though, it's incredible. PS, Android users are still waiting for the pop songs on our app. Seems like IOS is getting all the good stuff. Don't forget us Duo.

I used to really love this app. It was easy to use and makes learning fun. The problems are 1) It rigidly sticks to American English instead of understanding British English. 2) it would be really useful if, when you tap on an underlined word to remind you of it's translation, it would also tell you if it's masculine (m) or feminine (f). 3) The recent changes where you can only earn hearts if you run out, have robbed it of any enjoyment. Now it's just frustrating and I don't see the point.

It's like a guessing game with no explanation. In english Version you never know if 'you' is used as Singular our Plural. There are mistakes in the program and you have to repeat it wrong. Most of the words u'll never need for an every day conversation. The whole "demotion zone" is nonsense. Once you don't reach a certain amount of points you'll lose everything! Practicing is not possible anymore. Every single step you're asked to watch Ads. It's not free anymore!!!! It's a cheat!

Edit: i payed for it, and it's still so frustrating. In the 'learning alphabet' sections I unlock new letters at an extremely slow pace despite tens of lessons with no mistakes. The first words to learn are strange (why do you learn to 'garage' before 'hello'?). A few years ago it was still somewhat usable but now the ads are insane. Progress seems impossible and making mistakes is penalized in a really annoying way. But I guess that's what you get when something is for free nowadays

its easy to learn. important learning features are only for premium users. That's okay. but the duolingo premium advertisements are like after every learning session and taking a lot of time until you can skip them away. i would buy premium if i could affort. the advertisement is very annoing and i even would consider paying a fixed amount to get rid of the intern ads. in the free version additionally to duolingo premium ads you have to also watch ads so they can keep it for free.

I generally like the app other than the nonsensical phrases that we have to make. Not liking the fact that you can only earn two hearts once you lose them all. What a terrible way to go! Additionally, adding friends to get more hearts... Screw off. I'm not annoying my friends to be able to learn a language. I'll watch ads, but have to draw the line somewhere. The paywall has arrived to the detriment of the app!

I think that Duolingo is great! The only problem being whenever I complete a lesson or am doing a lesson, there are just no animation. There used to be animations but now there just gone! And in lessons, some of the characters have a 3 instead of an A. Please fix this! Edit note:This has even gotten worse! Two and threes are now being added into the options. And there are now zero animations! I'm going to delete the app if this is not fixed!!!

Duo is "ok" to learn French, until you get to intermediate levels. Very few explanations are given for the language or grammar or mistakes made, hints are very limited. I've a 1000+ days streak, and have learnt some, but at a mid B1 level, find more and more frustrating to progress. Offline mode is pretty useless, because it doesn't go past the reading modules, which is every second or third tile. I wanted to do lessons on the plane, but can only do 6 lessons. Probably switching...

It started as a great app to learn a new language for free, but it became an app with one goal: make more and more money off its users. Untill a year ago the amount of adds was okay and the app was functional without paying for it. Now every lesson is followed by 2! adds and if you make a few too many mistakes, you can't continue since you can only recharge to 2 hearts without paying, allowing at most 2 mistakes in the next lesson which will get you stuck. For free learning: look further!

I really like Duolingo. I have used it for years now on a daily basis . There are new algorithms now that I do not like. Before I could learn at my own pace and review at will. Now the Duolingo learning system is based on constant competition for points, and the system actively discourages review by rewarding far fewer points for doing it. So you could easily fall out of your designated level by trying to consolidate your knowledge by review. That is not a good thing, imo.

The app used to be fun, as you get to more complex levels the hearts system becomes a real drag if you fail a level and need to restart, it makes keeping a streak hard. They have also removed the option to practice to earn hearts unless you have no hearts which exacerbates the problem. It's really killed my motivation to use the application. It's not really a fun way to practice anymore. They also have grammatical errors in the courses which is frustrating. I have had issues closing ads as well.

I have been using this app for 5 years. It used to be great but bit by bit they are ruining it. They keep making too many changes to the way the learning is done, points are earned etc. More recently their changes have been geared towards making you part with money. The removal of the ability to 'Practice to earn hearts' is the final straw for me. I had built up a 2000 day streak so was committed to the app but will now be uninstalling it and finding a new app to continue my Spanish learning.

The app has recently removed for me the ability to practice to gain hearts unless you are entirely out of hearts. Then you can only gain one heart back - effectively meaning you have to be perfect to complete a section of the course. As such, this app has become far less useful or helpful when learning the language - it makes everything more stressful and frustrating if it's punishing mistakes to such a degree.

Very unhappy that with the latest update at the most advanced level the "path" is gone and the only possible option is the "daily refresh" which keeps repeating the same sentences almost every day!!! If I have completed the whole tree there are for sure more than three subjects to practice or refresh? Also, the "forum" feature was great to deepen the understanding of the grammar and nuances, it is a terrible minus that this is now gone. This is becoming less and less worth it to pay for...

I used to love this app since I love learning languages, but it seems like all the good features are taken out one after another. The regain hearts option was the only way to keep practising after a finished course, but now you can only do it once. Please fix the review lessons for finished courses or give back the option of regaining hearts (even if they're full). I don't need to write the same sentence five times a day if I forget about everything else of the language.

I used Duolingo for about 10 years. The app has become progressively worse. Comments from other users/native speakers to help have been removed. Practices are not there anymore either. More ads. Voice recognition doesn't recognise anything anymore. Generally unhappy with where this is going, and might switch to another app.

More of a game than a credible learning app. Been using this for over two years, last year as a paid user. I've put in at least 30 minutes every day but it's not productive. Grammatically I'm poor and I can barely speak. It's terrible at reinforcing things you've learned so I'm forgetting things now too. It's just designed to keep you 'playing', chasing XP but never really seriously teaches you anything. Possibly an accompaniment to other tools but overall, I'd suggest not wasting your time.

The inability to practice for hearts now has made me significantly less motivated to do anything within the app. I was on a roll collecting badges for 2024, being able to be competitive in the league, and most importantly being able to keep practicing lessons. Now we can barely do that, so I've honestly stopped caring about practicing. It has become such a chore, and a bore.

Increasingly dissapointing app . I've been using duolingo for 2 years, 1 year with a paid subscription, and every change they make makes the app less user friendly. It was never the best app for serious learners; the method is way to slow and there is barely any grammatical instruction, but now it's not even a good app for people who want to study in a relaxed way . If you want to have a taste of a foreign language, for tourisme or if you want to expand your vocab then it's a good choice.

Used the app daily for over a year but the free version is essentially useless now they've restricted the ability to earn back hearts (for the unfamiliar, you start with 5 of these and lose one for each mistake you make, and you will make mistakes, you're learning a new language). So when they're gone, you have to wait 4 hours or pay up. There are currently double the amount of adverts on there as well. It was never the best app but complimented other ways of learning well. Awful.

Incredibly intrusive ads and a very poor scheme of learning. There's no theory on what you're doing, you're just playing matching exercises. It's modelled like a mobile game and has ads every sixty seconds, most of which are telling you to spend £100 on Duolingo Plus (don't worry, it's on discount for a limited time only. Just do it, you don't have time to think about it)

I'm losing patience with this app. Losing the ability to recover hearts through practice may be the final straw. From not being rewarded for finishing quests to a useless speaking test where speaking anything quickly gives better results than taking your time to say it properly. This app does not encourage you to learn at your own speed. Instead it encourages you to rush through until you hit a wall and then does everything in its path to hinder you redoing stuff you didn't get the first time.

Not sure why the old profile pictures were replaced by stupid avatars. The app is getting worse over years. I liked the way you could discuss with others about translations. It's removed however. Whenever I do a lesson, after the lesson, there are about 5 buttons (more or less) you have to press, before you can continue. The sentences are awkward a lot of times. And since they removed the possibility to get full hearts by exercising, it's not challenging anymore to use the app a lot any more.

Edit: have stopped the ability to practice for hearts, unless on 0, has made it impossible to complete a quick lesson when short on time, or pass a tricky lesson as can only practice for 1 heart (5 previously). Have been a user for over 1,100 days... will find a other app if not fixed... ruined for me! Quick easy lessons to get you started learning a language. The premium version is outside of my budget but there is a lot you can do for free.

It has been easy to keep learning, an most of the lessons make it more fun. The problem I have, is after 52 days, it suddenly said I have completed upto level 119. I was in the 20-30 levels. So in order to actually go to where I am, I have to scroll to it every time. This is not very convenient and I also do not receive the full XP for completing lessons as they now are considered review am not learning.

This used to be a great app but since Dec'22, it is incredibly slow! They have completely lost the user-friendliness of their product!!! And let's be clear - if you have to display ads, at least do them in the language being studied!! I've been hoping to see improvements but they just keep adding defects so you succeed at challenges but they are not counted and there's no way to get help with it - the developers of this product are laughing at us for using it! When will they fix it!!

I used to love this app, but they've recently removed the option to practice and replenish hearts - instead, you are forced to buy them using gems, or wait several hours to regain one at a time. This change has sucked the fun out of the whole thing for me. I am now quite anxious about making a mistake, and it's much harder (or more expensive) to take advantage of x2 XP periods. I have no idea why they decided to make this change, but it's not for the better and has had a very negative impact

I've been using duolingo every day for more than 3 years now, but with every update they make the app harder and harder to use for people who aren't paying for super duolingo. The recent update has even removed the option to practice for more than 1 heart (or life, essentially), which makes completing lessons that are more challenging virtually impossible. They only seem to care about making more money, rather than actually improving their courses. Extremely disappointing. :(

Duolingo is a really great app to learn a language. It's user experience is satisfying, the lessons are designed in a way to help one learn while at the same time having fun. Also, the reminders usually come in handy as they help me to be consistent with it. However, the recent update doesn't allow me to refill all hearts with practice lessons and in a way, this has limited my participation on the app. I suggest that the app allows to fill all the hearts with practice lessons. Thanks

Wish there was the option to at least correct each mistake once before the answer is given and you lose a life. A Lot of the time I completely understand the sentence, but miss out a word by mistake, wanting to breeze through the sentences I'm finding easy and move to the next unit. The sentence with the missed word, doesn't even make sense in English, so it's obvious that it's not me not understanding, I've just missed out that word. It's put me off quite a bit from using the app.

Getting Worse. I've been using this app pretty intensively for over a year. (Top 1% w adds) And I am getting increasingly fed up with this app. Features are regularly removed, crippled (like limiting the practice lessons to earn hearts), or buggy. Bugs in the translations and pronunciation are also plentiful to the point that I'm genuinely unsure what the accurate translations and pronunciations are. Frankly, I don't trust Duolingo anymore.

I had to lower my score after the n-th unannounced change of rules to the app, all aimed at pushing people to pay for a subscription, the last one being the cancellation of the "practice to earn hearts" feature. I also find the complete lack of grammar explanations on the mobile app really annoying as well as the review exercise when you finish a course ALWAYS identical.

Downgraded review after they deactivated practicing to earn hearts. This was a key feature for many or us to progress at our own pace. Obvious cash grab. Also, the lack of any explanations as why one word is used instead of another is a hindrance to learning after a certain point. It's fine if you're just starting out in a language, or are already very proficient but not for intermediate students.

I always enjoyed learning languages with Duolingo, but over the years I ended up hating it. It just feels like a rip-off mobile game that trying to push its premium mode (you can join a real course for that price). Instead of keeping me motivated to learn a new language, it punishes you for making mistakes (you lose one of 5 hearts). Mistakes are natural when learning. Most courses also lack grammar explanations (except e.g. Spanish), so you'll mostly only learn vocabulary/phrases by heart.

Duolingo's controversial changes to the learning pathway have been refined over time, and I do now find it easy to understand where I am in the course. Annoying there's no British English option, and no more practicing to earn hearts is frustrating and makes me more determined to avoid paying, especially with so many ads. Also, if only they'd stop nagging us to upgrade to Max without offering us a trial like Super has - why would we pay for something we can't try first? Maybe it's rubbish...

It is overall a good app and my learning experience is also kinda good but I wish it had sanskrit (language) because I want to learn it and I think that this app teach you perfectly and not only me but a lot of people around me want to learn Sanskrit , that's the reason why I'm giveing Duolingo 4 on 5

Too many ads for the free version, too many paywalls to actually learn anything. And I don't think there's enough incentive to get a subscription other than being ad free, or having unlimited hearts and access to a few extra modes. You should have accompanying lessons to the quizzes to make it worth the cost. Maybe a one time cost, like other platforms would be worth it. Do yourself a favor and pick up a lesson book to give yourself peace of mind from the constant ads.

Duolingo is such a wonderful app, free, easy and efficient in teaching new somewhat hard languages. I really love it. And I recommend it. However, you could so better with the expressions used to teach (should be more realistic and real world examples). Another thing; Introduce chatting option for the people who follow each other.

Intuitive with a smooth learning curve. Loses a star because it freezes often on my Samsung phone and requires a force stop. Edit - loses another star because it has developed another bug which prevents progress. It keeps forcing me to do lesson 6 of 5. Edit 2 - quality has dropped massively. Some of the english translations are just plain wrong (grammatically incorrect). Also, I resent paying for the app and being asked to may more for additional features.

I didn't use the app for years because of the changes that erased my progress. Unfortunately it didn't improve much. What is really irritating is the aggressive advertising and toxic language and homescreen icon. There are so many opportunities for improvement, to make the language learning actually effective. The pace of the lessons is slow, and repetitive. Repeating somethig 20 times and after that never again. (spaced repetition guys!). Or, a new hanzi + pinyin, without giving the meaning??

Sas a great tool until it got to the point of ads after every single exercise. I used to be able to lose myself learning for an hour on my commute. Now if I ever hear that ad for super duolingo again I might strangle someone. I hardly use it now. Down from 30mins/1hr a day down to a couple of minutes. Will probably just delete the app now. Was good while it lasted.

The deterioration in DuoLingo over the last couple of months has been dramatic. 1. No more practice to regain hearts. 2. The "practice" lessons for finished courses are EXTREMELY limited in scope, cover a tiny fraction of acquired knowledge and neglects other parts. 3. More for post-course practice: there's very little of it. 4. Bonus hearts for completing lessons: gone! It also seems that in some languages, much of the advanced material has been removed. What the hell is going on???

Update: after 6 days the problem is solved. I have been using Duolingo for more than two years. I loved it. But on the recent weeks it started to provide errors, and although these errors get reported through the app nothing happens. The "practice to earn hearts" and the"ramp up challenge" doesn't or doesn't always work. If it's a tricky but nasty attempt to make the users of the free version pay, well, it's against the main slogan of Duolingo "free, fun and accessible for everyone"...

Duolingo is very expensive. It is promoted as a free learning app, but that is a fiction. If you don't pay the annual extortion fee of $129 AUD (or more if you pay monthly) you are subjected to endless adds, and have limited hearts, that are easily lost. Content is also limited. As an app it is okay at expanding your vocabulary, but it does not teach grammar well at all. You have to learn that yourself. There is also zero support if you have an issue, even with the paid version - no replies.

Was a great way to learn a language. Go up the leagues. Now they removed the feature to regain your hearts and you can only regain one!!!! It has taken out any motivation to do more than one lesson a day and keep your league. Because if you stumble in one lesson you know that you won't get it right with one heart. Super duolingo doesn't fill the gap because the regaining hearts feature helped with repeating old lessons to keep them in your long term memorie. Can't recommend it anymore.

Duolingo has really gone downhill. It was very helpful in the past to get feedback from other users explaining why certain answers were incorrect, but now you have to pay so that AI can explain it to you instead. Some of the new lesson types are also really unhelpful (looking at you, point and click adventure lessons). They also seem to have done away with the ability to report things as incorrect for some lessons, which is also disappointing.

This app has gone from one I recommend to all to a pay to win app. This was a good way to learn a language, currently learning french and have difficulty with the grammar. Used to use the practice to gain heart option the most for the grammar ,at least half an hour each day, which helps a lot! But there has been an update which now removes this option unless you're out of hearts. Unless you are willing to pay, you now have to fail at an exercise to empty the hearts to be able to practice.

Used to be a great app (~2.5 years of use), but now it's becoming unusable. Recent changes mean that as an unpaid user, you can only do more new lessons (i.e no revising, every lesson is just new words). I used duo alongside my university course. But the app has now become so predatory to get you to upgrade its basically useless for actually learning a language. No practice available and no grammatical focus. I'll give it a few weeks to see if they revert changes, if not I'm gone.

Used to be a good playful learning app, but the fact that one can't restore their lost hearts anymore with practice sessions is frustrating. Learning a language is not a game where you just need to move forward, it's a matter also of constantly reviewing older material. The reason I hadn't purchased the Super earlier was the very fact that by removing the need to review the older lessons it actually lowered its teaching quality. I am pissed and planning to move to a different app.

Have been using this app for a while and for a free language learning tool is ok overall. There has been a recent update that prevents practicing old stuff if you still have lives, and what is worse is that if you run out of lives then you can only practice to earn a maximum of one life. The ridiculous phrases (such as "the cows cleaned the farm yesterday") still exist. I like the fact the app introduces new exercises like the radio phone in which sharpens listening

Overall, a very great app. Only thing I have an issue with, is the absurd amount of time a lesson takes to load sometimes. Not my internet connection or anything but for like 20/30 minutes, Duo is loading with a quote for what seems like eternity. My double xp and my time is just wasting during all these. I just close the app, tbh. And most times, the lesson doesn't start with sound, can't hear a thing. Sometimes, I just have to skip the question. I'd like if something is done about these.

Having the option to practise to regain hearts taken away in the ad-supported version is preventing me from making good learning progress. Bugs that don't register rewards during quests, and that make it difficult to see the full selection of words to choose an answer from, are frustrating. I'm using the app less and less at the moment because it's no longer a fun learning experience.

The app provides an organised way of learning a new language and overall I find it useful. However, the app is terribly glitchy. I am currently stuck on an infinite level. I complete the lesson, gain the xp, then remain on the same level unable to progress. I am sure it will fix itself but this is just one example, things like this are always going wrong. Lessons also get quite repetitive.

App is very hard to navigate, this is like a textbook example of poor interface design. The design is both dumbed down and confusing. For example I wanted to find out about purchasing a subscription - I couldn't find it anywhere. I had to go to their webdite where I found the steps, apparently I need to go to the home screen, click on the GEMS which takes me to a store. I guess they really don't want to sell subscriptions.

It has been really useful to refresh my long forgotten foreign language learning. I have watched my child gain confidence day by day using the app. However, when they add new content and re sync your progress effectively rolling you back several levels, they really demotivate users! Think about how users are going to feel before you just roll out these things! Now we have a feature that blocks you from practicing and fully rebuilding your lives! It's starting to feel like the want rid of users!

The music program is ridiculous! At the end of a chapter, it makes you review ad infinitum and makes you "correct mistakes" that you never made--again, seemingly forever. The only way to get out of the loop is to back out--and lose all your points. If you try to jump ahead (because you've had 8 years of piano lessons!), and do a challenge, the challenge stops midway with a message to keep practicing. (I'd like to wring the developer's neck. Error.)

I use Duolingo (free) for 2 years, and I love this app. But I am disappointed with new updates. You removed filling heart by playing, there are 2 ads after every lesson (no 1), and after completing a group of lessons, points were doubling, but not anymore. You are making people buy premium options, but many of us just will stop using it

"Learn a language. For free. Forever." That used to be Duolingo's catchphrase. I used to recommend the app everywhere! The latest update in the app has effectively rendered it entirely inefficient and useless for free users. The paid subscription leaves much to be desired as AI is the one teaching now instead of real language teachers. I am deleting the app, my account and data and telling everyone I know that it's not worth the time and/or money anymore.

Downloaded this as I'd been meaning to try and learn a new language for some time. I chose French and am still learning daily over 200 days after I started. Duolingo is brilliantly laid out and progress is gradual, building on what you have learned in simple steps. Update: after using duolingo for over 3 years without much issue, duolingo have decided to mess with the heart system, which pushes you to upgrade to super/ max if you want to progress at a decent speed. Shame, it was a good free app.

the most recent changes have just made it considerably harder to progress, which takes away from the pleasure of learning through it. because you don't feel youre progressing. Now you can't just practice five times in a row to refillyour hearts, but are limited to topping up only one, so if you make a mistake again, you get caught in a loop!!

Ughh... It's such a pain. It's free language study but it's such an irritating app. It's barely usable without the expensive premium, so your choice is "free but horrible" or "expensive but usable". I'd love a cheaper, nerfed version. Besides that, double XP bonuses can't be stored or bought, so when you do get them it might be at a bad time and you feel forced into playing until it's up. Some tasks can be asinine to understand and Duolingo can be bad at explaining, especially French audio tasks

Overall, best learning app so far! I've at least learned basic Spanish sentences in, like, 2 weeks or so. Also been doing Dutch, Math, and Music lessons all at once. I also don't sorta have a problem with the prices for Super Duo. Problem?? Needs Data/WiFi, meaning when I go out and have to do my lessons, I won't be able to, since it requires only Data or WiFi. Still giving 5 Stars tho. 👍

To the Duolingo Team, I am a long-time user of Duolingo and have always appreciated its engaging approach and free access to language learning. This app has played a significant role in helping me stay motivated and committed to my language goals. However, I have recently been disappointed by some of the recent changes, particularly the increased limitations on hearts and the more frequent ads in the free version. Previously, it was possible to earn additional hearts by completing lessons or c

After using this app for 6 months or so learning Mandarin, it has stopped accepting any voice responses as correct. Not even partial answers. Apparently everything I was correct for before is now worth nothing and it gives no feedback about what exactly I am saying wrong. This has put me at a standstill. While I am able to learn some more grammar and words, my ability to speak more has effectively stopped. I wanted to learn Canto, but they only have Mando to Canto so this feels even worse.

I have a two year streak and pay the medium subscription of £48 per year. Quite disappointed that even paying to subscribe I've recently become more restricted in usage, it seems that we're being pressed to upgrade to Duo Max which will cost £10 per month. It's harder to progress now. I think that if me as a paying customer isn't having a great experience now, then it must hardly be worth bothering if you play the free version.

There are a lot of ways to try and learn a language in a year. I regret choosing Duolingo. Through the app you tend to learn by rote. This is helpful as you start but makes developing a better understanding of the language (or actually being able to communicate your thoughts) more difficult. The advertising spam has always been annoying but recent changes which prevent you from practicing to refill hearts have made the app incredibly frustrating to use.

I have purchased one-year subscription. However, whenever I open the app, it keeps on prompting to add the phone number. But I never receive the verification code required to add the number. So much so that I am totally unable to use the app now without adding cell number although I have an account based on email. Secondly, I'm learning arabic on the app. I have taken lessons on 40 consecutive days but the degree of difficulty is still very basic. There is no option to customise learning.

The Spanish course is so poorly structured. When you fail levels, you've got to train to get more hearts. Training is not on anything you are trying to learn. or the mistakes you've made. No. It's on stuff so many units ago that you'll most likely fail the same level again when you play it as you've got to pay or google your mistakes. Expect to take notes as this app struggles with teaching grammar and tense, and the games are full of words they never taught you. They should be later in the game

Hmm... I've been using this app to learn Italian for over six months and suddenly it's repeating the same lesson in a loop. Can't get past it. Over 300 days in and today it suddenly "demoted" me and won't let me move forward. What gives??

My biggest problem with the app is that I can't type all the answers myself. When it was possible I would do that exclusively. Typing everything out helps me memorize the vocabulary way better than seeing all the words in front of me and just arranging them. I will up the rating again if you add an option to switch to keyboard input only.

Don't waste your time. Starts great, but then the issues arise: failure to recognise spoken responses as correct (even when you record from the app and play back to it), insistence on teaching writing eg Hanji when you don't need it, and in a weird way (let's start with a full phase made up of multiple complex characters). And you can only make 5 mistakes before you are locked out for 24 hours. I was making good progress until this happened, then got stuck on the same lesson and I've given up

This app makes me wanna toss my phone into a volcano. You can tap the words for hints, only 90% of the time the hints are wrong, or vague at best, resulting in getting a wrong answer. It has different parameters for correct answers, on english-spanish or spanish-english. Even with google translate, i have failed some of the same lessons multiple times. On top of that, it is packed with commercials, and they have removed the ability to earn free hearts, in order to blackmail users to spend money.

I used to love this app but the latest changes, namely limiting the use of the 'practice to earn hearts', makes it increasingly hard and frustrating to continue on the harder lessons. This makes me feel irritated as I am not going to spend money on the 'Super Duolingo' as they probably want people to do, but instead spend less time on the app, thereby spending less time watching their ads, making them actually lose money...

For the first few lessons it's very good. But the constant adverts and the harsh penalties for mistakes make the more difficult lessons very grating. The removal of being able to do brush up lessons to gain hearts was a huge mistake and makes the overall experience significantly worse. Allow me to refill my hearts with brush up lessons again. There is no way this was tested for user impact. It's 100% an anti pattern for enjoyment and leading.

To move from one lesson to another, I need to watch some animations, claim some xp, move up some levels in a league, decide if I want to invite friends, watch two adds (which are ok actually since I use the free version), collect some gems, level up again but this time sideways... Incredible amount of distracting gamification which I think is just waste of time. I would not pay to remove adds, but I would happily pay to remove gamification from what could otherwise be a very useful app.

Used to be good before they started limiting free user's options. For example, you used to be able to renew hearts (taken away by making mistakes) by doing Practice lessons - but that was taken away. Explanations are locked behind another paid subscription (for which you get ads even if you're paying 'only' for a premium plan). Overall it's still usable but recently their decisions are making it worse.

I hate recent changes to duolingo. I get demoted and lose all my progress if I don't play enough and earn enough xp. But I could lose all 5 hearts in one lesson, which means I can't play on. When that happened in the past, I could practice to earn hearts. However, I can't do that anymore either. Also, I've lost all speaking exercises. I'm not feeling very encouraged to keep using this app. In fact, it's not teaching me anything. Just throws random phrases at you & says, "Here, work that out!"

Too many multi-choice options. You don't get taught sentence structure, you need to guess. It's as if the lessons trick you into using the wrong word order to make you lose hearts so you either buy super or spend gems. I want to learn a language, not play a game. There's too much focus on the graphics, not enough on teaching. Would rate lower if I could.

I am having a lot of fun learning Spanish with Duolingo. Uno problemo with it. Soy muy competitivo. The leaderboards would be nice for someone who is just having fun. I find it takes away my focus on learning and instead puts the emphasis on being first on the leaderboards. Can you please remove leaderboards. I like all the other builds, but I would like to learn at my own pace. If you can't, have an option where leaderboards don't affect your progress.

I have been using Duolingo for a year and the latest update is just awful. The course has become much more difficult, I now have to guess half of the phrases/words. This is a learning program, not a lottery. I am glad that you added video calls, but why do I need a video call? Better add more words to the dictionary. Bad experience, for an annual subscription

I really like the app at its core, but the ads have become incredibly intrusive. At the end of each lesson is now two ads, one from Duolingo, another from random games that use deceptive means such as fake buttons to prevent you from skipping them. Closing the app and restarting prevents you from progressing. They also started to block some of the useful feedback behind an absurdly expensive AI subscription. I would love to rate higher if they went back to how it used to be! Disappointed :(

I'm on a 1500+ day streak. Had my moments with Duolingo over the years, but they made it more fun again. However they really messed up now. They're shoving DuoLingo Super in your face more than ever before. The adverts are annoying, but I can look past that. You cannot regain your lost hearts anymore by doing practice rounds. You either have to wait hours upon hours to regain hearts, or it's all Duolingo Super. They've made their goals unattainable now by having done that. Really disappointed.

I enjoyed duolingo. But the course refresher features that you are forced to take everytime you dont use the app for a while is so annoying that Ive stopped using the app. To the devs: Make the course refresher optional and also more challenging. Having to relearn "yes", "no", "thank you" because I didnt use the app for a week is a waste of time and frustrating.

It's an excellent program, I've learned a lot of French over the last several years. It seems like more features are being hidden behind paywalls or rather there are more features available to help you but you need to pay for them. A small change happened with the last update, the option to practice to earn hearts used to always be available. I liked to be able to use that feature just to hone skills before moving on in regular lessons, now it's only available if you have zero hearts.

The app is not great but for a free app you get a lot of content (ignoring ads). With that said if you really want to learn a new language YOU MUST BE CONSISTENT. This shouldn't be your only tool for learning but it is definitely great as long as you practice everyday. Update: so they just removed the option to gain heart trough practicing. That completely negates this app's purpose. Very disappointed with this change.

This app is very entertaining to learn a language! I like it a lot and help me to keep learning each days ! But problem: some period of the year I do have a very busy schedule and I can't go seriously everyday on the app to keep learning and extending my streak to motivate me... It would be so much better to have a vacation mode, to put the streaks, notifications and everything else in sleep mode while my schedule busier, so when it is calmer I can give a better dedicated time to really learn.

I have been using this app for years now and the initial unpaid version was the best. It has improved from the first time they introduced hearts, but it feels like it becomes more and more aggressive to convince you to upgrade the longer you use it. Hearts are increasingly expensive, and it makes you lose hearts with questions that can't be answered. Also, some of the ads are buggy and run forever without the off button appearing (eg Mahjong).

90% of the app is now things that are unrelated to learning. It's just a commercial game now with far too many popups and notifications etc. It's also difficult to navigate between the lessons now with the new map. You can't even use the app offline anymore which is ridiculous. The only thing that makes it bearable is that's it's still free. Also, it truly sucks that you can only choose Latin American Spanish. You can't even choose whether you want to have your own profile picture or an avatar

I've been a paid subscriber for several years, but over the past few months the app has been changed in ways that make it of little use to learn the endangered minority languages that it used to trumpet itself as being about. All explanations have been removed, and there are entirely too few examples for implicit learning to teach you word order, cases, or other core grammatical features. I'm sure it's a fine resource for Spanish or German, but don't bother with it for minority languages.

In the recent update all the good features has been deactivated. There is no option to fill the hearts (and at the same time to review a lesson), one can only do it by paying. The daily chest has been turned off, and that was very motivating to work regularly. Chests for challenges give potions instead of crystals, which is not so usefull.

Duolingo is the ever iconic app you can never go wrong with! it is especially helpful if you have some basics down and know the rules of the language. it's also good for beginners but i look at it more for practice. it's gamified UI helps you keep pushing, it's lovely characters engaged in stories (both audio and visual) will keep you hooked to your phone. AND! it's almost impossible to break your language streak because it sends you constant reminders that you just cannot ignore. i must have!

The app is well made but the learning process is not adapted to a specific language. It's too generic. Also the multiple choices answers are too obvious, you can guess it even without knowing the language. And also the app is made so that you stay longer to learn a language better than learn it quickly and efficiently. So you stay longer on the app, good for them, not for us.

Duolingo's a really fun way to pick up a new language. It breaks everything down into bite-sized lessons, so it's easy to fit learning into your day. The way it mixes things up – sometimes you're translating, other times you're listening or speaking – helps you learn in a bunch of different ways. Plus, getting those streaks going is super motivating! The free version gives you a good feel for the app, but Duolingo Plus has even more lessons and features to make learning faster.

10/10 App, I've been using it for a while but twice now I've built a streak and on a day where I'm doing a lesson close to midnight and finish the lesson it will bug out at the end screen after the premium advert, losing the entire lesson, losing the streak. The streak calendar says I've completed lessons for the day that's missing, but the streak is still reset. It's very heartbreaking. Other than that, it is a very functional app. Could use a feature for a full pronunciation lesson, too.

It's a very good app, but I think it's teaching me the same thing over and over again which is a bit confusing. Is anyone else having this problem? anyway, it's a really good app, I highly recommend it. Duolingo teaches you different languages in a fun way. Also, if you keep a good streak with lots of perfect lessons, you get a few days of Free Super Duolingo.

The app is good, but the ads for the premium version are just relentless, they're constantly hammering you with it between every single lesson you do and it's getting really annoying. The language courses are very varying in quality depending on which language it is. French and Spanish are brilliant. Arabic has you do 40+ lessons without teaching you a single word. It's nothing but symbols and sounds for hour after hour, which kills any motivation or interest you might have had.

How can you always screw up with the new features?? Taking off the leassons to earn hearts just take away my motivation to keep going. Having busing advertising is exhausting and frustrating, the ads are basically the same every single time, and break the learning cicle. Other thing... You focus only on the main languages. Mandarin isn't even complete, Arabic is trash, and you are adding new features to English! Or French... The app was good, but only keeps getting worse and worse.

Been using Duolingo for many years. The latest changes were the worst in a long time! I don't care about XP boosters as a reward for lessons, I am not in a competition. I want gems so that I can buy the occasional streak freeze! Is it as the community keeps saying, a way to push people into paying for the subscription? I don't think the app is rated 1 star, but that's how I can voice my dissatisfaction. I may as well give up the streak, but that will make me give up the app altogether.

I am completely disappointed with this app. It is no longer possible to practice in order to gain more lives—now, you either pay, do lessons with just one life, or wait 5 hours to get one more. Of course, since practicing is no longer an option, you can't review mistakes and learn from them unless you PAY. I’ve always hated games with energy/life systems, and this is one of the worst systems out there, but Duolingo used it smartly before, encouraging practice. Now, it's just a way to make money

If you're planning to go abroad, the country speaks a different language, you should download Duolingo. It shows fun images if you put their icon on the home page, even the logo can change up to your streak. I am learning French, maths and music in this app and it is very fun to learn. There are leaderboards and if you get the top 3 you'll get awards, overall it's a very fun and good app. I'll rate it a 5 star.

Downloaded this app around 3 yrs ago after hearing from some of my friends. My learning experience on this platform has been great!! The way it teaches the language is the major thing that has kept me intrigued to it. Although now it has ads unlike before, I understand that because the extraordinary service it provides covers it all and the timing of the ads is also not disturbing. Overall, I just love this app and recommend this to everyone who seeks a good platform for language learning!!

It was good when you could practice old lessons to earn hearts, but they have removed this option. This is clearly to force you to buy their "super learner" subscription, but as a casual learner, I don't think I should have to. I'll most likely switch to a different app now. The app designers probably started out with good intentions, but it's now become a money grabbing exercise. Disappointing...

They've taken away the only reliable way to get random words and phrases by removing practice hearts. After you complete a course (and still can't speak the language) they throw same five words and phrases at you ad infinitum, so the only way to actually get practice in that language was the practice hearts section. The app used to be semi decent but after years of stripping away features has become a shell of what it used to be. Enshittification is in full effect.

This birdie can really help me learn languages. Limited hearts make me learn carefully without making mistakes. what's more, It doesn't feel boring to learn here, daily learning streak, leaderboard, leagues, being able to learn with friends, it almost feels like a game. super subscription doesn't feel bad either, I tried the trial for 2 days. It actually made me learn like crazy those two days, it was fun. The pay system does not feel like being pushed. but, I'm not buying it any time soon.

It was a 5 star rating (even with the occasional errors I come across) until it limited the ability to earn hearts with practice. Now I do 1 lesson a day (on duolingo) to keep my streak and remind me to set time for learning. But then I go to some other online options for further lessons. If and when I break my streak, I'll move on from this app unless it allows the practice options again.

over all a solid way to learn (japanese). but i find the ai hard to understand sometimes and the sentence structure seems disjointed. Also, there are no basic grammar lessons so it can be hard to put sentences together property. This is a bit frustrating. I do like the pace that you learn new words etc and how the topics are broken up into different real world scenarios.

Music and maths arrived on Android and was enjoying music greatly to start with. However, now i'm at a point where the songs have become more complex, the standard Duolingo approach just isn't working. I need to be able to slow down a song to practice and to scroll through the notes to prepare. I think I'm done for now until it gets better.

As of today, the 20th of November 2024 I can't recommend this app any longer for learning a language. It removed for free users the possibility to refill all hearts. There are 5 in total, and with the new version, they allow only 1 heart to be refilled when you watch an ad maximum 2. In addition to that, you have to use up every heart to refill 1 and if you are lucky, you get another one by watching and ad. The push seems deliberate to get free users to pay for the app.

They keep changing massive aspects of the app every couple of months. Just leave it alone! Having extra XP really encouraged people to do more lessons when combined with 2x XP for 15 mins. Now I just can't be bothered. It is a great app but the developers are ruining it...... This comment was two years ago. Today they've taken away the practice for hearts, why? Surely you want us to play yeah? Why discourage people from playing and learning? I've got to wait five hours now to play again. Awful.

The pay version is okay. One of its strengths is that it allows you to make use of odd lots of time, such as waiting for the bus. I had the pay version for a year. The free version isn't good, though. You get punished for mistakes, and it's constantly taking over your phone with ads. They say they provide it for free for millions, but they're not really giving away for free. But the free version is next to useless with so many ads. Also, the ads are irrelevant and uninteresting.

I'm learning Chinese, but the speaking exercises don't work. The time challenges to get to 3 stars are made to force the user into buying time boosts. The rounds of words are randomly generated. If you're lucky you get a few 1 word rounds, and you might be able to finish before time runs out. If you're unlucky you only get long sentences, for which there is no time to match all the words in time. It's obviously a way to make more money, and it's obviously ruining the user's experience.

Graphics: very amusing and well done. Gamification: amuzing first, but then you realise instead of learning the language you run around the points/tasks to complete an event. Getting a double bonus only when doing a lesson 6-12 & 18-24 puts you in an endless loop. Not being able to recover hearts until you lose all of them is a back stabbing decision too. Becomes quite difficult to do any progress on the free tier.

I think that getting this app really helped me with learning a foreign language, my teacher even thought that I was from a Spanish speaking country. It is easy to learn and it is easy to stay motivated with streaks and challenges, it's almost like playing a game while learning. I use Super Duolingo and it helps with having the infinite hearts (the default is 5) and I also like the radio feature because sometimes I prefer listening to someone speak it. I would recommend it to anyone. Ty duo!!!!!!

Duolingo was a FREE, amazing language app that was extremely accessible and fun. The monetization team has been slowly killing the app over the years with little tweaks such as taking away the opportunity to gain back your hearts without paying. The app is littered with ads to the point that it's insufferable. I will be uninstalling and looking for a better alternative that puts the learners first, not their wallets.

Double adds after each lesson, no longer being able to practise for extra hearts, inconsistent feedback (English to Italian course). Don't believe the 4.5 star average this app has: it no longer is what it used to be. I'm only still here because my dad gets excited about keeping our friend streak. But even he is annoyed with the app by now, so that might not last long

Used to love it, but the app has gotten worse with each update. Previously we could practice without limits, and doing so restored hearts. Now this option is disabled, unless I subscribe to have unlimited hearts. With this system I need to repeat an easy lesson before doing one to actually learn if I want to keep my streak, to not risk losing all hearts. Also, there are so many ads. Each lesson requires seeing an ad at the end.

The old version had a possibility to type answers, long ago it even was a requirement. Please bring this back. Too many useful features are being moved to Super. Want to train the words? Pay.
