Want to do the 2nd level of your lessons? Pay. Want to be able to make mistakes and learn from them? Pay! Pay pay pay pay pay. No cash, no learning. So ok, let's pay. €8 per month? Ok, that's doable... But wait, there's a catch: You can only pay for a whole year in advance! Again, in old versions...

The new logo is despicable. XP boosts are extremely frustrating to use now. Hearts cannot be recovered past 1 heart any more. You have to wait 5 hrs (instead of 4 previously). Many audio are missing in Chinese. Double xp isn't always rewarded, sometimes levels force you to re-do them right after completing it. Many alternate solutions are missing, especially to open-ended questions. Ambiguously gendered questions don't allow alternative answers, takes a life away instead

This used to be a good app but with the removal of practicing to earn hearts it now almost useless. You can now only practice for one heart and then you have to stop. You used to be able to practice continuously, which I found was very good at fixing the language in my head. Also, the discussion was removed. This was very helpful, because you could ask about grammar and there would be a number of experts to help. Now looking for something better. Memrise seems good and does Welsh.

Recent monetisation updates to the app, namely the removal of the ability to practice and recharge hearts, has negatively hampered my learning experience with this app. Sounds like the developers have moved away from their core values... Just like I'll be moving to a competitor's solution. Goodbye 2+ year streak, it was fun whilst it lasted.

I used Duolingo for over 4 years and benefitted a lot. But a series of recent changes aimed at monetizing the app have made it dramatically less fun and more frustrating. They limited free earning of hearts, removed explanations of errors and discussion forums, increased the gem cost of all of various extras. I get that companies need to earn money, but they've made this unusable unless you page absurd monthly subscription fees higher than netflix, amazon prime and spotify for one little app!

Don't expect to speak another language using this app. You need to speak to people who are native speakers. It is a great supplement to learning but probably only contributes to ~10% of what you'll need to get where you want to be. Watch news/media/movies in the language that you want to learn, but note that learning and memorising phrases, but most importantly - practicing them with natives, is what will make you successful. Duolingo is a successful app, but it won't make you successful.

I have ammended my previous 5 star review in protest of Duolingo dramatically altering the "Practice to earn hearts" function on mobile devices. This is certain to seriously affect the way non-premium learners use the app. I love progressing through my course, but I don't want to be forced to breeze through it. Long, dedicated learning sessions revising randomised parts of my course are essential to my learning, and I know others feel this too. It is demotivating, and greedy. Do better.

Maybe it's a good app, but i wouldn't know since after a week of trying, the ads in between the modules are getting borderline annoying, and it's massively putting me off investing more time. Show me it's worth subscribing to it by the content instead of annoying me every waking second out of the content.

There have been a lot of changes to the app recently, which seem unnecessary and have made things worse on the free version. The withdrawal of the option to do practice exercises to redeem mistakes (or just to do some practice) is a huge negative. I'm considering leaving after 900 days because of this. I could purchase a subscription, but it would not give me the practice option. My guess is that the advertising revenue has dropped so the free option isn't really an option for the company or u

I hate that they removed the option to "practice to earn hearts", now we can only use it if we have 0 hearts. I don't like being forced to learn new things when I haven't fully learned the previous contents, we need to be able to practice like we used to. To anyone interested: I found another free app that's really good called Busuu, it's like a better (but a bit more boring in my opinion, less gamified) version of Duolingo.

The app has gotten worse fast. First they changed it so you will always get promoted or demoted to another division, staying in the same division by scoring just enough points isn't an option anymore. Now the stories option is also gone. A perfect way to keep up your streak for those days you haven't got the time to do a full lesson. The focus is now on paying for the app. Keeping up my streak was the only motivation to return every day for a lesson. So now that motivation is gone too.

I have used it for Italian and, currently, French over the years to get some basic language skills for holidays. It's better than just a phrase book but the frequent push to upgrade to the paid version is intrusive. I understand they need to make money but I find only having so many 'lives' makes it more challenging. But now you can only get one heart, I think it is au revouir from me.

Doing Irish language, a lack of clarity in speakers' diction, almost like words are cropped in the audio editing. Some of the speakers and their diction sound very clear. However, other words and pronunciations sound like non native speakers reading from a phonetic script, lacking clarity and confidence, putting emphasis on the wrong things. A great idea, visually good, let down by poor execution. Duolingo, get your act together, revisit and redo all of the poor pronunciations to make this 5 ⭐️

Recently cancelled my Super Duo subscription. The app has really stagnated, and delays language learning in favor of time on the app. Development recently seems to be more focused on aesthetics. I would much rather have improved course content and real cultural knowledge, instead of math courses, music courses, a purple haired animation rolling it's eyes at me, and a lunatic bird threatening me. In my humble opinion it's a shame that this app lost touch with it's language learning roots.

it's hard to give a favourable rating to the free version as after each lesson (four in each segment) you're met with two adverts. one sponsored and one for Duolingo max. not fun spending literally double the time in an app due to advertising. not customer friendly. the apps itself is good so far, learning words. sadly due to the remodeling to maximize profit features that were free are now behind a wall. features that are crucial for understanding Grammer etc.

I liked the ease of use and the repetition but the new thing where I can't practice to earn hearts is disappointing. I'm thinking of quitting because I can't just go and earn hearts whenever I want. I liked reviewing old lessons and the reminders of words I have learned. Also, I'm not receiving all of my rewards. I get them, then they disappear. Also it ticks me off when time expires on a double point lesson and I only get half the points promised. App is getting glitchy.

I want to give a huge thank you to the Duolingo app! It's been an amazing tool for learning new languages in such a fun, interactive way. The lessons are structured in bite-sized exercises that make it easy to stay motivated, even with a busy schedule. I love how it mixes listening, reading, writing, and speaking to cover all language skills.And that cute and angry faces makes me motivate and pushes me to learn. At last, It's the best place to learn a language in my opinion ,

I don't how they get all these 🌟. I've been using the web version for many years and learnt a few languages but always only up to intermediate. After that, the practice is no longer useful. They don't teach grammar well. They just expect u to memorize it through the sentence. And lately, it's getting absurd. They don't teach the grammar but they use it to deduct the hearts. For some languages, the English is bad. This is critical at higher level for they deduct your hearts for their mistakes.

Was a good app up until update more recently, in which the system repeatedly doesn't count a lesson, even after it's shown me that my "daily streak" count was increased. And the only way I know/find out is opening the app the next day and it saying that I used a freeze up. And I don't recieve any notifications saying I still haven't completed my daily lesson. I know that's not main function of this, but it's deeply infuriating and has just added to other issues that I've had previously.

Fun to learn with and keep at, definitely a valid option. But very slow. If you're casual, it will take ages to become proficient at any language. If you're serious, I recommend putting in at least 20-30 minutes a day, supplementung with other apps and actually speaking to people of the language you want to learn. Then you will actually see good results soon.

I’ve been a loyal Duolingo user for more than a year and always appreciated the "practice to earn hearts" feature. It allowed consistent learning without interruptions and encouraged revisiting past lessons. Removing this feature is a huge letdown, especially for long-term users like me who value a seamless and free learning experience. This change feels unfair and demotivating. Please bring back the "practice to earn hearts" option—it made Duolingo stand out as a learner-friendly platform!

I am learning Vietnamese using this application, I haven't find any other application that does that. It is efficient if you are consistent. Edit : they have made it unplayable by removing the training option that gets you back hearts. Actually you can get only 1 heart back when training once you have no heart left. However you will not go through lessons without mistakes which will soft lock you progress in a "1 heart loop". Once again greedy monetisation pushing the wrong button ...

As someone learning the Turkish language, I find the Duolingo app incredibly useful. Its design is both engaging and intuitive, making it easy to stay motivated and interested in the lessons. The gamified approach, with interactive exercises and rewards, keeps the learning experience enjoyable and encourages consistent progress. The app's ability to adapt to my pace while introducing new vocabulary and grammar concepts is truly impressive. Whether you're a beginner or looking to enhance your ski

Learning Japanese has been fun on this app but I would rate it 5 stars if three things can be adjusted. 1. After completing a lesson (legendary),The new color (gold) doesn't show sometimes and it still gives me the option to take the exercise for legendary again. The XP of that lesson too wouldn't be added. 2. The Duo super 2 weeks free subscription doesn't work and it keeps on telling me to add credit or debit account. 3. The Duolingo app keeps disturbing my mail like anything. Thanks ❤️

It's good but some instructions are not clear, ie you're given a phrase and don't know if to speak phase or translate phrase (especially knowing how bad speech to text can be). Again you're 'warned' about accents, after, the fact when you may not know how to type them. The trumpeting sounds are wearing thin. The gamification is not helpful to learning especially if you go to correct mistakes. It basically kicks you out and you have to wait hours! Not at all motivational...

If the monthly subscription was £5 and you could cancel at any time, I would pay. Asking for a year upfront for the cheaper rate instantly makes me not want to commit to a subscription. Other than this company employing the same tactics as a lot of other learning apps to try and get you to pay a year upfront, I'm enjoying the free version, and it keeps me wanting to learn. I'm strong-willed enough to put up with advertisements for things I'm never going to buy and so are most other people.

I just want to learn a language. I am truly internally motivated to do so. The stupid gamification techniques applied in every interaction are annoying and take away the fun in learning. Also, it would be so helpful if you could turn off the sounds: 1. you can learn while listening to music, which would be helpful for me; 2. three characters have the most annoying voice. There are French words now that I dislike, because one of those voices taught them

The app is overall quite ok for learning a language, but... they keep pushing you to buy a paid version, but it's impossible to find pricing anywhere. The gamification part is getting messed up all time. My course was restructured multille times, causing doing the same excersises multiple times. Recently once you do one legendary lesson from a section, all of the previously available ones change to done without me completing them. The mini games are pointless. You do more tapping than learning

Its a very good app for learning and improve various language, math skills and music intellegence (seriously, I'm not making this up). It can be also be used as a social media app where you can get famous for being good in it. It is also the only app that can you use it to spend your free time in the productive way. Please add cantonese and hokkein. Also try to add other fields of math like algebra for example.

I've used Duolingo for 2 months after my son encouraged me to join. I found it more fun than I expected to learn a language after 25 years. My favourite feature was Practice to earn hearts as I could embed what I'd previously learnt. Now that feature has been removed, I feel too pressured to be perfect while being taught at a quicker speed than I want. The fun has been removed. I don't need to add stress into my life, so I will probably stop using the app, even though it will disappoint my son.

Duo has become extremely buggy and often will not move on from a level despite having gone through it one or more times. It just repeats the same lesson. Also from time to time it will not allow you put in a response. Very irritating . Further, it sometimes randomly uses incorrect english in the available options, e.g. I use to play with my dog. This should be used to and is most annoying.

I have been using it since 2017 and it was simple and good. Now it has deteriorated with the adoption of perfunctory AI audio and texts while laying off contractors. It starts forcing people to use word banks for very simple sentences, slowing down the pace and making errors more common. It is using the psychological manipulations commonly seen in free-to-play games now, a lot of them. Enough is enough, capitalists. Thank you and good bye, little owl.

Great app. I've learned a lot, but that was on the free trial. There is an ad after every lesson, which is what made me stop using it if it had a lot less ads I'd still use it but im probably going to move to a different app because it's too hard to learn with a add every minute for how much I do lessons at a quick pase to remember better.

Limiting the ability to practice for hearts (only up to 2 w/ ads) unless they are completely out feels like a strange way to punish your userbase. It does not push people towards buying subscriptions, but turns them away. The decision feels very unbecoming of the company, and sours one's experience. Being forced to go into practice lessons with only 1 heart, making one error, getting 1 heart back to try again creates a frustrating cycle that seems counterintuitive to learning. Disappointing.

Well it's fun though especially its boosting my education. But the annoying part is they always give me wrong even when I've not finished and I don't press check and it is really frustrating for those who don't have money because they have to practice to get one ♥ but if you play again and make a mistake the what's the point. And I can't also get super duolingo cause I'm just 9 years and I don't have money.

I know you've made some progress on animations (the words jump a little when you write the sentence and clicking 'Check'; or when you finish a unit and the clock's arrow spins fast (really cute) and many more. But I don't like the duoflation in your app (it used to be 30 gems now it is 100). Not to mention the two ads after just a lesson. Not cool, Duolingo. Not cool.

Enough with the meaningless xp and tournaments. These are detrimental to learning because all they do is encourage people to collect more xp as fast as possible instead of trying to learn. I wish it was as simple as choosing to ignore the fluff that xp and tournaments are but, unfortunately, for many of us, due to the way our brains are wired, the focus becomes the game aspect of the app, not the learning aspect

After 12 years on Duo, I've now deleted the app. Can you imagine, unlimited hearts and minimal ads and then boom, i wake up to 5 hearts and ads after every lesson?!! Not doing it. All duo is doing is herding you and forcing you towards paying for SUPER as a bid to escape the endless ads. It's not longer an app you can use with leisure. Everything is behind and ADs, even lives. An ad after EVERY lesson is ridiculous and a waste of time. Other avenues are there to learn from, use them up.

I'm unable to earn hearts, and it's severely limiting my enjoyment. I used to look forward to practising, but my enthusiasm has been replaced with frustration, and I no longer like using Duolingo. I'm much less interested in developing my language skills due to this small but significant change, and as a result, my time spent learning has dropped dramatically.

Removing the comments has seriously decreased the usefulness of the app. I paid for premium just to remove the ads that were talking at least 20% of the time in app. I had to adjust to being unable to repair hearts through practice. But without community comments I am loosing a lot of explanation. I will not update my subscription. And now the free mode is practically dead. It's clear removing comments was for the 2nd level subscription (MAX). I am looking for an alternative after 2 years.

an amazing app to learn a language. Well, it's best to learn a language in a language school. However, Duolingo allows you to see/oversee how the language work. With personified characters leading your journey and giving you the freedom to how you want to learn. there's only one problem. Those freedoms are stuck behind a paywall, not entirely but the "specific" type of lessons. Overall, still worth it.

Based on how the app used to work a few months back, I'd have given it 4/5 stars. But they effed it up. 1) Autoclick fail: skip a speak/listen task and get the next one failed for free. 2) Hearts of gold: you can no longer win back hearts unless you're at 0 - and even then, you can only get 1. 3) Win stupid prizes: no more bonus XP time after completing a set. Also, the daily bonuses don't always work - and there's no way to make up for that; good luck maxing XP with no bonus!

The new interface is a disaster. It's like the people making this never use the app. Going to previous lesson requires scrolling from your current. Which could be 200+ units. Each time. I had Spanish completed and now I'm somewhere half way. Spanish course has already changed multiple times in the past each time losing progress. Then the old adult stories were replaced with the current kindergarten ones. Why keep paying for premium for something that keeps changing from under your feet?

WHY IS THERE ONLY A PROMOTION AND DEMONTION ZONE? What happened to staying in the SAME league?!! I'm on week 36 in pearl league and I don't want to loose that streak! I still don't like the learning paths. You have no choice in your lesson, and because there's no pictures like before, you can't easily decide to revisit an old topic. Apart from that, the app is definitely getting better. I very much like the update on learning kanji, you have to read and recognize it in different contexts

Some of the language courses are okay but less common language courses are riddled with errors. Typographical errors are punished inconsistently which makes it far too easy to make mistakes even if you actually know the correct answer. The speech recognition is patchy, which again leads to hearts being removed without you making a mistake. Now, to add insult to injury, they've removed the option to practice to earn hearts so that you can't progress unless you use the paid subscription.

Great app!! Great for learning languages and practicing correct diction too. Don't like the heart system though , prefer the lingots system all the way. With the recent updates my practice to earn hearts button doesn't work, it's always offline even though everything else is good. Also recently the app has a tendency to jump back to past sections on the path, so you have to come out the app and go back in a few times for it to go back to where you actually were. Rather annoying.

A decent app but the learning experience has recently become worse due to trying to force an AI tier onto everyone. Now your mistakes aren't explained: you'll be wrong but not know why and have to just rote learn the correct answer. Duolingo was never perfect; it couldn't be as people learn differently. For me it was previously 4 stars but is now 3 stars. Solid in what it does but where failures like the lack of achievement progress bars could be overlooked, interfering with the learning cannot.

Slow learning process with many dummy exercises repeated over and over. But it would be fine, just time consuming. Half of sentences are there for political correctness and have no meaning for learning. This made me to decide about never supporting duo through buying full version. But at some point conditions of use changed and without paid version you practically cannot do more than one exercise per day. In such setup app is useless. After 290 days of streak, I am unistalling.

Fun and quite engaging. However, having used it a couple of times in advance of visiting a foreign country and putting in many weeks of practice, it doesn't take you far in gaining practical use of the language. Secondly, returning to a language that I've learnt in the past, it's really bad at finding your existing level of knowledge. Scored 100% on the test, it said my level had been determined and then set questions that were really easy and not improving my knowledge. App deleted

So nice to use. Great to work through bite sized chunks at my own pace and squeeze short lessons in whenever I'm free. Lots of reinforcement and testing helps as does its gamification. Edit 10/11/24 - I'm now nearing the end of my German course. But the developers have now suddenly dropped the option to "Pratice to earn hearts". I get why it's to drive subscriptions etc. but it's just proving frustrating, and for my kids, who're trying to build a good learning habit, this kind of sucks.

Good for learning languages but still very basic. There needs to be more advanced material heading into the higher B levels of CEFR, or even higher for those who want to go that far. UPDATE: I am cancelling my subscription. The aggressive marketing of the overpriced Max level is annoying considering the limits of the app as noted above. It's a shame. It used to be good. Not any more.

It WAS a great app. Recently they removed the option to practice for hearts which gives you opportunity to review random topics quickly not being afraid of making mistakes + earn extra XP. That's not fun anymore. PS. I even tried the paid version to practice previous content, but it's totally useless, you only review like 2 or 3 recent topics and see the same sentences every few minutes. Edit: Changed from 2 to 1 star due to tons of annoying ads! Maybe it's time to say goodbye to Duolingo.

Edit: changed to 2 stars because i keep finding bugs. I just received an achievement for 175 words learned. My list of learned words shows 130.... App is so full of these minor annoyances that show severe lack of care from the devs. Just drags down the experience. I wouldn't mind being incentivized to pay fos Super Duolingo if the app actually worked. can't recommend it Original review Very buggy. The gamification aspect is nice tho. Repetition is key to learning, works pretty good for that

I use this app to learn Chinese. For some reason, it stopped understanding what I'm saying in speaking exercises. It has a vocabulary, and apparently, I learned more than 1000 worlds, but I can't search these words. It's ok for practice if you are already learning online. However, I'm thinking of using another app. Substracted extra points for failing to give me proper xp boosts. It shows purple screen, I do exercise and don't get promised double xp.

I love this app, I practice my German in the morning and Spanish in the evening. I can practice listening, speaking as well as reading and writing. So why am I only giving it one star? Because it is now only possible to earn 1 star by practicing (a recent change). This means it's almost impossible to finish a lesson, as its so easy to make a small error, often because my keyboard spellchecks and changes my answer to something in its memory. This has changed the app from a useful tool to ****

I've been using duolingo since 2015 when it first launched and I must say it has gotten unbearable. I used to spend hours on it but I feel it's gotten way to gamified and the incessant ads after every single lesson make it unbearable. The animations make a 30 second lesson take 5x longer than it should and overall it just gets boring having to wait between the ads and the animations. The interface used to be much simpler and less complicated too. I think I'll be finding a new way to learn

This app does teach words, however it does a very poor job of explaining anything. Learning mix and match words only does so much. If I'd like any explanation of why something is incorrect, there is none. Except for the random 1 in 100 that explains. Unfortunately it feels like the free version is half an app, although even with paying it still doesn't feel good. Lowered the rating because you can no longer practice to earn hearts. What a joke.

Still a very good app for an easy start into learning languages, but... there's a reason why "Super Learners" are 4.2% more likely to finish their course. And it's not because they're better or faster or get more tools to learn... It's only because the free version is so full of ads at this point, that it's almost unbearable... I understand you need to make money, but it's a real shame to get that many, especially always the same for Duo Super...

Content is very limited and it attempts to make you buy the version for 89/year. That's not necessary. Take the free trial if you're new to the language or likely to make typos. Even 3 days are enough time to see all of the limited content if you use the skip function. A big minus for duolingo is that they don't explain grammar or why something is wrong. Use other sources for that! And also improve your l. skills through other means like reading/tv, texting/speaking with a native speaker

The app itself works okay, the odd glitch notwithstanding, but the lack of meaningful caching of content makes it almost useless. It's hard to understand why it requires near constant internet access, but not being able to spend some time practicing when you have no signal makes it frustrating to use regularly. That's especially true given that the annoying reminders don't seem to need uninterrupted internet access.

1. This app lines up all its buttons to make you have large accidental purchases. 2. Spams an ad for every minute or so of learning. 3. Super buggy and often robs you of in-game rewards for completing tasks. 4. Tournament feature is extremely P2W. Edit: Removing a star for restricting practice. Pretty much impossible to learn a language without this way.

I used to really really love Duolingo, but the latest update made it into pure frustration and dissatisfaction. You now only have 5 hears a day which you CANNOT earn back anymore (1 heart max, which is not enough to complete a lot of exercises). Also, you do not get double XP at the end of "chapters" like you used to. You only get them for quests but they don't work, too many bugs (tells me I got 30 mins of 3x XP but then it turns out to be only 9 mins of 1.5x XP). Etc, etc. Useless&frustrating!

On a 953 streak now, and I think it's safe to say that there are definitely better ways to learn a language than Duolingo. Lessons are very, very repetitive, answers are basically handed to you so you can easily complete lessons without even knowing the content, very few languages have speaking exercises, and there are the occasional connection bugs. Honestly not bad for basics, but if you want to get serious about a language, an actual human who speaks the language is the way to go.

Used to love this app, had a fun experience learning Spanish but the latest update has made it slow to progress since changing the option to earn hearts at your own pace. I was learning as a brain exercise more than anything but now it feels like they are trying to force us to pay subscription fees to make any reasonable progress, bad decision in my opinion, I can see a lot of users leaving. Otherwise a great app but it seems now more into gaining profit over offering free education

I was bombarded with ads to pay for their super account and given a "free trial" and told I'd be given a warning before I was charged a monthly fee. In fact, they charged me for an entire year and I don't think I can cancel it. I'm very upset.

Good app but 2 stars because they've recently restricted the ability to earn extra lives until you've fully run out, and then you can only practice once to get one extra life, rather than being able to top up. It's pretty demotivating if you're doing a tricky module as you can run out of lives and can't earn enough to move on. Please reinstate the ability to top up lives to five again.

Too many forced changes in the last year that go completely against the core of the app: learning a language. Changing the hearts system has made it downright impossible by punishing mistakes. I thought about getting super, but given the company's behaviour, I don't feel comfortable supporting their business model, and wouldn't trust them to not remove even more from the paid subscription.

the UI and lesson style is really good. I'm using the premium subscription to learn Korean. My issue with the app is the the translations itself, it's very poorly translated into English and clearly aimed at American English speakers. for example the phonetic translation into "u" sounds nothing like u, but more like "oo" like "too" or "uo". there's several other examples of poor phonetic transtranslation (bya actually pya). Can't download lessons for flight or train journeys which is a joke too.

This game WAS great, but recent changes to the game have meant that you can only use the practice to renew one life at a time, otherwise you have to use jem; then there's the push to purchase more jems or to pay for the Super Duolingo subscription. Therefore, you end up dropping down the leader boards or the leagues. It's very disheartening, when you've been playing for a while but are not anywhere near expert level to progress 😫

Reduced rating. This app used to be really good. I did subscribe for several years, but then had to take time off due to illness. I was enjoying coming back to it and using for free. However, the latest update has removed the facility of practising to earn up to 5 lives, and the ads constantly pestering you to upgrade to super or max are a nuisance. Unfortunately, I think I may now move to another language app.

Started off as a great app to learn languages. But... You don't get important language rules about sentence structure or word usage. The free usability has been downgraded to the point that it is useful maybe only to beginners. The experience turns unpleasant and frustrating very quick as you are bombarded by aps and forced to spend to be able progress quicker. Snail pace isn't conducive to learning.

Recent introduction of what is effectively a pay wall. Was useful up to later levels. Now I am unable to practice to earn beyond 1 heart, then at the end of exercises, they present grammatical structures that are entire levels above anything else and expect you to get it. It was a useful app, now, not so much. Good to start but you need to supplement to understand the rules and with recent changes, probably best to try elsewhere.

Not worth paying! Duo takes more feature away with each update. At first with the premium account you could do offline courses, that's gone. By adding more language variety, allowing flexible answers, and improving responsiveness to user feedback, the app could do alot more.

I used this application for years and I would write an essay: "Why Duolingo lessons don't teach much by themselves and how to extract benefit from them". The most important topics: 1) There is no normal interval repetition (see Anki for reference) 2) Lack of theory and explanations. 3) The learning material is not well organised (see KanjiDamage) Finally, there is no learning without effort, and the least effort that Duolingo suggests corresponds to the least learning.

I've also had to google a few words because they've been added in my lessons without any introduction and it left me unsure about how they're applied. For example, early in French lessons I had to choose Un and Une and the app never established what the difference was. The trial and error drills are helpful for some things but moments like that need more information and less gameplay.

This app is disgusting now. It used to be a fun little way to learn a few words while you're idle, but now it's become a 'pay to win' app. Learning a language involves making mistakes - but if you make ONE mistake, the app kicks you out of the lesson and makes you repeat easy words for zero reward. If you go for a "free trial", the app takes your money immediately and sticks the trial on the END of your paid period, not before paying. Such a shame to see such scummy scam tactics used now.
